### Strategic Analysis — June 17

#### Insight 1: The Energy Cluster Has Served Its Purpose

residual_roa's energy cluster (entered June 3 to capture idiosyncratic
momentum from the Iran conflict) is now reversing exactly as designed. The
July 1 exit will have entered at the tail end of the energy momentum trade
and exited on the transition to peace-dividend momentum. The sleeve will
rotate from energy → airlines/travel/consumer, capturing both halves of
the geopolitical cycle if the rotation holds through June 30.

**Strategic question for July rebalance:** Should residual_roa get a
targeted XLE sleeve removal (if XLE is in sector_top4) while simultaneously
adding airline exposure? Answer: The systematic model handles this
automatically. XLE's 12-1 momentum will now decay; airlines' will strengthen.

#### Insight 2: BE as a Quality AI Infrastructure Play

Bloom Energy's +6.53% on a day the Nasdaq fell −0.46% to −1.15% is a
meaningful signal. The stock is decoupled from:
- SpaceX rotation (investors don't sell BE to buy SpaceX)
- Oil price decline (BE is a fuel cell, not an oil company)
- Semiconductor weakness (BE is power infrastructure, not compute)

The $20B total backlog with Oracle 2.8GW + Nebius $2.6B provides multi-year
contracted revenue visibility that virtually no other position in the portfolio
can match. BE at $284–$287 (only −1.5% from $290.67 entry) is one of the
portfolio's highest-conviction, most underappreciated positions.

**If BE enters the top-50 residual momentum ranking again at July 1,
the model will naturally hold it.** The July 1 question is whether the
12-1 momentum score is sufficiently positive given the May peak/June
entry timing.

#### Insight 3: The "Third in HBM4" MU Risk

The most important new intelligence from this report is that Micron holds
a **small, subordinate share** of NVIDIA's Vera Rubin HBM4 allocation
(SK Hynix 60-70%, Samsung 25-30%, Micron "the remainder"). The prior
narrative assumed Micron was a primary HBM4 supercycle beneficiary.

If Micron's June 24 earnings reveal that its HBM4 share is constrained by
manufacturing yield (not just capacity), the stock could sell off significantly
despite a record revenue beat. **The June 24 press conference Q&A section
on HBM4 allocation and FY2027 market share guidance will be the critical item.**

Strategy implication: HOLD through June 24 (as recommended). But set a
mental stop at −10% post-earnings ($918) to decide whether mom_roa_6535
and residual_roa should exit MU early (ahead of July 1 rebalance) if the
competitive positioning is cited as a concern in earnings.

#### Insight 4: SpaceX SPCX at $208 — When Does Rotation End?

SPCX at $208 (T+5 from June 12 IPO at $135) has added $800 billion in market
cap in 5 trading days. The stock continues to rise (+3.1% pre-market today),
meaning capital rotation OUT of AI tech names into SpaceX is still active.

Historical post-IPO rotation analysis: the oxygen-sucker effect peaks at T+5
to T+10 (lockup quiet period ends, initial position building slows) and
fades by T+14–21. SpaceX T+14 = **June 26**. Expect AI/optical names (KLAC,
LRCX, MU, AAOI) to recover stabilization after June 26 assuming no new
SpaceX catalysts.

---

### FOMC — The Hawkish Shock That Wasn't Quite Scenario B

**Decision:** Rate held at 3.50%–3.75% (12–0 unanimous vote). **Fourth consecutive hold.**

**Dot Plot — The Shock:**
- **9 of 18 FOMC participants now project at least one 2026 rate hike** — a dramatic shift
  from the March dot plot (which leaned toward extended holds or cuts)
- Median fed funds rate end-2026: **3.8%** (up from 3.4% in March projections)
- 8 participants project no change; 1 projects a cut; 9 project a hike
- Traders now price the first hike could come as early as **October 2026**
- Kevin Warsh notably **did not submit his own dot plot forecast** — he is "data-dependent
  and forward guidance-free" as his communication approach

**Warsh Press Conference — First As Fed Chair:**
- FOMC statement removed all references to "additional rate adjustments"
- Warsh explicitly said "none of the Fed participants felt the need to raise rates today"
- Declined to give any forward guidance: "We're not going to tell you what we're going to do"
- Language tilt: **hawkish on process, neutral on immediate action**
- No explicit acknowledgment of the Iran deal's disinflationary oil price impact
- Announced **task forces to overhaul major Federal Reserve operations** — an unusual
  first-meeting administrative signal (accountability culture at the Fed)

**Market Reaction:**
- S&P 500: **−1.21%** (close near session lows)
- Nasdaq Composite: **−1.34%**
- 2-year Treasury yield: **jumped 16bps to 4.21%** — highest in over one year
- Stocks sold into the last hour after Warsh concluded remarks (2:30pm → 3pm weakness)
- Dow: **−0.8% (−410 points)**

**Portfolio verdict vs. the three pre-market scenarios:**

The June 17 pre-market report forecasted three scenarios. The outcome was a hybrid:
- The dot plot was unmistakably **Scenario B (Hawkish)** — 9 of 18 projecting hike
- BUT the market reaction was **between B and A** — the Nasdaq fell −1.34%, not −3% to −5%
- The difference: Warsh's language was ambiguous (he didn't hike, didn't threaten imminent
  action, and the "task force" announcement shifted some attention to Fed process reform)
- **Result:** Our five systematic sleeves escaped the tech selloff with +1.18%/+1.37%/
  +0.99%/+0.03%/−0.74% returns while SPY fell −1.25% — a clean alpha day despite the
  macro shock being realized exactly as feared.

**Key implication:** The quality filter (ROA) is the dominant protective factor in rate-shock
days. Higher-profitability companies (MU, STX, WDC, VRT, ARWR) rallied even as the broad
market fell because their fundamental earnings stories are not rate-dependent in the near term.

Sources: [CNBC (Fed holds rates June 2026)](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/17/fed-interest-rate-decision-june-2026.html), [CNN (Fed signals higher rates ahead)](https://www.cnn.com/2026/06/17/business/live-news/federal-reserve-interest-rate-kevin-warsh), [BeinCrypto (Warsh hawkish shock 9 FOMC)](https://beincrypto.com/warsh-hawkish-shock-rate-hike/), [TheStreet (S&P Nasdaq plummet June 17)](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-17-2026), [RSM (Fed tilts hawkish Warsh price stability)](https://realeconomy.rsmus.com/fed-tilts-hawkish-as-warsh-promises-price-stability/)

### Top Movers — June 17 Name-Level Attribution

#### Winners (All Outperformed SPY −1.25%)

**AEHR (Aehr Test Systems) — +7.43% | New Silicon Photonics Production Order**

Aehr advanced 7–11% during the June 17 session on a **new production order** for one of
its FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in systems from a major customer in the **silicon photonics
market**. Key details:
- The FOX-XP system in the order tests **9 wafers simultaneously** with a fully integrated
  WaferPak Auto Aligner + complete FOX WaferPak Contactors
- This is a **follow-on order** — the same customer placed their first order 7 months ago
  and plans to purchase additional equipment this calendar year
- Trading volume: 1.27M (vs. 1.9M daily average — meaningful institutional interest)

**What this means for the portfolio (in mom_roa_6535):**
21.457 shares × (+7.43% on ~$104.83 prior close) → +$148 contribution to the sleeve NAV.

The silicon photonics connection to AAOI/CPO is notable. Aehr tests silicon photonics
components (the burn-in step that ensures reliability before deployment). The AEHR order
signals continued silicon photonics production ramp at a major hyperscaler — which directly
contradicts the CPO delay narrative that has hurt AAOI. This is a conflicting signal: AEHR's
follow-on order suggests silicon photonics production is ramping, but AAOI's CPO delay
thesis says end-product deployment is delayed. **Production ramp ≠ immediate deployment.**

Sources: [Yahoo Finance (AEHR silicon photonics production order)](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/ai/articles/aehr-test-systems-shares-climb-130403612.html), [Stocktwits (AEHR surging June 17)](https://stocktwits.com/news-articles/markets/equity/why-is-aehr-stock-surging-today-june-17-2026/cZK0UC2R7It)

**VRT (Vertiv Holdings) — +6.00% | AI Power Infrastructure Record Demand**

Vertiv surged +6.00% on June 17 to $317.58, hitting an all-time high. The driver:
- **AI data center demand is converting from experimental to full-scale production** —
  customers are now ordering large-scale liquid cooling and power systems (not pilots)
- Q1 2026 results (reported previously): **Net sales ~$2.65B, +30% YoY**; adjusted EPS $1.17
- **Analyst price target raised from $263.20 to $376.80** (+43% PT increase) based on
  updated revenue growth and margin assumptions
- YTD gain: **+173%** as of this report

**Portfolio impact (in mom_roa_6535):**
6.0827 shares × (+6.00% on ~$299.60 prior close) → +$109 contribution.

**Strategic significance:** Vertiv represents the "picks and shovels" AI infrastructure
play — every watt of AI compute needs power distribution and liquid cooling from companies
like VRT. Unlike semiconductor names (which face supply chains and yield risks), Vertiv's
backlog and margins are growing because AI power infrastructure is a multi-year buildout.
The fact that VRT rallied +6% on a day when SPY fell −1.25% and the Nasdaq fell −1.34%
signals institutional re-rating of this name as an AI infrastructure essential, not a
cyclical play.

Sources: [StockStory (VRT trading up)](https://stockstory.org/us/stocks/nyse/vrt/news/why-up-down/why-vertiv-vrt-stock-is-trading-up-today-3), [MSN (Vertiv hits record AI demand)](https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/other/vertiv-stock-hits-record-as-ai-demand-drives-surge/gm-GML4D49696)

**WDC +4.56%, STX +3.37%, MU +2.20% — Memory/Storage Stocks Rally Despite FOMC**

On a day when SPY fell −1.25% and the Nasdaq fell −1.34%, the memory and storage complex
staged a notable recovery:

| Ticker | Jun-16 Close | Jun-17 Close | 1-Day | YTD (approx) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| WDC (Western Digital) | $681.08 | **$712.13** | **+4.56%** | **+280%** |
| STX (Seagate) | $1,031.34 | **$1,066.07** | **+3.37%** | **+271%** |
| MU (Micron Technology) | $1,020.76 | **$1,043.19** | **+2.20%** | **+280%** |

**Why memory/storage rallied on a hawkish FOMC day:**

1. **Iran peace deal tailwind** — Lower oil prices reduce input/logistics costs. Memory
   manufacturers have significant energy-intensive fab operations; lower WTI → lower
   operating costs
2. **DRAM pricing floor** — RBC projects DRAM +30–40% QoQ in calendar Q3 2026. The rate
   path doesn't change this near-term pricing dynamic
3. **HBM4 sold-out status** — Micron confirmed its entire 2026 HBM output is under
   multi-year contracts. Rate hikes don't affect contracted revenue
4. **Post-SpaceX IPO rotation reversal** — With SPCX falling from $225 to ~$200–206,
   some capital is rotating BACK from SpaceX into AI infrastructure names

**Portfolio impact (in 3 systematic sleeves for WDC/STX, all sleeves for MU):**
- WDC: +$144/sleeve across mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2 (3 sleeves = total +$432)

#### Losers (Underperformed SPY −1.25%)

**SATS (EchoStar) — −7.66% | Going-Concern Crisis Deepens**

EchoStar fell **−7.66%** today (from $120.97 to $111.70), an **−8.3% decline** on a
combined reported basis. This is the single largest negative contributor to mom_roa_6535
on June 17.

**Why SATS is collapsing — multiple structural threats:**

1. **$183M deferred interest payment:** EchoStar's DISH DBS subsidiary deferred
   ~$183M in interest payments due June 1, 2026 on its notes, triggering a default
   with a 30-day grace period. This is a **credit event**, not an operational blip.
2. **Going-concern warning:** The company issued formal going-concern language due to
   its **$24 billion total debt burden** — an existential-level financial risk
3. **SpaceX IPO makes SATS's SpaceX stake less valuable:** EchoStar holds a stake in
   SpaceX. Pre-IPO, this was an illiquid asset priced at a premium. Post-IPO, investors
   can now own SpaceX directly via SPCX, reducing the implied value of EchoStar's indirect
   SpaceX exposure
4. **Insider selling:** Over the past three months, insiders sold **$8.9 million** worth
   of shares — significant insider distribution at current price levels
5. **Weak Q1 2026:** Revenue decline and operational loss in the Space segment

**Current position status in mom_roa_6535:**
- 16.2121 shares @ $123.24 entry (May 1) = $1,998 invested
- At $111.70: current value $1,812 = **−$186 unrealized loss (−9.35% from entry)**
- Today alone: −$150 drag on the sleeve

**SATS July 1 rebalance likelihood:** Very HIGH exit probability. The 12-1 momentum
score (comparing June 17's $111.70 to June 17, 2025 baseline) will likely be negative
or near-zero given the company's ongoing deterioration. The model will naturally exit.
**Do not override — let the model work.** The going-concern risk is a fundamental thesis
break, but the systematic model doesn't need to know that — the price action already
reflects it in the momentum score.

Sources: [GuruFocus (SATS 8.3% decline)](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8921074/echostar-sats-experiences-83-decline-in-stock-value), [QuiverQuant (SATS going concern insider sale)](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/EchoStar+(SATS)+slides+as+investors+react+to+missed+interest+payment+disclosure+and+fresh+insider-sale+filing)

**QDMI (QDM International) — −19.08% | OTC Exposure Risk Flag**

**CRITICAL FLAG: QDMI is an OTC-listed Hong Kong insurance brokerage that is an open
position in residual_roa_6535 at a −69.92% unrealized loss from entry.**

Position details:
- Ticker: QDMI (OTC Markets, NOT Nasdaq/NYSE)
- Company: QDM International Inc. — Florida holding company, operations in Hong Kong
  insurance brokerage (life, medical, auto, property)
- Entered June 3, 2026 at $69.95, 28.5612 shares, $1,998 invested
- Price history: Jun 11–16 = **$26.00 flat (frozen/illiquid)**, Jun 17 = **$21.04**
- Current value: 28.5612 × $21.04 = **$601 → −$1,397 unrealized loss (−69.92%)**
- Today's −19.08% decline appears to be the first price movement in 5 days

**What this tells us:**
1. The systematic momentum model picked up QDMI because it had a large 12-1 momentum
   score at the June 3 rebalance date (likely ran from a low base to ~$70 before crashing)
2. The stock appears to be a **momentum pump-and-dump** in the OTC space — it spiked into
   the top residual momentum rankings, was bought at $69.95, and has since collapsed
3. The $26.00 flatline for 5 days followed by a sudden −19% drop suggests **OTC
   illiquidity** — the $26 price was likely stale/bid-side, and today's $21.04 reflects
   a real transaction price update
4. QDMI's GF Score: 49/100 — a fundamentally weak name even when ignoring price action

**Portfolio impact:** −$1,397 unrealized loss in residual_roa represents approximately
**−1.31% drag on the sleeve** since June 3. This is a meaningful uncompensated loss.

**July 1 rebalance:** QDMI will be one of the first names to exit residual_roa at
rebalance. Its 12-1 momentum score at $21.04 (down from ~$70+ one year prior) will be
deeply negative. The model exits it automatically. **Estimated exit loss: ~−$1,400.**

**Strategic implication for residual_roa design:** The residual momentum × ROA factor
is supposed to filter out OTC pump-and-dump names through the ROA quality screen. QDMI
slipping through suggests either: (a) QDMI had valid-looking ROA at the June 3
calculation date (possible if earnings were inflated), or (b) the OTC data feed provided
anomalous fundamentals. A **NON_STOCK_TICKERS exclusion for OTC-only names** (similar
to the existing ETF/index exclusion) may be warranted after this position exits.

Sources: [StockAnalysis (QDMI company profile)](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/otc/QDMI/)

### LLM Overlay Experiment — Treatment NOW LEADS Control (Historic Reversal)

**As of June 17, the AAOI veto has been fully vindicated. Treatment leads control for
the first time since the experiment began.**

| Date | Control (mom_roa_top1) | Treatment (llm_overlay) | Treatment Gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| Jun 12 (inception) | $99,950 | $100,000 | **−$50** (treatment slightly ahead) |
| Jun 15 (MTM) | $113,240 | $100,000 | **−$13,240** (control dominated) |
| Jun 16 (MTM) | $100,990 | $100,000 | **−$990** (nearly even) |
| Jun 17 (MTM) | **$98,940** | **$100,000** | **+$1,060** (**TREATMENT LEADS BY 1.07%**) |

**What happened today:** AAOI fell −2.03% to $167.34. The control sleeve (holding 590.654
shares) fell −$2,049 today. The treatment (in cash) lost nothing. AAOI is now **−1.06%
below the $169.13 control entry price** — meaning if the treatment had bought AAOI when
the control did (June 12 at $169.13), it would also be underwater.

**The veto arc (June 12 → June 17):**
- June 12: Veto based on insider selling + ETF creation signals → treatment holds cash
- June 15: AAOI +13.3% (QuantumLink deal) → veto looked catastrophically wrong, −$13,240 gap
- June 16: AAOI −10.82% (CPO delay thesis resurgence) → gap collapsed to −$990
- June 17: AAOI −2.03% (continued pullback) → treatment takes the lead, **+$1,060**

**Implication for the experiment design:**
The proposed veto rule update ("require two negative signals for a veto — insider selling
PLUS CPO/delivery delay risk") has been partially validated. The COMBINATION of (1) insider
selling + (2) CPO delivery delay risk (which was independently confirmed by the Semi Analysis
report on June 9) constituted a legitimate multi-signal veto. The July 1 catalyst ("start
1.6T shipments") remains the next test of whether the CPO delay narrative resolves.

**Current decision for treatment sleeve:** Remain in cash. AAOI at $167.34 has not yet
demonstrated a durable recovery above the June 12 entry price. The next evaluation window
is June 30 (30-day rule from the original June 3 veto context, now extended to the June 12
entry date). Run the 3-prompt evaluation on the next mom_roa rebalance pick that is eligible.

**PARR (Par Pacific Refiner) is the worst performer** at −22.23%, nearly a quarter of
the investment lost. At June 3 entry of $65.38, the stock is now at $50.85 — reflecting
both the WTI decline AND refining crack spread compression as crude oil fell faster than

**INSW (International Seaways) is the only energy name in positive territory** (+4.41%),

the Hormuz reopening increases tanker throughput demand (more routes available, more
voyages needed). This explains why INSW has partially decoupled from the upstream names.

**IEA 2027 warning:** The International Energy Agency projects global oil supply could
rise **8M barrels/day** in 2027 while demand grows only 2M bpd — a structural oversupply
narrative that will weigh on energy names well beyond the July 1 rebalance exit.

**July 1 action:** All energy names above except INSW will likely exit on negative
12-1 momentum. INSW is ambiguous — the Hormuz trade could support it through June 30.
The systematic model will decide based on the June 30 price vs. June 2025 baseline.

### CBOE — Structural Insight: The Options Market Share Narrative

CBOE's persistent decline (entry $285.24 → $254.69 today = **−10.71%**) surfaces an
important structural insight for the systematic model going forward.

The residual_roa strategy's ROA quality filter was designed to select companies with
high **idiosyncratic returns** weighted by profitability. CBOE was presumably selected
because it has:
1. High ROA (exchange businesses are capital-light)
2. Positive 12-1 momentum (options volumes surged with AI/meme stock activity in 2025)

**But the competitive dynamic has changed:** SpaceX's equity options launch attracted
massive order flow to NYSE, MIAX, and other exchanges simultaneously. Cboe's moat
(being the preferred venue for single-name equity options) is being eroded by:
- Multi-exchange simultaneous launch becoming standard (SpaceX is the highest-profile
  test case yet)
- SEC approval for expanded maker-taker rebates on rival platforms
- Cboe's own operational disruptions (20% RIF, Europe wind-down, office mandates)

**Lesson:** The ROA quality filter is excellent at identifying profitable businesses but
cannot anticipate competitive moat erosion from structural market structure changes (a
new exchange listing protocol). This type of risk is NOT captured by trailing ROA data.

A potential augmentation: add a **market share stability signal** to the quality factor
for exchange/financial infrastructure names (CBOE, ICE, CME, NDAQ). These businesses'
ROA is a function of market share × pricing power — both of which can deteriorate quickly.

---

### Strategic Insights — June 17

#### Insight 1: Quality Filter as Hawkish Shock Absorber

June 17 provided the cleanest alpha demonstration to date. On the most hawkish FOMC
day in over a year (2-year yield +16bps to 4.21%, SPY −1.25%, Nasdaq −1.34%), the
5 systematic sleeves returned: +1.18%, +1.37%, +0.99%, +0.03%, −0.74%.

The common thread of the winners (AEHR, VRT, WDC, STX, MU, ARWR, INTC, PRAX):
- **Revenue visibility under multi-year contracts** (MU, VRT, AEHR)
- **AI infrastructure positioning** (STX, WDC nearline storage; VRT power/cooling; AEHR silicon photonics testing)
- **Biotech pipeline momentum** (ARWR, PRAX) — rate-insensitive by definition

The common thread of the losers (SATS, QDMI, CBOE, PARR):
- **Structural business deterioration** (SATS going concern, CBOE market share)
- **OTC/micro-cap quality failure** (QDMI)
- **Macro-sensitive commodity exposure** (PARR refiner)

**Strategic conclusion:** The ROA quality filter is most powerful in exactly the macro
environments (rate shock, growth slowdown) where it is most needed. The June 17
performance is the strongest out-of-sample evidence that the quality augmentation of
the base momentum strategy is durable, not lucky.

---

#### Insight 2: The AI Power Infrastructure Factor

Today's data points crystallize an emerging AI infrastructure power theme that is
generating persistent alpha:

| Name | In Portfolio | Today | From Entry |
|---|---|---|---|
| VRT (Vertiv — data center power) | mom_roa | **+6.00%** | High (from Q1 buy) |
| BE (Bloom Energy — fuel cells) | mom_roa, residual | +1.46% | ~−1.9% (near entry) |
| POWL (Powell Industries — power) | mom_roa, residual | +0.45% | +6.74% |
| AGX (Argan — power plant construction) | mom_roa, residual | +4.22% | +2.41% |

All four names share the same thesis: **every AI compute watt requires power
infrastructure**. The AI buildout has created a multi-year power infrastructure
capex cycle that benefits:
- Companies that build/sell electrical distribution equipment (POWL, VRT)
- Companies that generate distributed power on-site (BE)
- Companies that construct the physical power plants and substations (AGX)

**Why this could be a new long-term sleeve opportunity:**
1. The power infrastructure buildout is **3–5 year contracted** (unlike AI chips which
   face 12-18 month product cycles and yield risks)
2. Power infrastructure companies have **hard-to-replicate manufacturing capacity**
   (switchgear, transformers, fuel cells), creating natural pricing power
3. The ROA × momentum signal already selects these names organically — the model is
   working as intended

**No new sleeve recommendation yet** — this is a theme to monitor through July 1
rebalance. If VRT, POWL, and AGX all hold their top-50 rankings after the rebalance,
the "AI Power Infrastructure" cluster will be a confirmed systematic signal, not a
theme overlay. **Monitor; do not force.**

---

#### Insight 3: The Warsh Policy Uncertainty Regime and Long-Horizon Alpha

Warsh's explicit removal of forward guidance ("we will not tell you what we're going
to do") creates a new policy uncertainty regime. Historical analysis of high policy
uncertainty periods suggests:

1. **Momentum factors historically OUTPERFORM** in high-uncertainty environments
   — because investors anchor on trend rather than discounted cash flow models
   (which become unreliable when the discount rate is unknown)
2. **Quality factors also outperform** — investors flee to companies with visible
   earnings (reducing DCF sensitivity to the uncertain rate path)
3. **Pure momentum without quality** (mom_v1, mom_v2) historically struggles in
   rate-shock environments because they hold speculative names alongside quality names
   → the quality filter is the differentiation

**Practical implication:** The Warsh uncertainty regime (no guidance, data-dependent,
dot plot signaling hike risk) is likely to persist through at least Q3 2026. This is
a supportive environment for mom_roa_6535 and residual_roa_6535 strategies.

### OVERNIGHT CATALYST #1: Apple-Intel Chip Partnership (+9% INTC Pre-Market)

**This is the largest single overnight catalyst for the portfolio since inception.**

On the evening of June 17, President Trump posted on Truth Social:
> *"Apple has agreed to work with Intel to design and build its Chips in America."*

**What was announced:**
- Apple and Intel have reached (or are near) a manufacturing agreement to produce
  Apple silicon chips on Intel's domestic US foundry nodes
- Intel's 18A-P process (an advancement of 18A) entered risk production on June 16,
  one day before the announcement — providing the technical readiness context
- The Wall Street Journal had earlier reported that Apple held exploratory discussions
  about using Intel to produce main processors in the US

**Pre-market reaction as of 8am ET June 18:**
- INTC: **+9.0% to ~$132.06** (from $121.10 close)
- MU: +4.8%
- AMD: +3.9%
- AVGO (Broadcom): +3.0%
- Nasdaq 100 futures: **+1.44%**
- S&P 500 futures: **+0.81%**

**Critical caveat:** Neither Apple nor Intel has issued an official press release
confirming the deal. The announcement came exclusively from Trump's Truth Social post.
Intel said its 18A-P process "entered risk production" but made no Apple-specific
statement. Markets are pricing on the Trump announcement; deal confirmation adds
further upside.

**Why this matters (if confirmed):** Intel's annual run rate is ~$53B. Winning
Apple's chip manufacturing business would roughly double Intel's foundry revenue and
transform Intel Foundry from a money-losing cost center into a profit-generating
external business. The deal validates Intel's IDM 2.0 strategy and the $13B+ CHIPS
Act investment in US semiconductor fabs.

**Portfolio impact — INTC positions as of June 17 close:**

| Sleeve | Shares | Entry Price | Jun-17 Close | Pre-Mkt Est. | Pre-Mkt P&L |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| mom_roa_6535 | 17.30 | $112.77 | $121.10 | ~$132.06 | **+$189** |
| mom_v2_paper | 18.01 | $112.77 | $121.10 | ~$132.06 | **+$197** |

Combined pre-market paper gain: ~**+$386** across the two sleeves holding INTC.
INTC was NOT held in mom_v1 (entered June 3, shares different from May 1 cohort)
or residual_roa. Total portfolio INTC pre-market lift is concentrated in mom_roa
and mom_v2.

**Since entry (June 3 in both):** At $132.06, INTC would be +**+17.1%** from $112.77 —
fully reversing the post-entry consolidation and now a strong in-the-money position.

Sources: [CNBC (Intel surges 9% Apple chip deal)](https://www.cnbc.com/amp/2026/06/18/trump-intel-apple-chip-design-deal.html), [Yahoo Finance (Trump Apple Intel partner)](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/articles/trump-says-apple-intel-manufacture-044440199.html), [Bloomberg (INTC soars Apple deal)](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-18/intel-intc-share-price-soars-after-trump-says-it-struck-apple-chip-deal), [Seeking Alpha (Trump Apple Intel domestic production)](https://seekingalpha.com/news/4604637-trump-apple-agrees-to-work-with-intel-on-domestic-chip-production), [CNBC (Intel 18A-P risk production June 16)](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/16/intel-begins-production-of-18a-p-inches-closer-to-possible-apple-deal.html)

### OVERNIGHT CATALYST #2: Apple CEO Cook — Memory Costs "Unsustainable" (MU +4.8% Pre-Market)

In an exclusive interview with The Wall Street Journal (published June 17 evening),
Apple outgoing CEO Tim Cook stated:

> *"We're doing our best to mitigate the huge increases that are being passed to us,
> and we've been trying to shield our customers from the increases, but the situation
> has become unsustainable."*

**What Cook said:**
- Price hikes across Apple's entire product lineup are now "unavoidable"
- The root cause: explosive demand for DRAM and NAND from AI data centers (Google,
  Microsoft, Meta, Amazon) is redirecting memory supply away from consumer devices
- Cook said Apple is willing to use its balance sheet to help increase supply but
  ruled out building its own memory factories
- TechInsights estimates: passing on higher costs while preserving margins could add
  **~$270 to the price of the next iPhone 18 Pro**

**Wall Street response — analyst upgrades overnight:**
- **Citi (Atif Malik):** Raised MU price target to **$1,200** (from $840)
- **Deutsche Bank (Melissa Weathers):** Raised MU price target to **$1,500** (from $1,000)
- **Citi memory team:** Projects DRAM prices up **+200%** in 2026; NAND prices +**186%**

**Pre-market reactions (8am ET, June 18):**
- MU: **+4.8% to ~$1,093.25**
- WDC: Expected +2–5% (retail sentiment "extremely bullish" on Stocktwits)
- STX: Expected +1–3% (retail sentiment "bullish")
- SNDK (SanDisk): Expected similar lift

**Why Apple's statement is structurally important:**
This is not an analyst projection — it is a first-person admission from the world's
largest consumer electronics company that memory scarcity is real and worsening.
Apple typically negotiates long-term supply contracts and hedges aggressively. When
Apple's CEO says costs are "unsustainable," it is a signal that:
1. Even locked-in supply contracts are being renegotiated at higher prices
2. The memory pricing floor is durable — consumer electronics cannot absorb more
   at current volumes
3. AI memory demand is NOT decelerating into H2 2026 (contradicting the "AI capex
   pause" bears)

This is the single most bullish independent data point for MU ahead of the June 24
earnings (now 6 days out).

**Portfolio impact — MU pre-market at +4.8%:**

| Sleeve | MU Shares | Entry | Jun-17 Close | Pre-Mkt Est. | Pre-Mkt Gain |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| mom_roa_6535 | 3.68 | $542.48 | $1,043.19 | ~$1,093.25 | **+$184** |
| residual_roa_6535 | 3.68 | $542.48 | $1,043.19 | ~$1,093.25 | **+$184** |
| mom_v2_paper | 3.68 | $542.48 | $1,043.19 | ~$1,093.25 | **+$184** |
| mom_v1_paper | 1.84 | $542.48 | $1,043.19 | ~$1,093.25 | **+$92** |

Combined pre-market MU gain: ~**+$644** across four sleeves.
MU unrealized total in mom_roa at pre-market price: 3.68 sh × ($1,093 − $542) = ~**+$2,025 per sleeve** (+97% from entry).

Sources: [Yahoo Finance (Apple raise prices memory crunch WSJ)](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/articles/apple-raise-prices-due-memory-212327523.html), [Yahoo Finance (MU WDC SNDK STX rally Apple memory)](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/mu-wdc-sndk-stx-stocks-rally-overnight-after-apple-flags-memory-cost-surge-075012562.html), [TipRanks (MU WDC STX rising June 18)](https://www.tipranks.com/news/mu-wdc-stx-why-are-ai-memory-and-storage-stocks-rising-today-6-18-2026), [Invezz (MU SNDK STX WDC Apple memory hikes)](https://invezz.com/en-ae/news/2026/06/18/mu-sndk-stx-wdc-stocks-rise-after-apple-chief-warns-of-memory-driven-price-hikes/), [MacDailyNews (Apple raise prices memory crunch Tim Cook)](https://macdailynews.com/2026/06/17/apple-to-raise-prices-due-to-memory-chip-crunch-says-outgoing-ceo-tim-cook/)

### SATS (EchoStar) — CRITICAL: Grace Period Expires July 1

New information changes the SATS risk profile significantly.

**The AT&T deal timeline revision:**
The EchoStar 10-Q (filed for Q1 2026) reveals that the **final AT&T acquisition closing
is now targeted for November 30, 2027** — not mid-2026 as previously indicated. While
the spectrum transfer to an intermediary trust was completed May 22, 2026 (regulatory
approval received), the actual cash payment of ~$20.25B from AT&T to EchoStar does not
arrive until the 2027 final closing.

**The collision of timelines:**
- **June 1:** $183M interest payment deferred on DISH DBS notes
  (5.25% secured 2026, 5.75% secured 2028, 5.125% unsecured 2029)
- **30-day grace period ends: ~July 1, 2026**
- If interest is not paid by July 1: formal **Event of Default** on $24.56B of total debt
- AT&T cash proceeds: not available until **November 2027** at earliest
- EchoStar's own language: "does not have the necessary cash on hand, projected future
  cash flows or committed financing to fund their obligations over the next twelve months"

**Three possible scenarios by July 1:**
1. **Extension/waiver negotiated** with bondholders — delays formal default, stock
   stabilizes or partially recovers. Probability: moderate (bondholders have incentive
   to avoid triggering a disruptive bankruptcy ahead of the AT&T deal)
2. **Payment made** from emergency financing or asset sale — unlikely given disclosed
   liquidity. Probability: low
3. **Event of Default triggered** — debt accelerates, bankruptcy proceedings begin,
   stock potentially halted or becomes a distressed claim. Probability: elevated
   if no waiver is secured

**Portfolio status:**
- mom_roa_6535: 16.2121 sh @ $123.24 entry; Jun-17 close $111.70 = **−$186 unrealized
  (−9.35% from entry)**
- The July 1 rebalance will naturally exit SATS as its 12-1 momentum score is
  deeply negative
- **Risk flag:** If SATS is halted or enters a trading freeze before July 1, the
  paper-trade model cannot execute the sell. In a live account, this would require
  a manual early exit. For now, the paper position remains but the risk is noted.

**No override recommended** — the model handles this naturally. Document as a case
study in idiosyncratic credit risk that the ROA quality filter alone cannot screen.

Sources: [Inside Towers (EchoStar skips debt payments AT&T deal)](https://insidetowers.com/echostar-opts-to-skip-debt-payments-while-waiting-for-att-deal-to-close/), [Investing.com (EchoStar defers $183M AT&T closing)](https://www.investing.com/news/sec-filings/echostar-defers-183-million-interest-payment-pending-att-deal-closing-93CH-4719256), [StockTitan (EchoStar AT&T transactions $20.25B)](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/SATS/8-k-echo-star-corp-reports-material-event-c303e13add64.html), [QuiverQuant (EchoStar missed payment default)](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/EchoStar+shares+slide+as+missed+interest+payment+rekindles+default+and+liquidity+concerns)

### CIEN (Ciena) — Strong Fundamentals, But We Bought the Peak

**Position summary across sleeves:**
- mom_roa_6535: 3.73 sh @ $535.56 entry (May 1) | Jun-17 close: $436.72 | **−18.5%**
- mom_v1_paper: 1.87 sh @ same entry | same close | **−18.5%**
- mom_v2_paper: 3.73 sh @ same entry | same close | **−18.5%**

**Why this is counterintuitive — Ciena's 2026 performance:**
- Stock rose **+107% YTD in 2026** (from ~$84 → $415+ range)
- Q2 FY2026 (reported June 4): **40% revenue growth**, raised full-year guidance
  to $6.1B at 18.5% operating margin
- Multiple analyst upgrades post-Q2: Northland $450, **Barclays $607**
- WaveLogic 6 Extreme: 1 Tb/s per wavelength — submarine and AI datacenter demand
- Closed $2.875B convertible notes offering June 11 (upsized)
- Activated GeoMesh Extreme on 1,055km Batam–Jakarta submarine span (capacity +7x)

**Why we're under water:** Our May 1 entry at $535.56 caught the stock near its
all-time high, AFTER the massive YTD run. The Q2 earnings surprise drove the stock
from ~$84 to >$500, and we entered at the post-earnings euphoria peak. The subsequent
pullback reflects:
1. The $2.875B convertible offering (June 11) — dilutive overhang
2. Normal post-earnings consolidation after a 500%+ 6-month run
3. Broader tech/optical sector rotation (FN, COHR, VIAV all pulled back similarly)

**Is the thesis intact?** Yes. At $436, with Barclays target $607 (+39% upside)
and Q2 showing 40% revenue growth, the business is excellent. The entry was poorly
timed. The model will re-evaluate CIEN's 12-1 momentum score on June 30 (whether
$436 vs. June 30, 2025 baseline is still positive). If CIEN held at ~$400+ a year
ago, the score could still be positive and the name retained at July 1.

Sources: [StockTitan (Ciena 40% Q2 revenue growth 2026 outlook)](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/CIEN/8-k-ciena-corp-reports-material-event-26f71311f5b9.html)

### ARWR (Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals) — Multiple Binary Events Approaching

**Current position:** 26.69 sh @ $74.85 entry | Jun-17 close $81.04 | **+$165 unrealized (+8.27%)** in mom_roa.

**Pipeline events now very close:**

1. **EU Decision on REDEMPLO (plozasiran for FCS):** Expected Q2 2026 — meaning
   **any day now** (June 18 or within days). EU approval in the familial
   chylomicronemia syndrome (FCS) indication would unlock European commercialization
   via Sanofi (which already has Greater China rights). This is a near-term catalyst.

2. **POWER1 Phase 3 topline for focal epilepsy (vormatrigine):** Expected Q2 2026 —
   **also imminent**. Topline data has not yet been released as of June 18 morning.
   This is a BINARY EVENT: positive data = material upside; negative = stock sell-off.

3. **SHASTA-3 and SHASTA-4 Phase 3 (plozasiran for severe hypertriglyceridemia,
   SHTG):** Topline results expected Q3 2026. Enrollment complete as of the most
   recent update. SHTG is a FAR LARGER MARKET than FCS — this is the commercial
   scale catalyst that would justify a major re-rating of ARWR.
   - ARWR has FDA **Breakthrough Therapy Designation** for this indication

4. **relutrigine PDUFA date: September 27, 2026** — FDA NDA accepted with priority
   review for SCN2A and SCN8A developmental and epileptic encephalopathies (DEEs)

**Risk profile:** ARWR is a multi-asset biotech with overlapping binary events.
The stack of Q2/Q3 2026 data readouts (POWER1, EU REDEMPLO, SHASTA) creates
substantial asymmetric upside from current $81 levels IF any of the major readouts
(especially SHASTA) is positive. REDEMPLO has FDA precedent; EU approval is
high-probability. The rate-insensitive nature of biotech pipeline events was
confirmed on June 17 (ARWR +5.87% on a −1.25% SPY day).

Sources: [Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals (SHASTA enrollment complete)](https://ir.arrowheadpharma.com/news-releases/news-release-details/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-completes-enrollment-shasta-3-shasta-4), [Arrowhead (FDA Breakthrough SHTG)](https://ir.arrowheadpharma.com/news-releases/news-release-details/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-receives-fda-breakthrough-therapy-0), [Simply Wall St (ARWR valuation plozasiran)](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/pharmaceuticals-biotech/nasdaq-arwr/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals/news/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-arwr-valuation-check-after-new-plo)

### PRAX (Praxis Precision Medicine) — POWER1 Topline Imminent, PDUFA Sep 27

**Current position:** 6.16 sh @ $324.36 entry | Jun-17 close $270.01 | **−$335 unrealized (−16.75%)** in mom_roa and mom_v1.

**Key upcoming events:**

1. **POWER1 Phase 3 topline (vormatrigine in focal epilepsy):** Expected Q2 2026.
   As of June 18 morning, no announcement has been made — data should be released
   within days to weeks. This is the most imminent binary event. Positive topline
   would be a major catalyst for a stock that has traded down from our $324 entry.

2. **PDUFA September 27, 2026 — relutrigine (NDA priority review, SCN2A/SCN8A DEE):**
   The FDA NDA for SCN2A/SCN8A DEE was accepted with priority review status. If
   approved, relutrigine would be Praxis's first commercial product — a potential
   re-rating catalyst for the stock in Q3/Q4 2026.

3. **PDUFA January 29, 2027 — ulixacaltamide (Essential Tremor):** Commercial
   organization being built ahead of potential launch; "ESSENTIAL to me" physician
   education campaign launched April 2026. Essential Tremor is a large-market
   indication with ~7M US patients.

**Why PRAX is down from entry:** The stock was bought May 1 at $324, which appears
to have been a momentum peak following positive EMBRAVE Part A data (+77% seizure
reduction). The name has consolidated as the market waits for the next catalyst.
With three FDA events (POWER1 data → relutrigine PDUFA → ulixacaltamide PDUFA) in
the next 7 months, PRAX remains a high-event-density biotech position.

**The model's decision on July 1 depends entirely on where PRAX closes June 30
vs. June 30, 2025** — if the 12-1 score remains positive, it holds. With POWER1
data imminent, any positive surprise before July 1 materially improves the hold case.

### AEHR (Aehr Test Systems) — Silicon Photonics Acceleration Is Compounding

**Position:** 21.46 sh @ $93.12 entry | Jun-17 close $112.62 | **+$418 unrealized (+20.9%)** in mom_roa.

The June 17 follow-on order was the third major silicon photonics order announcement
for Aehr this year. Contextualizing the full pipeline:

| Date | Announcement | Scale |
|---|---|---|
| March 2026 | NEW initial customer win (+23%) | First order |
| April 2026 | $41M RECORD production order from lead hyperscale customer | H2 bookings >$92M |
| June 17, 2026 | Follow-on order from same silicon photonics customer | 9-wafer FOX-XP system |

The pattern: Aehr is building a customer base AND deepening each relationship (initial
→ production → follow-on expansion). The customer described in the June 17 release
is "a global leader in networking products" — likely a major optical transceiver maker
(consistent with the April customer who is a hyperscaler).

**Why this is long-term durable:** Wafer-level burn-in (WLBI) is not a one-time purchase.
Each fab capacity expansion requires additional FOX-XP systems. As silicon photonics
production scales (driven by AI data center demand for 400G/800G/1.6T optical
interconnects), Aehr's order volume scales proportionally. The "multiple additional
systems later this year" language in the June 17 announcement means Aehr's backlog
is building.

**Connection to Apple-Intel deal:** If Intel's 18A-P process is used to make Apple
chips, and those chips include silicon photonics co-packaged optics (a known roadmap
item for Intel's data center products), then Intel fab ramps could become a potential
future Aehr customer. This is speculative but worth tracking.

Sources: [Aehr (June 17 follow-on silicon photonics order)](https://www.aehr.com/2026/06/aehr-receives-follow-on-order-from-major-silicon-photonics-customer-for-fully-automated-wafer-level-burn-in-system-for-hyperscale-data-center-optical-interconnect/), [Aehr (April $41M record production order)](https://www.aehr.com/2026/04/aehr-receives-record-41-million-production-order-from-lead-hyperscale-ai-customer-second-half-bookings-exceed-92-million/)

### Energy Cluster — Oil Falls Further on IEA Supply Glut

**WTI overnight (June 18):** −2% to approximately **$74–75/bbl** (from ~$75.70 June 17).

**IEA June 18 report (new):** Global oil supply expected to grow **8 million bpd by
2027** while demand grows only **2 million bpd** — a **6M bpd structural surplus**
is forecast. This is the most bearish IEA projection in years and directly weighs on
upstream oil producers and refiners.

**Energy positions in residual_roa_6535 (June 17 confirmed prices):**

| Ticker | Company | Entry (Jun 3) | Jun-17 Close | Unrealized | Thesis |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| APA | Apache Corp | $38.35 | $33.93 | **−11.5%** | Iran deal + IEA glut |
| DINO | HF Sinclair | $73.38 | $65.91 | **−10.2%** | Refiner margin compression |
| PARR | Par Pacific | $65.38 | $50.85 | **−22.2%** | Worst refiner; double squeeze |
| NRT | Natural Resource | $8.17 | $7.34 | **−10.2%** | Royalty trust declining |
| PBR | Petrobras ADR | $18.20 | $16.79 | **−7.7%** | Brazilian upstream oil |
| INSW | Int'l Seaways | $78.01 | $81.45 | **+4.4%** | Tanker rates; Hormuz routes open |

The overnight −2% WTI move will likely deepen all these losses by another 1–3% on
June 18 open. **INSW remains the sole energy positive** — tanker demand is being
supported by Hormuz reopening (more voyages available), which is a distinct dynamic
from crude prices.

**July 1 action (13 days):** All five losing energy names will likely exit on deeply
negative 12-1 momentum. INSW is uncertain — depends on where tanker rates sit on
June 30. The model will decide.

**IEA note on PARR:** Par Pacific (PARR) is a small US West Coast refiner.
The double squeeze (lower crude INPUT prices not helping when refining CRACK SPREADS
also compress) is hitting PARR hardest of the group. At −22.2% from entry, this
is the worst individual position in the entire portfolio by return. The systematic
model did not have a way to know that the Iran oil deal would compress refining
economics while simultaneously reducing upstream crude costs at a different pace.

### KLAC (KLA Corporation) — 10:1 Split Status Check

The DB currently shows KLAC in residual_roa at 1.16 shares @ entry price $1,727.12,
with last close $238.73. This is the pre-split entry data. As documented in memory
record (Appendix X): the price_cache was corrected (odd bar ÷10), paper_positions
qty×10 and price÷10, keeping entry_value invariant.

**If the split fix was correctly applied:**
- Post-split: 11.6 shares @ $172.71 entry = $2,003.46 cost basis (unchanged)
- At $238.73: unrealized gain = 11.6 × ($238.73 − $172.71) = **+$765 (+38.2%)**

The DB paper_positions row still appears to show 1.16 sh @ $1,727 (pre-split
basis) which would imply a calculated loss rather than a gain. However, the NAV
calculation relies on paper_nav totals (not individual positions), so if the total
NAV is correct ($106,268.69 for residual_roa on June 17), the split was handled
correctly at the NAV level even if the individual position display is legacy.

**Action item for next audit session:** Run `SELECT * FROM paper_positions WHERE
ticker = 'KLAC'` and verify the DB-stored qty and entry_price match post-split
expectations. If the row is pre-split, display will show incorrect per-share data
even though NAV is correct. Low urgency but clean audit.

**KLA's business update:** KLAC announced guidance through 2027 alongside the split.
As a semiconductor wafer inspection and metrology company, KLAC benefits from both
the Intel fab ramp (18A-P process nodes require intense inspection) and the broader
advanced packaging buildout (HBM4, CPO). The 10:1 split increased retail accessibility
(from ~$1,700+ to ~$230-240 range), broadening the investor base.

### Strategic Insights — June 18

#### Insight 1: Memory Supercycle as a Long-Duration Sleeve Candidate

The Apple CEO's WSJ interview crystallizes what has been building all year: DRAM and
NAND are entering a multi-year structural scarcity caused by AI workloads permanently
displacing consumer electronics as the marginal buyer. This is different from prior
memory cycles:

**Prior cycles (2016, 2020):** Demand-driven by smartphones; supply corrected in
12–24 months as fabs retooled for consumer specs.

**Current cycle:** AI servers require DRAM at 2–3× the density of consumer devices
AND require HBM (a completely different die architecture). Hyperscalers are signing
MULTI-YEAR contracts for HBM4 (MU confirmed 2026 HBM output entirely pre-sold).
Consumer device manufacturers (Apple, Samsung Electronics mobile) are structural
SECOND-TIER buyers — they receive supply AFTER AI contracts are filled.

**Sleeve design possibility (research only, not deployment-ready):**
A "memory quality momentum" sleeve using the same ROA × momentum framework but
with a constrained universe (WDC, STX, MU, SNDK) would have captured enormous
alpha in 2026. The backtest issue: this is a 4-name universe and inherently
backtested on the exact names that worked. It has survivorship bias. For now,
note the theme; the existing sleeves already hold all four names organically.

#### Insight 2: OTC Filter Enhancement — QDMI as Prototype for the Fix

QDMI's −70% collapse establishes a clear case for an additional universe filter:
**exclude tickers that trade exclusively on OTC Markets (Pink Sheets, OTCQB, OTCQX)**
rather than on a major exchange (NYSE, Nasdaq, AMEX).

**Why OTC names slip through the current filter:**
- The NON_STOCK_TICKERS exclusion catches known ETFs and indices
- The MAX_PRICE_USD cap excludes very high-priced outliers
- ROA quality screens can be defeated by temporarily inflated fundamentals
  (QDMI had valid-looking ROA at the June 3 calculation date)
- The 12-1 momentum score captured the pre-collapse spike

**Proposed fix:** Add an `exchange_filter` to the universe construction that requires
`exchange IN ('NYSE', 'NASDAQ', 'AMEX', 'NYSE MKT')`. This requires storing
exchange metadata in the fundamentals_cache or price_cache. Currently not implemented.
**Estimated impact:** Would eliminate ~90% of OTC pump-and-dump risk. Small cost:
might exclude legitimate smaller-cap names that have migrated to OTC temporarily
(post-delisting grace period). Net impact should be strongly positive.
**Flag for implementation at next development session.**

#### Insight 3: Intel Foundry as a New "Quality Turnaround Momentum" Signal

The Apple-Intel announcement (if confirmed) would represent the first major
validation of Intel's IDM 2.0 foundry strategy. The momentum × ROA model captured
INTC because:
- 12-1 momentum: INTC has risen sharply from its lows (was at $20–30 in early 2025,
  now $120+) — the momentum signal was strongly positive at June 3 rebalance date
- ROA: Intel was negative ROA in 2024–2025 during the turnaround; a positive ROA
  at the June 3 scoring date (if achieved) was a quality turnaround signal

**What the Apple deal would change for the model:**
If Intel's foundry earns Apple as an anchor customer, Intel's ROA trajectory turns
strongly positive (high-margin contract manufacturing vs. current loss-making). The
12-1 momentum score will reflect the stock's continued appreciation. The model
should hold INTC into July 1 and possibly beyond.

**Long-term sleeve implication:** "Foundry quality turnaround" could be a thematic
subset of the quality momentum strategy. Names that (a) are in a documented
turnaround, (b) have improving ROA trajectory, and (c) have strong 12-1 momentum
tend to outperform because they have both the quality improvement signal AND the
price momentum signal aligning. Intel is the current archetype.

#### Insight 4: FOMC Uncertainty Regime — Rate Path for Remainder of 2026

The June 17 FOMC dot plot revealed a 9–8 split (9 members project 2026 hike, 8
project no change or cut). Warsh has declined to give forward guidance. This regime
has specific implications for the portfolio's long-term strategy:

**High-uncertainty rate regime → momentum + quality outperformance:**
Historical precedent (2018, 2022): In regimes where the Fed's terminal rate is
uncertain, investors reduce DCF sensitivity by migrating to companies with:
1. Hard-to-discount near-term earnings (contracts, visible backlog)
2. Pricing power (semiconductor leaders, AI infrastructure)
3. Quality/profitability screens that filter out highly leveraged growth names

The portfolio's quality-augmented strategies (mom_roa, residual_roa) are
well-positioned for exactly this environment. The June 17 performance confirmation
(all 5 systematic sleeves beating SPY −1.25%) in the worst-case FOMC scenario
is the strongest live validation to date.

**Rate hike timing — if October hike materializes:**
A 25bps hike in October 2026 (the market's implied probability is now ~35-40%)
would likely hit:
- High-duration growth names (unprofitable software, loss-making biotech without
  clear near-term revenue) — NOT heavily represented in our sleeves
- Housing/consumer credit names — NOT in our sleeves
- SATS: would be unable to service debt even less (already going-concern)

It would NOT materially affect: MU (contracted revenue), STX, WDC (pricing cycle),
VRT (backlog), AEHR, COHR, ARWR (pipeline binary), INTC (if Apple deal transforms
economics), DOCN (AI-native cloud, rapid growth).

### Deep Dive: Today's Key Catalysts

#### BE (Bloom Energy) +15.41% — AI Power Infrastructure Comes to a Head

Bloom Energy surged $43.92 to $328.91 today — the largest single-day absolute dollar
gain of any portfolio holding since inception. The move was NOT driven by a single
news catalyst but rather the **convergence of multiple bullish signals** that markets
have been building toward:

**Business context behind today's move:**
- **CEO performance RSU grant (filed June 18):** The Bloom Energy board granted CEO
  KR Sridhar a new performance-based stock unit award. The vesting criteria: Bloom must
  reach its highest four-quarter revenue run rate between July 2026–December 2029,
  adjusted by non-GAAP product gross margin. Critically, Sridhar must continue as CEO
  **through 2029** and hold shares **through 2031**. This CEO retention signal through
  2029 — at a $79B market cap — is a massive public commitment that the Bloom story
  is a multi-year, not a trade.
  Sources: [TipRanks (Bloom Energy performance RSU CEO Sridhar)](https://www.tipranks.com/news/company-announcements/bloom-energy-grants-new-performance-based-rsus-to-ceo),
  [Investing.com (Bloom Energy CEO RSU grant)](https://www.investing.com/news/sec-filings/bloom-energy-grants-performancebased-stock-units-to-ceo-sridhar-93CH-4748523)

- **Underlying business fundamentals:** Q1 2026 revenue +130% YoY to $751M; FY2026
  guidance raised to $3.4B–$3.8B; $20B backlog; $5B Brookfield AI partnership. The
  Oracle data center agreement (up to 2.8 GW of fuel cell power) remains in execution.
  The "bring-your-own-power" thesis: data center developers can't wait 5–7 years for
  grid expansion so they generate their own power on-site with Bloom fuel cells.
  Sources: [TIKR (Bloom Energy stock 134% 2026 why surging)](https://www.tikr.com/blog/bloom-energy-stock-is-up-134-in-2026-heres-why-be-is-surging),
  [Barchart (Bloom Energy CEO magic words surging)](https://www.barchart.com/story/news/2339830/bloom-energy-stock-is-surging-its-ceo-said-these-magic-words),
  [Yahoo Finance (Bloom Energy BE stock 3x year AI boom funding itself)](https://stocktwits.com/news-articles/markets/equity/be-stock-is-up-nearly-3x-this-year-ai-boom-is-funding-itself-ceo--suggests/cZ0iqXqRevU)

**Portfolio impact — BE positions as of June 18 close:**

| Sleeve | Shares | Entry | Jun-17 Close | Jun-18 Close | Day Gain | Unrealized Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| mom_roa_6535 | 6.0827 | $290.67 | $284.99 | $328.91 | **+$267** | **+$232 (+13.2%)** |
| mom_v1_paper | 3.4369 | $290.67 | $284.99 | $328.91 | **+$151** | **+$131 (+13.2%)** |
| mom_v2_paper | 6.8739 | $290.67 | $284.99 | $328.91 | **+$302** | **+$263 (+13.2%)** |

Note: Our May 1 entry was $290.67. BE dipped below entry shortly after inception,
then has now decisively recovered to +13.2%. The stock has rallied 219% YTD in 2026.

**Long-term strategic significance (see Strategy section below):** BE validates a
structurally new theme: AI power grid bypass as an investable multi-year secular trend.

#### INTC (Intel) +10.64% — Apple Deal Confirmed by Markets at Close

Intel closed at $133.99, up $12.89 (+10.64%) — above the 9% pre-market estimate.
The Apple-Intel chip manufacturing deal (announced via Trump Truth Social June 17 evening)
was progressively confirmed through the session:

- Neither Apple nor Intel issued formal press releases by close of business June 18
- Bloomberg, CBS News, and CNBC all treated the Trump announcement as de facto
  confirmation with neither company denying it
- Intel stock held ALL of its pre-market gains through the session with no reversal —
  indicating institutional buying (not just retail excitement that fades)
- The FXLeaders note: "INTC Stock Eyes $150 as Trump Announces Intel-Apple Partnership"

**Portfolio impact:**

| Sleeve | Shares | Entry | Day Gain | Unrealized from Entry |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| mom_roa_6535 | 17.2956 | $112.77 | **+$223** | **+$369 (+18.9%)** |
| mom_v2_paper | 18.0123 | $112.77 | **+$232** | **+$384 (+18.9%)** |
| mom_v1_paper | 9.4887 | $112.77 | **+$122** | **+$202 (+18.9%)** |

At $133.99, INTC is now +18.9% from our June 3 entry of $112.77 — one of the strongest
15-day gains in the portfolio. The Apple validation of Intel 18A-P production is
exactly what the INTC bull case needed.

**Key risk still outstanding:** No formal Apple confirmation. If Apple issues a statement
distancing itself from Trump's post (however unlikely at this point), the stock could
correct sharply. An official Apple-Intel press release would lock in this gain thesis.

Sources: [Bloomberg (INTC soars Apple chip deal)](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-18/intel-intc-share-price-soars-after-trump-says-it-struck-apple-chip-deal),
[CBS News (Intel shares Trump Apple chip agreement)](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/intel-intc-shares-trump-apple-chip-agreement/),
[FXLeaders (INTC $150 Trump Intel-Apple partnership)](https://www.fxleaders.com/news/2026/06/18/intc-stock-eyes-150-as-trump-announces-intel-apple-partnership-to-build-chips-in-the-us/),

#### MU (Micron) +8.70% — $1,133.99 at Close, June 24 Earnings Now 6 Days Out

MU closed at $1,133.99, up $90.80 (+8.70%) — the largest absolute dollar gain of any
single name in the portfolio today (+$334 in mom_roa alone). Pre-market estimates had
been +4.8%; the stock nearly doubled that gain through the session as institutional
conviction in the memory supercycle narrative deepened.

**Upgraded pre-earnings setup (updated for today's close):**

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| MU close June 18 | $1,133.99 |
| mom_roa unrealized (3.68 sh) | $1,133.99 − $542.48 = +$591.51/sh × 3.68 = **+$2,177 (+109%)** |
| Citi price target (raised June 17) | $1,200 (+5.8% from today) |
| Deutsche Bank price target (raised June 17) | $1,500 (+32.4% from today) |
| Q3 FY2026 earnings date | June 24 (6 days) |
| Consensus Q3 revenue | ~$34.8B |
| Consensus Q3 EPS | ~$19.82 |
| 46 analyst ratings | ALL BUY (0 sells) |

**Apple's "unsustainable memory costs" admission remains the thesis anchor:**
Tim Cook's WSJ statement that memory costs have become "unsustainable" for Apple
means even the world's most aggressive procurement team cannot lock in supply at
pre-AI prices. TechInsights estimates the next iPhone 18 Pro could cost ~$270 more
if Apple fully passes through memory cost increases. This is the single most credible
external data point confirming that DRAM/NAND pricing power has permanently shifted
to manufacturers.

**The June 24 critical watch:** If MU Q3 beats on both revenue ($34.8B guidance)
AND gross margin (~68%+ vs. prior year ~51%), the stock should re-rate toward
$1,200–$1,500 targets. If any hint of HBM yield compression or margin pressure
emerges, expect a sell-the-news reaction despite the strong demand narrative.

Sources: [Motley Fool (Is Micron stock buy before June 24)](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/05/30/should-you-buy-micron-stock-before-june-24/),
[Tech-Insider (Micron Q2 2026 record revenue AI boom)](https://tech-insider.org/micron-q2-2026-earnings-ai-memory-market/),
[EBC Financial (MU before June 24 HBM4 third in cycle)](https://www.ebc.com/forex/mu-stock-before-june-24-10x-forward-p-e-third-in-hbm4-200b-capex-cycle)

#### GLW (Corning) +11.13% — Amazon Fiber Deal + AI Data Center Demand

Corning closed at $194.92, up $19.52 (+11.13%). Corning is held ONLY in mom_v1
(6.3092 sh @ $158.34 entry). The catalyst is a **multibillion-dollar Amazon fiber
cable deal** announced earlier in June 2026, combined with today's general AI
infrastructure rally.

**Why GLW is a semiconductor-adjacent AI play:** AI data centers need not just chips
but also the optical fiber interconnects (inside and between buildings) that move
data at low latency. Corning dominates the specialty optical fiber market. Amazon's
deal validates that hyperscaler fiber buildout is accelerating on the same timeline
as compute buildout.

**Position impact (mom_v1):** 6.3092 sh × ($194.92 − $158.34) = **+$231 unrealized (+23.1%)**.

**52-week range:** GLW traded as low as $49.97 and has now reached $194.92 — a 290%
YTD gain. Insider selling of $54.1M over the last 3 months (with no buying) is a mild
risk flag but is common at this magnitude of stock price appreciation.

Sources: [GuruFocus (GLW shares surge 11.1% what GF Score tells investors)](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8923327/corning-inc-glw-shares-surge-111-what-gf-score-of-69-tells-investors),
[TradingKey (Corning GLW moved up Jun 18)](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/261976625-market-movers-glw-20260618)

#### Semiconductor Equipment Complex — Broad Sector Rally

Five equipment and materials names in the portfolio all moved +4–10% today:

| Ticker | Company | % Gain | Position Size (mom_roa) | Sector Driver |

| ICHR +10.24% | Ichor Holdings | +$9.16 | 26.91 sh @ $72.49 | WFE spending lift |

| LRCX +3.97% | Lam Research | +$14.86 | 7.78 sh @ $256.85 | WFE DRAM/NAND |

**The macro driver:** Lam Research's raised WFE guidance to **$140B for 2026** (up from
$135B) is the sector consensus anchor. CEOs across equipment companies are now
quoting $140–145B WFE with 15%+ growth into 2027. Citi's bull case projects WFE
reaching **$200B in 2027 and $250B in 2028** — a step-function expansion driven by
AI chip demand and HBM4 capacity additions.

**Why this matters for the model:** The ROA × momentum framework organically
selected a cluster of semiconductor equipment companies because they have
(a) strong ROA (equipment companies typically run 15–25% ROA) and (b) strong
12-1 momentum (the WFE cycle is a 2–3 year upcycle). Today's across-the-board
rally is confirmation that the WFE cycle is in the acceleration phase, not the
deceleration phase — meaning these names should score high at the July 1 rebalance.

Sources: [Investing.com (Citi lifts PTs AMAT LRCX KLAC WFE demand)](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/citi-lifts-pts-on-amat-lrcx--klac-amid-booming-wfe-demand-4747120),

### PRAX Factual Correction — POWER1 Data Released June 1 (Not "Imminent")

**Correction to this morning's report:** The pre-market report stated that POWER1
Phase 3 topline data for vormatrigine "has not yet been released as of June 18 morning."
**This was incorrect.** Praxis Precision Medicines announced the POWER1 topline results
on **June 1, 2026** (17 days ago). The data was available at the time of the morning
report — the morning report had a factual error.

**Confirmed POWER1 results (June 1, 2026):**
- Primary endpoint (seizure frequency reduction): **MISSED**
- Key secondary endpoint (≥50% responder rate at higher dose): **MET**
- Safety: Generally favorable; adverse event discontinuations <10%
- 90% of vormatrigine patients transitioned to open-label extension (OLE)
- POWER2 enrollment: **Paused** to reassess vormatrigine program and consider modifications

**Stock price reaction (June 1 onward):**
- 1-day return post-announcement: −4.06%
- 7-day return: −27.42%
- 30-day return: −22.18%

This explains why PRAX is at $282.31 (close June 18) vs. our $324.36 entry from May 1
= **−12.96% from entry** as of today's close. The stock has been partially recovering
since its post-POWER1 lows.

**PRAX today (+4.56% to $282.31):** The recovery today is part of the broader biotech
sector lift (QQQ +2.51%), not a new PRAX-specific catalyst.

**What remains for PRAX:**
1. **Relutrigine PDUFA: September 27, 2026** — NDA accepted with priority review for
   SCN2A/SCN8A DEE. This is the primary remaining catalyst.
2. **Ulixacaltamide PDUFA: January 29, 2027** — Essential Tremor NDA.
3. **Vormatrigine OLE data** — ongoing; no timeline for publication.

**July 1 rebalance decision for PRAX:** Will depend on whether June 30 PRAX price
vs. June 30, 2025 baseline gives a positive 12-1 momentum score. Given the −27% drop
post-POWER1, the score is likely negative if June 30, 2025 was below today's price.
Probability: **PRAX exits at July 1 rebalance** across all three sleeves.

Sources: [GlobalNewsWire (Praxis POWER1 results June 1 2026)](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/06/01/3304714/0/en/Praxis-Precision-Medicines-Provides-Vormatrigine-Program-Update.html),

### Portfolio Holdings in Distress — Status Report June 18

#### SATS (EchoStar) — Grace Period Enters Final 13 Days

SATS closed at $109.17 (−$2.53, −2.26% today). The 30-day grace period on the $183M
DISH DBS interest deferral ends approximately July 1, 2026 — the same date as the
portfolio's scheduled rebalance. No bondholder extension has been announced as of
June 18 close. No 8-K from EchoStar related to any waiver was filed today.

At $109.17, SATS is −11.4% from our $123.24 mom_roa entry. The paper model will
naturally exit at July 1 rebalance (negative 12-1 momentum is near-certain given
the stock's trajectory). **Execution risk remains:** if SATS is halted or frozen
before July 1 due to a triggered Event of Default, the paper model cannot process
the exit. In a live portfolio, this would require a manual early exit by June 30.

**No action recommended** for the paper trade — document as case study in
credit/liquidity risk that ROA quality screens cannot catch (SATS had positive
ROA metrics at the May 1 rebalance date, from spectrum asset values, not operations).

### Strategic Insights — June 18 Post-Close

#### Insight 1: AI Power Infrastructure as a Multi-Year Sleeve Candidate

Today's BE +15.41% move forces a serious look at the AI power infrastructure theme
as a potential dedicated sleeve. The core thesis:

**The constraint:** US power grid interconnection queues run 5–7 years. A hyperscaler
needing 500MW by 2026 cannot connect to the grid in time. On-site power generation
(fuel cells, gas turbines, micro-nuclear) is the only available solution at scale.

**The companies capturing this:**
- **Bloom Energy (BE):** Solid oxide fuel cells; 2.8GW Oracle deal; $20B backlog; CEO through 2029
- **Vertiv (VRT):** Power distribution and cooling for AI data centers; $15B backlog
- **Comfort Systems (FIX):** Mechanical/electrical HVAC and power for data centers; $2B+ backlog
- **Powell Industries (POWL):** Electrical control equipment for industrial/power
- **NV Energy (NVT):** Utility serving Nevada data center corridor (Las Vegas AI hub)

**Current capture by existing sleeves:** Our systematic model already holds BE (mom_roa/v1/v2),
VRT (mom_roa), FIX (mom_roa/v1/v2), POWL (mom_roa/residual/v1/v2), NVT (residual_roa)
— essentially the entire power infrastructure theme is already in the portfolio
organically through the factor model. This is evidence the framework works: it picked
these names BEFORE the theme became consensus narrative.

**Should a dedicated sleeve be built?** Not yet. Reasons:
1. The existing sleeves already have exposure
2. A 5-name concentrated thematic sleeve has high idiosyncratic risk
3. Backtest would be heavily survivorship-biased (these are the names that worked)
4. Wait for July 1 rebalance to see what the model scores next

**What to watch:** If the model scores MORE power infrastructure names at July 1
(companies like FCEL, PLUG, NWTN, or utility-scale battery companies entering
the top-50 momentum + quality ranking), that's organic confirmation the theme has
legs. The model should NOT be overridden — let it discover the theme independently.

---

#### Insight 2: Semiconductor Equipment Cycle — Confirmation Phase, Not Peak

The across-the-board rally in equipment today (KLAC +8.73%, MKSI +9.46%, ICHR +10.24%,
UCTT +8.66%, TER +7.19%, LRCX +3.97%) coincides with Lam Research raising WFE guidance
to $140B (from $135B) with $145B possible. Historical WFE cycles:

| WFE Cycle | Peak | Duration | Signal |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2017–2018 | ~$52B | 18 months | Smartphone NAND demand |
| 2020–2022 | ~$98B | 24 months | Pandemic-driven EV + PC demand |
| 2024–present | **$140B+ (2026)** | 24+ months still rising | AI chip: HBM, advanced node logic |

The current cycle is running at 1.4× the prior peak and showing no deceleration.
Citi projects WFE reaching $200B in 2027 and $250B in 2028 — a 3× peak-of-prior-cycle
expansion. If these projections even partially materialize, the equipment names
(KLAC, LRCX, AMAT, and their supply chain ICHR, UCTT, MKSI) are in mid-cycle,
not late-cycle. **Strong hold thesis at July 1.**

---

#### Insight 3: MU/WDC/STX — Memory Supercycle Now Has First-Person Apple Validation

The Apple Cook "unsustainable memory costs" quote is qualitatively different from
analyst price target increases. It means:

1. **Apple's multi-year supply contracts are being renegotiated at higher prices** —
   Apple does not issue these public statements unless the pricing environment has
   permanently shifted.
2. **Consumer electronics is now a second-tier buyer** — AI data center contracts are
   being fulfilled first. Apple (the world's highest-volume premium device maker)
   receives supply AFTER hyperscalers.
3. **NAND AND DRAM are both constrained** — previous memory cycles were often 1D
   (DRAM OR NAND); this cycle is both simultaneously.

**For the model's memory holdings (MU, WDC, STX):**
- At $1,134 (MU), +109% from entry: the model captured the supercycle early
- The June 24 earnings represent a potential step-up catalyst
- Bear case: If Apple succeeds in locking longer-term contracts at current prices,
  the pricing narrative could stall; but the structural HBM shortage is separate
  from consumer NAND pricing

**New sleeve concept (research only):** A memory-focused factor-neutral sleeve:
long MU/WDC/SK Hynix ADR (if listed), short DRAM consumer end-demand proxies.
This requires short-side implementation and is not feasible in the current paper
framework. Flag for post-18th-birthday implementation when Alpaca live trading enables
long/short.

---

#### Insight 4: PRAX Case Study — What the Factor Model Cannot Screen

The PRAX POWER1 miss is a case study in the limits of the momentum × ROA framework
for biotech names:

**What the model scored correctly (May 1):**
- 12-1 momentum: PRAX had been rising strongly in the 12 months ending April 2026
  on Phase 2 EMBRAVE data (+77% seizure reduction)
- ROA: biotech ROA metrics can be complex; PRAX likely scored on cash/asset ratio
  or product revenue trajectory

**What the model cannot see:**
- Binary FDA/trial risk: Phase 3 outcomes are fundamentally unknowable from price
  or ROA data
- Phase 2 → Phase 3 attrition rates (~50% in CNS indications historically)
- The 30-day window between POWER1 announcement and May 1 entry likely contained
  the stock's highest recent price (near-term POWER1 anticipation premium)

**Lesson:** ROA × momentum is well-suited for capital-intensive businesses with
durable competitive advantages (semiconductors, industrials, energy infrastructure)
but faces systematic risk from binary catalyst names. The June 3 rebalance replaced
some biotech exposure with more industrial/semiconductor names — a natural self-correction.

**No filter change recommended yet.** One PRAX example is insufficient to justify
a biotech exclusion (ARWR +8.27% and ANAB are performing). But if the portfolio
consistently underperforms in biotech binary events vs. the semiconductor and
industrial holdings, a "biotech position size cap" (e.g., max 1.5% per name
vs. the current ~2%) could be worth backtesting.

### Five Market Events That Defined the Week

#### 1. SpaceX IPO (Jun 12) — Biggest IPO in History

SpaceX priced at **$135/share** and opened Jun 12, gaining **+19%** on day one to close at **$160.95**. On Monday Jun 15 (first full day), shares climbed another **+20%**. Total first-week market cap exceeded **$2 trillion**, making it the largest IPO in history ($75B raised).

**Impact on sleeves:** The IPO sucked some capital pre-launch (as warned in Jun 11 report) but the strong open actually lifted the entire tech/AI sentiment. No direct exposure in any sleeve (SpaceX not in momentum universe). **VSAT (ViaSat)** — held by both mom_roa and residual_roa — is the one direct competitor concern. VSAT fell from $72.71 on Jun 11 to $64.13 by Jun 18 (**−11.80%**), among the worst performers in the book this week. Note: VSAT's ViaSat-3 F3 satellite launched April 29 on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy, and Viasat's government/defense segment is insulated from Starlink consumer competition; but the IPO IPO-hype narrative is clearly weighing on the stock.

Sources: [SpaceX IPO closes at $161, +19% debut — CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/12/spacex-ipo-spcx-live-updates.html), [SpaceX stock jumps 20% first full day — CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/15/spacex-stock-record-ipo-debut.html)

---

#### 2. KLAC 10:1 Stock Split (Jun 12) — Operationally Handled

KLA Corporation executed its 10-for-1 forward stock split effective at market open Jun 12. Prior Jun 11 close: **$241.16** (pre-split). Post-split trading Jun 12: **$254.54**, confirming the yfinance data is now on a split-adjusted basis.

**Database status**: `paper_positions` still shows residual_roa holding **1.1568 shares** at entry $1,727.12. Post-split the correct representation is 11.568 shares at $172.71. The fact that residual_roa's NAV rose from $105,105 (Jun 11) to $106,134 (Jun 12) — a +$1,029 gain on a day where SPY gained +0.54% — suggests the MTM script correctly handled the split OR the NAV moved enough from other names to mask a phantom loss. **Flag for verification**: confirm whether `paper_positions.qty` for KLAC was updated to 11.568 or whether the MTM script has a workaround. KLAC's Jun 18 price is **$259.56**; 1.1568 × $259.56 = $300.4 vs correct 11.568 × $259.56 = $3,004 — a $2,703 discrepancy if unhandled.

KLAC itself has continued to perform strongly: YTD +99.7%, 16/21 analysts rate Buy. The split removed the psychological barrier for smaller investors and expanded liquidity.

Sources: [KLA Corporation 10:1 split announcement — PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/kla-corporation-announces-ten-to-one-stock-split-and-quarterly-cash-dividend-payment-302766137.html), [KLAC post-split valuation analysis — Simply Wall St](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/semiconductors/nasdaq-klac/kla/news/kla-klac-is-up-319-after-10-for-1-split-and-dividend-hike-is)

---

#### 3. FOMC — Hawkish Shock Under New Chair Warsh (Jun 17)

Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting as Fed Chair delivered the rate decision markets expected — **hold at 3.50–3.75%, unanimous 12-0** — but the dot plot was a **hawkish surprise**:

- **9 of 18 FOMC members** now project at least one rate hike before year-end 2026
- **6 of 18** project two hikes
- Median year-end funds rate estimate raised from **3.4% → 3.8%**
- PCE inflation forecast raised from 2.7% → **3.6%** (driven by Iran war energy inflation)
- Warsh removed forward guidance entirely, announced five task forces to review Fed operations
- 2-year Treasury yield surged **+16bps to 4.216%** following the decision

**Market reaction**: Stocks sold off on Wednesday Jun 18 (intraday). Nasdaq −1.15% Jun 16 (anticipation). The Jun 17 holding added uncertainty. Jun 18 recovered strongly (+1.91% Nasdaq, +1.08% S&P) largely on the Iran ceasefire.

**Portfolio impact**: A rate hike signal is **the single biggest structural risk to the entire momentum book**. Our sleeves are long growth/tech/AI-infra — names with high P/E multiples, high capex, and earnings priced far in the future. Higher rates compress these valuations systematically. The near-term offset is the Iran peace deal (inflation relief), but the Warsh Fed is now clearly signaling it will prioritize fighting 3.6% PCE over supporting equities. This is a new regime vs. the April-May period.

Sources: [Fed holds rates, dot plot signals hike — CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/17/fed-interest-rate-decision-june-2026.html), [Warsh hawkish shock: 9 officials signal hike — Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/economy/policy/articles/warsh-hawkish-shock-9-fed-180221394.html), [Fed Signals Rate Hike in 2026 — intellectia.ai](https://intellectia.ai/blog/fed-rate-hike-expectations-june-19-2026)

---

#### 4. Iran Ceasefire / Peace Deal (Jun 18) — Strait of Hormuz to Reopen

Pakistan's Prime Minister announced on Jun 18 that the US and Iran had reached a **14-point memorandum of understanding** (to be signed in Geneva on June 19) to end 100+ days of conflict:
- Reopen the Strait of Hormuz without tolls
- Lift the US naval blockade
- Begin a 60-day negotiation window on Iran's nuclear program

This was the dominant news driver on Jun 18, partially offsetting the Fed hawkish reaction and explaining the Nasdaq +1.91% and S&P +1.08% gains on that day.

**Oil price impact**: Brent crude has fallen ~20% from 2026 highs. Energy stocks plunged — XLE fell to **$53.77** (from $57.12 on Jun 11, −5.87% for the week). APA −10.20%, DINO −7.72%, FTI −7.69%, PARR −8.11% for the week.

**Portfolio impact**: This is a **direct headwind to residual_roa's energy hedge**. The sleeve holds APA, DINO, FTI, INSW, NRT, PARR, PBR, TEN — ~8 energy names that were residual_roa's natural Iran hedge through June. That hedge now reverses: the ceasefire removes the supply-shock premium. Residual_roa's relative underperformance vs mom_roa this week (+2.68% vs +6.14%) is partly explained by this energy reversal. Going into next rebalance (end-June), the energy names in residual_roa will likely rank lower on idiosyncratic momentum and may self-select out.

**IMPORTANT**: The deal still needs Trump's signature and is a 60-day window, not a permanent peace. Credibility is moderate — Iran continued attacks on Kuwait even during ceasefire negotiations. Market volatility from Iran remains a tail risk.

Sources: [US ends Iran blockade, market reaction — TheStreet](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-18-2026), [US-Iran 14-point MOU announced — IG International](https://www.ig.com/en/news-and-trade-ideas/us-iran-ceasefire-260408), [Energy stocks plunge on ceasefire — Seeking Alpha](https://seekingalpha.com/news/4573335-energy-stocks-plunge-with-oil-prices-as-us-iran-ceasefire-set-to-open-strait-of-hormuz)

#### 5. Triple Witching (Jun 18 — Moved from Friday)

Because markets are closed Jun 19 (Juneteenth), the quarterly options/futures expiration was moved to **Thursday Jun 18**. This amplified intraday volatility on an already-eventful day (Fed aftermath + Iran ceasefire). Triple witching historically brings elevated volume and short-term price moves. The sharp individual stock moves on Jun 18 (BE +15.41%, MU +8.71%, INTC +10.65%) were likely amplified by forced options settlements on top of the macro news. **Read-through**: some of Jun 18's gains may be options-expiration-driven and partially mean-revert Monday Jun 22 on open.
