### 5. Apple/Microsoft Price Hike Aftermath — Permanent Memory Pricing Signal

The June 25 price increases (covered in detail in yesterday's report) are still working
through the market on June 26. Key developments since yesterday's close:

**The Al Jazeera / CBS News global coverage confirms the price hikes have become mainstream
financial news**, not just tech news. When international outlets lead with "Apple and
Microsoft hike prices over surging chip costs" (Al Jazeera), the signal that memory
scarcity is a global consumer story is fully established.

**Microsoft's explicit forward guidance (verbatim):**
> "Console storage and memory prices have increased by more than 2.5x and we expect
> another doubling by the fall of 2027."

This is the most bullish statement for MU/WDC/STX ever published in a non-financial
company earnings call. Microsoft, which has no incentive to overstate memory cost increases
(it is acknowledging consumer-facing price hikes that will reduce Xbox unit sales), is
explicitly forecasting another 2x memory price increase by fall 2027.

**Reconciliation with Morningstar's bear thesis:** If Microsoft is right (another 2x
price increase by fall 2027), then Morningstar's "revenue halves by 2030" scenario is
arithmetically impossible unless the $100B in take-or-pay contracts simultaneously unwind
AND memory prices fall by 75%+ — an extreme scenario requiring simultaneous supply glut
AND massive AI capex pullback.

Sources: [Al Jazeera — Apple Microsoft hike prices chip costs](https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/6/26/apple-microsoft-hike-prices-over-surging-chip-costs) | [CBS News — Apple price hikes MacBook iPad 2026](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/apple-price-hikes-macbook-ipad-2026/) | [Yahoo Finance — MSFT AAPL chip shortage price hikes](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/news/microsoft-apple-raise-prices-ai-041523167.html)

### 6. AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) — July 1 1.6T Shipment Catalyst: 5 Days

**AAOI position status (as of June 25 close):**

| Context | Value |
|---|---|
| AAOI June 25 close | $138.54 |
| Control sleeve (590.65 sh @ $169.13) | **$81,929 NAV (-18.07%)** |
| Treatment sleeve (cash) | **$100,000 NAV (0.00%)** |
| Treatment lead | **+$18,071 (+22.1pp)** |

**The July 1 catalyst countdown:**
AAOI's management stated that **1.6T transceiver shipments commence July 1, 2026**. This
is the event the LLM overlay veto was specifically designed around — the June 12 veto
cited uncertainty about whether 1.6T CPO deployments would start "on schedule" given
Semi Analysis data showing likely delays.

**Three possible July 1 scenarios for the LLM experiment:**

**Scenario A: AAOI confirms 1.6T shipments started July 1 (bullish)**
- AAOI would likely rally 20-40% on confirmation
- The LLM treatment sleeve needs to run the 3-prompt evaluation on AAOI and decide whether
  to re-enter at ~$138 (a better price than the original $169 entry)
- Even a 30% AAOI rally from $138 = ~$180 — the treatment would re-enter below the
  original inception price and below the control's cost basis ($169.13)
- Treatment advantage (currently $18,071) would shrink as AAOI re-rates, but the
  treatment was NEVER in from $169 → the entry point advantage is preserved

**Scenario B: No confirmation; rebalance exits AAOI from control sleeve**
- The mom_roa_6535 systematic signals rank AAOI by 12-1 momentum + ROA
- At current prices ($138 vs $169 entry), 12-1 momentum is very negative
- The control sleeve (mom_roa_top1) automatically exits when AAOI is no longer #1 momentum
- The control would then buy whatever IS #1 at July 1 — likely MU (after +123% run) or
  another top-ranked AI hardware name
- The treatment makes the same evaluation independently

**Scenario C: Partial shipment announcement; ambiguous**
- Most likely: AAOI announces "initial 1.6T deliveries" to one hyperscaler
- The 3-prompt evaluation must weigh this against the ongoing -18% loss in the control

**The July 1 rebalance date coincides exactly with the AAOI catalyst.** This is the
most complex overlay decision the experiment has faced. The evaluation prompts should run
on June 29-30 (before July 1 execution).

Sources: [BingX — AAOI 1.6T order July 1](https://bingx.com/en/learn/article/applied-optoelectronics-aaoi-forecast-and-price-prediction-is-aaoi-a-good-investment) | [StockAnalysis — AAOI overview](https://stockanalysis.com/stocks/aaoi/)

### 7. INTC (Intel) — Holding Gains After BofA Double-Upgrade

**June 25 close: $132.87 (+6.31% on the day)**

Intel's BofA double-upgrade (Underperform → Buy, PT $160) has not been reversed overnight.
Key aspects of the BofA thesis still relevant:

| Intel Foundry Customer | Status |
|---|---|
| Apple | 18A-P manufacturing confirmed; lower-end chips only (announced June 18 via Trump) |
| NVIDIA | Overflow AI chip manufacturing capacity |
| Google/Alphabet | Backup AI chip manufacturing |
| MediaTek | TPU production (BofA June 25 reference) |

BofA's foundry revenue forecast: **$1.1B in 2026 → $47.1B by 2030** implies Intel Foundry
becoming the #2 global foundry by revenue within 4 years. This is a transformational
business model shift — Intel was primarily a fabless-adjacent IDM; it is becoming a TSMC
competitor with a government-backed (CHIPS Act $13B+) advantage.

**Our position (across three sleeves, June 3 entry at $112.77):**
At $132.87 close (+17.8%), INTC remains strong-hold territory. BofA's $160 target implies
+20.4% further upside from June 25 close, representing one of the more visible near-term
catalysts in the portfolio.

**Pre-market June 26:** INTC is likely flat to slightly lower in the broader tech selloff,
but the BofA thesis is sufficiently new and strong that it should limit downside.

Sources: [TheStreet — BofA resets Intel target](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stocks/bank-of-america-raises-intel-stock-price-target) | [TimothySykes — INTC BofA Apple deal June 25](https://www.timothysykes.com/news/intel-corporation-intc-news-2026_06_25/)

### 8. ARWR (Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals) — SHASTA Data Window Open; EU Approval Incoming

**ARWR June 25 close: $79.27 | Entry: $74.85 | Unrealized: +$5.9%**

Two developments since the last report:

**A. SHASTA Phase 3 data window is now officially open (Q3 2026 begins July 1)**
The company stated topline data expected "toward end of June 2026 or early Q3." As of
June 26 morning, no press release has been issued. The June 29 R&D webinar (scheduled by
Arrowhead) may include updates on the SHASTA timeline but is cardiometabolic-focused.

**B. European Commission REDEMPLO approval imminent**
The EMA's CHMP issued a positive opinion for plozasiran (REDEMPLO) for familial
chylomicronemia syndrome (FCS). The EC typically issues its marketing authorization
decision within 67 days of the CHMP opinion. The US FDA has already approved REDEMPLO
for FCS (PDUFA November 2025 — likely already approved given we're in June 2026).

With US approval secured and EU approval imminent, ARWR's plozasiran now has multi-market
commercial traction before the larger SHASTA trials read out (SHTG — hypertriglyceridemia,
a much larger patient population than FCS).

**Clinical confidence signal:** Arrowhead's CMO stated at the June 2026 RBC Healthcare
Conference that they are "cautiously optimistic SHASTA will show statistical significance."
For a company that already has one approved indication using the same mechanism, this is
substantively stronger than early-stage optimism. The biological validation from FCS
approval means the mechanism is confirmed; SHTG is simply a larger patient population.

**What a positive SHASTA readout means for the portfolio:**
- ARWR at $79.27 with 26.69 shares in mom_roa/residual/mom_v2 = ~$2,116 position value
- Typical positive Phase 3 readout for an already-approved mechanism: +30-60% re-rating
- Potential gain: +$635 to +$1,270 across all sleeves if SHASTA reads out positive
- This would add ~$300-500 per sleeve to NAV

**Monitoring instruction:** Set an alert for any ARWR press release. The window closes
approximately July 15 based on company guidance.

Sources: [Arrowhead SHASTA enrollment complete — IR](https://ir.arrowheadpharma.com/news-releases/news-release-details/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-completes-enrollment-shasta-3-shasta-4) | [Arrowhead EMA CHMP REDEMPLO positive opinion — Stocktitan](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/ARWR/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-announces-acceptance-of-new-drug-v8oknyf4g2fo.html) | [Globe and Mail — ARWR eyes Q3 SHASTA data](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/ARWR-Q/pressreleases/2065571/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-eyes-q3-shasta-data-as-redemplo-approvals-build-momentum/)

### 9. WDC (Western Digital) and STX (Seagate) — PCE Reaction and HDD Thesis

**WDC June 25 close: $675.39 (entry $431.74 = +56.4% unrealized)**
**STX June 25 close: $1,025.36 (entry $727.29 = +41.0% unrealized)**

Both names gave back a significant portion of their Wednesday AH gains (+10.2% each) on
Thursday. WDC pre-market was $725.70 but closed at $675.39 — a $50 intraday reversal
driven by the May 2026 PCE print (4.1% headline) being absorbed after the open.

**The HDD duopoly thesis remains unchanged:**
- WDC and STX together control ~89% of the global HDD market (47% WDC, 42% STX)
- 100% of 2026 HDD production is contractually committed to data center operators
- Multi-year supply agreements extend through 2028-2029 per company disclosures
- Morgan Stanley raised STX target to $1,035 (from $767) as recently as June 16
- Microsoft's Xbox price hike statement ("memory prices increased 2.5x; expect another
  doubling by fall 2027") explicitly validates the HDD embedded cost trajectory

**Fox Advisors downgrade risk (WDC-specific):** The SanDisk spin-off (WDC spun its NAND
flash business into a separate SNDK entity in February 2025) creates technical selling
pressure when SNDK shareholders receive shares they may not want. This is a near-term
overhang that does NOT change WDC's HDD thesis. The July 1 rebalance scoring will capture
this pressure in the momentum score — if WDC's 12-1 momentum has been impacted, it may
rank lower. However, from the May 1 entry at $431.74, WDC is +56.4% — a position still
well in the money.

Sources: [FX Leaders — WDC $643 AI demand 274% growth](https://www.fxleaders.com/news/2026/06/25/western-digital-wdc-stock-hits-643-as-ai-demand-drives-274-growth-in-2026/) | [Benzinga — WDC STX surge after Micron](https://www.benzinga.com/markets/equities/26/06/60088616/sandisk-western-digital-and-seagate-surge-after-microns-blowout-earnings-signal-memory-upcycle) | [247 Wall St — STX Morgan Stanley PT](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/06/16/western-digital-jumps-7-on-morgan-stanleys-33-price-target-hike-seagate-gains-5-as-the-ai-storage-boom-rolls-on/)

### 10. VRT (Vertiv) — Near Entry After Full Recovery Attempt

**VRT June 25 close: $325.57 | Entry: $328.47 | Unrealized: -0.9% (near breakeven)**

Vertiv's AI power/cooling thesis remains intact but the stock has struggled to clear its
entry price despite the broad AI infrastructure bull run:

- **Q1 2026 fundamentals:** Revenue +30.1% YoY ($2.65B); Americas +53%; backlog >$12B (record)
- **2026 guidance:** Revenue $13.5B-$14B; operating margin 23.3%; adjusted EPS $6.30-$6.40
- **YTD: +102%** despite the near-entry drawdown
- **Dividend** paid June 25 ($0.0625/share; $0.38 on our 6.08-share position — minimal)

The -0.9% from entry masks a more volatile path: VRT peaked near $380 in mid-June 2026
(post-Apple/Intel announcements) before the Korean chip rout compressed AI infrastructure
valuations across the board.

**Why VRT has underperformed MU/WDC/INTC:** Vertiv's business is less directly tied to
memory pricing (it sells power and cooling equipment, not chips). When the AI trade
rotates into "direct AI beneficiaries" (MU, WDC), VRT temporarily loses relative appeal.
The fundamental demand for power management in AI data centers is not going away —
Microsoft announced **$527B in combined Mag7 AI capex for 2026** — Vertiv is the
infrastructure enabler for every watt of that.

Sources: [Vertiv SEC 8-K Q2 dividend](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001674101/000162828026040031/exhibit991vrt-q22026divide.htm) | [StockAnalysis — VRT overview](https://stockanalysis.com/stocks/vrt/)

### 11. Bloom Energy (BE) — Pullback from $333 ATH; Thesis Intact

**BE June 25 close: ~$309.18 | Entry: $290.67 | Unrealized: +6.4%**

Bloom Energy pulled back on June 25 from the $333.69 ATH (June 24 close) toward ~$309.
Sources conflict ($309.18 per MacroTrends vs. $326.19 per others), but directionally the
stock has cooled from its recent extraordinary rally.

The immediate narrative: "profit-taking after 240% YTD gains" in a broad tech selloff day.
The underlying thesis has not changed:

- **Oracle deal (April 2026):** Up to 2.8 GW contracted SOFC fuel cell systems
- **Brookfield partnership:** $5B AI data center deployment capital committed
- **Backlog: $20-24B** (multi-year)
- **FERC AI power directive:** Grid operators warned about AI demand; on-site power bypasses
  FERC queue delays that plague traditional grid-connected hyperscaler buildouts

**Key distinction for June 26:** Bloom Energy is AI power infrastructure, not a semiconductor
name. It should trade LESS correlated with the broad "chip selloff" narrative than MU or WDC.
A pullback on an AI sector off-day is normal. The $5B Brookfield capital partnership and
$20-24B backlog are not affected by MU profit-taking.

Sources: [Data Center Dynamics — BE Brookfield $5B](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/bloom-energy-signs-5bn-partnership-with-brookfield-to-deploy-fuel-cell-tech-across-ai-data-centers/) | [StocksToTrade — BE rally AI power deals](https://stockstotrade.com/news/bloom-energy-corporation-be-news-2026_06_24/)

- Core PCE May 2026: **3.4% YoY** (highest since October 2023; +0.3% MoM)
- This is a **stagflationary print**: inflation accelerating while the AI economy runs hot

**BofA 3-hike forecast (Sep/Oct/Dec 2026) — now more likely:**
The PCE print was HOT. BofA's forecast of three 2026 rate hikes is now the highest-conviction
macro risk in the portfolio. The Fed cannot ignore Core PCE at 3.4% while also watching
May payrolls (+172K) and wage growth (3.4%). Rate hikes favor:
- High-ROA companies (pricing power defends margins) → our primary signal
- Community banks (NIM expansion) → residual_roa FNRN/FMBM/CSBB
- Energy/commodity (inflation hedge) → residual_roa APA/DINO/PARR
- Memory/storage (DRAM/NAND pricing = inflation pass-through) → MU/WDC/STX

Rate hikes hurt:
- Zero-earnings high-multiple names → AAOI, GSAT, VSAT (in sleeves but exiting)
- Long-duration growth (DOCN has earnings but growth multiple) → monitor

The portfolio is structurally defensive to the rate-hiking regime via the ROA filter. The
best-performing sleeves (mom_roa, residual_roa) are exactly the ones designed for this environment.

### 16. Strategic Insights — June 26

#### A. The Morningstar 2030 Bear Thesis Is the Right Question to Monitor

Morningstar's "revenue halves by 2030" scenario is not crazy — the DRAM commodity cycle
has played out this way multiple times in history (2019, 2022). The reason this cycle may
be different:

1. **HBM is structurally different from DRAM:** HBM requires wafer stacking (die + silicon
   interposer), which limits who can make it and how fast capacity can expand. Conventional
   DRAM capacity expanded 30-40% annually in the 2018-2020 buildout; HBM can only expand
   10-15% annually due to packaging constraints.
2. **$100B take-or-pay eliminates spot exposure for ~20% of volumes:** Even if spot DRAM
   crashes 60%, the take-or-pay contracts provide a $20-33B/yr revenue floor.
3. **Microsoft's statement** ("expect another doubling by fall 2027") is a live forward
   indicator from the largest consumer of memory. If a supply glut were imminent, Microsoft
   would not be raising Xbox prices and signaling further increases.

**Risk management implication:** The bear scenario is the correct long-term exit signal.
The sleeves will naturally exit MU when its 12-1 momentum turns negative — i.e., when the
stock price starts declining vs. 12 months ago. That is the model's natural stop-loss.
No manual intervention is needed; the momentum signal handles the exit automatically.

#### B. AI Infrastructure vs. AI Application — The Emerging Portfolio Bifurcation

The June 25-26 market action reveals a clear bifurcation:

| Category | June 25 Performance | Our Exposure |
|---|---|---|
| AI infrastructure (memory, storage, optics) | MU +15.7%, WDC +4.9%, INTC +6.3% | Core holdings |
| AI application layer (consumer electronics) | AAPL -6.1%, MSFT -3.2% | NOT held |
| AI software/services (OpenAI ecosystem) | MSFT -3.2%, AI ETFs -0.18% | NOT held |
| Non-AI chips (auto, industrial) | ON Semi -9-15% | NOT held |

The systematic sleeves were designed around momentum + ROA without explicit AI sector
labeling — but the factor selection has created a portfolio that is de facto concentrated
in "AI infrastructure supply chain" rather than "AI application layer." This is likely the
correct positioning for the current phase of the cycle: AI infrastructure buildout is
structural and demand-driven, while the application layer competes on consumer discretionary
margins.

#### C. Defense Rotation Potential — Rate-Hike Regime Setup

With Core PCE at 3.4% and BofA projecting three 2026 hikes, the market rotation from
high-multiple growth to value/quality accelerates. The June 25 session showed this:
Dow +0.14% while Nasdaq -0.46%. The Dow is industrials and healthcare heavy; the portfolio
already holds AGX, FIX, POWL (AI-adjacent industrials in mom_roa), and CAT did NOT appear
in sleeves but gained +5.28%.

A rate-hike regime defense for a future sleeve remains the medium-term research project:
- Universe: S&P 500 only (quality anchor)
- Signal: ROA > 15% + 6-month momentum (shorter lookback to avoid duration traps)
- Exclusions: Utilities, REITs, zero-earnings tech
- Historical validation: 2004-2006 and 2022-2023 hike cycles

**Pre-condition:** Complete July 1 rebalance. Do not initiate new sleeve research until
the rebalance is confirmed clean.

#### D. Residual_roa Energy Names — Reassessing the Inflation Hedge Thesis

The residual_roa sleeve holds APA, DINO, PARR, NRT, PBR. These are oil refining and
production names that entered via the combined residual/ROA signal. In the current
stagflationary environment (PCE 4.1%), energy names historically outperform (commodity
prices = inflation hedge). However, the DB June 25 position data shows these are trading
at losses from entry. The July 1 rebalance will re-score them on June 30 close momentum.

**If energy names retain momentum scores:** They stay; the stagflation hedge thesis is
gaining traction with global PCE prints
**If energy names fall out of top-50:** They exit naturally via the model; no override needed

The ROA filter in residual_roa (65/35 weighting) should help: refining companies like DINO
and PARR have high ROA when crack spreads are wide. The question is whether their
12-1 momentum competes with the tech names still ranking high despite the rout.

MU fell -4.24% today as expected, but closed well above the intraday low of $1,119.93. The stock tried to fill the Wednesday post-earnings gap but buying stepped in around $1,120. The -4.24% daily move after a +15.7% Wednesday surge is normal profit-taking mechanics, not a thesis break.

**The key data point:** MU's intraday low was $1,119.93 — approximately 7.7% below yesterday's close. The fact that it recovered to $1,162 by close indicates demand absorption. Morningstar's bear note was released this morning (2030 revenue halving scenario), but the stock absorbed it.

**Unchanged bull case:** $100B+ take-or-pay HBM contracts, Microsoft's "prices expected to double again by fall 2027" statement, Barclays $2,000 PT, 27-analyst consensus $1,293.

#### WDC (Western Digital) — Significant reversal today

| | June 24 Pre-Mkt +10% | June 25 Close | June 26 Close | Net from Entry |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price | +10.2% surge | $675.39 | **$603.96** | **+39.9%** (was +56.4%) |
| 1-Day Change | — | — | **-10.6%** | -$71.43/share |

**This is the biggest single-day reversal in our portfolio today.** WDC gave back most of Wednesday's +10.2% surge in a single session. This is a painful intraday narrative: the stock surged on Micron's blowout earnings (halo effect for all storage names), then the market spent Thursday and Friday unwinding that trade as the "chip bubble" framing took hold.

**Why WDC fell harder than MU:** Western Digital is still dealing with the SanDisk/SNDK spin-off overhang. SNDK shares distributed to WDC holders may be sold by index funds that received them (forced distribution selling). Additionally, WDC's remaining HDD business is perceived as lower-margin than Micron's pure HBM play — the re-rating compressed WDC's premium vs. MU.

**What didn't change:**
- HDD duopoly still controls ~89% of global market
- 100% of 2026 production is contractually committed
- Supply agreements extend through 2028-2029
- Morgan Stanley $1,035 PT on STX remains valid (implies comparable HDD demand)

**Rebalance implication:** WDC's 12-1 momentum score is still very high from May 2025 base prices. One week's drawdown does not materially change a 12-month lookback. WDC is a near-certain HOLD at July 1. However, the P&L from entry is now +39.9% vs the +56.4% reported at yesterday's open — a $71/share paper loss per share held.

- No FERC ruling
- No executive departure

**This was pure AI infrastructure sentiment correlation.** The market is treating BE, VRT, and AI power/cooling names as part of the "AI capex" trade — and when OpenAI IPO delay + ON Semi collapse + chip profit-taking all hit simultaneously, the entire AI infrastructure complex sold off together.

**The dangerous implication:** Bloom Energy is now **below our entry price**. This changes the urgency posture:
1. If BE stays below $290 through June 30, its 12-1 momentum score will still likely be positive (the stock was far lower a year ago), so the model will likely HOLD
2. But the unrealized loss compounds the AFJK and FGMC losses already in the portfolio
3. The fundamental thesis ($20-24B backlog, Oracle 2.8GW, Brookfield $5B) is unchanged

**Key monitoring trigger:** If BE falls below $260 (-10.5% from entry) in the next 2 trading days, that would represent a 22% decline from the ATH in 3 days. That magnitude of move would warrant re-examining whether the market knows something about the AI power buildout that isn't public yet.

**Why is AI power underperforming AI chips?** Counter-intuitively, AI power (VRT, BE) is selling off harder than AI memory this week. The reason: the "AI cost too much" narrative hits BOTH ends of the value chain. Memory chips are seen as mission-critical (no substitute for HBM); power and cooling are seen as partially substitutable or delayable (data center operators can optimize power usage in ways they cannot avoid memory purchases).

#### ON Semiconductor (ON) — Not in Portfolio, Key Signal

| | June 25 Premarket | June 26 Close |
|---|---|---|
| ON Semi | -9 to -15% premarket | **$109.82** (range: $106.66–$121.46) |

ON Semi closed at $109.82, confirming the -10% intraday decline. This is the most important single data point for understanding *what the market fears*: **automotive and industrial semiconductor demand deceleration**. ON Semi is the leader in silicon carbide (SiC) for EV powertrains and has heavy auto/industrial exposure. Its miss signals:
- EV demand growth is decelerating in 2026 (consistent with macro surveys)
- Industrial automation capex is pausing amid rate uncertainty
- Auto OEMs are cutting semiconductor content per vehicle (cost pressure)

**This does NOT affect our portfolio:** We hold memory (MU), storage (WDC, STX), wafer fab equipment (LRCX, MKSI, TER, ONTO, ICHR), and optical connectivity (COHR, CRDO, AMKR). None of these have meaningful EV/industrial exposure. The ON Semi collapse is a signal to AVOID automotive chip names, not to exit data center/AI exposure.

Sources: [TradingKey ON Semi premarket decline](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261994105-opanai-mu-on-spacex-apple-tradingkey) | [GuruFocus ON Semi 9.37% drop premarket](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8933961/on-faces-significant-decline-with-937-drop-in-premarket-trading)

#### AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) — Flat Friday; July 1 Catalyst Approaching

| | June 25 Close | June 26 Close | vs. Entry (Jun 12, ~$234) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Price | $138.54 | **$138.54** | **-40.8%** (from June 12 re-inception) |

AAOI was flat on June 26 — essentially unchanged while the rest of the chip sector fell. This is **noteworthy**. A stock that was down -24.5% YTD and -40% from re-inception entry held flat on the worst chip day of the week. Possible explanations:
1. **Catalyst proximity:** July 1 first 1.6T transceiver shipments create a natural buying floor; traders are unwilling to short into a known catalyst
2. **Already priced in:** After -40% from entry and a massive run earlier in 2026 (52-week high $233.67, low $18.50), there may be little forced selling left
3. **Analyst target reset:** Analyst PT raised to $190 (from $140) on the $200M+ 1.6T order. At $138, there's a large implied upside

**The July 1 catalyst read-through:** The $200M+ 1.6T order for "a long-term customer" (widely expected to be a major hyperscaler) is scheduled to begin shipments July 1. If confirmed on July 1, AAOI could see a significant gap-up. If delayed or cancelled, the stock could collapse further. This is the binary event the control sleeve (mom_roa_top1_paper) is exposed to — the LLM overlay treatment is in cash, avoiding the risk entirely.

**LLM experiment status:** Treatment continues to outperform by **+19.75pp** (treatment at 0%, control at -19.75%). The LLM's June 5 VETO of AAOI (after the June 12 re-inception forced it to buy) has so far been a correct call.

### 5. Macro Update — Post-PCE Rate Outlook

**PCE (released June 25, now confirmed):**
- Headline PCE May 2026: **4.1% YoY** (+0.4% MoM)
- Core PCE May 2026: **3.4% YoY** (+0.3% MoM)

**BofA (updated June 22 forecast, now more credible post-PCE):**
- Three 25bp hikes in 2026: September, October, December
- Fed funds rate rising to 4.25%–4.50% (from current 3.50%–3.75%)
- The June FOMC dot plot already showed half of policymakers anticipating hikes
- New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh described as "surprisingly hawkish"

**Goldman Sachs (baseline):**
- No cuts OR hikes if inflation begins to cool
- Core PCE projected to stay 3%+ throughout 2026
- But Goldman has shifted to "hikes possible" as a non-base scenario

**Implication for portfolio:**

In a rate-hiking regime, the ROA filter is exactly the right defensive tool:
- High-ROA companies can pass costs through (inflation hedge via pricing power)
- They have lower P/E multiples than zero-earnings growth names
- The residual_roa energy names (APA, DINO, PARR, PBR) are direct inflation hedges
- Community banks (FNRN, FMBM, CSBB) benefit from NIM expansion as rates rise
- The WFE names (LRCX, MKSI, TER) have non-cyclical demand from TSMC/Samsung/Micron capex commitments

The portfolio is structurally well-positioned for the BofA three-hike scenario. The risk is if the Fed hikes cause a recession (stagflation → deflationary spiral), which would compress ALL earnings including high-ROA names. But that's a 2027+ risk, not an immediate one.

Sources: [Fortune BofA rate hike outlook June 22](https://fortune.com/2026/06/22/fed-rate-hikes-outlook-sticky-inflation-kevin-warsh-job-growth-oil-prices/) | [TheStreet BofA bombshell rate outlook](https://www.thestreet.com/fed/bank-of-america-bombshell-fed-interest-rate-outlook) | [Goldman Sachs rate cut outlook 2026](https://www.goldmansachs.com/insights/articles/the-outlook-for-fed-rate-cuts-in-2026) | [Fed June 2026 dot plot](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomcprojtabl20260617.htm)

### 8. Strategic Insights — June 26 Post-Close

#### A. The Critical Lesson: AI Infrastructure Is One Trade, Not Diversification

Today's BE -12.3% drop is the clearest evidence yet that "AI power/cooling" is NOT a diversifier from "AI semiconductor" exposure within the portfolio. When the market decides to reprice AI infrastructure risk, it sells ALL of it:
- AI memory chips: MU -4%, WDC -10.6%
- AI storage: STX -5%
- AI foundry: INTC -3%
- AI power: BE -12%, VRT -4%
- AI optics: COHR (est. -3-5%), CRDO (est. -3-5%)

The only true diversifiers in the portfolio this week were:
- **ARWR** (pharmaceutical — uncorrelated binary catalyst)
- **Residual_roa defensive names**: JNJ (+0%), MO (+0%), GOOG (+?%), CAT, CBOE, CASY
- **Community banks**: FNRN, FMBM, CSBB (benefited from rate-hike narrative → NIM expansion)

**Strategic implication for sleeve design:** Any new sleeve that wants to hedge AI infrastructure concentration must go to:
1. Healthcare (ARWR, JNJ-type names) — uncorrelated to AI capex cycle
2. Financials/banks — anti-correlated to rate-hike fears (they BENEFIT)
3. Energy commodities — inflation hedge, uncorrelated to AI chip demand

The residual_roa sleeve's (+6.58%) outperformance over mom_roa (+5.80%) and mom_v2 (+3.33%) is driven by exactly this diversification — the residual/idiosyncratic component reduces the tech cluster concentration.

#### B. Potential New Sleeve Concept: "Quality Defense" — ROA + Rate-Hike Beneficiaries

The June 2026 experience strongly suggests a new backtesting project:

**Proposed signal:** ROA > 15% + 6-month momentum (not 12-1) + explicit **sector exclusions** for Technology, Consumer Discretionary, and Real Estate.

**Rationale:**
- ROA > 15% selects high-quality companies with pricing power
- 6-month momentum (shorter lookback) avoids duration traps from last year's tech rally
- Sector exclusions ensure the universe is Financials + Healthcare + Industrials + Energy + Staples
- This is the perfect "rate-hike hedge" — the BofA 3-hike forecast is the driver

**Historical validation periods to backtest:** 2004–2006 (the last aggressive Fed hike cycle) and 2022–2023 (the fastest hike cycle in 40 years). If the signal outperforms SPY in BOTH periods, it's a credible candidate.

**Pre-condition:** This is research-only until the July 1 rebalance is executed cleanly. Do not initiate backtesting until July 2 at the earliest.

#### C. Bloom Energy (BE) — Threshold Monitoring

BE is now the portfolio's most vulnerable name: -6.7% from entry, down -18.8% from the recent ATH of $333.69. The question is whether this is a short-term correction or the beginning of a trend reversal.

**Watch levels:**
- $271 (today's close): Entry -6.7% → model HOLDS (12-1 momentum still very positive)
- $260: Entry -10.5% → model still HOLDS (12-1 momentum YoY still positive)
- $250: Entry -14.0% → model still HOLDS but approaching stress test territory
- $200: Entry -31.2% → 12-1 momentum YoY turns negative; MODEL EXITS at next rebalance

The model's built-in exit mechanism protects against permanent capital loss in BE. If BE's AI power thesis deteriorates to the point where the stock is lower than a year ago, the momentum signal automatically exits. No manual override is needed for BE — trust the model.

**One exception:** If company-specific negative news emerges before July 1 (Oracle deal cancellation, Brookfield withdrawal, major earnings miss), that would warrant a manual evaluation. The current drawdown is sentiment-driven, not fundamental-driven, so no action is required.

#### D. The OpenAI IPO Delay — Portfolio-Level Analysis

Bloomberg and the New York Times reported June 25 that OpenAI is leaning toward delaying its IPO until 2027. This spooked the market because:
1. OpenAI's IPO was seen as a valuation catalyst for AI-adjacent names
2. The delay signals OpenAI itself may not be as confident in near-term monetization
3. SoftBank (a major OpenAI investor via $30B+ commitment) saw its shares tumble on the news

**Impact on our portfolio:** Minimal direct impact. None of our holdings depend on OpenAI revenue. The indirect sentiment effect (AI monetization timeline extends → capex delay narrative) is what drove Friday's tech selloff.

**But here's the counter-argument:** The things OpenAI needs to monetize — HBM memory from Micron, data center storage from WDC/STX, cooling from VRT, power from BE, connectivity from COHR/CRDO — all need to be built BEFORE OpenAI can generate revenue. The buildout precedes the monetization. A 2027 IPO doesn't delay 2026 capex.

The memory/storage infrastructure for OpenAI's current model training is already being purchased and deployed. The question is whether the 2027-2028 capex wave (driven by a wave of new foundation models and inference scaling) materializes as expected. Microsoft's statement ("storage prices increased 2.5x; expect another doubling by fall 2027") is the best real-time indicator — Microsoft has visibility into their AI capex plans 18 months out, and they're confirming the demand.

Sources: [Bloomberg OpenAI IPO delay 2027](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-25/openai-leans-toward-waiting-until-2027-for-ipo-ny-times-says) | [Bloomberg SoftBank shares tumble](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-26/softbank-s-shares-tumble-after-report-of-openai-s-ipo-delay) | [Al Jazeera Apple Microsoft price hikes](https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/6/26/apple-microsoft-hike-prices-over-surging-chip-costs)

### 2. Overnight and Weekend Developments

#### A. US-Iran Ceasefire — The Sunday Night Catalyst

The dominant overnight driver is a **US-Iran ceasefire announcement** late Sunday June 28:
> "Both sides will stand down for now and vessels can move freely."

**Sequence of events:**
- Saturday Jun 28: Iran launched new drone attacks on vessels near Kuwait/Bahrain; US struck Iranian radar installations
- Saturday evening: Iran threatened "complete halt to peace talks"
- Sunday Jun 28: Qatar brokered a halt; Axios reported both sides agreed to pause and meet in Doha on **Tuesday July 1**
- Sunday night: Trump administration confirmed ceasefire; Supreme Court rejected the firing of Fed Governor Lisa Cook (removes institutional uncertainty)

**Pre-market June 29 reaction:**
| Index | Futures |
|---|---|
| S&P 500 | **+0.7%** |
| Nasdaq-100 | **+1.1%** |
| Dow Jones | **+0.4%** |

**Energy:** Oil still slightly volatile — the ceasefire is fragile (both sides escalated just Saturday) and Qatar talks on July 1 are not guaranteed to produce a framework. APA, DINO, PARR, PBR, FTI in residual_roa face ambiguous energy signals: Iran-supply-return risk re-emerges with each peace signal.

**Net portfolio read:** Broad risk-on primarily benefits the tech/AI infrastructure names. Semis and AI-adjacent names (MU, LRCX, WDC, STX, INTC, COHR, CRDO) should open higher. residual_roa's energy cluster is a modest headwind.

Sources: Fortune — markets feel Iran ceasefire relief | Yahoo Finance — stock market today June 29 | RTT News — futures open moderately up

### 3. Catalyst and Macro Review

#### A. Week-Ahead Calendar — Compressed Holiday Week

| Date | Event | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| **Mon Jun 29 (TODAY)** | Pre-rebalance deadline; Iran/Qatar dynamic | **Last day for all DB fixes; overlay eval** |

| **Thu Jul 2** | **NFP Jobs Report** (moved from Fri) + Factory Orders | Consensus 170K; key Fed rate-hike input |
| **Fri Jul 3** | Early close 1pm ET | Holiday prep |
| **Sat Jul 4** | **Markets CLOSED — Independence Day** | — |

**The NFP July 2 report is the macro event of the week.** Consensus: 170K jobs. Q1 2026 averaged only 73K/month (Iran war disruption). A June reading at 170K+ confirms the Q2 recovery is real and gives Fed Chair Warsh data to hold or hike. A miss below 150K would be dovish and benefit growth/tech names.

#### B. Iran-Qatar Talks (Tuesday July 1)

Both sides meet in Qatar on the same day as the rebalance execution. A preliminary framework agreement (30-day Strait-of-Hormuz reopening timeline) would:
- Cause oil to re-test $80 lows → **headwind for residual_roa energy cluster**
- Trigger a "peace dividend" risk-on rally → **tailwind for AI infrastructure, semis**

The portfolio is positioned on the correct side of the AI trade to benefit from a peace risk-on; the energy cluster (APA, DINO, PARR, NRT, PBR) faces further headwind if Iranian oil supply returns.

#### C. PCE and Fed Rate Path

Core PCE: 3.4% (May 2026). BofA three-hike forecast (Sep/Oct/Dec → 4.25%-4.50%) remains the macro backdrop. The Supreme Court rejection of the Cook firing removes one tail risk (Fed independence intact). The ROA quality filter continues to be the correct defensive posture:
- High-ROA companies have pricing power → inflation pass-through
- Community banks (FNRN, FMBM, CSBB in residual_roa) benefit from NIM expansion
- Energy/commodity names are direct inflation hedges

Sources: CNBC — stock market week outlook June 29-July 3 | Heygotrade — week ahead jobs data | Schwab — market update open

**AAOI July 1 Catalyst — CONFIRMED ON SCHEDULE:**
Applied Optoelectronics confirmed that **1.6T transceiver shipments begin July 1, 2026**. The $200M+ order from a long-term hyperscale customer ships Q3 2026 and completes Q4 2026. Production capacity targeting 500,000+ combined 800G/1.6T units/month by year-end. Analyst PT raised to $190 (from $140 post-order).

**Three July 1 scenarios for the LLM experiment:**

**Scenario A — AAOI confirms shipments started (bullish):**
- Stock gaps up 20-40% from $135 → ~$162–$189
- Treatment sleeve must run 3-prompt eval: re-enter at ~$135 (below the original $169.13 entry and below the $169.13 control cost basis)
- Treatment advantage ($19,750) would narrow if AAOI re-rates, but the entry price advantage is structural
- Key scoring question: does the July 1 confirmation resolve the "CPO delay uncertainty" invalidation reason?

**Scenario B — AAOI remains flat or declines (control exits at rebalance):**
- The control sleeve mom_roa_top1 signal exits AAOI when it is no longer #1 momentum
- At $135.69, the 12-1 momentum is approximately -20% from June 2025 → likely sub-threshold
- Control buys new #1 name (possibly MU or another AI infrastructure top-ranker)
- Treatment independently evaluates the new #1 name via the 3 prompts

**Scenario C — Partial confirmation:**
- "Initial deliveries" to one hyperscaler with more expected
- Treatment must weigh ambiguity; the $200M+ total order remains intact even with partial initial shipment

**Action required TODAY:** Run the 3-prompt LLM overlay evaluation on AAOI with the July 1 catalyst confirmation as the key input.

#### DATA FLAG — KLAC Split Not Reflected in paper_positions

**Confirmed in DB query June 29:**
- paper_positions (residual_roa): KLAC qty=**1.1568**, entry_price=**$1,727.12**
- price_cache June 26 close: KLAC = **$248.64** (post-split price)
- Current MTM: 1.1568 × $248.64 = **$287.7** (WRONG)
- Correct post-split MTM: 11.568 × $248.64 = **$2,877.4**
- **Undervaluation = ~$2,590**
- **residual_roa true inception return ≈ +9.22%** (vs reported +6.63% — a +2.59pp discrepancy)

The June 12 report stated "split confirmed handled correctly" but the current DB does not reflect this. This is either a regression (DB restore wiped the fix) or the June 12 report was premature. The fix is required before June 30 MTM.

**Fix SQL:**
```sql
UPDATE paper_positions
SET qty = 11.568, entry_price = 172.71
WHERE ticker = 'KLAC' AND strategy_name = 'residual_roa_6535_paper';
```

### 6. Strategic Insights

#### A. residual_roa Proves Consistent Alpha in Volatile Regimes

**Second consecutive volatile episode where residual_roa outperforms:**
- June 3-11 episode: residual -1.12% vs SPY -3.82% = **+2.70pp** (described as real asymmetric alpha in the capture analysis)
- June 20-26 week: residual -1.12% vs SPY -2.38% = **+1.26pp**

The mechanism is consistent: the idiosyncratic/residual momentum factor selects names with price strength independent of market and sector beta. When a dominant sector theme (AI infrastructure) gets re-priced, the portfolio exposure is naturally diversified. This is not a hedge — the June 11 capture analysis showed 39% up-capture alongside 29% down-capture — it is genuine asymmetric risk-adjusted return.

Since inception (May 1, 2026):
- **residual_roa: +6.63%** (potentially +9.22% after KLAC split fix)
- SPY: +1.16%
- **Alpha: +5.47pp** (or +8.06pp with KLAC correction)

#### B. mom_roa Volatility Is the Trade-Off

mom_roa had the biggest week-over-week swing: peaked at $114,081 (Jun 22) then fell to $105,797 (Jun 26) = -7.3% in 4 days. Yet it still leads inception at +5.86%. The AI infrastructure concentration (MU, LRCX, WDC, STX, INTC, COHR, CRDO) creates extreme volatility: +8% on MU earnings day, -8% when OpenAI IPO delay hit.

The portfolio has BOTH: residual_roa for defensive consistency and mom_roa for AI infrastructure concentration upside. Over a full cycle, the combination should outperform either alone. Track the divergence over the next 6 months.

#### C. July 1 Rebalance — The Most Complex in the Portfolio History

The July 1 rebalance will be the first clean rebalance with ALL data quality issues addressed (assuming ECHO/KLAC/AFJK fixes land today). Expected material changes:
- **~8-10 exits** across the 5 systematic sleeves (AFJK, FN, QDMI, ECHO, LASR, NBR, CLS, AAOI in diversified, possibly FSLY/NVTS/ALB in mom_v1)
- **~8-10 new entrants** from top-momentum names in the clean June 30 signal
- **LLM overlay decision** on AAOI (July 1 1.6T catalyst) — first real binary catalyst overlap with rebalance

The outcome of this rebalance will significantly reshape the portfolio. The new names selected will reflect the clean state of the data after all the 2026 fixes.

#### D. New Sleeve Research Queue (Post-July 1)

Pre-condition: clean rebalance confirmed. Then:
1. **"Quality Defense" sleeve**: ROA >15% + 6-month momentum + sector exclusions (Tech/Disc/RE). Backtest 2004-2006 and 2022-2023 hike cycles. Rate-hike regime hedge.
2. **HBM/Memory supercycle concentrated sleeve**: MU, STX, WDC, LRCX, TER + packaging (5-10 names). Wait for August MU guidance to confirm the multi-year thesis before building.
3. **Biotech binary filter**: Exclude clinical-stage names with no product revenue from mom_v1/v2. Eliminates SNSE/NKTR-type -50%+ blowups.
4. **Earnings proximity filter (±5 days)**: Prevents CIEN-type buy-before-earnings blowups. Needs earnings calendar API integration.

### 1. Market Summary — June 29, 2026

#### A. Index Closes

| Index | Close | Day Change | Notable |
|---|---|---|---|
| **S&P 500** | **7,440.43** | **+1.18%** | Tech sector leads broad rally |
| **Nasdaq Composite** | **25,820.14** | **+2.07%** | AI infrastructure + Alphabet Dow inclusion |
| **Dow Jones** | **52,182.74** | **+0.59%** (+306.63 pts) | **ALL-TIME HIGH** — first ever close above 52,000 |
| **SPY ETF** (portfolio benchmark) | **$741.00** | **+1.65%** | DB price_cache confirmed |

Source: [TheStreet — Stock Market Today June 29](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-29-2026) | [Yahoo Finance — Stock Market Today](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-monday-june-29-224230573.html)

#### B. Primary Market Drivers

**1. Alphabet Joins the Dow Jones Industrial Average**
Alphabet (GOOGL) replaced Verizon in the 30-stock DJIA effective today. GOOGL surged ~5% on its first day as a Dow member. Given the Dow's price-weighting, Alphabet at ~$355 carries roughly 7× Verizon's influence. Passive index tracking triggered substantial rebalancing flows — institutional managers who benchmark to the Dow had to buy GOOGL and sell VZ before the close. Source: [Yahoo Finance — Alphabet Joins Dow Jones](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/alphabet-joins-dow-jones-index-170003693.html)

**2. Iran-Qatar Ceasefire Talks Proceeding Tuesday July 1**
Both sides halted weekend hostilities and agreed to meet in Qatar on July 1 to resolve differences over the Strait of Hormuz. Oil futures rose modestly (~+0.33% to $69.46 WTI) as markets remained somewhat cautious, but the ceasefire framing provided a risk-on backdrop for equities. The Iran war context (largest supply disruption in IEA history) means any ceasefire progress is equity-positive and energy-negative. Source: [Yahoo Finance — US Iran Ceasefire Stock Futures](https://finance.yahoo.com/economy/policy/article/us-iran-agree-to-ceasefire-sending-stock-futures-higher-and-oil-lower-225858774.html) | [Fortune — Iran Ceasefire Talks](https://fortune.com/2026/06/28/stock-market-today-dow-futures-oil-prices-us-iran-ceasefire-talks-oil-prices-hormuz/)

**3. Micron Blowout Q3 Earnings Ripple Effect (from June 25/26)**
Micron's record Q3 results ($41.46B revenue +346% YoY, 84.6% gross margin) continued to lift WFE, storage, and semiconductor equipment names across the board. LRCX +8.4%, MKSI +7.0%, ICHR +12.2%, ONTO +8.5%, MU +1.1%, WDC +11.2%, STX +7.6%.

**4. Russell Index Reconstitution**
Annual FTSE Russell reconstitution drove mechanically large flows into added growth names. GLW was reclassified into Russell 1000 Growth, triggering institutional passive buying and a +15.7% single-session move. Source: [GuruFocus — Corning GLW Soars 15.7%](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8936915/corning-glw-soars-157-amid-sector-rotation) | [TradingKey — GLW June 29 Market Movers](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/261998588-market-movers-glw-20260629)

#### C. Sector Performance (portfolio ETF holdings, price_cache closes)

### 3. Key Position Analysis

#### A. Top Movers — June 29 Gainers (price_cache close June 29 vs June 26)

**VSAT (Viasat) — +23.8% — Largest single-name move in the portfolio today**

Today's close (price_cache): $76.69 vs prev $61.94. Present in: mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2. Entry $65.15. Inception P&L: **+17.7%**.

The surge was driven by a stack of catalysts landing simultaneously:
- U.S. Space Force selected Viasat to build, launch, and deliver the first fleet of maneuverable GEO satellites under the Protected Tactical SATCOM-Global (PTS-G) program, covering design, manufacturing, launch, and on-orbit checkout of anti-jam satellites ($437.7M award)
- Deutsche Bank more than doubled its VSAT price target from $48 to **$97**; Needham raised to **$90** from $58; Oppenheimer set **$140** PT (+96% upside)
- Rocket Lab announced an $8B acquisition of Iridium, making Viasat — which competes with Iridium — an immediate M&A speculation target for other space/defense buyers
- A new strategic partnership with Xperi's TiVo OS and a Lockheed Martin contract for NOAA hurricane-hunter aircraft provided additional thesis support

This is the first time VSAT has shown a genuine fundamental thesis stack (government contract + M&A speculation + analyst upgrades) on top of its momentum signal. Source: [Timothy Sykes — VSAT Space Force Contracts](https://www.timothysykes.com/news/viasat-inc-vsat-news-2026_06_29/) | [FinancialContent — Why VSAT Shares Soaring](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/stockstory-2026-6-29-why-are-viasat-vsat-shares-soaring-today) | [Blockonomi — VSAT Oppenheimer $140 PT](https://blockonomi.com/viasat-vsat-stock-surges-as-oppenheimer-sets-140-price-target-a-96-upside-potential/)

**GLW (Corning) — +15.7% — Russell Reconstitution Event**

Close: $255.69 vs prev $220.91. Present in: mom_v1 only. Entry $158.34. Inception P&L: **+61.5%** (now the second-best performing position in the portfolio by inception return after MU and ELA).

The move is entirely mechanical: FTSE Russell's annual reconstitution reclassified Corning from value benchmarks into Russell 3000 Growth, Russell 1000 Growth, and Russell Top 200 Growth. Passive funds tracking these indices had to buy GLW aggressively at close. Trailing P/E >90x and implied volatility spiking to 81.7% — this is a one-day event, not a thesis shift. The 12-1 momentum signal has been driven by GLW's AI fiber-optic cable narrative (AI data centers require high-density fiber interconnects; Corning is the dominant supplier). Source: [GuruFocus — Corning Soars 15.7%](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8936915/corning-glw-soars-157-amid-sector-rotation) | [TradingKey — GLW June 29](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/261998588-market-movers-glw-20260629)

### 4. LLM Overlay Treatment vs. Control

#### A. mom_roa_top1: Control vs. Treatment

| Inception return (from Jun 12) | **-11.24%** | **0.00%** |
| Day move | **+$8,511 (+10.61%)** | **$0 (0.00%)** |
| Treatment lead | — | **+$11,243 (+11.24pp)** |

**Key development today:** Treatment lead **NARROWED sharply from +19.75pp (Jun 26) to +11.24pp (Jun 29)**. The AAOI rally (+10.61% per price_cache, with the caveat above about data discrepancy) significantly closed the gap. This is the largest single-day narrowing since the experiment began. 

**LLM overlay log — most recent decision:** June 12, 2026. Ticker: AAOI. Verdict: VETO (score 4.0). Invalidation level: $150.00. Rationale: "Extreme valuation ~110-215x fwd with net losses after a ~10x 12m run; momentum rolling over -24pct 1m, analyst targets at/below spot."

**Invalidation level NOTE:** If price_cache has AAOI at $150.10 today, it has touched and slightly exceeded the VETO invalidation level of $150.00. This does not automatically trigger a re-eval, but it is notable — the invalidation level was set as the upside risk threshold when the VETO was issued at $169.13 (AAOI was already below entry at the time of VETO on June 12).

**July 1 scenario for the experiment:**
- The control sleeve (mom_roa_top1) tracks the #1 momentum name by signal. AAOI has negative 12-1 momentum (current price below June 2025 level) → AAOI will be **EXITED** at July 1 rebalance in the control sleeve, regardless of the LLM experiment.
- Control will ENTER the new #1 momentum name (to be determined by June 30 close prices). This creates a fresh decision node for the treatment: does the LLM overlay agree to BUY the new #1 name?
- **Overlay eval is OVERDUE.** The last decision was June 12. The pre-market report flagged this as CRITICAL. No entry was logged in `llm_overlay_log` today.

**Treatment advantage interpretation:** Treatment is winning because AAOI has been in structural decline since the June 12 VETO. However, the +11.2% intraday bounce today (largest since June 12) narrows the margin. If AAOI had instead rallied +10.6% every day, the treatment would eventually fall behind. The real test is July 1: what is the new control name, and what does the treatment decide?

**Assessment of structural edge at 60 days:**
The four sleeves are not the same portfolio — they disagree on 30-50% of positions. mom_roa and residual_roa are the most distinctive. Both are showing excess returns across the period. The 60-day forward result is directionally positive but below the statistical confidence threshold needed to declare edge (minimum 12-18 months per the strategy design). Continue tracking.

### 6. Strategic Insights

#### A. Today's Rally Pattern Reveals AI Infrastructure Regime Persistence

Monday's session pattern — SPY +1.18%, Nasdaq +2.07%, tech/semi names +5-15% — is now the third time since May 1 that a macro-driven selloff was followed by a sharp, overshooting recovery. The pattern:
- June 3-11: Energy/SPAC blowup risk-off → recovery +2-4% in one session
- June 20-26: OpenAI IPO delay / hawkish Fed → recovery +4.56% (mom_v2) today
Each recovery is larger than the preceding selloff in the AI infrastructure names. This is textbook momentum persistence: the factor exists because institutional investors systematically sell momentum names on macro news, then buy them back when the news fades.

**Implication:** The sleeves are designed for this regime. The underperformance of the sector_top4 (low beta structure, 1 slot in cash) and llm_overlay_sector_top4 today (+0.31%/+0.35%) versus the systematic sleeves (+2.15% to +4.56%) is the expected tradeoff: less drawdown in selloffs, less upside in rips.

#### B. GLW's +15.7% Move — A Warning About Reconstitution Events

GLW entering Russell 1000 Growth creates a one-day mechanical spike. The stock's P/E is >90x. The momentum signal that put GLW in mom_v1 was driven by genuine AI-fiber demand (Corning sells high-density fiber to every hyperscaler building AI clusters). But a 15.7% move in one day from index reconstitution is not thesis-driven — it is flow-driven. If GLW is not exited at July 1 (the 12-1 momentum signal would need to have degraded for an exit), the position will face a natural reversal as passive index funds complete their buying. **Monitor closely for overshooting.** The reconstitution buying was done at close, so tomorrow's trading will price on fundamentals again.

#### C. VSAT Now Has a Real Thesis Layer on Top of Momentum

VSAT entered portfolios purely on 12-1 momentum (the satellite communications sector has been on a run since the Iran war began — government demand + spectrum assets). Today, the PTS-G Space Force award adds a multi-year government contract backlog, and the Iridium acquisition by Rocket Lab creates a realistic M&A angle. Even if VSAT is eventually exited on a momentum signal, the position now has a secondary safety net: the business value has improved.

#### D. residual_roa — KLAC Fix Is Urgent

If the KLAC split correction (+$2,898) is applied, residual_roa becomes the portfolio's best-performing sleeve at +11.77% inception. The KLAC position's DAY MOVE today (+12.0% on the split-adjusted price_cache price) means the understatement is growing — each day KLAC rises, the gap between reported and true NAV widens. **This is the highest-priority operational fix before June 30 close.**

#### E. July 1 Rebalance Will Be a Portfolio Reset

The rebalance (executing on June 30 close prices) will involve:
- **Exits (near-certain):** AFJK, QDMI, SATS/ECHO, FN, AAOI (control only — likely negative 12-1), NBR, CLS, FSLY, NVTS, LASR, CELC, CENX, ALB, PTN, possibly CBOE
- **New entrants:** The clean June 30 signal from the top-100 (mom_v1) and top-50 (mom_v2/mom_roa) momentum names will reflect today's big movers. VSAT, GLW, ICHR, VICR, UCTT (at all-time highs or near them) will likely retain/strengthen their signals. New names entering the top ranks from today's 10-15% moves could appear in next rebalance.
- **LLM overlay decision:** The new mom_roa #1 name (post-AAOI exit) needs a 3-prompt evaluation before July 1. This eval is overdue.

**New sleeve research queue (post-July 1, pre-condition = clean rebalance):**
1. Quality Defense (ROA >15% + 6-month momentum + sector exclusions)
2. HBM/Memory sleeve concentrated (MU/STX/WDC/LRCX/TER, wait for August MU guidance)
3. Biotech binary filter (exclude clinical-stage names from mom_v1/v2)
4. Earnings proximity filter (±5 days, requires earnings calendar API)

### 3. Catalyst and Macro Review

#### A. Today: Q2/H1 End — Signal Lock Day for July 1 Rebalance

June 30, 2026 is the **last trading day of Q2 2026 and H1 2026.** Today's 4pm closes are the inputs to the momentum scoring that determines the July 1 rebalance. All five systematic sleeves (mom_v1, mom_v2, mom_roa, residual_roa, sector_top4) plus the LLM overlay controls/treatments will rebalance based on today's close prices.

Implication: window dressing creates a final bid for the biggest winners (VSAT +24% June 29, KLAC +7%, UCTT all-time high, VICR guidance raised) — these names likely get one more lift today before institutions rotate out post-rebalance tomorrow.

#### B. Nike NKE Q4 FY2026 Earnings After Bell (Not Portfolio Name — Market Mover)

Nike releases Q4 FY2026 results at ~1:15pm PT. Analyst consensus: revenue $10.85B (-2% YoY), EPS $0.13 (-7% YoY). Focus: CEO Elliott Hill turnaround (inventory clearance, wholesale restoration), Greater China outlook (-20% revenue decline risk). NKE results will influence consumer/macro sentiment for the final hours of Q2 trading.
- Source: [StockTitan — Nike Q4 FY2026 Earnings Date](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/NKE/nike-inc-announces-fourth-quarter-fiscal-2026-earnings-and-iwzljao8o4by.html)

#### C. Jobs Report Thursday July 2 — Holiday-Shortened Week

The June Employment Situation releases Thursday July 2 at 8:30am (bond market closed Friday July 4 for Independence Day). May 2026 baseline: +172K nonfarm payrolls, unemployment 4.3%. The jobs report will directly feed July/September FOMC rate expectations.

**FOMC context:** Paused at 3.50–3.75% (June 16–17 meeting). Core PCE revised upward to 3.3% for Q4 2026 (well above 2% target), keeping the pause posture in place. Markets are NOT pricing a cut until late 2026 or 2027 at earliest under current trajectory.
- Source: [BLS — May 2026 Employment Situation](https://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.nr0.htm) | [IC Markets — June 30 Fundamental Forecast](https://www.icmarkets.com/blog/ic-markets-global-europe-fundamental-forecast-30-june-2026/) | [FRED Blog — FOMC June 2026 Projections](https://fredblog.stlouisfed.org/2026/06/fomc-summary-of-economic-projections-june-2026/)

#### D. AAOI — 1.6T Transceiver Shipments START July 1

The most significant overnight catalyst: Applied Optoelectronics' milestone calendar shows **"Start shipments for 1.6T order" = July 1, 2026** (tomorrow). The $200M+ first volume order from a major hyperscaler begins delivering. This is the first tangible revenue delivery of the 1.6T product line.

Additional AAOI context:
- Q1 2026 revenue +51% to $151.1M; fifth straight quarter of record sales
- Q2 2026 guidance: $180–$198M revenue, 29–30% non-GAAP gross margin
- Manufacturing capacity expansion: 388,000 sq ft in Pearland TX, Texas Semiconductor grant $20.85M
- Target: 650,000+ units/month of 800G and 1.6T by late 2026
- June 29 confirmed close: **$138.54** (not $150.10 in DB — unresolved price error)
- 12-1 momentum: strongly **POSITIVE** (~+399% YoY; year-ago June 2025 ~$28 vs current $138.54)

**CRITICAL CORRECTION to June 29 report:** The prior report stated "AAOI: If June 2025 base was ~$169, today's $136-$150 range = -11% to -20% 12-1 → BELOW THRESHOLD → likely exit at July 1 rebalance." This is the deepest Micron/AI model provider integration to date.

#### H. GLW Corning — Post-Reconstitution Reversal + CEO Insider Selling

GLW +14.03% on June 29 was purely mechanical (Russell 1000 Growth reconstitution passive buying). June 30 data shows -1.72% reversal already in progress as reconstitution buying is complete.

**Two new negatives:**
1. **CEO Insider Selling:** CEO Wendell Weeks + multiple SVPs sold 160,000+ shares worth $30.7M with **zero offsetting insider buying**. Significant negative signal for medium-term holders.
2. **Solar Plant Problems:** Production bottlenecks and startup inefficiencies at the new solar wafer plant: extended maintenance shutdown + hardware upgrades → **$30M additional Q2 2026 operating expenses**.

Analyst consensus PT: $204.30 (11 analysts, Buy) — stock was ~$255 post-spike, implying ~20% overvaluation vs. consensus. The AI fiber narrative (dominant supplier to hyperscalers building AI data center interconnects) drives the 12-1 momentum signal but the stock is now trading well above analyst targets.

#### I. WDC/STX — Fox Advisors Double Downgrade (New Caution Flag)

Both WDC ($651.88, +11.2% June 29) and STX ($968.53, +7.6% June 29) received simultaneous Fox Advisors downgrades to Equal-Weight from Outperform. Concern: "expectations for HDD pricing may be getting ahead" of likely increases. This is a valuation-stretch thesis after 250%+ YTD gains.

Counter: Cantor Fitzgerald raised WDC to $900 (Overweight) and STX to $1,300 (Overweight); Melius Research initiated WDC at Buy with $1,050 target. Seagate redeeming $150.7M in debt (balance sheet strengthening). The AI training-data-lake demand for high-capacity HDD is structural, but pricing expectations may be running ahead.

Both names held across all four systematic sleeves. The downgrade is a watch flag, not a thesis break.

US-Iran MoU in force; Strait of Hormuz reopened; naval blockade lifted. WTI crude: **$73.61** (Brent $77.68) — approximately 20% below 2026 peak ($120+). XLE declined even on June 29's strong day (+0% or negative) while XLK rose 2.4%.

**Sector overlay XLE veto (June 12, score 3) fully vindicated.** The original rationale — "Hormuz supply-shock trade reversing on ceasefire news" — has played out exactly as predicted. The control sector_top4 sleeve holds XLE at 25% weight (entered $58.88, May 1) and is losing on this position versus the treatment's cash slot.

Whether XLE remains in the top-4 sectors for July 1 scoring is unknown without the signal data, but with oil at $73.61 vs. 2026 highs of $120+, XLE's 12-1 momentum has severely weakened. The July 1 sector rebalance may naturally eject XLE from the top 4, resolving the veto position.

#### N. AFJK Aimei Health — July 6 SPAC Going-Concern Deadline

AFJK (held in mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2 at $40.89 entry) has a signed Business Combination Agreement with United Hydrogen dependent on CSRC (Chinese regulatory) approval. Q1 2026 10-Q contains **explicit going-concern language: "substantial doubt about going concern if no deal is completed by the July 6, 2026 combination deadline."** Outside-trust cash: $18,516; working capital deficit: ~$3.5M as of March 31, 2026.

Most recent available price: $43.00 (June 9). If the deal fails by July 6, the SPAC liquidates at trust NAV (~$10/share) — a ~75% loss from the June 9 price.

**July 1 rebalance should exit AFJK** from all three sleeves. The momentum signal that originally placed it in portfolios has turned deeply negative; the July 1 score almost certainly drops it below any threshold.

**AAOI price_cache confirmed error:** Web sources confirm AAOI June 29 close = **$138.54** (Simply Wall St, Yahoo Finance). DB price_cache = $150.10. Overstatement: 590.654 shares × $11.56 = **$6,827.96**. True control NAV: $81,929.22, not $88,757.18.

**Last decision:** June 12, 2026. VETO on AAOI (score 4.0, invalidation $150.00). AAOI has since fallen from $169.13 to $138.54 (-18.1%). VETO correct.

**1.6T shipment catalyst (July 1):** If AAOI 8-K confirms shipment start tomorrow, this is bullish for AAOI price. Even if AAOI rallies, the treatment leads by $18.2K true — the veto was the right call. The experiment enters a new phase at July 1 when the control exits AAOI (or retains it if rank holds) and potentially takes a new #1 position.

**CRITICAL: New evaluation required.** The July 1 rebalance will change the control sleeve's holding to whatever name is ranked #1 in mom_roa signal on June 30 close. The treatment must evaluate this new name before the July 1 buy order executes. This eval is OVERDUE.

**CORRECTION to prior report's AAOI momentum analysis:** The prior report stated AAOI would exit July 1 on "negative 12-1 momentum." This was based on confusing the June 12 sleeve entry price ($169.13) with the June 2025 base price (~$27-28). AAOI's 12-1 return is ~+399% YoY — the momentum signal is strongly positive. AAOI exits the top-50 (if it does) on RANK, not on negative signal.

### 6. Strategic Insights

#### A. H1 2026 Final Scorecard — 60 Trading Days of Forward Evidence

At today's 5:15pm MTM, the H1 record is sealed. Based on June 29 data (essentially the H1 final):
- All four systematic sleeves beat SPY (+2.82%) by 4–9pp inception
- ROA-filtered sleeves (mom_roa +10.36%, residual_roa +11.76%) outperform pure momentum (mom_v1 +6.86%, mom_v2 +8.07%)
- Both sector sleeves underperformed SPY: sector_top4 +2.42%, llm_overlay_sector +0.25%
- LLM stock overlay treatment holds cash and leads control by $18.2K (true) — a successful VETO, but not positive alpha from LLM discretion

60 trading days is directionally positive for the factor approach but below the 12–18 month threshold for statistical edge declaration. The ROA filter's outperformance over pure momentum across this specific period is the most interesting signal: the quality-profitability requirement adds defensive properties (lower drawdown in June 23-26 selloff) while maintaining upside capture. Continue tracking.

#### B. Window Dressing = Final Inflated Signal for July 1 Scoring

Today's institutional buying of Q2 winners artificially inflates the momentum signal scores at the close. This means the July 1 rebalance buys names at slightly inflated prices (window dressing is done by 4pm). However, this bias is symmetric — the exit prices for names leaving the portfolio also benefit from the window dressing bid. Net impact: likely small, but the first few days of July may see a modest reversal of June 30's window-dressing spike in individual names.

#### C. KLAC Short Interest Spike Is the Portfolio's Most Unusual Market Microstructure Signal

36.6% of float sold short in KLAC is remarkable. The most likely explanation: the 10-for-1 split dramatically increased the share count and share borrowing availability, creating new short-selling capacity that didn't exist at $1,727/share. The short sellers are betting on valuation mean reversion (70x P/E). The counter is that KLAC's WFE tools are in structural demand for every advanced chip fab worldwide (AI, HBM, advanced packaging). A sustained squeeze scenario is plausible given the extreme short positioning.

**Portfolio impact is modest** (KLAC is one position in residual_roa at ~$3,222 value), but the dynamic is worth watching for the broader semiconductor equipment thesis.

#### D. AAOI: The 12-1 Momentum Signal Is Positive — July 1 Outcome Is a Rank Question

Correcting the prior report's error: AAOI has ~+399% 12-1 return. It will only exit the portfolios if its score ranks below the top-N threshold. With the 1.6T shipments starting July 1 and Q2 guidance of $180-198M revenue (another record), the fundamental backdrop remains intact. The -22% price decline over the past month reflects valuation mean-reversion from a stretched level, not fundamental deterioration.

**For the LLM experiment:** If AAOI exits the control sleeve on rank (not negative signal), the experiment gets a new test case tomorrow. If AAOI remains in the top-50 and the control retains it, the treatment must decide whether to buy (after its prior VETO). Either way, a decision is required before July 1 execution.

#### E. The Biotech Cluster in mom_v1 — Natural Culling Expected at Rebalance

Mom_v1's accumulation of clinical-stage biotechs (CAPR, PTN, CELC, PRAX, STRO, RVMD, CNTA, COGT, SION, FBRX, PVLA, FTH, TYRA, NKTR, INBX, JBIO, DRUG, AMLX, SRZN) is the momentum factor doing its job: biotech stocks with clinical momentum get elevated prices, triggering the signal. But many of these have 30-60 day momentum turning negative (PTN -23.4%, CAPR -13.9%, CELC -18.4%), and the natural rebalance will cull them without requiring any manual action. The CAPR PDUFA (Aug 22) is the highest-priority binary biotech risk still in the portfolio.

#### F. Sector_top4 Underperformance Analysis

Both sector sleeves underperformed SPY in H1 (+2.42% control, +0.25% treatment vs SPY +2.82%). The underperformance drivers:
1. XLE allocation (25% weight) in the control dragged returns as oil fell 20% from peak
2. The sector rotation strategy has a structural lower-beta profile vs. the AI-driven individual stock sleeves
3. The llm_overlay_sector treatment gave up the XLK/XLI/XLB gains by holding 25% cash

The sector strategy's long-term raison d'être is diversification and drawdown control, not raw return maximization. At 60 days of data, the appropriate response is to continue tracking, not to alter the strategy.

#### A. Index Closes

| Index | Close | Day Change | Q2 Return (Apr 1-Jun 30) | Notable |

| **Nasdaq Composite** | **26,213.72** | **+1.52%** | **+20.0%** | Best Q since Q4 2020 |
| **Dow Jones** | **52,319.20** | **+0.26%** | ~+8% | New all-time high close |
| **SPY ETF** (portfolio benchmark) | **$746.77** | **+0.78%** | — | DB price_cache confirmed |

Source: [TheStreet — Stock Market Today June 30](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-30-2026) | [Yahoo Finance — Stock Market News Jun 30](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/stock-market-news-jun-30-133900372.html) | [Schwab — Stocks Open Flat to End Best Quarter](https://www.schwab.com/learn/story/stock-market-update-open) | [BNN Bloomberg — US Stocks Rise and Trim June Losses](https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/markets/2026/06/30/us-stocks-are-drifting-to-the-close-of-a-weak-june/)

#### B. Primary Market Drivers

**1. Quarter-End Window Dressing**
Today was the final session of Q2 2026 — the best quarter for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq since 2020. Institutional funds executing quarter-end window dressing (buying winners, selling losers before sending client statements) provided a systematic tailwind for high-momentum names. AI infrastructure, semiconductors, and our portfolio's concentrated momentum positions all benefited from this mechanical bid. The semiconductor ETF SMH climbed more than 3% on the day.

**2. Broad AI Semiconductor Continuation**
The Micron Q3 blowout (June 25, $41.46B revenue, +346% YoY) continued to reverberate through the supply chain. AMD +7.7%, Nvidia +2.6%, Intel +6%, Lam Research +5.46%. The AI infrastructure upcycle narrative is deepening: Micron alone contributed 4.5 points of S&P 500 EPS growth per Seeking Alpha analysis, anchoring the AI capex ROI argument that Oracle's Q4 (June 9) had challenged. Source: [Intellectia AI — Chip Stocks Rebound Strategy June 2026](https://intellectia.ai/blog/chip-stocks-rebound-investment-strategy-june-2026)

**3. Iran-Qatar Ceasefire Stability**
Both sides maintained the ceasefire and proceeded to Doha talks. Oil remained range-bound (WTI ~$73; Brent ~$77). No escalation means energy names continued to underperform vs. tech on this session — XLE -0.88% vs XLK +2.76%. The Iran-Hormuz risk premium continues to fade from equities while the peace dividend accrues to AI infrastructure and broad risk-on assets. Source: [Al Jazeera — Oil Prices Rise as US-Iranian Strikes Threaten Strait Reopening](https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/6/29/oil-prices-rise-as-us-iranian-strikes-threaten-strait-of-hormuz-reopening) | [CNBC — Oil Drops 20% from 2026 Peak on Ceasefire Optimism](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/29/oil-prices-iran-ceasefire-us-trump-strait-hormuz-energy-costs.html)

**4. Nike Earnings Beat (After Bell)**
Nike (NKE) reported Q4 FY2026 after the close: EPS 20c vs 13c expected (+54% beat), revenue $10.97B vs $10.86B expected. Full-year revenue $46.4B. Q4 gross margin expanded +8.9%, aided by a ~$986M tariff refund following Supreme Court action on global tariff duties. Weakness: Greater China -12%, Converse -30%+. Nike is not held in any portfolio sleeve, but the beat is a positive macro signal. Source: [NIKE SEC 8-K Q4 FY2026](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000320187/000032018726000076/q4fy26exhibit991er.htm) | [CNBC — Nike NKE Q4 2026 Earnings](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/30/nike-nke-q4-2026-earnings.html) | [MarketBeat — NKE Q4 2026 Report](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/reports/2026-6-30-nike-inc-stock/)

**5. JOLTS + Consumer Confidence (June 30 AM)**
Both the June JOLTS Job Openings and Conference Board Consumer Confidence were released pre-market. Based on available reporting, both releases appeared to beat or meet expectations (JOLTS forecast 7.29M, CB Consumer Confidence forecast 94.4). The market's positive reaction is consistent with beat readings. Exact actuals were not confirmed in available search sources — NFP Thursday July 2 (consensus 170K) is the more market-moving labor release. Source: [XTB — Economic Calendar JOLTS Consumer Confidence Nike](https://www.xtb.com/int/market-analysis/news-and-research/economic-calendar-markets-await-us-jolts-consumer-sentiments-and-nike-earnings) | [YouTube — JOLTs and Consumer Confidence Beat Expectations](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qaFf-P7uDFA)

**MXL (MaxLinear) — +18.03% — Portfolio's Largest Single-Name Day Gain**

| | Jun 29 Close | Jun 30 Close | Inception P&L (entry $91.44) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Price | $108.47 | **$128.03** | **+40.0%** |

Two Wall Street analysts more than doubled their price targets on June 30:
- **Northland Capital:** PT raised to **$110** (Outperform), citing rising AI optical demand and multi-year AI infrastructure upcycle
- **Stifel:** PT raised to **$105** (from $49), highlighting rerating of AI-focused data center chip names

Additional catalyst: MaxLinear announced a collaboration with Los Alamos National Laboratory demonstrating up to ~39x write and ~7x read speedups for OpenZFS storage — reinforcing the high-performance data center infrastructure thesis. MXL makes high-speed analog/mixed-signal semiconductors for broadband and data-center interconnect. Present in: mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2. - Fundamental thesis unchanged: $20-24B backlog, Oracle 2.8GW, Brookfield $5B partnership

### 4. LLM Overlay Treatment vs. Control

#### A. Experiment 1 (mom_roa_top1) — CLOSED at June 30 Re-Inception

Both the control (`mom_roa_top1_paper`) and treatment (`llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper`) were reset to $100,000 today (June 30) in preparation for the July 1 rebalance.

**Final standings at experiment close-out:**

| | Control (AAOI concentrated) | Treatment (cash since Jun 12 VETO) |
|---|---|---|
| Last DB NAV (Jun 29) | $88,757.18 (overstated — AAOI price error) | $100,000.00 |
| True estimated NAV (Jun 29) | **~$81,829** (-18.17%) | **$100,000** (0.00%) |
| AAOI June 30 close (DB) | $148.16 | — |
| True estimated final NAV | **~$87,400 (~-12.6%)** | **$100,000** |
| **Treatment lead at close-out** | — | **~+12.6pp to +18.2pp** |

*Note: AAOI June 29 DB price of $150.10 was flagged as potentially overstated in the pre-market report (web sources cited ~$138.54). Final treatment advantage range depends on which June 29 AAOI base is used. Both scenarios show the treatment materially outperforming.*

**Experiment 1 Summary:** The treatment (cash since the June 12 VETO of AAOI at $169.13) outperformed the control (hold AAOI) by approximately **12-18pp** over the 18-day live experiment. The LLM's VETO rationale ("extreme valuation ~110-215x fwd with net losses after ~10x 12m run; momentum rolling over") was directionally correct — AAOI declined from $169.13 to $148.16 over the period, a -12.4% move vs cash flat. The gap was as wide as +19.75pp (June 26) before AAOI's +10.6% recovery on June 29 narrowed it.

#### B. llm_overlay_sector_top4 — CLOSED at Re-Inception

Final standing: Treatment $100,250 (+0.25%) vs Control `sector_top4_full` $103,452 (+3.50%) = **Control leads by +3.25pp**. The XLE veto was directionally correct (XLE -0.88% today, continuing its post-Iran-peak decline), but the XLK, XLI, and XLB allocations in the control outperformed the cash slot in the treatment. Being right on ONE sector veto is not enough to overcome losing the returns on 3 fully-deployed positions.

#### C. New Experimental Sleeves (initialized June 30)

Two new sleeves appeared in the DB today:
- **`llm_cascade_top1_paper`** — $100,000 at inception (June 30). Likely a "cascade" LLM decision architecture (multi-stage evaluation) vs. the prior single-prompt overlay.
- **`llm_cascade_sector4_paper`** — $100,000 at inception (June 30). Sector equivalent.

Both sleeves have no positions and no logged decisions yet. Their design intent is inferred from their names; no documentation was found in available DB tables. Additionally:
- `sector_top4_paper` was re-incepted at $100,000 — appears to be a fresh control sleeve.
- All original overlay sleeves also reset: `mom_roa_top1_paper`, `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper`, `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper`.

**AAOI July 1 1.6T Catalyst (imminent for new experiment decisions):**
The $200M+ 1.6T transceiver shipment to a long-term hyperscaler customer begins **tomorrow (July 1)**. AAOI's 12-1 momentum (current $148 vs ~$169 Jun 2025 baseline) is likely now negative, meaning AAOI probably exits the control's signal and a new name enters. The new control position TBD — requires running overlay_prep.bat and identifying the new #1 mom_roa name. Source: [GlobeNewswire — AOI Receives First Volume 1.6T Order](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/03/09/3251675/9986/en/AOI-Receives-First-Volume-Order-of-1-6T-Data-Center-Transceivers-from-Major-Hyperscaler-Customer.html)

### 5. Q2 2026 Period Summary (2-Month Live Period: May 1 to June 30)

Today marks the close of the second calendar quarter of 2026 and the completion of the portfolio's first two months of live paper trading.

**Q2 2026 macro context:** The S&P 500 returned +13.5% for the full quarter (April through June 30), its best quarterly performance since Q1 2020, despite the ongoing Iran war. The Nasdaq returned +20%, its best quarterly gain since Q4 2020. Technology led (+28% for the quarter) while healthcare, industrials, and financials rotated higher in late June. Source: [TheStreet — Tech Recovery Nasdaq S&P Jumping June 30](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-30-2026) | [Alexandria Capital — 2026 Mid-Year Review](https://alexandriacapital.com/blogs/weekly-market-note/2026-mid-year-review-second-half-outlook)

**Our portfolio's May 1 to June 30 (61 trading days) performance vs. SPY:**

| Sleeve | May 1 NAV | Jun 30 NAV | Return | vs. SPY (+3.62%) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **mom_roa_6535_paper** | $99,950 | **$114,399** | **+14.46%** | **+10.84pp** |
| **residual_roa_6535_paper** | $99,950 | **$112,936** | **+12.99%** | **+9.37pp** |
| **mom_v2_paper** | $99,950 | **$111,357** | **+11.41%** | **+7.79pp** |
| **mom_v1_paper** | $99,950 | **$109,869** | **+9.92%** | **+6.30pp** |
| **spy_benchmark_paper** | $100,000 | $103,625 | +3.62% | — |
| **sector_top4_full_paper** | $99,950 | $103,452 | +3.50% | -0.12pp |

**The two-month period included six major regime events:**
1. **June 5 Broadcom guidance miss** — $1.3T semiconductor wipeout; worst tech day in 2026
2. **June 10 Iran escalation + CPI 4.2%** — dual risk-off selloff; Dow -900 pts
3. **June 12-13 history-gap backfill re-inception** — six contaminated sleeves reset
4. **June 25 Micron Q3 blowout** — +346% YoY, HBM supercycle confirmed; memory/storage rips
5. **June 26 AI capex doubt selloff** — BE -12.3%, WDC -10.6%, OpenAI IPO delay uncertainty
6. **June 29-30 Iran ceasefire + quarter-end window dressing** — tech/AI names recapture losses

Despite six significant regime events, all four systematic factor sleeves materially outperformed SPY over the full period. This is directionally positive evidence for factor alpha, though the 61-trading-day sample is far below the minimum 12-18 months needed for statistical confidence.

**Key statistical notes:**
- The history-gap re-inception on June 13 was a portfolio reset; prior data (May 1-June 12) for the reconstituted sleeves should be weighted cautiously
- sector_top4_full (-0.12pp vs SPY) is essentially tracking SPY — the sector rotation factor has not yet demonstrated edge in this market regime
- residual_roa's outperformance is partially attributable to the KLAC split correction which understated its NAV until the June 29 fix
