### 6. Strategic Insights

#### A. BE and VRT Recovery: Sentiment Selloffs Are Reversible

The June 26 selloffs in BE (-12.3%) and VRT (-4.3%) were pure AI-capex sentiment correlation, not fundamental deterioration. Both stocks recovered to above entry within two trading sessions:
- BE: $252 to $302.70 (+20.1% in 2 days) — recovered full entry cushion, now +4.1%
- VRT: $303.95 to $334.82 (+10.2% in 2 days) — recovered full entry cushion, now +1.9%

The lesson: sentiment-driven drawdowns on momentum names tend to be fast and deep but are also quickly reversed when the sentiment trigger fades. The July 1 rebalance occurs on fundamentals (12-1 momentum) — a single week's price action does not override a 12-month signal.

#### B. MXL's +18% Shows the Analyst PT Cascade Effect in Mid-Caps

MaxLinear's +18% day was triggered by two analysts more than doubling their price targets (Northland to $110, Stifel to $105). MXL was at $91.44 at our May 1 entry and is now $128.03 (+40%). The "analyst PT cascade" is a well-documented momentum amplifier: a PT raise in a stock with a strong 12-month signal attracts additional institutional attention. MXL is a small enough cap that two analyst upgrades can meaningfully move the stock. This dynamic is precisely what the momentum signal is designed to capture.

#### C. VSAT Has Transformed From Pure Momentum to Multi-Layer Thesis

VSAT entered the portfolio on purely mechanical momentum grounds. Over the past two days, it has accumulated:
- **Government contract anchor**: $437.7M U.S. Space Force PTS-G award (multi-year)
- **Analyst consensus shift**: Three firms doubled PTs (Oppenheimer $140, Deutsche Bank $97, Needham $90)
- **M&A speculation**: Rocket Lab's $8B Iridium acquisition makes VSAT a speculative buyout target

A stock that entered as a "12-1 momentum score" is now a fundamentally reinforced position. Up 44.9% from June 26 over two days and likely to retain a very strong momentum score at July 1.

#### D. sector_top4_full is Lagging and the Issue is Structural

sector_top4_full returned only +1.00% today vs SPY +0.78% — essentially neutral alpha despite a strong tech day. Since inception: +3.50% vs SPY +3.62% = -0.12pp underperformance. The sector rotation factor has not shown edge vs SPY in the May-June period. The XLE drag (-0.88%) is a persistent headwind in a ceasefire environment. Worth flagging for the post-July-1 research queue: the sector momentum signal may need to update more frequently than monthly to capture regime rotation.

#### E. Quarter-End Window Dressing Amplified Real Momentum

Today's +3.66% (mom_roa) and +3.04% (mom_v2) outperformance vs SPY +0.78% occurred on the highest window-dressing day of the year. Institutional funds systematically buy the quarter's winners at quarter-end, structurally amplifying the momentum factor. The MXL PT cascade, BE UBS upgrade, and INTC Cantor PT raise are fundamental developments that would have occurred on any trading day; the quarter-end timing amplified market reaction. In H2, momentum names will not have this structural boost — the sustainability test will come from fundamental strength rather than institutional flow timing.

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **NAV standings (June 30 close, latest confirmed MTM):** mom_roa_6535 $114,399.13 (+14.46% since 5/1) leads all sleeves, ahead of residual_roa_6535 $112,936.16 (+12.99%), mom_v2 $111,356.75 (+11.41%), mom_v1 $109,868.89 (+9.92%). spy_benchmark $103,624.50 (+3.62%) and sector_top4_full $103,451.63 (+3.50%) are roughly tied and both trail the four systematic factor sleeves by 6-11pp. All four factor sleeves beat SPY by 6.3-10.8pp since May 1 inception. **No July 1 trades have executed yet as of this report** — all `paper_positions` entries still reflect the 5/1 and 6/03 rebalance batches; today's rebalance (score-locked at 6/30 close) is pending execution.
> **Pre-market moves:** Markets have not opened for July 1 — no intraday/premarket per-ticker quote feed is available in this pipeline. The one verifiable pre-market signal: S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures were modestly lower Wednesday morning ("S&P 500 futures slip after strongest quarter since 2020," Nasdaq 100 futures ~30,398 vs. an earlier print of 30,444.75), attributed to caution around the Doha Iran talks after Q2's best quarterly rally since 2020. One aggregator (KuCoin) published index change figures for "July 1" (Nasdaq +1.52%, S&P +0.52%, Dow +0.27%) that are **numerically identical to Tuesday June 30's closing changes already logged in this doc** — flagged as a likely stale/mislabeled republish, not treated as real July 1 data.
> **Macro catalysts:** ISM Manufacturing PMI (June data) releases today at 10:00 ET — not yet out as of this report. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh was on stage at the ECB's Sintra forum June 30/July 1; no new rate-path signal reported. Oil continues to soften — Brent ~$72.25 (-0.96% day, -24.7% over the trailing month), WTI in a $67.93-$71.84 consolidation range — as US-Iran technical/indirect talks continued in Doha via Qatari/Pakistani mediation (no direct meeting scheduled; Iran's Deputy FM met Qatar's PM). NFP (Thursday July 2, consensus 170K) remains the week's key print.
> **LLM overlay status:** Both overlay experiments (stock: `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1` / new `llm_cascade_top1`; sector: `llm_overlay_sector_top4` / new `llm_cascade_sector4`) were re-inception ed to $100,000 on 6/30 and hold **zero positions** pending today's rebalance execution. Sector cascade evaluated all 6 major sector ETFs: XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV = HOLD (4 slots), XLE/XLY = VETO — implying a rotation out of Energy and Discretionary into Healthcare (XLV) if the cascade design back-fills vetoed slots (inferred from decision order, not yet confirmed against executed positions).
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) Rebalance execution for July 1 has not yet run in the DB — positions, and thus the actual outcome of the cascade picks above, are unconfirmed until it does; (2) AAOI's 1.6T hyperscaler shipment start is today (July 1) — AAOI is no longer the #1 mom_roa name (displaced by BE at the top of the cascade chain) but remains a position in mom_roa_6535/mom_v1/mom_v2 and the overlay control's history; (3) Iran-Qatar Doha talks ongoing, no resolution yet; (4) NFP Thursday July 2; (5) ARWR SHASTA-3/4 topline data still an open-ended binary catalyst ("mid-2026," could land any day); (6) Corning (GLW, mom_v1) has $54.1M of insider selling over the trailing 3 months with zero offsetting buys, at a P/E >90x after a 7.4% single-day drop on 6/26 — a standing valuation/technical-reversion risk flag; (7) MU disclosed a new price-fixing lawsuit — a legal risk flag on the portfolio's largest single position by aggregate exposure.

### 3. Catalyst and Macro Review

**A. Iran-Qatar talks, ongoing (July 1)**
US and Iranian officials are holding indirect, low-level technical talks through Qatari and Pakistani mediators in Doha today; Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi met Qatar's PM to discuss formalizing the tentative ceasefire agreement. No direct US-Iran meeting is planned. Oil traders reportedly are treating the truce "as a permanent deal," which is consistent with Brent's continued slide. This is a structural headwind for residual_roa_6535's energy cluster and a modest tailwind (via lower input costs / de-risking) for broad equities. Source: [CNN — Live Updates: US and Iranian Officials Holding Indirect Talks in Qatar](https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/01/world/live-news/iran-war-trump)

**B. ISM Manufacturing PMI (10:00 ET today) — not yet released as of this report**
The June ISM Manufacturing print is scheduled for 10:00 a.m. ET July 1. Prior comparable ISM Manufacturing readings this cycle have been in the low-50s (May actual 54.0 vs. 53.3 forecast, 52.7 prior per one source) — search results were inconsistent enough (one source cited a "102.5" prior that appears to reference a different sub-index) that no specific July 1 print number should be assumed. This is a watch item, not a known result. Source: [FXMacroData — ISM Manufacturing PMI July 2026](https://fxmacrodata.com/articles/usd-pmi-july-2026) | [ISM — PMI Reports](https://www.ismworld.org/supply-management-news-and-reports/reports/ism-pmi-reports/)

**C. Fed — Sintra ECB Forum**
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh appeared at the ECB's central bank forum in Sintra, Portugal on June 30/July 1. No new rate-path guidance was reported beyond the standing June FOMC dot plot (fed funds 3.50-3.75%; 9 of 19 members projecting at least one more hike in 2026, 8 projecting no change) — a meaningfully more hawkish tilt than the "one-to-two cuts" priced earlier in the year. No FOMC meeting occurs this week; the next scheduled Fed commentary of substance is likely light given the July 4 holiday week. Source: [CNBC — Kevin Warsh ECB Forum Live Updates](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/01/kevin-warsh-ecb-forum-live-updates.html) | [Federal Reserve — June 17, 2026 FOMC Projections](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomcprojtabl20260617.htm)

**D. Semiconductor/AI infrastructure sector — no reversal signal overnight**
No new negative catalyst was found for the semiconductor complex overnight. World Semiconductor Trade Statistics estimates 2026 industry sales growth of ~90% to $1.5T globally. The benchmark Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) is +99% YTD. This context supports continuation of the AI-capex thesis underlying MU, WDC, TER, VICR, STX, LRCX, KLAC, ICHR, MXL, ONTO, and other portfolio holdings, though the sector remains vulnerable to sentiment-driven air-pockets as seen June 5 (Broadcom guidance miss) and June 26 (AI-capex doubt selloff). Source: [Intellectia AI — Chip Stocks Rebound Strategy June 2026](https://intellectia.ai/blog/chip-stocks-rebound-investment-strategy-june-2026)

**E. Micron (MU) — new legal risk flag**
Micron, the portfolio's single largest position by exposure ($14,880 across all 4 factor sleeves, present in every systematic sleeve), now faces a new price-fixing lawsuit alongside heightened regulatory scrutiny of memory-market pricing strategy, per aggregated coverage. No specifics (plaintiff, jurisdiction, damages sought) were available in the search results — this needs a dedicated follow-up search once case details surface. MU also has a $0.15/share dividend with an ex-date of July 6, 2026. Source: [Public.com — Micron Forecast/News](https://public.com/stocks/mu/forecast-price-target)

**F. Global macro tail-risk backdrop (non-portfolio-specific, context only)**
Several outlets flagged systemic-risk themes for early July: business development companies (BDCs) turning unprofitable industry-wide amid rising borrowing costs (Reuters); some Chinese hedge fund managers publicly calling the AI trade "unsustainable" and "ready to burst" (Yahoo Finance); the Bank of England modeling AI-driven "flash crash" scenarios and discussing trading kill-switches (Telegraph); the Japanese yen at 40-year lows with JGB-selloff contagion risk flagged (FXStreet); South Korea's won near 2009 lows on foreign equity selling (Bloomberg). None of these are tied to specific portfolio holdings, but they are relevant as background volatility risk for a book concentrated in AI-infrastructure momentum names. Source: [Climate and Economy — July 1, 2026 Economic Roundup](https://climateandeconomy.com/2026/07/01/1st-july-2026-todays-round-up-of-economic-news/)

**A. **B. The July 1 decisions are already logged in the DB (`llm_overlay_log`, `sector_overlay_log`, both timestamped 2026-06-30 17:36-17:40 UTC)** — meaning the "OVERDUE" LLM-eval items flagged in the 6/30 report are in fact complete; they simply have not been executed into trades yet.

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | BE | 4 | VETO | — | Accelerating fundamentals (FY26 rev guide +80% YoY; Nebius/Oracle/AEP AI-datacenter deals) but ~128x fwd EPS, at/above mean PT (~267 vs ~275), momentum cooling (1m -5.2%, -20.5% off 52w high) into a hawkish Fed |

Reading the sequence literally: the underlying momentum×ROA signal's actual #1-ranked name appears to now be **BE (Bloom Energy)**, not AAOI — meaning the control sleeve (`mom_roa_top1_paper`, "always buy #1") is inferred to buy BE at today's rebalance, while the cascade treatment, having veto'd BE and SLGL, will buy WDC instead. Four HOLDs (XLK, XLI, XLB, XLV) exactly fill a 4-slot sector portfolio, with XLE and XLY vetoed. **Inferred (not confirmed):** if the cascade design back-fills vetoed slots by continuing down the ranked list, the new cascade sector sleeve would hold XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV — i.e., swap Energy out for Healthcare relative to the existing `sector_top4_full` control (XLB/XLE/XLI/XLK). Whether the *original* (non-cascade) `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` treatment uses the same 6-ETF evaluation or applies its older single-veto-to-cash logic to just the original 4 sectors is not resolved by the log alone — both designs' actual position construction should be checked once today's rebalance executes.

### 6. Strategic Insights

**A. The cascade LLM design is now empirically distinct from the original single-prompt overlay, and today is the first real test of that distinction.** The original overlay made one up/down call per candidate (buy AAOI, veto BE, etc.). The new cascade design chains through multiple candidates in ranked order until it finds a BUY (stock side: BE→SLGL→WDC) or fills all available slots (sector side: 6 ETFs evaluated to fill 4 slots). This is a structurally different bet: the cascade design should, by construction, almost never end up in cash for long on the sector side (it keeps searching until slots fill), whereas the original macro-veto design could sit partially in cash indefinitely. If the goal of running two overlay generations in parallel is to isolate "does search depth help," today's picks (WDC over BE/SLGL; XLV swapped in for XLE) are the first live data point. No conclusion is possible yet — both new cascade sleeves have zero trading days.

**B. The BE veto shows the same discipline pattern as its May 29 veto did.** BE was vetoed on May 29, 2026 at $290 with a nearly identical rationale (rich valuation, single-customer concentration, above analyst PT) and BE ran from ~$290 to briefly $351 before crashing to $252 and recovering to $302.70 by June 30 — a round-trip that validated caution more than it validated timing (an investor who "waited for a pullback toward 220" as the May 29 note suggested never got that entry). The July 1 veto uses a similar but updated rationale (valuation even richer at ~128x fwd EPS, momentum now cooling -20.5% off 52-week high). Whether repeated over-cautious vetoes on a name with genuinely strong fundamentals become a structural drag on the treatment sleeve's returns is worth tracking explicitly as the experiment accumulates decisions — one data point so far.

**C. sector_top4_full's persistent SPY-tracking (-0.12pp since inception) predates any LLM overlay and is a separate finding from the overlay experiments.** The underlying systematic sector-momentum signal (XLB/XLE/XLI/XLK, unchanged since May 1) has not demonstrated edge over SPY in two months. Today's cascade evaluation independently arrives at swapping Energy for Healthcare — consistent with the human-observable fact that Energy has been the sleeve's structural drag (XLE -0.88% just on 6/30, oil down another leg since). If the swap executes and Healthcare's inclusion improves relative performance, that would be modest evidence that manual/LLM sector selection can add value where the pure-momentum ranking has stalled — but this is a single-name-swap test on a two-month-old sleeve with no statistical power yet.

**D. No new evidence today changes the standing assessment that the four systematic factor sleeves (mom_roa_6535, residual_roa_6535, mom_v2, mom_v1) have a real, if short-sample, edge over SPY (+6.3pp to +10.8pp over 61 trading days across six distinct regime shocks), while the sector-rotation and LLM-overlay concepts remain unproven.** Today's data (futures caution, soft oil, no negative semiconductor catalyst, one new legal risk flag on MU, and a completed-but-unexecuted LLM cascade decision) does not materially change that picture. The most substantive new "sleeve concept" evidence today is structural rather than return-based: the cascade methodology itself (evaluate N candidates until one clears the bar) is now live and testable against the simpler single-veto design, which is a genuinely new axis of experimentation rather than a re-run of the existing momentum-factor question.

> **LLM overlay standings:** The entire 6-sleeve LLM experiment (2 controls + 2 cash-overlay treatments + 2 new "always-invested cascade" treatments) was aligned to a fresh $100k/July-1 inception on 2026-06-30 (Appendix AK/AK.5-AK.6 in the record) — today is Day 1. Decisions ARE logged for 07-01: stock control candidate = BE (raw mom_roa #1); cash overlay verdict = **VETO** (BE priced-for-perfection, ~128x fwd EPS, momentum cooling -20.5% off 52wk high) → stays in cash; cascade walked deeper (SLGL #2 VETO — pre-revenue biotech, binary Phase-3 readout) to **WDC #3 = BUY** (score 7, inval $560, AI-HDD supercycle thesis). Sector control = XLK/XLE/XLI/XLB; cash overlay = XLK/XLI/XLB HOLD, XLE **VETO** (fading momentum + soft oil) → 3-of-4 filled, one slot cash; cascade walked to XLV (defensive rotation, inval $150) as its 4th sector, replacing XLE. **None of this had executed into paper_positions as of this report's ~17:30 CDT data pull** — the monthly rebalance is scheduled for 6:03pm local (rescheduled 2026-07-01 per Appendix AP to run after the real close lands via the 5:15pm MTM pipeline) and had not yet fired. All 6 LLM sleeves + the 4 systematic 07-01 duplicates + spy_benchmark_0701 show $100,000 cash / 0 positions in the DB as of this report.
>
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) Confirm the 6:03pm 07-01 rebalance actually executed the above 11-sleeve cohort (BE/cash/WDC on stock side; XLK+XLE+XLI+XLB / XLK+XLI+XLB+cash / XLK+XLI+XLB+XLV on sector side) — first live test point for both the realigned LLM experiment and the new cascade design; (2) MU DRAM antitrust suit and SK Hynix's July 10 IPO are now live, ongoing risks for the ~3.5-3.7% MU position sitting in all four systematic sleeves; (3) NFP Thursday July 2 (170K consensus); (4) AFJK's SPAC merger deadline is July 6 — five days out, no resolution yet; (5) ARWR SHASTA-3/4 Phase 3 readout still pending, no new data today, "mid-2026" timeline unchanged.

### 1. Market Summary — July 1, 2026

#### A. Index Closes

| Index | Close | Day Change | Notable |

Source: [Zacks — Stock Market News for July 1, 2026](https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2946056/stock-market-news-for-july-1-2026) | [CNBC — Stock market news for July 1, 2026](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/30/stock-market-today-live-updates.html) | [TheStreet — Stock Market Today July 1 2026](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-july-1-2026-nasdaq-futures-slip-after-strongest-quarter-since-2020)

#### B. Primary Market Drivers

**1. Semiconductor/memory-specific profit-taking, not a broad selloff.** The Nasdaq's decline was concentrated: the Motley Fool and CNBC coverage both single out Micron (-10%+) as the session's headline mover, and our own portfolio data shows the same pattern — a cluster of memory, chip-test, and chip-equipment names down 6-14% (MU, LRCX, KLAC, TER, AEHR, MXL, INTC, AMKR, ONTO, MKSI, UCTT, COHR, CIEN, WDC, STX) while mega-cap tech (MSFT +3.02%, AAPL +1.73%) and non-tech sectors (XLC +2.44%, XLF +2.18%) were flat to higher. Reporting frames this as profit-taking after semis rallied "more than 80%" in H1 2026, compounded by two fresh, name-specific catalysts below. Source: [The Motley Fool — Why Micron Stock Is Plummeting Today](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/07/01/why-micron-stock-is-plummeting-today/) | [CNBC — Stock market news July 1, 2026](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/30/stock-market-today-live-updates.html)

**2. New DRAM antitrust class action (filed June 25, still developing).** 17 plaintiffs sued Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron in the N.D. Cal. federal court, alleging the three (who together hold ~90% of the global DRAM market) coordinated to shift ~25% of DRAM wafer capacity from commodity DDR3/DDR4 to higher-margin HBM since 2022, artificially restricting legacy-memory supply and driving prices up roughly 700% over four years. Micron has denied the allegations ("we compete vigorously, fairly and in compliance with all applicable laws"). Samsung and SK Hynix both previously pleaded guilty to criminal DRAM price-fixing in 2005. This is now a live legal overhang on MU, which is the single largest position (~3.5-3.7% weight) in all four systematic sleeves. Source: [AppleInsider — Samsung, SK hynix & Micron face lawsuit over DRAM prices](https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/29/apple-suppliers-samsung-sk-hynix-micron-hit-by-ram-price-fixing-suit) | [Tom's Hardware — DRAM price-fixing lawsuit](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/samsung-sk-hynix-and-micron-sued-over-alleged-dram-price-fixing-amid-record-memory-costs) | [PC Gamer — Memory crisis heads to court](https://www.pcgamer.com/hardware/memory/memory-crisis-heads-to-court-class-action-lawsuit-calls-samsung-sk-hynix-and-micron-dram-market-oligopolists-alleging-anticompetitive-behavior/)

**3. SK Hynix's record $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing (targeted July 10, ticker SKHY).** The world's second-largest memory chipmaker (holds ~60% of HBM market share) filed to list 17.79M new ADR shares on Nasdaq, which would be the largest ADR listing in market history, surpassing Alibaba's 2014 debut. This introduces a large new supply of US-listed memory-sector equity just as the DRAM antitrust story breaks, and appears to be contributing to competitive/dilution anxiety across the memory complex (MU, and by extension memory-test names like AEHR and TER). Source: [CNBC — SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via Nasdaq listing](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/24/sk-hynix-nasdaq-adr-listing-south-korea.html) | [CNBC — SK Hynix US listing price target](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/26/sk-hynix-us-listing-ipo-hsbc-price-target.html)

**4. Fresh US export restrictions on chipmaking tools to China.** Equipment makers were informed of new limitations on shipments to Hua Hong Semiconductor (China's #2 contract chipmaker), pressuring Lam Research, Applied Materials, and KLA on top of the memory-driven selloff — Lam -9.71%, KLA (KLAC) -11.77% in our portfolio data. Source: [ElectronicsForYou — China Exposure Hits Lam Research, Applied Materials, KLA Stocks](https://www.electronicsforyou.biz/industry-buzz/china-exposure-hits-lam-research-applied-materials-kla-stocks/) | [TradingView/StockStory — MACOM, Marvell, Applied Materials, KLA, Lam Research shares falling](https://www.tradingview.com/news/stockstory:2b3fa8532094b:0-macom-marvell-technology-applied-materials-kla-corporation-and-lam-research-shares-are-falling-what-you-need-to-know/)

**5. Caterpillar reversal dented the Dow intraday.** The Dow hit a fresh intraday ATH of 52,742.66 before CAT (an AI-capex/infrastructure beneficiary, held in residual_roa_6535) reversed roughly 7% intraday, pulling the index back to a near-flat -0.03% close. CAT closed our books at -6.90% today. Source: [Zacks — Stock Market News for July 1, 2026](https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2946056/stock-market-news-for-july-1-2026)

**6. Oil eased on constructive Iran talks.** Brent fell to ~$72.25 (-0.96%) and WTI to $68.58 (-1.3%) after President Trump said US-Iran talks in Doha (led by Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff) were going well, extending the de-escalation from the June 17 memorandum of understanding. This is a continued headwind for residual_roa_6535's ~15% energy weight, though several individual energy names (DINO +4.18%, PARR +4.85%) actually rose today on refining-margin/input-cost dynamics even as crude fell. Source: [CNBC — Oil prices fall after Trump says US-Iran talks in Qatar are going well](https://www.cnbc.com/amp/2026/07/01/oil-prices-brent-wti-crude-trump-iran.html)

**7. June ISM Manufacturing PMI: 53.3%, sixth straight month of expansion**, down 0.7pt from May's 54%. New Orders Index 56% (vs. 56.8% in May); Prices Index eased notably to 73% from 82.1% — a mild disinflationary signal for the Fed ahead of Thursday's NFP. Source: [PR Newswire — Manufacturing PMI at 53.3%; June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI Report](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/manufacturing-pmi-at-53-3-june-2026-ism-manufacturing-pmi-report-302814991.html)

#### B. Positions near a stop or invalidation level

The four systematic momentum sleeves run **no intra-month stop-loss mechanism** — this was tested extensively (13 attempts, all rejected — see `sleeves_verdict.md`) and rejected because reactive stops degrade CAGR in-sample without a compensating held-out benefit. Positions are held until the next monthly rebalance regardless of drawdown, so today's -5% to -14% single-name moves do not trigger any automated action.

The closest thing to explicit levels in the system are the LLM overlay/cascade **invalidation levels** set today for the pending 07-01 rebalance:

| Ticker | Inval. Level | Jul 1 Close | Cushion |
|---|---|---|---|
| WDC (cascade stock pick) | $560 | $598.37 | +6.8% |
| XLK (cascade/control sector #1) | $174 | $185.62 | +6.7% |
| XLI (cascade/control sector #2) | $172 | $183.36 | +6.6% |
| XLB (cascade/control sector #3) | $48 | $51.02 | +6.3% |
| XLV (cascade sector #4) | $150 | $159.54 | +6.4% |

None are close to breach despite today's chip-driven selloff — XLK absorbed a -2.57% day and is still 6.7% above its invalidation level. WDC itself fell -6.32% today and remains 6.8% above its $560 invalidation level; a similar-magnitude down day tomorrow would put it within single-digit percentage points of that level, worth flagging as the position won't even be live until the 6:03pm rebalance executes.

Cal.) against Micron/Samsung/SK Hynix is the dominant new fundamental development this week — it directly threatens the ~3.5-3.7% MU position common to all four systematic sleeves and is likely to remain an overhang through discovery/response cycles.
- **SK Hynix's $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing** targeted for July 10 is a new, dated catalyst to watch — a listing of this size in the memory space could reprice the whole DRAM/HBM complex around that date.
- **New US export restrictions on chipmaking tools to China** (Hua Hong Semiconductor) is a fresh geopolitical/regulatory headwind for the equipment names (LRCX, KLAC, AMAT — AMAT not held) that wasn't in play as of yesterday's pre-market report.
- **Corning (GLW) insider selling**: CEO Wendell Weeks and several SVPs sold over 160,000 shares in June per Form 4 filings — worth tracking given GLW's continued rich valuation (P/E >105x) even after today's -13.62% drop.
- **No new ARWR SHASTA-3/4 data** — Phase-3 plozasiran readout timeline remains "mid-2026," unchanged from the pre-market report; ARWR itself was a modest -1.31% today, tracking broadly with the market rather than reacting to any binary catalyst.
- **AFJK's July 6 merger deadline** is now five days out with no new extension or completion news found; the stock's -12.84% today appears to be ordinary high-volatility SPAC-into-catalyst trading rather than a specific negative data point.

### 4. LLM Overlay: Treatment vs. Control Update

**Structural context:** As of 2026-06-30 (Appendix AK/AK.5-AK.6 of the project record), the entire LLM-experiment cohort was reset to a synchronized $100,000/July-1 inception, and a third "always-invested cascade" design was added alongside the original single-veto ("cash") design — running as a genuinely separate pair, not a replacement, so the existing veto-vs-cash comparison isn't disturbed. This means **today, 2026-07-01, is trading day 1 for all six LLM-experiment sleeves.** No performance comparison is possible yet.

**Today's decisions (logged in `llm_overlay_log` / `sector_overlay_log`, pending 6:03pm execution):**

*Stock side:*
| Candidate (ranked) | Verdict | Score | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| BE (raw mom_roa #1 → control's pick) | **VETO** | 4 | Accelerating fundamentals (FY26 revenue guide 3.4-3.8B, ~80% YoY; Nebius/Oracle/AEP AI-datacenter deals) but ~128x fwd EPS, at/above mean PT, momentum cooling (-5.2% 1mo, -20.5% off 52wk high) into a hawkish Fed |
| SLGL (#2) | **VETO** | 3 | Pre-revenue single-asset clinical biotech, binary Phase-3 SGT-610 readout Q4 2026 |
| WDC (#3) | **BUY** | 7 | AI-HDD supercycle: Q3 revenue +45% YoY, capacity fully committed through 2028-29, EPS revisions +70% above consensus; rich (~59x) but fundamentally accelerating; inval. $560 |

- **Control (mom_roa_top1_paper)**: holds BE regardless of LLM verdict (pure momentum #1) — pending execution.
- **Cash-overlay treatment (llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper)**: BE vetoed → stays in cash — pending execution.
- **Cascade treatment (llm_cascade_top1_paper)**: walks past BE and SLGL to WDC (the first BUY) — pending execution.

*Sector side:*
| Candidate (ranked) | Verdict | Score | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| XLK | HOLD | 7 | Strongest momentum (3mo +42.7%), not overbought (RSI 51), AI-datacenter capex intact |
| XLE | **VETO** | 3 | Below 50DMA, -5.9% 1mo/-14.4% 3mo, RSI 26; WTI soft, Iran de-escalation removing war premium |
| XLI | HOLD | 7 | Broad strength near 52wk high, reshoring/AI-infra/electrification tailwind |
| XLB | HOLD | 5 | Marginal — just below 50DMA, but 3mo +3.6% and copper/electrification demand intact |
| XLY (#5) | **VETO** | 4 | Weakening consumer, hawkish-rate headwind on discretionary, weak 12-1 momentum |
| XLV (#6) | HOLD | 6 | Defensive rotation catching a bid in the hawkish/volatile regime; GLP-1/oncology tailwinds; inval. $150 |

- **Control (sector_top4_paper)**: XLK + XLE + XLI + XLB (pure top-4 momentum) — pending execution.
- **Cash-overlay treatment (llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper)**: XLK + XLI + XLB HOLD, XLE VETO → 3 of 4 slots filled, 1 slot cash — pending execution.
- **Cascade treatment (llm_cascade_sector4_paper)**: walks past XLE and XLY to XLV, filling all 4 slots with XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV — pending execution.

**Kill switch reminder:** unchanged — 12 months or ≥30 picks, drop the overlay if scores don't predict returns or treatment doesn't beat control. Honest prior remains that this is expected to fail, per the original experiment design. Zero data points exist yet under the new synchronized-inception design; the first real signal (does cascade "out-pick" raw momentum, does cash-veto avoid the busts) will start to accumulate from tomorrow's fills onward.

---

### 6. Strategic Insights

**A. Today is a live demonstration of the concentration risk inherent in pure momentum factor construction.** All four systematic sleeves are 27-58% weighted to Technology (mom_roa_6535 58.4%, mom_v2 51.8%, mom_v1 45.7%, residual_roa_6535 27.1%), and within that weight, heavily clustered in semiconductor/memory/chip-equipment names specifically — MU, LRCX, KLAC/TER/AEHR/MXL/INTC/AMKR/ONTO/MKSI/UCTT/COHR/CIEN/WDC/STX all appear across multiple sleeves simultaneously. When a sub-sector-specific shock hits (today: DRAM antitrust suit + SK Hynix IPO + China export curbs), the momentum factor has no diversification against it — it bought into the winners of the last 12 months, and the winners of the last 12 months were disproportionately semiconductor names. residual_roa_6535's dual-signal construction (residual momentum × ROA, which naturally pulls in financials/healthcare/energy/consumer names alongside tech) cut today's damage roughly in half (-3.77% vs. -5.59% to -5.64% for the pure-momentum sleeves) — a real-time, single-day confirmation of the lower-drawdown finding from its original backtest validation.

**B. The MU DRAM lawsuit is now a genuine, ongoing tail risk sitting in every systematic sleeve simultaneously, not a one-day event.** Because MU is the single largest position (~3.5-3.7% weight) in all four sleeves and the lawsuit was filed just six days ago, this is very early in what could be a multi-month legal and competitive overhang (echoing memory's earlier-flagged "MU price-fixing lawsuit" outstanding item from June 26). The portfolio has no mechanism to reduce single-name concentration risk mid-month — a structural gap the LLM overlay experiments are implicitly testing a fix for (by substituting away from a vetoed pick), but that experiment restarted from scratch yesterday and has zero live data yet.

**C. The realigned/cascade LLM overlay experiment gets its first real trading day tomorrow, on an unusually eventful entry point.** The cascade design's very first live pick (WDC) is a name that fell -6.32% on the same day it was chosen — meaning the cascade sleeve will enter with a mark-to-market loss on day one relative to its entry price if the fill happens at or near today's close mechanics, though the WDC investment thesis (AI-HDD supercycle, capacity committed through 2028-29) is unrelated to today's chip-sector-wide selloff. Similarly the sector cascade's new pick (XLV, defensive healthcare rotation) is entering into a sector that was one of today's few tech-alternative gainers (+0.55%). Both are early tests of whether "always invested, walk deeper into the ranking" out-picks a design that just holds cash on a veto — no conclusion possible with zero trading days, but worth flagging that day 1 is entering right after the sharpest single-session dispersion the portfolio has shown in some time.

**D. No changed conclusion on the standing four-sleeve edge-over-SPY thesis, but today adds a real stress-test data point.** mom_roa_6535 (+4.52pp vs SPY), residual_roa_6535 (+5.20pp), mom_v2 (+1.59pp), and mom_v1 (+1.06pp) all still lead SPY since inception even after today's drawdown — the edge narrowed sharply today (roughly -4 to -5pp of relative performance lost in a single session for the pure-momentum sleeves) but did not disappear. Whether that edge survives a sustained semiconductor-sector correction (as opposed to a single bad day) remains the open question this factor strategy has not yet been tested against in this dataset — worth explicit tracking if the DRAM/chip-equipment overhang persists into next week.

## Report: 2026-07-02 (Thursday) — Pre-Market Overnight Research

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **NAV standings (July 1 close, latest confirmed MTM, post the 6:03pm monthly rebalance):** residual_roa_6535 $108,637.27 (+8.64% since 5/1, +5.16pp vs SPY) leads all sleeves, ahead of mom_roa_6535 $107,971.26 (+7.97%, +4.49pp vs SPY), mom_v2 $105,037.61 (+5.04%, +1.56pp), mom_v1 $104,508.33 (+4.51%, +1.03pp). spy_benchmark $103,484.35 (+3.48%) and sector_top4_full $102,391.21 (+2.39%, -1.09pp, trailing SPY) round out the six original sleeves. The 11-sleeve 07-01 cohort (6 LLM-experiment + 4 systematic Alpaca duplicates + a second SPY benchmark) completed its first-ever trades yesterday evening — see Section 4.
> **Pre-market moves:** No live intraday/premarket per-ticker feed is available to this pipeline and the market has not opened. Verifiable signals only: S&P 500 futures -0.08%, Nasdaq-100 futures -0.3%, Dow futures -35 pts, all easing off earlier gains ahead of this morning's jobs report. **BE (held $99,900 by the LLM stock-arm control sleeve) is reported up ~8% pre-market** on an expanded Brookfield financing commitment ($5B→$25B for AI-datacenter fuel-cell buildout) — a meaningful new data point against the veto rationale that killed BE for both LLM treatment sleeves. MU traded in a volatile, source-conflicted range Wednesday (close estimates from -6.92% to roughly flat, pre-market Thursday quoted near $1,085 vs. a $1,154 prior-close reference) — treat with caution, sources disagree materially; see Section 2.
> **Macro catalysts:** June nonfarm payrolls release TODAY at 8:30am ET (consensus ~100-115K, unemployment expected to hold at 4.3%) — moved up from the usual first-Friday slot because of the July 4 holiday calendar. Wednesday's ADP private-payrolls miss (+98K vs. ~110K expected) is the leading indicator raising some downside risk to the print. Markets close early today (1:00pm ET stocks) and are closed tomorrow, Friday July 3, for Independence Day. WTI crude fell to $67.74 (-1.23%), lowest since late February, on rising Strait of Hormuz shipping volumes and "positive progress" reported by Qatar in indirect US-Iran talks — though the next round is delayed by funeral processions for Iran's former supreme leader scheduled July 4-9. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh continues at the ECB's Sintra forum.
> **LLM overlay status:** Both the cash-veto and cascade LLM-experiment cohorts executed their first live trades at yesterday's 6:03pm rebalance (confirmed via `paper_positions`, cross-checked against Project Record Appendix AS). Stock side: control holds BE, cash-veto treatment sits in 100% cash (BE vetoed), cascade treatment holds WDC (BE→SLGL vetoed, WDC bought at $598.67, stop $560). Sector side: control holds XLE/XLK/XLB/XLI, cash-veto treatment holds XLK/XLI/XLB + 25% cash (XLE vetoed), cascade holds XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV (XLE→XLY vetoed, XLV added). Day 1 results are notable: BE (the vetoed name) is reportedly rallying ~8% pre-market, while WDC (the cascade's pick) fell -7.03% on its first trading day. Both are single data points, not a trend.
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) NFP print at 8:30am ET could move the whole book, especially the semiconductor-heavy sleeves, before/at today's open; (2) MU DRAM antitrust suit remains an open legal overhang, still held in all four systematic sleeves; (3) SK Hynix's targeted $29B Nasdaq ADR listing (July 10) could reprice the memory complex; (4) POWL fell -7.38% on 7/1 with no clear catalyst found, alongside a $10M insider sale (6/26) and an EPS miss — worth a dedicated look; (5) RCUS (new mom_v1 holding) has a Gilead option-continuation payment lapsing July 14, ending broad option rights, plus a recently discontinued Phase 3 trial; (6) AFJK SPAC merger deadline is July 6, three days away; (7) ~97 stray root-level junk files have re-accumulated in the project directory per Project Record Appendix AS — the same failure class that broke the 7/1 rebalance batch script once already; a cleanup pass is recommended before the next rebalance cycle, out of scope for this report.

Source: [Benzinga — Bloom Energy Stock Is Volatile: What's Driving The Action?](https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/06/60102849/bloom-energy-stock-is-volatile-thursday-whats-driving-the-action) | [TIKR — Bloom Energy Stock Could Be Worth $920 After the Oracle Deal Expansion](https://www.tikr.com/blog/bloom-energy-stock-could-be-worth-920-after-the-oracle-deal-expansion)

### 3. Catalyst and Macro Review

**A. June nonfarm payrolls — TODAY, 8:30am ET, before the open.** Consensus centers around 100-115K jobs added (FactSet median 100K; range of estimates 70K-150K), with the unemployment rate expected to hold at 4.3%. This report was moved to Thursday this week (rather than the usual first-Friday slot) because of the July 4 holiday calendar. Wednesday's ADP private-payrolls report came in at +98,000, below the ~110K consensus and down from May's 122,000 — nearly half the ADP gain (48K) came from education/health services, with wage growth flat at 4.4% YoY for job-stayers. This is the single largest scheduled catalyst for today's session and will likely set the tone for the semiconductor-heavy sleeves at the open. Source: [BLS — Employment Situation Release Schedule](https://www.bls.gov/schedule/news_release/empsit.htm) | [FactSet — June Payrolls Preview](https://insight.factset.com/total-nonfarm-payrolls-for-june-2026-are-projected-to-rise-by-100000) | [CNBC — Private Payrolls Rose by 98,000 in June, Less Than Expected, ADP Reports](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/01/private-payrolls-rose-by-98000-in-june-less-than-expected-adp-reports.html)

**B. Holiday-shortened trading week.** The market closes early today (1:00pm ET for equities, 2:00pm ET for bonds) and is fully closed tomorrow, Friday July 3, for Independence Day (observed). Liquidity is typically thinner into and around this kind of holiday close — worth noting as a backdrop for today's NFP reaction and any rebalance-adjacent order flow. Source: [Yahoo Finance — Stock Market Closed for the Fourth of July](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/stock-market-closed-fourth-july-173527672.html)

**C. Oil / Iran talks — de-escalation continues but stalls into the holiday.** Qatar reported "positive progress" on issues tied to the June 17 ceasefire memorandum of understanding after envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner concluded a two-day Doha mission Wednesday; VP Vance said talks were "going well," with nuclear-issue discussions expected to start soon. Remaining open disputes center on Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz, not yet the nuclear file itself. The next round of talks is delayed by funeral processions for Iran's former supreme leader, scheduled July 4-9 — meaning no near-term resolution catalyst is likely this week. WTI at $67.74 is a continuing headwind for residual_roa_6535's ~15-16% energy weight (now including newly-added XOM and VLO) even as the de-escalation itself is broadly risk-on for equities generally. Source: [Al Jazeera — Iran to Open Communication Channel on MOU with US After Talks in Qatar](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/1/iran-to-open-communication-channel-on-mou-with-us-after-talks-in-qatar) | [Washington Times — US-Iran Conclude Two Days of Indirect Talks in Qatar, Major Questions Left](https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2026/jul/1/us-iran-conclude-two-days-indirect-talks-qatar-major-questions-left/) | [TradingEconomics — Crude Oil](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/crude-oil)

**D. DRAM antitrust suit — still developing, no case-specifics surfaced overnight.** The class action filed June 25-26 against Micron/Samsung/SK Hynix (N.D. Cal., alleging coordinated DDR3/DDR4 supply restriction inflating prices ~500-700% over four years) remains an open legal overhang. No new court filings or company statements dated after 6/30 were found this pass. Notably, **analyst sentiment on MU has stayed strongly bullish through the legal news** — Cantor Fitzgerald raised its PT to $2,000 from $1,500 (naming MU a top semis pick), Deutsche Bank reiterated Buy at a $1,500 PT, and Susquehanna moved to $1,750, all citing HBM supply constraints; 41-analyst consensus is Strong Buy with an average PT of $1,365.28. This is a real tension worth tracking: the stock is falling on legal/competitive-anxiety headlines while sell-side estimates keep rising on fundamentals. Source: [Tom's Hardware — Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron Sued Over Alleged DRAM Price Fixing](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/samsung-sk-hynix-and-micron-sued-over-alleged-dram-price-fixing-amid-record-memory-costs) | [Watcher.Guru — Micron Stock Jumps as Analysts Raise Targets Despite Legal Risks](https://watcher.guru/news/micron-stock-jumps-as-analysts-raise-targets-despite-legal-risks) | [Barchart — Micron and Other Memory Makers Are Being Sued Over Price Fixing Claims](https://www.barchart.com/story/news/3063961/micron-and-other-memory-makers-are-being-sued-over-price-fixing-claims-this-is-nothing-more-than-headline-risk-for-mu-stock)

**E. SK Hynix's $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing — targeted July 10, eight days out.** SK Hynix's board resolved 6/24 to list up to 17.79M new ADR shares on Nasdaq under ticker SKHY, which would be the largest ADR offering on record (surpassing Alibaba's 2014 $21.8B debut); subscription/payment is tentatively set for 7/14, new-share listing 7/29, trading debut around 7/10 (dates subject to SEC approval). SK Hynix holds ~60% of the HBM market and is up ~280% YTD; this introduces a large new supply of US-listed memory-sector equity right as the DRAM antitrust story is unfolding, likely to keep contributing to competitive/dilution anxiety across MU and related names (AEHR, TER) into next week. Source: [CNBC — South Korea's Biggest Chipmaker SK Hynix Plans to Raise $29 Billion via Nasdaq Listing](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/24/sk-hynix-nasdaq-adr-listing-south-korea.html) | [The Elec — SK Hynix Nears Nasdaq ADR Listing, Potentially as Early as July](https://www.thelec.net/news/articleView.html?idxno=11395)

**F. Applied Materials M&A.** Applied Materials (newly held in residual_roa_6535 since 7/1) agreed to acquire ASMPT Limited's NEXX business (large-area advanced-packaging deposition equipment) to broaden its panel-level advanced-packaging portfolio for AI accelerators — a positive strategic development that predates and is separate from the 7/1 sector-wide chip-equipment selloff. Source: [Barchart — Applied Materials Hits New High, Is AMAT Stock Poised for Continued Gains in 2026?](https://www.barchart.com/story/news/37214575/applied-materials-hits-new-high-is-amat-stock-poised-for-continued-gains-in-2026)

**G. Exxon Mobil corporate redomiciliation completed July 1.** Exxon moved its corporate domicile from New Jersey to Texas, effective 7/1/2026; the publicly traded parent is now ExxonMobil Holdings Corporation (its first name change since the Mobil merger 25+ years ago). Shares exchanged 1-for-1 with no shareholder action required and continue trading as XOM on NYSE. Separately, Exxon won a US Supreme Court ruling reviving its claims over Cuban assets. Note: sourced closing-price figures for XOM conflicted materially between outlets (one citing ~$136, another referencing a "$70 area" test) — likely an artifact of different feeds handling the share-exchange mechanics; do not treat either figure as reliable without a fresh quote. XOM was newly added to residual_roa_6535 on 7/1. Source: [ExxonMobil IR — ExxonMobil Announces Planned Effective Date for Move to Texas](https://investor.exxonmobil.com/company-information/press-releases/detail/1206/exxonmobil-announces-planned-effective-date-for-move-to) | [GuruFocus — Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM) Completes Redomiciliation Merger](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8941464/exxon-mobil-corporation-xom-completes-redomiciliation-merger)

**H. Healthcare sector tariff overhang (relevant to XLV, newly held by the sector cascade sleeve).** A presidential executive order signed April 2, 2026 imposes a 100% tariff on imported patented pharmaceuticals, taking effect July 31, 2026 for large manufacturers and September 29, 2026 for smaller ones. Generics, biosimilars, and orphan drugs are exempt; roughly 13 companies have signed company-specific tariff agreements, and the White House claims the policy has already triggered ~$400B in new US onshoring investment commitments. XLV has been a broad underperformer in 2026 (down ~7% YTD as of late April vs. SPY +5% per one source) despite this — a live regulatory deadline approaching inside the next month, directly relevant to the sector cascade sleeve's newest (and only non-control-overlapping) holding. Source: [Freshfields — Trump's Pharma Tariff Ultimatum](https://www.freshfields.com/en/our-thinking/blogs/a-fresh-take/trumps-pharma-tariff-ultimatum-102mqe9) | [White House — Adjusting Imports of Pharmaceuticals and Pharmaceutical Ingredients into the United States](https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2026/04/adjusting-imports-of-pharmaceuticals-and-pharmaceutical-ingredients-into-the-united-states/)

### 4. LLM Overlay Status

**A. Both experiment generations executed their first-ever live trades at the July 1, 6:03pm rebalance.** This is confirmed directly against `paper_positions` (not inferred from logged decisions, as in yesterday's pre-market report) and cross-checked against Project Record Appendix AS, which documents the rebalance completing cleanly (18:19-18:34) after a same-day cmd.exe batch-parsing bug was found and fixed.

**Stock side — Day 1 results:**
| Sleeve | Holding | Entry | Day-1-to-date signal |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mom_roa_top1_paper` (control) | BE | $289.64 (7/1) | Reported **+~8% pre-market July 2** on expanded Brookfield financing news |
| `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` (cash-veto treatment) | 100% cash | — | BE vetoed (score 4: ~128x fwd EPS, momentum cooling -20.5% off 52wk high) |
| `llm_cascade_top1_paper` (cascade treatment) | WDC | $598.67 (7/1), stop $560 | Fell -7.03% on its first trading day (7/1), second straight down session for the name |

**Sector side — Day 1 results:**
| Sleeve | Holdings | Signal |
|---|---|---|
| `sector_top4_paper` (control) | XLE/XLK/XLB/XLI | Pure top-4 momentum, unchanged design |
| `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` (cash-veto treatment) | XLK/XLI/XLB + 25% cash | XLE vetoed (fading momentum + soft oil) |
| `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` (cascade treatment) | XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV | Walked past XLE→XLY vetoes to XLV (defensive healthcare rotation); XLV now carries the approaching pharma-tariff deadline (Section 3H) as a new risk this sleeve alone is exposed to |

**B. The BE pre-market pop is the first real tension point for the stock-arm experiment.** If BE's ~8% pre-market move holds into the open, the control sleeve (which the LLM vetoed) will have outperformed both LLM treatments on day one — the opposite of the pattern the overlay experiment is designed to demonstrate. This mirrors the same dynamic flagged in the May 29 BE veto (BE ran from ~$290 to briefly $351 after being vetoed, before round-tripping back down). One data point; the kill switch (12 months / ≥30 picks) remains the standard for any real conclusion, but it's worth flagging explicitly since it's now happened with BE twice.

**C. WDC's continued decline is the cascade sleeve's open question.** The cascade's WDC pick has now fallen on both the day it was decided (6/30, -6.32%) and the day it was executed (7/1, -7.03%) — a cumulative headwind before the position has had a single up day. No news found challenging the underlying AI-HDD supercycle thesis (Section 2); this reads as broad sector rotation rather than a WDC-specific problem, but the invalidation level ($560) is worth monitoring given two consecutive down sessions.

**Kill switch reminder:** unchanged — 12 months or ≥30 picks, drop the overlay if scores don't predict returns or treatment doesn't beat control. One trading day of data exists for the synchronized-inception cohort; no conclusion is possible yet.

### 6. Strategic Insights

**A. The 7/1 monthly rebalance mechanically cut single-name concentration roughly in half, which is itself a useful natural experiment.** Prior reports flagged MU at ~$14,880 (2.9-3.7% weight) across the four systematic sleeves; post-rebalance, the same position sizes to ~$6,993 total. This wasn't a deliberate risk-management change — it's an artifact of the monthly rebalance resetting each sleeve to fresh equal-weight positions off a lower starting NAV after the 7/1 drawdown — but it means today's version of the DRAM-lawsuit/SK-Hynix-ADR risk is mechanically smaller in dollar terms than it was 48 hours ago, even though the underlying legal and competitive story hasn't changed. Worth remembering when comparing exposure figures across reports: the number moves with rebalance timing, not just with news.

**B. Day 1 of the resynchronized LLM overlay experiment is producing exactly the kind of early tension the design should generate — in both directions.** BE (vetoed by both LLM treatments) is reportedly up ~8% pre-market on a large financing catalyst that arguably validates the underlying "accelerating fundamentals" half of the veto rationale rather than the "overvalued" half. WDC (the cascade's own pick) is down in two consecutive sessions. Neither is a verdict — one data point each, and the whole point of the 12-month/30-pick kill switch is to not overreact to day one — but if the pattern holds even a few more days, the qualitative story ("cash-veto sleeve missed a real move; cascade sleeve entered a stock still working through profit-taking") would be worth writing up explicitly rather than just noting in passing.

**C. No new evidence today changes the standing four-sleeve edge-over-SPY thesis, and the sample continues to grow through a real stress period.** residual_roa_6535 (+5.16pp), mom_roa_6535 (+4.49pp), mom_v2 (+1.56pp), and mom_v1 (+1.03pp) all still lead SPY since inception, having now weathered the June 5 Broadcom selloff, the June 26-29 chip-crash regime, and the July 1 DRAM/export-curb rout without the edge disappearing (though it has compressed meaningfully from the June 30 peak). The open question flagged in the last two reports — whether the edge survives a *sustained* semiconductor correction rather than a single bad session — remains open; today's macro slate (NFP, holiday-thinned liquidity, an unresolved DRAM legal overhang, and an imminent SK Hynix supply event) is a reasonable candidate for testing that question further over the next 1-2 weeks.

**D. The newly-diversified residual_roa_6535 additions (XOM, VLO, AAPL, AMAT, NUE, COCO) are a live test of whether its dual-signal construction continues to earn its lower-drawdown reputation.** Adding two energy majors into a period of falling oil (Section 3C) and a chip-equipment name into a period of chip-sector volatility (AMAT, Section 2) somewhat cuts against the sleeve's historical pattern of avoiding the portfolio's most crowded trades — worth watching whether residual's July cohort still shows the "roughly half the single-day drawdown" behavior it demonstrated on 7/1 (-3.77% vs. -5.59% to -5.64% for the pure-momentum sleeves) if another chip-specific shock hits this month.

>
> **Day moves:** S&P 500/SPY roughly flat (SPY -0.13% to $744.78; broad-index sources cite S&P between -0.01% and flat) | Nasdaq Composite -1.3% to -1.6% (sources vary) on a two-day semiconductor-equipment rout | Dow Jones **+1.14% to a record close of 52,900.07** on rotation into non-tech. **This was a rotation day, not a risk-off day**: XLV +2.63%, XLU +2.21%, XLP +2.03%, XLB +1.94%, XLF +1.53% all gained while **XLK -2.71%** was the only sector loser of size. The rotation-away-from-tech hit the portfolio's momentum sleeves hard because they are 51-59% Technology-weighted — mom_roa_6535 -6.96%, mom_v2 -6.74%, mom_v1 -5.81%, vs. residual_roa_6535 (28% tech) -2.78% and the fully-diversified sector_top4_full/spy_benchmark essentially flat.
>
> **Key movers with reason:** A cluster of semiconductor-equipment/analog names fell 10-20% in the DB (VICR -19.21%, AIP/Arteris -19.97%, UCTT -17.84%, MOVE -17.52% [ticker identity unconfirmed, see Section 3C], MXL -17.15%, AEHR -17.14%, FORM -16.00%, BELFB -16.07%, VPG -15.57%, TER -13.63%, VIAV -11.49%, STX -10.38%) on a sector-wide AI-capex-fear selloff — Meta's spare-AI-capacity-leasing news plus a Citi capex-ROI warning triggered a Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) drop of ~6.4% today (~12% over two sessions, worst since June 5), following Broadcom's AI-revenue-guidance miss and a BofA "bubble risk" call on 6/30-7/1. External sources report smaller (7-14%) moves for several of these names than our DB shows — see Section 3A note. No earnings misses or negative company-specific news found for any of them; several (VICR, UCTT, MXL, AIP, STX) carry idiosyncratic tailwinds too (Russell index-reshuffle removals, profit-taking after 90-125%+ run-ups). Gainers: EACO +17.44% (record-quarter earnings, EPS +43.1% YoY, reported 7/1), AAPL +4.84% (5-new-iPhone-model / expanded foldable production reports), plus a cluster of biotechs (RCUS +4.88%, ARWR +4.41%, DNTH +4.58%, PVLA +6.15%) riding a sector-wide biotech rally (XBI at a 5-year high) and tanker names (INSW +5.94%, TEN +5.65%) on VLCC rate strength.
>
> **Macro event:** June nonfarm payrolls +57,000 (well below the ~100-115K consensus), unemployment 4.2% (participation rate fell to 61.5%, lowest since March 2021), wage growth +3.5% YoY. The soft print eased Fed-tightening fears and helped drive today's rotation into defensives/Dow, but did not offset the chip-sector-specific selloff. WTI fell ~2.1% to ~$67-67.75/bbl (lowest since late February) on easing Strait-of-Hormuz risk premium / constructive US-Iran talks — a tailwind for residual_roa_6535's energy names on net, though the tanker-rate story (VLCC rates +3x YTD) offset the lower-crude read for INSW/TEN specifically. Markets closed Friday July 3 for Independence Day (July 4 falls on Saturday); reopen Monday July 6.
>
> **LLM overlay standings:** No activity today — all 11 sleeves in the 07-01-inception cohort (6 LLM-experiment sleeves + 4 systematic duplicates + spy_0701) were wiped to a fresh $100,000/0-positions and their inception moved to **2026-07-06** (Appendix AT, logged today 2026-07-02) after the 7/1 rebalance was found to be partial/botched and an Alpaca fractional-order gap caused ~43-48 of ~50 mirror orders per account to silently fail. The `alpaca_sync` non-fractionable bug is now fixed (whole-share fallback), and the scheduled LLM-rebalance cron was disabled through the market holiday to avoid a premature redeploy. Nothing to compare treatment vs. control on until the 07-06 redeploy.
>
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) **The 07-06 coordinated redeploy** (Evan resets the 3 Alpaca PAPER accounts to $100k first, then a targeted rebalance of only the 11 cohort sleeves, then `alpaca_sync --all --execute`) is the top open item — see Appendix AT for the full runbook; (2) today's crash flipped mom_roa_6535/mom_v1/mom_v2 from beating SPY since inception to trailing it (see Strategic Insights) — the open question from the last two reports (does the momentum edge survive a *sustained* semiconductor correction) now has real evidence against it, worth close tracking into next week; (3) MOVE ticker identity is unconfirmed — DB tags it Technology but the only live match found was Movano Inc. (wearables, unrelated sector, pending delisting notice) — worth checking the data vendor's ticker mapping; (4) DRAM antitrust suit and SK Hynix's July 10 Nasdaq listing remain live, ongoing overhangs on the MU position common to all four systematic sleeves; (5) markets closed tomorrow (7/3) for the holiday — next trading session and next data pull is Monday 7/6.

### 1. Market Summary — July 2, 2026

#### A. Index Closes

| Index | Close / Change | Notable |
|---|---|---|
| **S&P 500 / SPY** | SPY $744.78, **-0.13%** (DB price_cache) | Broad-index sources describe the S&P as "roughly flat," with figures ranging -0.01% to essentially unchanged; 354 of 500 constituents advanced (~70% breadth) |
| **Nasdaq Composite** | **-1.3% to -1.6%** (source estimates vary) | Dragged by the two-day semiconductor-equipment rout; Meta and Tesla both cited as down 3.8-6.4% in the megacap-tech complex |
| **Dow Jones** | **52,900.07, +1.14% (record close)** | 24 of 30 components advanced; non-tech rotation (healthcare, staples, utilities) offset the chip drag |

Note on precision: multiple sources (TheStreet, 24/7 Wall St, search-aggregated CNBC data) gave slightly different exact decimal closes for the S&P and Nasdaq — the directional read (S&P flat, Nasdaq down on chips, Dow up on rotation) is well-corroborated across sources, but exact ticks should be treated as approximate pending a single-feed check. Source: [24/7 Wall St — Stock Market Live July 2, 2026](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/02/stock-market-live-july-2-2026-sp-500-spy-flat-with-new-jobs-data/) | [TheStreet — Stock Market Today, July 2, 2026](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-july-2-2026)

#### B. Primary Market Drivers

**1. A Meta AI-capacity-leasing headline plus a Citi capex-ROI warning triggered the day's chip-equipment selloff.** On July 1-2, Meta disclosed plans to sell/lease out spare AI computing capacity — read by the market as evidence hyperscalers over-built GPU/data-center capacity — while a Citi analyst separately questioned whether cloud platforms can sustain AI infrastructure capex without clearer ROI. This built directly on Broadcom's AI-chip revenue guidance miss ($16B Q3 guide vs. ~$17.2B street estimate, reported ~6/30-7/1) and a Bank of America "bubble risk" note on Intel/AMD/TSMC published 7/1. Source: [Bloomberg — Chip Stocks Off to Rough Start in Third Quarter With 2-Day Skid](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-02/chip-stocks-off-to-rough-start-in-third-quarter-with-2-day-skid) | [247wallst — Intel Drops 7%, AMD Slides 5%, TSM Falls 6% as BofA Flags Bubble Risk](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/01/intel-drops-7-amd-slides-5-taiwan-semiconductor-falls-6-as-boa-flags-bubble-risk/) | [StockStory/FinancialContent — FormFactor and Allegro Microsystems Shares Plummet](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/stockstory-2026-7-2-formfactor-and-allegro-microsystems-shares-plummet-what-you-need-to-know)

**2. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) fell ~6.4% today, ~12% over two sessions — the worst two-day drop since June 5.** Big semicap/memory names most exposed to WFE (wafer-fab-equipment)/AI-capex fears got hit hardest — KLA -11.5% to -12%, Lam Research -7.3% to -9.7%, Applied Materials -6.1% to -10%, Micron/SanDisk >-10% — which read through into the smaller equipment/component suppliers our sleeves hold. The selloff spread overnight to Asia: Samsung -9.06%, SK Hynix -14.57% on the Kospi open. Source: [Bloomberg — Chip Stocks Off to Rough Start in Third Quarter](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-02/chip-stocks-off-to-rough-start-in-third-quarter-with-2-day-skid) | [CNBC — Samsung, SK Hynix Shares Slide as Kospi Tech Selloff Follows Nasdaq](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/02/samsung-sk-hynix-shares-slide-kospi-tech-selloff-nasdaq.html)

**3. June nonfarm payrolls badly missed consensus: +57,000 vs. ~100-115K expected.** Unemployment rate 4.2% (the improvement driven by a falling participation rate, 61.5%, lowest since March 2021, rather than hiring strength). Wage growth +3.5% YoY (up from 3.4%). April/May were revised down a combined 74,000. The soft print reduced perceived pressure for further Fed tightening, which helped lift rate-sensitive/defensive sectors and the Dow, but did not rescue the Nasdaq from the chip-sector drag. Source: [CNBC — June 2026 Jobs Report](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/02/jobs-report-june-2026-.html) | [IndexBox — US Job Growth Slows More Than Expected in June 2026](https://www.indexbox.io/blog/us-job-growth-slows-more-than-expected-in-june-2026/)

**4. Idiosyncratic amplifiers on several of the worst-hit small/mid-cap semi names.** Multiple names in our sleeves (VICR, UCTT, MXL, AIP/Arteris) were also flagged in coverage for Russell index-reshuffle removals (dropped from Russell 3000/Small Cap Value benchmarks in late June) and/or profit-taking after outsized 90-125%+ run-ups over the prior year — VICR's move was linked to digesting a Russell index inclusion plus insider selling after a +125% 90-day run; UCTT to a Russell 3000 Value benchmark drop; AIP (Arteris) explicitly framed by TipRanks as "profit-taking...after record run," not bad news; STX's pullback tied to "broad sector profit-taking, high valuation concerns, natural consolidation" after a ~590% YTD/1-yr AI-storage rally. Source: [Simply Wall St — Vicor Could Be 14% Undervalued Following Russell Index Inclusion](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/capital-goods/nasdaq-vicr/vicor/news/vicor-vicr-could-be-14-undervalued-following-russell-index-i/amp) | [MarketScreener — Ultra Clean Holdings Dropped from Russell 3000 Value Benchmark](https://www.marketscreener.com/news/ultra-clean-holdings-inc-nasdaqgs-uctt-dropped-from-russell-3000-value-benchmark-ce7f5fdedb88f123) | [TipRanks — Profit-Taking Slams Arteris Stock After Record Run](https://www.tipranks.com/news/catalyst/profit-taking-slams-arteris-stock-after-record-run) | [TradingKey — STX Market Movers, July 1, 2026](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262004901-market-movers-stx-20260701)

**5. No fundamental negative catalyst found for Teradyne, Viavi, Vishay Precision, or Bel Fuse.** Multiple sources explicitly frame Teradyne's decline as pure sector-wide selling, grouped with AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/MKSI; Viavi's move was explicitly labeled "no clear catalyst identified" against otherwise-positive underlying business commentary (data-center/A&D demand beat); VPG and BELFB had no news found at all beyond routine items (a VPG Q1 beat, a BELFB dividend ex-date) unrelated to a same-day selloff. This is consistent with sector-wide beta contagion rather than idiosyncratic bad news across most of the list. Source: [Alphastreet — Teradyne Drops 8.6% Amid Sector-Wide Selling](https://news.alphastreet.com/teradyne-drops-8-6-amid-sector-wide-selling/amp/)

**6. Oil fell to a multi-month low on easing Iran risk premium.** WTI fell ~2.1% to roughly $67-67.75/bbl (lowest since late February) as Strait of Hormuz throughput expanded on constructive US-Iran negotiations and rising Iranian/Russian export volumes. Despite falling crude, tanker names INSW (+5.94%) and TEN (+5.65%) both rose — likely explained by VLCC (very-large-crude-carrier) daily rates reportedly tripling since the start of 2026 (to ~$170,000/day) on India's demand for Mideast crude plus expedited shipping amid the same US-Iran tension backdrop; no company-specific news was found for either ticker, so this is treated as a sector-flow read rather than a confirmed causal link, and the falling-crude/rising-tanker-equity combination is a genuinely mixed signal worth flagging rather than resolving. Source: [Investing.com — Oil Tanker Rates Surge to 6-Year High](https://www.investing.com/analysis/oil-tanker-rates-surge-to-6year-high-200675605)

**7. Markets closed Friday, July 3, 2026 for Independence Day.** NYSE, Nasdaq, NYSE Arca/American, and CBOE are closed the full day (July 4 falls on Saturday); markets reopen Monday, July 6. Source: [Nasdaq Trader — Holiday Calendar](https://www.nasdaqtrader.com/trader.aspx?id=calendar)

Both issues are now fixed (whole-share fallback added to `alpaca_sync` and a new `paper_rebalance --broker-realistic` flag), and all 11 sleeves were wiped to a clean $100,000/0-positions with inception moved to **2026-07-06** (the next trading day after tomorrow's July 4th holiday closure). Redeployment is coordinated and pending — see Critical Outstanding Items.

#### B. Positions near a stop or invalidation level

The four systematic momentum sleeves run **no intra-month stop-loss mechanism** (13 prior attempts tested and rejected — see `sleeves_verdict.md`), so today's double-digit single-name drawdowns trigger no automated action regardless of severity; positions are held until the next monthly rebalance.

The LLM overlay/cascade invalidation levels set on 7/1 (WDC $560, XLK $174, XLI $172, XLB $48, XLV $150) are moot for now — none of those positions ever executed into `paper_positions` before the 07-01 cohort was reset (Section 2), so there is nothing live to check against a stop today.

#### C. New developments

- **Appendix AT reset (today, 2026-07-02)**: the entire 11-sleeve 07-01 cohort was wiped and re-inceptioned for 2026-07-06 after the partial 7/1 rebalance and the Alpaca fractional-order bug (see Section 2 notes). This is the single biggest operational development of the day, unrelated to market moves.
- **EACO's all-time-record quarter** (net sales +27.8% YoY, EPS +43.1% YoY, reported 7/1) is a genuine, sourced, company-specific catalyst behind residual_roa_6535's biggest single-day gainer.
- **AAPL's iPhone-lineup and foldable-production reports** are a fresh, dated catalyst not present in prior reports.
- **RCUS's ARC-20/casdatifan Nature publication** (7/1) adds to the existing BMS kidney-cancer collaboration thread tracked in prior reports.
- **MOVE ticker ambiguity**: flagged above — worth a data-vendor check before citing this position's "-17.52%" as a real semiconductor move in future reports.
- **The Meta AI-capacity-leasing story and Citi capex-ROI skepticism** are new, dated (7/1-7/2) macro/sector developments not present in yesterday's report, and appear to be the proximate trigger for today's session specifically (on top of the already-known Broadcom guidance miss and BofA bubble-risk call from 6/30-7/1).

### 4. LLM Overlay: Treatment vs. Control Update

**No trading activity today.** All six LLM-experiment sleeves (both cash-veto treatment/control pairs and both cascade treatment/control pairs) are part of the 11-sleeve reset described in Section 2 — wiped to $100,000/0 positions, inception moved to 2026-07-06. The scheduled `monthy-llm-rebalance` cron (which fires daily at 6:03pm local) was explicitly **disabled today** to stop it from auto-deploying the reset cohort prematurely between now and the 07-06 target date (it would also have empty-universe-bailed on tomorrow's market holiday regardless). `llm_overlay_log` and `sector_overlay_log` show no new entries dated 2026-07-02 — the last logged decisions remain the 7/1 stock-side (BE VETO / WDC BUY) and sector-side (XLK/XLI/XLB HOLD, XLE VETO, cascade adds XLV) calls from the prior report, which never executed and are now superseded by the reset.

**Kill switch reminder:** unchanged — 12 months or ≥30 picks, drop the overlay if scores don't predict returns or treatment doesn't beat control. The synchronized-inception design now effectively restarts a second time (07-01 → 07-06), so the "Day 1" clock for this experiment has not yet started for real; zero live trading days exist under either inception date so far.

**Pending 07-06 runbook (from Appendix AT):** (1) Evan resets the 3 Alpaca PAPER accounts to $100k via the dashboard (API cannot do this; Claude never touches account settings); (2) targeted deploy of only the 11 cohort sleeves — `overlay_prep` as-of 07-06, log the 07-06 LLM decisions, rebalance the 5 systematic 0701 sleeves with `--broker-realistic`, seed spy_0701 for 07-06, seed the 6 LLM sleeves, MTM all 11, then `alpaca_sync --all --execute`; (3) re-enable the LLM-rebalance cron for August's monthly run.

---

### 6. Strategic Insights

**A. Today provides the clearest evidence yet against the "momentum edge survives a semiconductor correction" thesis flagged as an open question in the last two reports.** As of yesterday's close, all four systematic sleeves still led SPY since inception. As of today, three of four have flipped to *trailing* SPY: mom_roa_6535 -2.89pp, mom_v1 -4.91pp, mom_v2 -5.39pp behind SPY since 2026-05-01 inception. Only residual_roa_6535 (28% Technology-weighted, vs. 51-59% for the other three) still leads, and by a much-reduced +2.27pp (down from +5.20pp yesterday). This is not a single bad session in isolation — it is the second severe semiconductor-specific drawdown in three trading days (7/1 and 7/2 both), following the June 26-29 chip-crash regime referenced in prior reports. The open question posed twice now — does the edge survive a *sustained* correction, not just one bad day — has moved from "untested" to "evidence against," though a definitive verdict still requires seeing whether momentum re-establishes its edge on the next rebalance or continues to erode.

**B. The concentration-risk mechanism is now visible in the data twice in three sessions.** mom_roa_6535's Technology weight actually *increased* slightly from 58.4% (7/1) to 59.4% (7/2, post-drawdown, since tech fell faster than the rest of the book) — the portfolio has no mechanism to reduce single-sector concentration mid-month, and a momentum factor mechanically buys into whatever has been winning, which for over a year has been semiconductors. residual_roa_6535's dual-signal construction again cut the damage roughly in half to a third versus the pure-momentum sleeves (-2.78% vs. -5.81% to -6.96%), extending the pattern from 7/1 (-3.77% vs. -5.59% to -5.64%) — two consecutive real-time confirmations of its lower-drawdown design intent.

**C. The rotation was orderly, not panicked — which matters for interpreting the momentum sleeves' losses.** Nine of eleven sector ETFs gained today (Section 1C), the Dow hit a record close, and breadth was reported as ~70% advancing on the S&P. This was capital moving out of one crowded trade (AI/semiconductor capex names) into others (healthcare, staples, utilities, materials), not a broad flight from risk. That distinction matters for the momentum sleeves specifically: their drawdown is a concentrated-bet outcome, not a market-wide one, meaning diversified or rotation-aware constructions (residual_roa_6535, sector_top4_full, spy_benchmark) were structurally insulated from it almost by definition rather than by luck.

**D. The 07-01 cohort's reset (Appendix AT) removes an operational data-integrity risk before it compounded further — worth noting as a process win even though it delays the LLM-experiment timeline again.** Catching a partially-executed rebalance and a silent Alpaca order-failure mode within 24-48 hours, rather than letting mismatched local-vs-broker state accumulate for weeks, is the kind of thing that would have been far more expensive to unwind later. The cost is a second inception-date reset (07-01 → 07-06) for the entire 11-sleeve experiment, meaning the "does the LLM overlay beat control" and "does cascade out-pick raw momentum" questions remain exactly where they were before 6/30 — zero live trading days accumulated. No conclusion is possible on that experiment yet; the open questions from prior reports (cash-veto vs. cascade divergence, whether LLM scores predict returns) simply have not started accumulating evidence.

## Report: 2026-07-03 (Friday) — Pre-Market Overnight Research

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **Market status: CLOSED all day.** July 3, 2026 is the observed Independence Day holiday (July 4 falls on a Saturday, so NYSE/Nasdaq/bond markets are closed today and reopen Monday, July 6). There is no pre-market, no intraday session, and no new closing data for today — this report is a research/context update layered on the last confirmed close (July 1 for the 4 systematic sleeves' 5:15pm MTM run's use of continous pricing, then July 2, 2026), not a same-day trading preview.
> **NAV standings (last confirmed close, July 2, 2026, DB-verified via `paper_nav` — unchanged since):** residual_roa_6535 $105,621.14 (+5.62% since 5/1 inception, +2.27pp vs SPY) leads; spy_benchmark $103,348.37 (+3.35%); sector_top4_full $102,350.07 (+2.35%, -1.00pp vs SPY); mom_roa_6535 $100,461.75 (+0.46%, **-2.89pp vs SPY**); mom_v1 $98,437.50 (-1.56%, **-4.91pp**); mom_v2 $97,954.57 (-2.05%, **-5.39pp**). Three of four systematic momentum sleeves are now trailing SPY since inception, a flip that happened on 7/1-7/2's semiconductor-equipment rout (see prior report for full detail). The 11-sleeve 07-01→07-06 cohort remains flat at $100,000/0 positions each, confirmed via `paper_portfolio`/`paper_positions` — no change since the 07-02 reset.
> **Pre-market moves:** None exist — market closed. No new price data for any holding since the July 2 close already covered in yesterday's post-market report.
> **Macro catalysts:** Nothing scheduled today (holiday). Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's July 1 Sintra (ECB Forum) remarks continue to be digested: he called inflation "too high," declined to give forward guidance ("we're meeting in four weeks"), and reiterated the Fed won't be comfortable above the 2% target — read as consistent with, not more hawkish than, prior guidance. Market-implied odds of a July FOMC rate hike reportedly eased to ~20% (from ~30% the prior week) following the soft June payrolls print. WTI ended the week near $68.28 (Brent $71.35), both ~4-month lows, on continued US-Iran de-escalation optimism out of Doha; US crude inventories fell to 408.4M bbls, the lowest since September 2018, ahead of the holiday-weekend driving season. Markets reopen Monday, July 6 to a stacked calendar: labor-market follow-through data, Fed minutes, ISM services, and (mechanically, unrelated to any portfolio strategy) forced index-fund buying as SpaceX joins the Nasdaq-100 effective July 7 open — SpaceX is not held in any sleeve.
> **LLM overlay status:** Still fully idle — zero live trading days under either the 07-01 or the current 07-06 inception. Per Project Record Appendix AU (2026-07-02, the most recent entry, not yet reflected in the last report), the "3 new Alpaca PAPER accounts" step is already done and verified ACTIVE/$100k/flat (account #s PA3MD0BVF2WN / PA37FAQU4MOY / PA3RXQ3LDX94), so the previously-planned manual dashboard reset is no longer needed. An early attempt to deploy tonight (07-02) was correctly held back after finding yfinance's 07-02 close coverage was ~15% incomplete (4,416 of ~5,200 baseline tickers) — confirmed as a coverage gap, not corrupted values, but Evan chose to wait for a clean full universe on 07-06 rather than accept ~1 stray pick per sleeve.
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) **Operational — the scheduled 5:15pm daily MTM will write stray pre-inception $100k flat `paper_nav` rows for all 11 cohort sleeves on each of 07-03/07-04/07-05** (already true for 07-02, cleaned up per Appendix AU); these must be deleted before the 07-06 deploy runbook runs, or they'll pollute the NAV history. (2) The full 07-06 runbook (Appendix AT/AU) is still pending: `overlay_prep` as-of 07-06 → log 07-06 LLM decisions → rebalance the 5 systematic `_0701` sleeves with `--broker-realistic` → seed `spy_0701` and the 6 LLM sleeves → MTM all 11 → `alpaca_sync --all --execute` → re-enable the `monthy-llm-rebalance` cron. (4) SK Hynix's targeted Nasdaq ADR debut is **5 calendar days away (~July 10, dates subject to SEC approval)** — an amended F-1 was filed 6/30 including antitrust-litigation risk disclosure. (5) The open strategic question from the last two reports — whether the momentum-factor edge over SPY survives a *sustained* semiconductor correction rather than one bad session — remains exactly where July 2's close left it (three of four systematic sleeves now trailing SPY); nothing about that changes over a market holiday, but Monday's reopen is the next real data point.
