## Report: 2026-07-09 (Thursday) — Post-Market Close Analysis

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **CRITICAL: today's automated daily MTM was SKIPPED.** The coverage gate (M2.2, wired into `daily.bat` at 13:30 today, its first live day) failed at 17:17: only **4,381** `price_cache` closes published for 2026-07-09 vs. a 10-day baseline of ~5,245 and a hard floor of 5,000 — `check_coverage` correctly refused to MTM on incomplete data (see §2). **No official `paper_nav` row exists for 07-09.** This report's NAV figures below split into (a) the last **official** close, 2026-07-08, and (b) **unofficial, informally-computed** 07-09 marks I calculated directly from real `paper_positions` qty × real `price_cache` 07-09 closes + cash — same methodology as the pipeline, explicitly labeled, not the system's own output. 156/158 held tickers *did* publish 07-09 closes (only `EACO`/`FMBM`, both in `residual_roa_6535_paper`, carried forward at 07-08 price) — the gate's shortfall came from ~860 missing tickers elsewhere in the broader universe, not the portfolio's own holdings, which is why an informal reconstruction is possible at all.
> **NAV standings (informal 07-09 marks, since-inception, best→worst):** `llm_cascade_top1_paper` **+9.50%** ($109,501, day +5.04%, holds WDC) · `residual_roa_6535_paper` +6.77% ($106,767, day +0.68%*) · `mom_v2_0701_paper` +4.67% ($104,675, day +4.12%) · `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper` +4.36% ($104,357, day +3.18%) · `spy_benchmark_paper` +4.31% ($104,310, day +0.85%) · `mom_v1_0701_paper` +3.58% ($103,576, day +3.10%) · `residual_roa_6535_0701_paper` +2.63% ($102,627, day +1.04%) · `sector_top4_full_paper` +2.51% ($102,508, day +0.42%) · `sector_top4_paper` +0.56% ($100,556, day +0.33%) · `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` +0.19% ($100,190, day +0.70%) · `spy_benchmark_0701_paper` +0.06% ($100,057, day +0.85%) · `mom_roa_6535_paper` +0.01% ($100,014, day +2.79%) · `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` 0.00% ($100,000, cash, VETO) · `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` -0.21% ($99,794, day +0.68%) · `mom_v2_paper` -1.77% ($98,228, day +3.25%) · `mom_v1_paper` -1.80% ($98,199, day +2.97%) · `mom_roa_top1_paper` **-2.62%** ($97,380, day +1.07%, holds BE). *residual figure uses 07-08 carried-forward prices for EACO/FMBM (2 of 50 names).
> **Day moves:** Broad rally — Nasdaq **+1.30%** (26,206.89), S&P 500 **+0.81%** (7,543.64), Dow **+0.27%** (52,487.41, +139.02 pts). Semiconductors led (SMH +2.5%) on Micron's surprise $250B U.S. investment plan; oil **fell** (WTI -0.2% to $73.37, Brent -0.21% to $77.86), reversing the two-day Hormuz-driven spike even as US strikes on Iran continued into a second day.
> **Key movers (07-08→07-09 close, held names, real `price_cache` data):** Up — LASR +27.32% ($44M-$627M laser-weapon defense contract), AEHR +12.26% (silicon-photonics follow-on order), MXL +11.33%, BAND +11.28% (B.Riley PT $55→$85), AAOI +6.79%, FORM +7.03%, AMKR +7.85%, ICHR +8.59%, WDC +5.04% (Susquehanna PT raise + Micron $250B halo). Down — AFJK -51.70% (serial-extension SPAC, deadline pushed to 08-06, no single dated catalyst pinned), VATE -11.11% (thin microcap, unexplained — 2nd report in a row), ALIT -8.45% (no distinct catalyst), INSW -5.92%/APA -5.05%/PARR -4.42%/XOM -2.60% (oil giving back Tue/Wed's spike).
> **Macro event:** Initial jobless claims 215,000 (-4,000 w/w), lowest since 05-23. Existing home sales -2.4% m/m to 4.9M annualized. Oil reversed despite continued US-Iran strikes — traders reassessing actual Hormuz supply disruption as limited.
> **LLM overlay standings (informal, unofficial pending real MTM):** Stock arm — cascade (WDC) $109,501 (+9.50% cohort) > treatment (cash) $100,000 (0.00%) > control (BE) $97,380 (-2.62%); BE also drew a same-day short-seller attack (Hunterbrook Capital) it "categorically rejected," and still closed +1.07%. Sector arm — **first-ever daily flip**: overlay (no XLE) +0.70% and cascade (XLV) +0.68% both beat control (holds XLE) +0.33% today, as XLE was the only sector ETF to fall (-1.40%) on oil's reversal — the XLE veto's "fragile geopolitical prop" thesis scored its first win after 3 straight losses.
> **Critical outstanding items:** **No official 07-09 NAV exists — rerun/backfill status needs Evan's attention**, this is the coverage gate's first live trigger (deployed today 13:30) and it worked as designed, but leaves a genuine data gap. BE short-seller dispute (Hunterbrook) is a new named risk on top of the existing earnings-date conflict (07-28 vs 07-30, still unresolved). EACO/FMBM still missing prices (residual_roa_6535 only). No LLM overlay/cascade decisions logged since 07-07.

### 1. Market Summary — Index Closes & Sector Performance (2026-07-09)

US equities rallied broadly Thursday even as the US-Iran conflict continued to escalate. The **Nasdaq Composite gained 1.30% to 26,206.89**, the **S&P 500 rose 0.81% to 7,543.64**, and the **Dow Jones Industrial Average added 139.02 points (+0.27%) to 52,487.41** [Yahoo Finance — Stock Market News for July 9, 2026](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/stock-market-news-july-9-093800837.html), [TheStreet — Stock Market Today July 9](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-july-9-2026).

**Semiconductors were the standout sector.** The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) climbed 2.5%, led by Micron (+~7-8% on the session) after the company announced it will invest **more than $250 billion in U.S. chipmaking through 2035** — adding $50B to its prior $200B commitment, plus up to $3B for the domestic supply-chain ecosystem, with a goal of making 40% of its DRAM in the U.S. within a decade. The company also poured first concrete at its new Clay, NY fab (to be the largest semiconductor manufacturing site in U.S. history) [CNBC — Micron stock pops on more US investment](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/09/micron-stock-us-chipmaking.html), [Bloomberg — Micron boosts US spending to $250 billion](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-09/micron-boosts-us-spending-to-250-billion-amid-memory-demand). Sandisk also popped +7.6% on the same memory-tightness narrative [Yahoo Finance — Stock Market News July 9](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/stock-market-news-july-9-093800837.html).

**Oil reversed lower** despite the U.S. military confirming a second straight day of strikes on Iran: Brent fell 0.21% to $77.86/bbl, WTI fell 0.2% to $73.37/bbl, both pulling back from Wednesday's highest levels since June 22. Traders reportedly reassessed the situation — vessel-tracking data suggested substantial Hormuz crude volumes had kept moving even through the ceasefire breakdown, implying actual physical supply disruption has been more limited than the price action implied [CNBC — Oil prices fall as markets weigh impact of US strikes on Iran / mediaselangor.com syndication](https://mediaselangor.com/en/2026/07/383623), [Fortune — Current price of oil as of July 9, 2026](https://fortune.com/article/price-of-oil-07-09-2026/).

**Sector ETF performance (07-08 close → 07-09 close, real `price_cache` data):**

| Sector ETF | 07-08 | 07-09 | Day % | Note |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| XLK (Technology) | $181.40 | $185.35 | **+2.18%** | Semiconductor/memory rally on Micron's $250B plan |
| SPY (S&P 500 proxy) | $745.40 | $751.71 | +0.85% | Broad rally |
| XLI (Industrials) | $180.42 | $181.11 | +0.38% | |
| XLB (Materials) | $50.16 | $50.26 | +0.20% | |

**Macro — jobless claims / housing:** Initial jobless claims fell to a seasonally adjusted **215,000** for the week ended 07-04, down 4,000 from the prior week and the lowest total since May 23 — a continued cooling from early June's spike. Existing home sales unexpectedly declined 2.4% m/m in June to a seasonally adjusted annualized 4.9 million [Yahoo Finance — Stock Market News for July 9, 2026](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/stock-market-news-july-9-093800837.html).

**Macro — geopolitical:** SK Hynix's U.S. IPO priced ahead of its planned 07-10 debut (a secondary memory-supply-chain datapoint); the US-Iran conflict itself showed no de-escalation, with Trump reiterating the "ceasefire is over" framing from Wednesday and warning of a possible naval blockade, even as oil traded down on the day [TheStreet — Stock Market Today July 9: Nasdaq, S&P 500 rise despite Iran conflict flare-up](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-july-9-2026).

### 2. NAV — Official (07-08) and Informal/Unofficial (07-09)

**Why there is no official 07-09 NAV:** `daily.bat`'s `check_coverage` gate (M2.2, wired into the pipeline this morning at 13:30, its first day live) ran at 17:17 and found only **4,381** `price_cache` closes for 2026-07-09, against a 10-day median baseline of **5,245** and a hard floor of **5,000**. Per design, it printed `COVERAGE FAIL` and **skipped all MTM and overlay ops** rather than mark sleeves against an incomplete price snapshot. Verbatim from `var/last_daily_run.log`:
```
2026-07-09 17:17:49,856 INFO check_coverage: Coverage check: date=2026-07-09  closes=4381  baseline(median of 10)=5245  floor=5000 [max(5000, 90%*5245=4720)]
2026-07-09 17:17:49,856 ERROR check_coverage: COVERAGE FAIL: only 4381 closes on 2026-07-09 (< floor 5000). Likely incomplete publication (record Appendix AU) - do NOT MTM on this data.
COVERAGE FAIL - incomplete price publication. Skipping all MTM and overlay ops today.
```
The daily price refresh log shows heavy `yfinance` "possibly delisted" errors and one rate-limit error across the run — consistent with a broad, largely-illiquid-ticker publication gap rather than anything wrong with the portfolio's own holdings: **156 of 158 held tickers across all 17 sleeves DO have a real 07-09 close** in `price_cache` (only `EACO` and `FMBM`, both in `residual_roa_6535_paper`, are missing — a pre-existing multi-session gap flagged in prior reports). This report does not fabricate an official NAV; instead, §1 of the DAILY SUMMARY reconstructs an **unofficial, informally-computed** mark for every sleeve, directly from real `paper_positions` (qty) × real `price_cache` 07-09 closes, plus real cash — the same arithmetic the pipeline itself performs, just run by hand here and explicitly labeled as unofficial. This is a genuine data gap that needs Evan's attention (does the pipeline retry, or does 07-09 simply never get an official NAV row?) — reported as an open item in §5/§6, not resolved here.

### 3. Key Position Analysis

#### A. Semiconductor sector — the day's dominant theme, driven by Micron's $250B announcement

The reported chip bounce flagged as unconfirmed in this morning's pre-market report is now confirmed at the close, and it was much bigger than a generic rebound — it was driven by a specific, dated catalyst: **Micron committed more than $250 billion to U.S. chipmaking through 2035**, adding $50B to its existing $200B pledge (New York/Idaho/Virginia fabs) plus up to $3B for the broader U.S. semiconductor supply chain, aiming for 40% domestic DRAM production within a decade. The company poured first concrete at its Clay, NY site the same day [CNBC — Micron stock pops on more US investment](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/09/micron-stock-us-chipmaking.html), [Bloomberg — Micron boosts US spending to $250 billion](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-09/micron-boosts-us-spending-to-250-billion-amid-memory-demand).

| Ticker | Day % | Held in (approx sleeves) | Note |
|---|---:|---|---|
| WDC (Western Digital) | +5.04% | 9 (8 systematic + cascade) | **Sourced:** Susquehanna raised its price target; the Micron announcement reinforced the "memory is a bottleneck for AI infrastructure" narrative lifting the whole storage/memory complex [TradingKey — WDC market movers 07-09](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262020762-market-movers-wdc-20260709), [Benzinga — Western Digital stock lifts following Micron's $250B investment](https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60371599/western-digital-stock-lifts-following-microns-250-billion-investment) |

### 4. LLM Overlay Treatment vs. Control

No new overlay/cascade decisions were logged 07-09 (`llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` checked directly; latest entries remain 07-07 — consistent with no rebalance cycle). All figures below are the informal/unofficial marks from §2, clearly not the pipeline's own output.

**Stock arm:** Bloom Energy (`mom_roa_top1_paper`'s sole holding) drew a new, material development today: short-seller **Hunterbrook Capital** disclosed a short position and alleged BE understated its reliance on Chinese suppliers for scandium (used in its fuel cells), questioning whether BE can secure enough scandium oxide for its long-term manufacturing targets. Bloom "categorically rejected" the claims as "false and misleading," reasserted its 25 GW/year production capability, and the stock — after trading as low as $235.64 intraday — closed at $257.02, **+1.07% on the day** [Seeking Alpha — Bloom Energy rebounds after rejecting short-seller claims](https://seekingalpha.com/news/4612638-bloom-energy-rebounds-after-rejecting-short-seller-claims), [GuruFocus — Bloom Energy rebounds 7.4% amid rejection of short-seller claims](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8951520/bloom-energy-be-rebounds-74-amid-rejection-of-shortseller-claims). This is a new, named risk layered on top of the still-unresolved 07-28-vs-07-30 earnings-date conflict, and it is exactly the kind of idiosyncratic single-name risk the overlay's original BE veto (score 4/10) was designed to avoid — the treatment sidestepped it entirely by sitting in cash. Informal cohort standing (07-06 inception): **cascade (WDC) $109,501 (+9.50%) > treatment (cash) $100,000 (0.00%) > control (BE) $97,380 (-2.62%)** — unchanged ranking from 07-08, control still trailing both LLM-mediated variants.

**Sector arm — first daily flip in the veto's favor:** Control `sector_top4_paper` (holds XLE) informally +0.33% today; overlay treatment (no XLE) +0.70%; cascade (XLV instead of XLE) +0.68%. **Both LLM variants beat the raw control for the first time since the 07-06 cohort inception**, because XLE was the only one of the four sector ETFs to fall today (-1.40%), exactly the reversal the original veto rationale flagged as possible ("a fragile geopolitical prop that could reverse fast"). The XLE veto's scoreboard moves from 0-for-3 to (informally) 1-for-4 on directional accuracy. Informal cohort standing: control $100,556 (+0.56%) > overlay $100,190 (+0.19%) > cascade $99,794 (-0.21%) — the control still leads cumulatively, but the gap narrowed today rather than widened for the first time. One day does not resolve a multi-week thesis; still far short of the 30-pick kill-switch threshold.

### 5. Week / Period Summary

Extending the 07-06 cohort's now-four-day life: Mon 07-06 (chip-led rally, record Dow) → Tue 07-07 (Samsung-earnings-triggered chip/memory rout, base sleeves -3.8/-4.7%) → Wed 07-08 (partial reversal, energy led) → Thu 07-09 (broad rally, Micron's $250B plan drove a much larger, confirmed chip bounce; oil gave back its spike). Informally, every 07-06-cohort sleeve is now net positive since inception except `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` (-0.21%) and the stock-arm control (-2.62%, BE-specific). The older 06-13-inception momentum book (`mom_v1`/`mom_v2`/`mom_roa_6535_paper`) had its best single session of the week (+2.79% to +3.25%) but remains net-negative-to-flat since inception, still well below its 07-06 peak. `residual_roa_6535_paper` set a fresh informal cohort high (+6.77%) despite a comparatively muted day (+0.68%, dragged slightly by the two carried-forward tickers), extending its run as the best-performing sleeve of the older cohort. Caveat: this whole section rests on the informal §2 marks pending confirmation once official 07-09/07-10 NAVs exist in `paper_nav`.

### 6. Strategic Insights — Structural Edge or New Sleeve Concepts?

**A. The M2.2 coverage gate just did its job for the first time, and that is itself the most important operational finding of the day.** Deployed at 13:30 today, it caught a real ~16% shortfall in the daily price publication at 17:17 and correctly refused to mark 17 sleeves against incomplete data rather than silently producing a wrong NAV. This validates the ops-infrastructure investment (M2 series: coverage gate, anomaly detector, cache-gap auditor) exactly as designed. It also leaves a genuine open question this report cannot answer: does the pipeline retry coverage tomorrow and simply never backfill an official 07-09 NAV, or does something need to be re-run manually? That is an operational decision for Evan, not something this report should attempt or assume.

**B. The sector-arm LLM veto scored its first directional win today, right after the FOMC-minutes/Hormuz thesis had gone 0-for-3 against it.** The XLE veto's stated worry — that the Hormuz-driven oil premium was "fragile" and could reverse fast — is exactly what happened today (oil fell despite continued strikes, XLE was the sole losing sector ETF). This is one data point, not a trend reversal, and the control still leads the cohort cumulatively, but it is the first session where the pattern flagged in the last two reports (control winning every day) broke.

**C. Micron's $250B announcement is a distinct, larger catalyst than the "generic oversold bounce" this morning's pre-market report could only describe directionally** — it explains why today's chip rally (SMH +2.5%, several held names +5-11%) was materially bigger than Wednesday's partial reversal. This reinforces the observation (flagged 07-08) that the momentum book's heaviest concentration (semiconductors/memory/AI-infrastructure) continues to swing on dated, idiosyncratic company news as much as on macro sentiment — a three-session round-trip (rout → partial bounce → confirmed rally) that has now resolved to the upside, at least for one day.

**D. Bloom Energy's short-seller episode adds a new, specific tail-risk to the single largest concentration position in the book** (`mom_roa_top1_paper` is 100% BE, no stop by design). The stock absorbed the attack and closed positive, which is mild evidence the market didn't find the scandium-supply-chain allegations credible enough to sell into — but it is a fresh, named risk that didn't exist in prior reports and is worth tracking alongside the earnings-date conflict.

**E. No new long-term-viable sleeve concept is confirmed or refuted by today's data.** The day's real news was operational (the coverage gate) and about existing theses gaining or losing evidence (residual's diversification, both LLM overlay arms), not about a new sleeve idea. Watch-and-accumulate posture unchanged.

## Report: 2026-07-10 (Friday) — Delayed Same-Day Update (resumed mid-afternoon, not pre-market)

> **OPERATIONAL NOTE (read first):** This task is scheduled to fire at 08:07am local and did (`lastRunAt` 08:08am CDT confirmed via the scheduler). The session was interrupted/compacted after the initial state-read and resumed at **~2:43pm CDT (~3:43pm ET)** — about 17 minutes before the 4:00pm ET close. Per standing "don't fabricate" policy, this report does **not** pretend to be pre-market: it is a same-day update compiled near the close, using real `var/trades.db` data as of query time (`price_cache` 07-10 still populating intraday — 4,352 of ~5,245 baseline tickers priced at query time) plus news published through the afternoon. Official 07-10 `paper_nav` does not exist yet (`TradingDailyMTM` fires 5:15pm local); all 07-10 figures below are **informal**, computed directly from `paper_positions` × `price_cache` (same methodology the pipeline itself falls back to, per the 07-09 post-market entry above). Flag for Evan: this is the second session-interruption-driven gap in as many days (07-09's pre-market report footer also landed out of order at the end of the file above instead of right after its own section — pre-existing, not touched here).

> **Today's moves (informal, near-close 07-10):** Broad-market-tracking sleeves are flat-to-up (spy_benchmark +0.45% day, sector_top4_full +0.73%, sector_top4 +0.73%) while the multi-name momentum book is down (mom_v1/v2/roa_6535 0701-cohort -2.08% to -2.69% day; legacy mom_v1/v2/roa_6535 -1.56% to -2.19%), dragged by **DOCN -6.99%** (held in all 8 momentum-family sleeves) despite broad market strength. Single-name control `mom_roa_top1_paper` (BE) fell **-4.79% day** on continued short-seller pressure; `llm_cascade_top1_paper` (WDC) rose **+0.93% day** on an analyst price-target raise. `residual_roa_6535_paper` -0.41% day (EACO stale, no new close).
> **Macro catalyst:** SK Hynix's US IPO (raised $26.5B, largest-ever US listing by a foreign company, shares +15-17% on debut) dominated Friday's tape and tested the AI-memory trade; broader indices modestly positive (Dow/S&P/Nasdaq each ~+0.2-0.3% per midday reporting). Iran: Trump again declared the ceasefire "over" (second time in three days) after US strikes ~90 targets across Iran and Iran's IRGC hit US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain overnight, but both sides say talks continue; oil eased (WTI ~$71/bbl, Brent ~$75/bbl) despite the escalation. Jobless claims (week of 07-04, released 07-09): 215,000, better than the 217,000 consensus, layoffs still low.
> **LLM overlay status:** No new decisions logged since 07-07 (checked both logs through 07-10; none found — no rebalance cycle due). Stock arm: cascade (WDC) > cash treatment > BE control, unchanged ranking. Sector arm: control (XLE-inclusive) still ahead of both overlay variants.

BE short-seller situation (Hunterbrook Capital) is now a multi-day overhang, unresolved. Monthly rebalance next fires 2026-08-01.

### 3. Catalyst and macro review

**SK Hynix US IPO — the day's dominant single event.** SK Hynix raised $26.5B in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company; shares priced at $149 and traded around $170-173 midday, a 14-17% pop. Demand was reportedly >7x the shares offered (~$185B in orders). SK Hynix supplies high-bandwidth memory to Nvidia and other AI-hardware makers, positioning it as a direct Micron competitor — sources describe the semiconductor sector "splitting into winners and losers based on AI proximity," with SK Hynix/Nvidia on one side. VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) reported +2.5%, Micron +4.5%, SanDisk +7.6% per midday reporting — though our own `price_cache` shows MU essentially flat (-0.28%) 07-09→07-10 near-close, a discrepancy likely explained by the two figures being measured from different reference points (midday-vs-midday move vs. our prior-close-to-near-close snapshot); not reconciled further given time constraints. [Motley Fool — Nasdaq Composite Treads Water While SK Hynix Makes a $26 Billion Splash](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/07/10/nasdaq-treads-water-while-sk-hynix-makes-a-splash/), [CNN Business — SK Hynix IPO](https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/10/business/sk-hynix-us-listing-ai-chip-boom-intl-hnk), [US News — SK Hynix hits the U.S. stock market](https://www.usnews.com/news/business/articles/2026-07-10/sk-hynix-hits-the-u-s-stock-market-as-demand-for-memory-chips-soars-amid-ai-frenzy), [Yahoo Finance — Stock market today, Friday July 10](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-friday-july-10-dow-sp-nasdaq-113921604.html).

**Iran conflict — ceasefire declared over for a second time in three sessions.** Trump again said the ceasefire is over after the US struck ~90 targets across Iran (Bushehr nuclear-plant perimeter and a fishing pier among them, per Iranian state media IRNA) and Iran's IRGC struck US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain overnight. Trump also said the US has agreed to continue talks with Iran despite the ceasefire's collapse. Reported cumulative US strikes: ~170 in two days. Oil eased despite the escalation — WTI ~$71/bbl, Brent ~$75/bbl per midday reporting, a pullback from Wednesday/Thursday's spike. [CNN — Live updates: Trump again declares ceasefire over](https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/10/world/live-news/iran-war-trump), [Al Jazeera — US strikes Iran for second night](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/9/us-strikes-iran-for-second-night-is-the-peace-process-all-over-now), [NBC News — U.S. military completes latest strikes on Iran](https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/live-blog/live-updates-iran-attacks-gulf-us-strikes-tehran-ships-hormuz-oil-rcna353439).

**Jobless claims (released 07-09, week ending 07-04):** 215,000 initial claims, down 2,000 w/w, beating the 217,000 consensus; 4-week average 218,750. Layoffs remain historically low — no signal of labor-market deterioration. [Bloomberg — US Jobless Claims Little Changed](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-09/us-jobless-claims-little-changed-last-week-as-layoffs-remain-low), [US News — Jobless claims dip to 215,000](https://www.usnews.com/news/business/articles/2026-07-09/us-jobless-claims-dip-modestly-to-215-000-last-week-as-layoffs-remain-at-historically-healthy-levels).

**BE (Bloom Energy) — multi-day short-seller overhang.** A Hunterbrook Capital report (first surfaced ~07-08) alleging misleading statements about BE's scandium supply chain and production capacity continues to pressure the stock; BE fell further 07-10 (-4.80% per our data) after an initial partial rebound Wednesday/Thursday when BE "strongly denied" the claims. Sources describe a volatile, still-unresolved situation — BE traded in a wide range intraday ($253-$277 per one source) with conflicting "current price" snapshots depending on the article's timestamp. [TradingKey — Bloom Energy (BE) moved down 6.01% on Jul 10](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262023134-market-movers-be-20260710), [GuruFocus — Bloom Energy (BE) stock drops amid short seller report](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8953748/bloom-energy-be-stock-drops-amid-short-seller-report), [Yahoo Finance — What's Going On With Bloom Energy Stock Thursday](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/whats-going-bloom-energy-stock-123858815.html).

**WDC (Western Digital) — analyst price-target raise.** Wells Fargo raised its WDC price target to $730 from $575 on demand-outlook strength (reported same-day, 07-10), consistent with the stock's continued AI-infrastructure-driven run; 22 of 23 covering analysts rate it Buy/Strong Buy, average target $606 (likely stale vs. the fresh $730 call). [CNBC — WDC quote and news](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/WDC).

**DOCN (DigitalOcean) — profit-taking after a historic run, no new negative catalyst found.** DOCN fell -6.99% (our data) to -10% (one source's "latest session" framing) despite no company-specific bad news located. Drivers cited: (a) plain profit-taking after a 99%/30-day and 127%/90-day run, (b) broader AI/software-infrastructure de-risking after Fed commentary suggesting rates stay higher for longer, and (c) index-rebalancing mechanics — DOCN was promoted from Russell 2000 to Russell 1000 this week, and the forced Russell-2000-fund selling reportedly outpaced Russell-1000-fund buying, a "sell the news" pattern. Fundamentals unchanged: revenue growth accelerating, 25% net margin, record prelim Q2 results announced 07-07. [TipRanks — Why DigitalOcean's stock is suddenly sinking](https://www.tipranks.com/news/catalyst/why-digitaloceans-hot-cloud-stock-is-suddenly-sinking), [Quiver Quantitative — Why DigitalOcean (DOCN) stock fell today](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Why+DigitalOcean+(DOCN)+Stock+Fell+Today), [MarketBeat — DigitalOcean shares down 7%](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/digitalocean-nysedocn-shares-down-7-heres-why-2026-07-01/).

### 4. LLM overlay status

No new overlay decisions logged after 2026-07-07 (checked `llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` directly through 07-10; none found — no rebalance cycle due; consistent with prior reports).

**Stock arm:** Control `mom_roa_top1_paper` holds BE (re-entered 07-07 @ $263.94, score 8.8/10), now -2.62% since 07-09 official close and an informal -7.29% since 07-06 cohort inception — the short-seller overhang has erased essentially the entire cohort gain. Treatment `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` remains 100% cash (BE VETO'd 07-07 @ $220.59, score 4/10, "dup-check" reasoning logged). Cascade `llm_cascade_top1_paper` holds WDC (BUY 07-07 @ $480 invalidation, score 7.4-6.0/10 across logs), now the best performer in the entire 17-sleeve roster at +10.52% informal since-inception. Over the cohort's life: **cascade (+10.52%) > cash treatment (0.00%) > BE control (-7.29%)** — the strongest pro-overlay evidence to date, now spanning a fourth trading day.

**Sector arm:** Control `sector_top4_paper` (XLE/XLK/XLI/XLB) +1.29% informal since-inception. Overlay treatment (no XLE, VETO'd 07-07 @ $56.86 on weak technicals + bearish EIA oversupply outlook) +0.86%. Cascade (XLV substituted for XLE) +0.27%. Control still leads both overlay variants — the XLE veto remains behind on realized return even as oil has been volatile on the Iran conflict.

Kill-switch status unchanged on both arms: 12-month / ≥30-pick window, not yet triggered.

### 5. Risk flags and upcoming events

- **`EACO` price-cache gap now spans 4 sessions** (last real close 07-06 @ $101.01; 07-07 through 07-10 all show either no row or a repeated stale $101.01 close) — confirmed via direct query. Thin OTC name (volume rows show 200-500 shares), no halt/delisting news found. This is the longest the gap has run since first flagged 07-08; escalating, not resolving.
- **BE short-seller situation unresolved** — `mom_roa_top1_paper` remains 100% concentrated in BE by design (no stop), now the single largest identifiable drawdown driver in the roster (-7.29% cohort return, informal).
- **DOCN concentration** — held across all 8 momentum-family sleeves; today's -6.99% (informal) is the largest single-name drag on the momentum book despite a flat-to-positive broad market.
- **No intraday circuit-breaker** exists for any sleeve (accepted, previously-reviewed design gap per `sleeves_verdict.md`'s 13 rejected reactive-stop experiments).
- **Operational**: two consecutive scheduled-task sessions (07-09 pre-market, 07-10 pre-market) have now been interrupted mid-run and resumed late/out of order, producing (a) a misplaced footer in the 07-09 pre-market section above and (b) this report itself running ~6.5 hours late and covering near-close rather than pre-market conditions. Worth a look if it recurs a third time.
- **Monthly rebalance** next fires 2026-08-01 per the `monthy-llm-rebalance` scheduled task (confirmed still enabled, `lastRunAt` 2026-07-10 03:34 UTC — a no-op check outside the trading-day-1 window).

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. The diversification-vs-concentration pattern from the 07-08/07-09 reports held again today, on a calmer macro tape.** With the broad market flat-to-up and no acute portfolio-specific shock, the diversified sleeves (`spy_benchmark` +4.78%, `sector_top4_full` +3.25%, `residual_roa_6535` +6.33% informal since-inception) still comfortably outperform the concentrated momentum book (0701-cohort momentum sleeves +1.4-2.0%, legacy momentum sleeves -1.5% to -3.9%, both dragged by DOCN's single-name -6.99% today). This is now consistent across a volatile session (07-07/08), a mixed session (07-09), and a calm session (07-10) — the pattern isn't just a crisis-alpha story, it holds on ordinary days too, which is a somewhat stronger claim than what the 07-08/07-09 reports could support. Still not a new finding, but the sample is thickening in the diversification thesis's favor.

**B. The LLM stock-overlay's WDC cascade pick is now the best-performing sleeve in the entire 17-sleeve roster** (+10.52% informal since 07-06, vs. the next-best `residual_roa_6535_paper` at +6.33%). One name over four trading days is a small sample and today's WDC gain was driven by a single analyst price-target raise (Wells Fargo, +27% target increase) rather than fundamentals shifting — worth resisting the urge to over-read this as validating the overlay concept broadly; the kill-switch's 30-pick threshold exists precisely because single-name, few-day performance is noisy. The more informative comparison remains cascade-vs-control-vs-cash on the *same* underlying signal, which continues to favor the LLM veto (BE) and BUY (WDC) decisions.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** SK Hynix's IPO is a notable one-off market-structure event (largest foreign US listing ever) rather than a repeatable signal; nothing here suggests a new systematic edge distinct from what's already being tracked (idiosyncratic-momentum diversification, LLM veto/BUY discretion). Today's honest read: the portfolio's known mechanisms behaved as expected, no surprises, no new evidence either direction on kill-switch thresholds.

> **DAILY SUMMARY** — 2026-07-11 (Saturday, no trading session): Markets were closed all day; there is no new NAV to report. The last **confirmed/official** NAV remains 2026-07-09 (Thursday) for all 17 sleeves, ranked best-to-worst since inception: **llm_cascade_top1_paper** +9.50% (5 sessions, incepted 07-06), **residual_roa_6535_paper** +6.82% (since 05-01), **mom_v2_0701_paper** +4.67%, **mom_roa_6535_0701_paper** +4.36%, **spy_benchmark_paper** +4.31%, **mom_v1_0701_paper** +3.58%, **residual_roa_6535_0701_paper** +2.63%, **sector_top4_full_paper** +2.56%, **sector_top4_paper** +0.56%, **llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper** +0.19%, **mom_roa_6535_paper** +0.06%, **spy_benchmark_0701_paper** +0.06%, **llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper** 0.00% (flat, no position), **llm_cascade_sector4_paper** -0.21%, **mom_v2_paper** -1.72%, **mom_v1_paper** -1.75%, **mom_roa_top1_paper** -2.62%. 2026-07-10 (Friday)'s official NAV is **still coverage-PENDING** — unchanged from Friday's report, confirmed below. **Day move (07-08→07-09, last confirmed):** all 17 sleeves up, +0.00% to +5.04% (llm_cascade_top1_paper led on WDC). **Key movers/reason:** WDC (Wells Fargo PT raise to $730 from $575, dated on/before 07-10) continues to drive the cascade/top1 book; DOCN closed -7.49% on 07-10 (profit-taking after its 07-07 preannouncement rally, not new news); BE remains under a short-seller overhang (Hunterbrook report, 07-08) but held a Susquehanna PT raise to $298. **Macro event, new since Friday's report:** Iran's IRGC navy declared the Strait of Hormuz "closed until further notice" after attacking a Cyprus-flagged container ship (M/V GFS Galaxy) on 2026-07-11, triggering a third round of US CENTCOM strikes — a real escalation beyond what Friday's report captured. Brent ~$75.5/WTI ~$71.2 as of 07-10 close, both up on the week (+4.7%/+3.5%) but off intraday highs. **LLM overlay standings:** unchanged since 07-07 (last decision date) — stock overlay holds WDC (BUY, stop $480)/no BE (VETO); sector overlay/cascade hold XLK/XLI/XLB(+XLV for cascade), both excluding XLE (VETO'd 07-07) while the systematic control sleeves (`sector_top4_paper`, `sector_top4_full_paper`) do hold XLE — a live divergence now facing a real test given the weekend Hormuz escalation. **Critical outstanding items:** (1) 07-10 NAV remains officially PENDING (4,394 of ~5,241 baseline closes on 07-10, unchanged since Friday's 17:37 CDT `daily.bat` run — `TradingDailyMTM` did not fire today, correctly, since it's a non-trading Saturday; catch-up will occur on the next weekday run, Monday 07-13); (2) the Hormuz closure is a fast-moving, unresolved situation into Monday's open — watch XLE (held by control sector sleeves, vetoed by LLM overlay/cascade) and any energy-sensitive names; (3) EACO (residual_roa_6535_paper, $2,073 entry value, immaterial) has had no new close since 07-02, still carried flat at $101.01 — thin/illiquid micro-cap, not a data bug.

### 1. Market summary

No trading session occurred today (Saturday, 2026-07-11). The last completed session was **Friday, 2026-07-10**: Dow 30 closed 52,637.01 (+149.60, +0.29%), S&P 500 +0.4%, Nasdaq Composite 26,281.61 (+74.72, +0.29%) — a "choppy week" capped by SK Hynix's US listing debut (largest foreign US IPO on record) and ongoing monitoring of US-Iran ceasefire talks. [Yahoo Finance — Stock market today, Friday July 10](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/stock-market-today-friday-july-10-dow-sp-nasdaq-113921604.html)

Over the weekend, the security situation deteriorated further than what Friday's report captured. Iran's Revolutionary Guards navy declared the Strait of Hormuz "closed until further notice and until the end of US interference," after firing on and igniting a fire aboard a Cyprus-flagged container ship (M/V GFS Galaxy) it accused of taking an "unapproved" route. [Al Jazeera — IRGC navy declares Strait of Hormuz closed](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/7/11/iran-war-live-us-demands-iran-publicly-state-strait-of-hormuz-open-for-all) US Central Command launched a third round of strikes on Iran in response. [Times of Israel — US strikes Iran again, liveblog July 11](https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog-july-11-2026/) Iran and Oman discussed shipping-security mechanisms on Saturday without resolving disagreements over how the strait should be managed; Oman separately proposed toll-free passage. [RFE/RL — US insists Iran does not control Strait of Hormuz](https://www.rferl.org/a/iran-war-us-hormuz-oil-blockade-gulf-israel/33640284.html)

Oil: Brent slipped to ~$75.5/bbl and WTI to ~$71.2/bbl on Friday but both remained up on the week (+4.7% and +3.5% respectively) as shipping through Hormuz stayed well below normal levels and a risk premium persisted. [TradingEconomics — Brent crude oil](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil) Earlier in the week, WTI had spiked as high as +4.4% intraday to $73.52 and Brent +5.2% to $78.02 on the Trump administration's threat to bomb Iran and reimpose a naval blockade, before giving back some gains into Friday's close. [CNBC — Oil prices jump more than 4%](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/08/oil-prices-brent-wti-iran-us-hormuz.html)

Looking to next week: bank earnings (Citi, Goldman Sachs, Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, Bank of America Tuesday; BlackRock and Morgan Stanley Wednesday) and semiconductor-equipment earnings (ASML, TSMC) are the next scheduled catalysts, alongside continued Fed-path uncertainty (10Y yield approaching levels that could reprice rate-cut odds; futures currently price a 61% chance of a September cut per CME FedWatch). [CNBC — Stock market next week: Outlook for July 13-17](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/stock-market-next-week-outlook-for-july-13-17-2026.html)

### 3. Key position analysis

**Movers (last confirmed session, 07-08→07-09, and 07-10 informal per Friday's report):** WDC remains the standout — Wells Fargo raised its price target to $730 from $575 (a further increase from the $700 level cited in Thursday's decisions), with Susquehanna separately at $500 (Neutral). [Yahoo Finance — WDC fair-value boost](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/western-digital-wdc-stock-gets-153104306.html) The name is carried by `llm_cascade_top1_paper` (BUY, stop $560 per the 07-01 cascade pick) and remains the best single driver of that sleeve's +9.50% since-inception lead. DOCN closed 07-10 down 7.49%, following its 07-07 preannouncement pop (+3.5% on 07-08) — the pullback reads as profit-taking/execution-cost concern after the initial rally, not a new negative catalyst. [TradingKey — DOCN closed down 7.49% July 10](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262023622-market-movers-docn-20260710) BE remains volatile: Hunterbrook Media's 07-08 short report (questioning supply chain/production targets) drove an 11% one-day drop; Susquehanna raised its PT to $298 despite the report. [GuruFocus — BE shares plunge 11% following short report](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8950035/bloom-energy-be-shares-plunge-11-following-short-report) BE is not currently held by any sleeve (VETO'd both 07-01 and 07-07 by the stock overlay).

**Approaching stop/target:** No new information since Friday's report — no official 07-10 price data exists to check against invalidation levels. WDC's cascade stop is $560 (07-01 pick) / $480 (07-07 stock-overlay BUY); both are well below the ~$560-585 trading range WDC occupied on 07-10, so no proximity concern as of the last confirmed prices.

**New developments:** The Iran/Hormuz escalation (Section 1) is the one genuinely new item since Friday's report closed. It bears directly on the sector-ETF sleeves: `sector_top4_paper` and `sector_top4_full_paper` (systematic control) hold XLE; the LLM sector-overlay and cascade sleeves vetoed XLE on 07-07 (rationale: below 50-DMA, RSI 38, weak 1m/3m momentum, EIA demand-forecast cut) and hold XLK/XLI/XLB (+XLV for cascade) instead. This is now a live, dated test of that veto call against a real, escalating geopolitical shock to the exact sector it excluded — worth tracking explicitly into next week rather than reading anything into it yet, since energy-equity reaction to a supply shock is not mechanically identical to the crude-price reaction.

**EACO** (residual_roa_6535_paper, $2,073 entry value at 07-01, ~2% of the sleeve): no new close since 2026-07-02, still carried flat at $101.01/share. This is the same NOCLOSE anomaly flagged by the 07-10 daily.bat anomaly scan — consistent with a thin/illiquid micro-cap that trades sporadically, not a new data-quality problem; immaterial to sleeve-level NAV.

### 4. LLM overlay treatment vs. control

No new overlay decisions have been logged since 2026-07-07 (last `llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` entries), consistent with no rebalance trigger between 07-07 and today. Standings, unchanged from Friday's report:

- **Stock overlay** (`llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` vs. control `mom_roa_top1_paper`): treatment holds WDC (BUY 07-01, score 7, stop $560 / re-affirmed 07-07 at stop $480), control holds BE (currently down on the short-seller report). Cascade variant (`llm_cascade_top1_paper`) also holds WDC and is the top-performing sleeve in the entire 17-sleeve roster at +9.50% since 07-06.
- **Sector overlay** (`llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` vs. control `sector_top4_paper`, plus `llm_cascade_sector4_paper`): both overlay variants vetoed XLE on 07-07 and hold XLK/XLI/XLB (cascade adds XLV); control holds the systematic top-4 including XLE. Overlay currently ahead of its direct control on a since-inception basis (+0.19% vs. +0.56% — actually behind on the raw number as of 07-09; note the control is marginally ahead here, the opposite of the stock-overlay case). Cascade sector variant is at -0.21%, the weakest LLM-experiment sleeve.

One name (WDC) driving both winning overlay variants over a 4-5 trading-day sample remains a small, noisy base per the standing kill-switch criteria (12mo/30 picks) — this is unchanged guidance from prior reports, restated for continuity.

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

No new evidence either direction since Friday's report; today's status check surfaces one dated, trackable setup rather than a new finding:

**A. The XLE control-vs-veto divergence is now a live, real-world test, not a hypothetical.** The 07-07 sector-overlay veto of XLE was made on valuation/momentum grounds (below 50-DMA, RSI 38, EIA demand cut, negative 1m/3m) explicitly *despite* acknowledging "near-term supply cuts are a fragile geopolitical prop that could reverse fast." That prop is being tested directly by this weekend's Hormuz closure and third round of US strikes — a genuine escalation, not the on-again-off-again ceasefire noise of the prior week. If energy equities rally hard on the supply shock Monday, the systematic control sleeves (holding XLE) would benefit while the LLM-overlay sleeves (which excluded it) would not — a clean, dated comparison to watch, not a conclusion to draw yet.

**B. The diversification-vs-concentration pattern (flagged in the 07-08 through 07-10 reports) has no new data point today** — no trading occurred. The pattern's next real test is Monday 07-13.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed.** The weekend's dominant news (Hormuz escalation, bank/semi earnings ahead) is exogenous macro/geopolitical risk already within the scope of existing sector and momentum sleeves' natural exposure — nothing here suggests a new systematic signal distinct from what's already tracked.

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **NAV standings (official, 2026-07-10 close, since-inception):** `llm_cascade_top1_paper` **+10.36%** ($110,360, WDC) · `residual_roa_6535_paper` +6.28% ($106,226) · `spy_benchmark_paper` +4.76% · `sector_top4_full_paper` +3.16% · `residual_roa_6535_0701_paper` +2.24% · `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper` +1.87% · `mom_v2_0701_paper` +1.75% · `mom_v1_0701_paper` +1.23% · `sector_top4_paper` +1.15% · `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` +0.67% · `spy_benchmark_0701_paper` +0.49% · `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` +0.07% · `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` 0.00% (cash, BE vetoed) · `mom_roa_6535_paper` -1.60% · `mom_v1_paper` -3.80% · `mom_v2_paper` -4.04% · `mom_roa_top1_paper` **-7.32%** ($92,683, BE). This is the official, `verify_run`-confirmed (17/17 PASS) mark for 07-10 — the coverage gate that was PENDING as of Saturday's report cleared over the weekend (5,241/5,241 baseline closes settled), no manual intervention needed.
> **Pre-market moves (2026-07-13):** SK Hynix — whose Nasdaq ADR debut drove last Friday's chip rally — reversed violently in Seoul trading, **-15.4%, its largest single-day drop ever**, on fears its HBM4 shipment ramp is behind expectations; this halted the KOSPI (-9%, 20-min circuit breaker) and dragged European chip names (ASML, ASMI, BESI) down 1-2% and US futures lower pre-market: **Nasdaq futures -0.95%, S&P 500 futures -0.29%, Dow futures roughly flat**, with specific weakness flagged in memory names (MU, SNDK, SKHY). **WDC — the single largest concentrated position in the entire 17-sleeve roster (`llm_cascade_top1_paper`'s sole holding) — is down ~5.4% pre-market to ~$551**, which sits below the **$560 invalidation level** set on the sleeve's original 2026-07-01 cascade pick (though **the cascade sleeves have no automated stop-loss mechanism** — see §5). Oil is up sharply on renewed Hormuz disruption: **Brent ~$78-79/bbl (+2.8-4%), WTI ~$73-74/bbl (+2.4%)**.
> **Macro catalyst:** The Iran/Strait-of-Hormuz conflict escalated further over the weekend, not resolved — the US and Iran traded fresh strikes into Monday morning (US using one-way attack drones for the first time), Iran hit US-linked bases in Kuwait/Bahrain/Jordan/Qatar/Oman, and CENTCOM and Iran's strait authority give **conflicting claims on whether the Strait is even open**. This week's calendar is unusually heavy: **June CPI/PPI print Tuesday 07-14** (CPI forecast +3.8% YoY, PPI +6.2% YoY, both decelerating from May), **JPMorgan/Goldman Sachs/Citigroup/Wells Fargo/Bank of America earnings Tuesday**, **Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the House Financial Services Committee Tuesday and Wednesday**, **Morgan Stanley/BlackRock/ASML Wednesday**, **TSMC/Netflix + jobless claims/retail sales/Philly Fed Thursday**. **Flagged discrepancy, not resolved:** last Saturday's report cited a ~61% September rate-**cut** probability (CNBC, 07-10); today's research instead surfaces that the Fed, under new Chair Kevin Warsh (confirmed mid-June 2026, replacing Powell), turned hawkish and CME FedWatch had priced **60-67% odds of a September rate-hike** as of Warsh's 06-18 press conference. These two claims are contradictory and this report cannot reconcile them from today's research pass alone — see §3.
> **LLM overlay status:** No new decisions logged since 2026-07-07 (checked both logs through today). Stock arm unchanged: cascade (WDC) remains the best-performing sleeve in the entire roster; overlay treatment stays in cash (BE vetoed); control holds BE (-7.32%, still under a short-seller overhang). Sector arm unchanged: control holds XLE, overlay/cascade excluded it 07-07 — this is now a **live, sharpening test**, since Monday brings both a real Hormuz-driven oil spike (favors the control's XLE) and a memory-chip selloff (weighs on both sides' XLK exposure).
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) WDC pre-market print sits below the cascade's original $560 stop level, but **no automated invalidation check exists for either cascade sleeve** (`llm_cascade_top1_paper`/`llm_cascade_sector4_paper` — confirmed via code search, only the overlay sleeves' `check-invalidation` runs in `daily.bat`); this is a design gap worth a decision from Evan, not something this report should act on. (2) BE's earnings date is now **company-confirmed as 2026-07-28** (BusinessWire/Bloom IR press release), resolving the prior 07-28-vs-07-30 ambiguity. (3) The Fed-chair/rate-direction discrepancy above needs reconciling before citing either figure again. (4) EACO (residual_roa_6535_paper) remains stale, no new close since 07-02/07-06 depending on source — immaterial, thin micro-cap, not a new issue.

### 2. Overnight / pre-market moves (2026-07-13, before the open — no intraday data exists yet)

**Semiconductor/memory complex — the dominant pre-market theme, and a direct reversal of last Thursday's chip rally.** SK Hynix, whose Nasdaq ADR debut on 07-10 raised $26.5B (the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company) and closed +12.8% that day, cratered **-15.4% in Seoul trading Monday — its largest single-day drop in company history** — on fears that HBM4 shipment volumes are ramping slower than the market had priced in. The KOSPI fell 9% and was halted for 20 minutes; European chip-equipment names (ASML, ASMI, BESI) were down 1-2% by pre-market. [CNBC — SK Hynix shares fall after stellar Nasdaq debut](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/13/sk-hynix-shares-fall-after-stellar-nasdaq-debut.html), [Bloomberg — SK Hynix shares drop in Seoul after much-hyped US trading debut](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-13/sk-hynix-shares-drop-in-seoul-after-much-hyped-us-trading-debut), [Yahoo Finance — SK Hynix record plunge drags](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/ai/articles/sk-hynix-record-plunge-drags-091841687.html). US index futures are lower pre-market as a result: **Nasdaq -0.95%, S&P 500 -0.29%, Dow roughly flat**, with memory names (MU, SNDK, SKHY) called out specifically. [Yahoo Finance — Stock market today, Monday July 13](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-monday-july-13-dow-sp-nasdaq-113249278.html)

**WDC (Western Digital) — the portfolio's single largest concentrated position — is down ~5.4% pre-market to ~$551**, caught in the same memory-sector selloff. [Yahoo Finance — Western Digital shares fall amid global chip selloff](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/western-digital-shares-fall-global-122257559.html) One source describes WDC posting strong results and trading $560-585 (+8.1%) on **"July 12"** — this date lands on a Sunday and is almost certainly a reporting/dating error in the source (no exchange trades or reports earnings on a weekend); the underlying move may belong to 07-10 or an after-hours release, but this report cannot confirm which and flags it as unresolved rather than asserting a corrected date. [Simply Wall St — Western Digital (WDC) is up 8.1% after strong Q3 results and AI](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/tech/nasdaq-wdc/western-digital/news/western-digital-wdc-is-up-81-after-strong-q3-results-and-ai). No news found for WDC beyond the already-known Wells Fargo $730 PT (07-10).

**Oil — climbing again on the Hormuz re-escalation.** Brent is trading **$78-79/bbl (+2.8-4%)**, WTI **~$73-74/bbl (+2.4%)** pre-market Monday, as the Strait of Hormuz situation remains unresolved (see §3). [CNBC — US-Iran war, Hormuz, oil](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/13/us-iran-war-hormuz-oil-trump.html), [Al Jazeera — Oil prices jump as US and Iran trade attacks over Strait of Hormuz](https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/7/13/oil-prices-jump-as-us-and-iran-trade-attacks-over-strait-of-hormuz), [The National — Oil jumps amid fears of prolonged disruption in Strait of Hormuz](https://www.thenationalnews.com/business/energy/2026/07/13/oil-jumps-amid-fears-of-prolonged-disruption-in-strait-of-hormuz/). No confirmed pre-market print was found for XLE itself (its 07-10 official close was $55.08, +0.47% day) — flagged as not found rather than estimated.

**Other names:** No ticker-specific news dated 07-11 through 07-13 was found for AAOI, CIEN, TER, MRVL, LRCX, ALAB, APLD, STX, COHR, BAND, or AMKR — all are presumed exposed to the broad memory-sector selloff above but nothing name-specific surfaced. Intel/AMD remain in the rout that predates this weekend (INTC -21% on the week of 07-08 on 18A-yield-delay reports; AMD -5-8% same window) with no new 07-11–07-13 item found beyond that continuation. [Phemex — Intel stock crash, 18A delay, AMD data-center surge](https://phemex.com/blogs/intel-stock-crash-18a-delay-amd-data-center-surge)

### 3. Catalyst and macro review

**Iran / Strait of Hormuz — escalated further over the weekend, not resolved.** After Saturday's report described the Strait as declared "closed until further notice," the situation worsened: the US struck roughly 140 Iranian military targets overnight 07-11→07-12, followed by a further wave beginning ~5pm ET 07-12 hitting air-defense systems and IRGC small boats near Hormuz; Iran retaliated against US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan, Oman, and Qatar, and a security guard was killed by a projectile strike on a water-pumping station in Mahshahr County, Iran. [Bloomberg — US says it began launching more strikes against Iran at 5pm ET](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-12/us-says-it-began-launching-more-strikes-against-iran-at-5pm-et), [Al Jazeera — US launches new wave of strikes against Iran aimed at degrading military](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/12/us-launches-new-wave-of-strikes-against-iran-aimed-at-degrading-military). Into Monday, both sides traded fresh strikes — the US using one-way attack drones for the first time — while Trump/CENTCOM claim the Strait is open and Iran's strait authority says passage is "not possible"; Iran's Foreign Ministry called the strikes a "serious threat to international peace and security." [CNBC — US-Iran war, Hormuz, oil, Trump](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/13/us-iran-war-hormuz-oil-trump.html), [Bloomberg — US and Iran trade fresh strikes, dispute whether Hormuz is open](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-13/us-and-iran-trade-fresh-strikes-dispute-whether-hormuz-is-open). Only ~14 vessels (half Iranian-flagged) were transiting Hormuz as of last weekend, well below normal. [The National — Is the US-Iran ceasefire over? A timeline](https://www.thenationalnews.com/news/2026/07/09/is-us-iran-ceasefire-over-a-timeline-of-how-agreement-has-come-to-brink-of-collapse/)

**Fed leadership / rate path — a flagged discrepancy, not resolved.** Saturday's report (sourced to a CNBC 07-10 "week ahead" piece) cited "futures currently price a 61% chance of a September cut per CME FedWatch." Today's research instead surfaces that the Fed is now chaired by **Kevin Warsh**, confirmed mid-June 2026 as Jerome Powell's replacement, and turned hawkish: the Fed held rates 06-17 but signaled hikes ahead, and CME FedWatch reportedly priced **60-67% odds of a September rate hike** (not cut) after Warsh's 06-18 press conference, up from 27-49% pre-meeting. [CNBC — Markets are set for a much more hawkish Warsh Fed than expected](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/18/markets-are-set-for-a-much-more-hawkish-warsh-fed-than-expected.html), [CNN — Federal Reserve interest rate, Kevin Warsh](https://www.cnn.com/2026/06/17/business/live-news/federal-reserve-interest-rate-kevin-warsh). No current (07-13) rate-cut probability was found in this research pass. **These two claims — a 61% cut odds and a 60-67% hike odds — are contradictory and this report does not attempt to resolve which is stale or in error; flagged for Evan to reconcile before either is cited again.** Separately confirmed: 10-year Treasury yield was 4.56-4.57% at Friday 07-10 close. [Advisor Perspectives — Treasury yields snapshot, July 10, 2026](https://www.advisorperspectives.com/dshort/updates/2026/07/10/treasury-yields-snapshot-july-10-2026)

**This week's calendar is unusually dense.** Tuesday 07-14: June CPI (forecast +3.8% YoY, decelerating from May's 4.2%) and PPI (forecast +6.2% YoY, decelerating from May's 6.5%) both release 8:30am ET; JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, and Bank of America report earnings; Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the House Financial Services Committee, 10am ET (day one of two). Wednesday 07-15: Morgan Stanley, BlackRock, and ASML report. Thursday 07-16: TSMC and Netflix report, alongside initial jobless claims, June retail sales, and the Philly Fed manufacturing index (all 8:30am ET). JNJ and UnitedHealth also report this week (exact day not specified in the source). [CNBC — Stock market next week: Outlook for July 13-17, 2026](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/stock-market-next-week-outlook-for-july-13-17-2026.html)

No new overlay/cascade decisions have been logged since 2026-07-07 (`llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` checked directly through 07-13; none found — consistent with no rebalance trigger before 08-01).

**Stock arm:** Control `mom_roa_top1_paper` holds BE, now **-7.32%** since 07-06 cohort inception (official 07-10 mark) — the short-seller overhang plus Friday's fuel-cell-sector-wide unwind (FuelCell Energy -11%, Plug Power -6%, BE ~-8% same session) has erased essentially the entire cohort gain. [24/7 Wall St — FuelCell Energy drops 11%, Bloom Energy slides 8%, Plug Power falls 6% as fuel-cell stocks unwind massive 2026 gains](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/10/fuelcell-energy-drops-11-bloom-energy-slides-8-plug-power-falls-6-as-fuel-cell-stocks-unwind-massive-2026-gains/) No BE-specific news was found dated 07-11 through 07-13 pre-market — the weekend was quiet on this name specifically. Cascade `llm_cascade_top1_paper` holds WDC, still the best-performing sleeve in the entire 17-sleeve roster at +10.36% — but see §2/§5 on this morning's pre-market weakness. Standing since 07-06: **cascade (+10.36%) > cash treatment (0.00%) > BE control (-7.32%)** — unchanged ranking, gap continuing to widen in the overlay's favor.

**Sector arm:** Control `sector_top4_paper` (XLE/XLK/XLI/XLB) +1.15% since inception; overlay treatment (ex-XLE) +0.67%; cascade (XLV substituted) +0.07%. The control still leads both LLM variants cumulatively. This is now the sharpest version yet of the live test flagged since 07-07: Monday brings a genuine Hormuz-driven oil spike (favors the control's XLE holding) landing on the same morning as a memory-chip-driven tech selloff (both sides hold XLK, so this cuts evenly rather than favoring either side). Kill-switch status unchanged on both arms: 12-month/≥30-pick window, not yet triggered.

### 5. Risk flags and upcoming events

- **WDC pre-market weakness below a stated stop level, with no automated enforcement.** WDC (~$551 pre-market) sits below the **$560** invalidation level set on `llm_cascade_top1_paper`'s original 07-01 pick (though above the separate **$480** level set on the stock-overlay's 07-07 re-affirmation). Code search of `scripts/momentum/daily.bat` confirms only `llm_overlay_ops check-invalidation` and `sector_overlay_ops check-invalidation` run automatically — there is **no equivalent script or log entry for either cascade sleeve** (`llm_cascade_top1_paper`/`llm_cascade_sector4_paper`); grepping `scripts/momentum/` for "cascade" returns no matches. This means WDC could trade well below its originally-stated stop with no automatic exit — an operational gap worth a decision from Evan (manual review vs. building a cascade stop-check), not something this report should act on unilaterally.
- **Iran/Hormuz remains the dominant unhedged macro risk**, now in its second week of escalation with no resolution and conflicting claims about whether the strait is passable at all (§3). This is the same risk flagged in each of the last four reports; nothing here suggests it has abated.
- **Fed-chair/rate-direction discrepancy (§3)** needs reconciling before either the "cut" or "hike" framing is used again in this record.
- **BE earnings date resolved**: company-confirmed 2026-07-28 after market close, per Bloom Energy's own investor-relations press release — the prior 07-28-vs-07-30 ambiguity noted in earlier reports is closed. [Bloom Energy IR — Bloom Energy to Announce Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results on July 28, 2026](https://investor.bloomenergy.com/press-releases/press-release-details/2026/Bloom-Energy-to-Announce-Second-Quarter-2026-Financial-Results-on-July-28,-2026/default.aspx)
- **AEHR earnings Tuesday 07-14 after market close** (FY26 Q4/full-year, conference call 5pm ET; estimates EPS -$0.02, revenue $18.69M, three consecutive quarters of EPS beats) — held in 6 sleeves. [Aehr Test Systems — press release](https://www.aehr.com/2026/07/aehr-test-systems-to-announce-fiscal-2026-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-financial-results-on-july-14-2026/)
- **DOCN concentration**: held across all 8 momentum-family sleeves; its 07-10 close of -7.49%/day is confirmed mechanical (Russell 2000→1000 index-reclassification forced selling), not a fundamentals problem — next earnings confirmed 2026-08-12.
- **EACO** (residual_roa_6535_paper) remains flat/stale in `price_cache` across 07-08 through 07-10 (~$101.01 each day) — thin OTC micro-cap, immaterial to sleeve NAV, unresolved but not escalating further.
- **No intraday circuit-breaker** exists for any sleeve (accepted, previously-reviewed design gap).
- **Monthly rebalance** next fires 2026-08-01 per the `monthy-llm-rebalance` scheduled task; `rebalance_log.md` confirms last rebalance 07-01, no activity since.

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. The diversification-vs-concentration pattern (flagged 07-08 through 07-11) faces its first real stress test this week, not just a calm-day confirmation.** Prior reports noted diversified sleeves (`spy_benchmark`, `sector_top4_full`, `residual_roa_6535`) beating the concentrated momentum book on both volatile and calm sessions. This week adds a genuinely new stressor — a sector-specific memory-chip selloff (SK Hynix) layered on top of the ongoing Hormuz/oil shock — hitting the momentum book's heaviest concentration (WDC/MU/semiconductor names) directly. If the pattern holds through a week with two simultaneous, unrelated shocks rather than one, that would be somewhat stronger evidence than anything gathered so far; if it breaks, that's informative too. No conclusion drawn today — Monday's close is the first real data point.

**B. The sector-arm LLM veto's XLE exclusion is being tested by conflicting forces simultaneously, which complicates rather than clarifies the read.** The 07-07 veto rationale explicitly worried the Hormuz oil premium was "fragile" and could reverse — instead it has intensified for a second consecutive week. Ordinarily that would be expected to favor the control (XLE holder) this week. But the same morning brings a memory-chip-driven tech selloff that both the control and overlay/cascade sleeves are equally exposed to (both hold XLK). The net effect on the sector-arm comparison specifically is not something this report can predict — worth checking directly once 07-13 closes.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** The week's news (Hormuz re-escalation, SK Hynix's reversal, a Fed-chair discrepancy needing reconciliation, and a genuinely heavy earnings/data calendar) is exogenous macro/company-specific risk already within the scope of existing sleeve mechanisms — nothing here suggests a new systematic signal distinct from what's already tracked. The more actionable finding today is operational: the WDC/cascade stop-enforcement gap in §5, which is a process question for Evan rather than a research finding about markets.

> **DAILY SUMMARY** — since-inception ranking (self-computed 07-13 mark, see note below; best→worst): `llm_cascade_top1_paper` **+5.24%** ($105,243, WDC) · `residual_roa_6535_paper` +5.18% ($105,180) · `spy_benchmark_paper` +3.96% ($103,958) · `sector_top4_full_paper` +2.74% ($102,743) · `residual_roa_6535_0701_paper` +1.22% · `sector_top4_paper` +0.93% · `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` 0.00% (cash) · `spy_benchmark_0701_paper` -0.28% · `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` -0.32% · `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` -0.84% · `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper` -2.61% · `mom_v1_0701_paper` -3.01% · `mom_v2_0701_paper` -3.23% · `mom_roa_6535_paper` -5.88% · `mom_v1_paper` -7.81% · `mom_v2_paper` -8.17% · `mom_roa_top1_paper` **-11.53%** ($88,474, BE). **Day moves (07-10→07-13, all sleeves down):** momentum-family sleeves -4.1% to -4.9% (worst), sector/diversified sleeves -0.2% to -1.0% (best), cascade-top1 (WDC) -4.64%.
> **Market:** S&P 500 -0.79% to 7,515.34, Nasdaq -1.55% to 25,873.18, Dow -0.26% (-138pts) to 52,498.64 — energy cushioned the Dow while tech/semis led losses.
> **Key movers:** ALAB -12.3% (insider selling + index-rebalance profit-taking, semis weak), SK Hynix -15% in Seoul (the proximate trigger, "Parabolic 7" chip-basket unwind), MRVL -6.3%, MU -4.9-5.8%, INTC -6.1%, LRCX -5.8%, WDC -4.6% (concentrated in `llm_cascade_top1_paper`, now ~$555.55, still below its $560 cascade-pick level — no automated stop exists, see §5); on the winning side XLE +3.0%, XOM +2.8-4.1%, VLO +5.1%, PARR +7.8%, DINO +4.6% on the Hormuz oil spike.
> **Macro event:** Trump reinstated an Iranian-shipping "blockade" through the Strait of Hormuz Monday morning, sending Brent to ~$78.86 (+3.8%) and WTI to ~$74.36 (+4.1%); this is the proximate cause of the energy/tech divergence today.
> **LLM overlay:** No new decisions (last: 07-07). Stock arm: cascade (WDC, +5.24% since-inception) still beats cash-treatment (0.00%) beats BE-control (-11.53%) — gap widened further as BE dropped another day, unrelated to WDC's weakness. Sector arm: control (XLE-holding, +0.93%) now clearly ahead of both overlay (-0.32%) and cascade (-0.84%) — the first day this week's setup (Hormuz-favors-control vs. chip-selloff-hits-both-equally) resolved cleanly in the control's favor, since XLE's oil-driven gain wasn't offset by any XLK differential (both sides hold it).
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) Official `paper_nav` mark for 07-13 is **not yet posted** — coverage 4,386/5,000-floor tickers, `mtm_catchup` left it PENDING (heals later, same pattern as 07-10); all "today" NAV figures in this report are self-computed from `paper_positions` × `price_cache` closes, not verify-confirmed. (2) WDC closed ~$555.55, still under the cascade's $560 stop with no automated enforcement (unresolved from this morning's report). (3) EACO/KFII stale again (no 07-13 close, thin micro-caps, immaterial, previously tracked).

### 1. Market summary

The S&P 500 fell 0.79% to 7,515.34, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 1.55% to 25,873.18, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.26% (-138.37 pts) to 52,498.64 — the Dow's smaller decline reflects its lower tech weighting and exposure to rallying energy names. [Yahoo Finance — Stock market today, Monday July 13](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-monday-07-13-dow-sp-nasdaq-113249278.html), [Motley Fool — Energy Stocks Cushion Dow as Tech Slides on Geopolitical Tensions](https://www.fool.com/coverage/stock-market-today/2026/07/13/stock-market-today-july-13-energy-stocks-cushion-dow-as-tech-slides-on-geopolitical-tensions/)

**Sector performance:** Energy was the standout sector, boosted by the Hormuz-driven oil spike — XLE (portfolio holding) closed **+3.01%** at $56.74 (prior close $55.08). Technology/semiconductors were the weakest sector — XLK (portfolio holding) closed **-2.42%** at $181.28 (prior $185.78), dragged by a "Parabolic 7" chip-basket unwind (SanDisk/Marvell/Micron/Intel/Dell/AMD/Broadcom — the basket that had vastly outperformed the Magnificent 7 and SOX index since mid-2025) triggered by SK Hynix's historic -15% Seoul plunge. [24/7 Wall St — Intel, AMD, and Applied Materials Drop 4% as SK Hynix Rout and Oil Spike Hit Chip Stocks](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/13/intel-amd-and-applied-materials-drop-4-as-sk-hynix-rout-and-oil-spike-hit-chip-stocks/), [Yahoo Finance — Micron, SanDisk, and Marvell Plummet as "Parabolic 7" Trade Unwinds](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/micron-sandisk-marvell-plummet-parabolic-145117645.html) Industrials (XLI, portfolio holding) -0.85% to $180.37, Materials (XLB, portfolio holding) -0.61% to $50.58, Healthcare (XLV, portfolio holding) +0.35% to $161.41 — the only other portfolio-held sector ETF to close green, consistent with the defensive/GLP-1 rotation flagged 07-07.

The macro driver was explicit and singular: President Trump announced Monday morning he was reinstating what he called a blockade on Iranian shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, escalating a conflict that had already run through the weekend, and sending oil sharply higher while risk sentiment (especially in already-extended AI/chip names) soured. [Yahoo Finance — Stock market today, Monday July 13: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as chip stocks tumble, oil surges on US-Iran tensions](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-monday-july-13-dow-sp-nasdaq-113249278.html)

### 3. Key position analysis

**Chip/memory complex — the day's dominant theme, hitting momentum-family sleeves hardest.** SK Hynix's historic -15.4% Seoul plunge (largest single-day drop in its history, on an 8%-below-consensus Q2 profit estimate from South Korean brokerage KIS citing slow HBM4 ramp) triggered a broad unwind of the "Parabolic 7" basket (SanDisk/Marvell/Micron/Intel/Dell/AMD/Broadcom) that had outperformed the Magnificent 7 all year. [Yahoo Finance — Micron, SanDisk, and Marvell Plummet as "Parabolic 7" Trade Unwinds](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/micron-sandisk-marvell-plummet-parabolic-145117645.html) Within the portfolio: **ALAB -12.3%** to $362.05 (insider Form-4 liquidations plus index-rebalance profit-taking compounding the sector move — still +113% YTD despite today's drop) [Benzinga — Why Is Astera Labs Stock Falling Monday?](https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60422589/why-is-astera-labs-stock-falling-monday); **MRVL -6.3%** to $217.53 (despite Marvell having just raised FY27/FY28 guidance on record Q1 revenue) [TradingKey — Marvell Technology Inc Stock (MRVL) Moved Down by 6.26% on Jul 13](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262026850-market-movers-mrvl-20260713); **MU -4.3 to -5.8%** (sources vary) to $937.00, testing the $900 support zone on weakening memory-pricing-power concerns despite Micron's own $18.7B FQ2 revenue guide [FX Leaders — MU Stock Tests the $900 Zone Again](https://www.fxleaders.com/news/2026/07/13/mu-stock-tests-the-900-zone-again-as-rising-supply-and-margin-risks-weigh-on-micron/); **INTC -6.1%** to $103.12, **LRCX -5.8%** to $329.92, **AMAT -4.5%** to $575.39, **STX -5.5%** to $860.66, **AMD -4.2%** to $534.39 — all part of the same complex.

**WDC (Western Digital) — `llm_cascade_top1_paper`'s sole holding, the single largest concentrated position in the roster — closed -4.6% at $555.55**, caught in the same memory selloff despite carrying a Buy rating and $570.44 average analyst price target (48 analysts). [TradingKey/Benzinga — Western Digital Stock Drops: SK Hynix Reversal Is Shaking Up Chip Sector](https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60418479/western-digital-stock-drops-sk-hynix-reversal-is-shaking-up-chip-sector) This is the second consecutive close below the sleeve's original **$560** invalidation level (pre-market was ~$551 this morning; official close $555.55 recovered slightly but remains below it). WDC still sits well above its 200-day ($304.22) and 100-day ($430.19) moving averages — this is a pullback within an intact uptrend, not a trend break, but **no automated stop-check exists for either cascade sleeve** (confirmed via code search this morning — only the overlay sleeves' `check-invalidation` runs in `daily.bat`). This remains an operational gap for Evan to decide on, not something acted on here.

**BE (Bloom Energy) — control sleeve `mom_roa_top1_paper`'s sole holding — closed -4.5% at $233.49**, extending the short-seller-driven slide (Hunterbrook Media's 07-08 scandium/China-dependency report, which BE rejected in an SEC filing) that has now cost the control sleeve over 17% in the past week. Wall Street is split: UBS reiterated Buy calling the pullback "an attractive buy opportunity," and Susquehanna raised its price target to $298 from $293 (Positive), while the stock remains down from its highs. [Yahoo Finance — Bloom Energy's Biggest Selloff In Months Has Created A Wall Street Divide](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/bloom-energy-biggest-selloff-months-061829587.html), [MarketBeat — Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) Shares Gap Down](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/bloom-energy-nysebe-shares-gap-down-heres-why-2026-07-13/) No new company-specific catalyst dated 07-13 beyond continued digestion of last week's short reports; earnings remain confirmed for 2026-07-28.

**DOCN (held across all 8 momentum-family sleeves) — no new 07-13 news found; the operative catalyst remains the 07-07 Q2 pre-announcement** (29% YoY revenue growth guide, RPO jumping >$550M to $800M+ on AI-inference contract wins), which drove Citi/Barclays price-target hikes to $185/$184. [GuruFocus — DigitalOcean (DOCN) Anticipates 29% Revenue Growth in Q2](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8947660/digitalocean-docn-anticipates-29-revenue-growth-in-q2-shares-rise-10) DOCN's day move was not independently confirmed against the broader chip-selloff tape in this pass — treated as in line with the tech-sector decline absent contrary evidence.

**Energy complex — the one bright spot, driven entirely by the Hormuz oil spike.** XOM +2.8-4.1% (higher crude adding an estimated $3.5-3.9B to Q2 upstream earnings vs. Q1) [StocksToTrade — XOM Stock Climbs As Oil Windfall Lifts Q2 Earnings Outlook](https://stockstotrade.com/news/exxonmobil-holdings-corporation-xom-news-2026_07_13/); VLO +5.1-5.4% to a new 52-week high (Jefferies raised its PT to $312 citing a likely Q2 EPS beat and a $2.4B buyback; Barclays/Goldman also raised targets on tight US refining capacity) [MarketBeat — Valero Energy (NYSE:VLO) Sets New 52-Week High](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/valero-energy-nysevlo-sets-new-52-week-high-heres-what-happened-2026-07-13/); DINO +4.6-4.7%, PARR +7.8%. None of these are directly held (XLE is the portfolio's energy vehicle, held in the three sector sleeves), but they confirm the sector-level move driving XLE's +3.0% close.

**Nothing approaching a stop or target today** beyond the already-flagged WDC/cascade situation above — no held position crossed a documented invalidation level for the first time today.

### 4. LLM overlay treatment vs. control

**Stock arm:** Cascade (`llm_cascade_top1_paper`, WDC) +5.24% since inception vs. cash-treatment (`llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper`, BE VETO'd 07-07) 0.00% vs. control (`mom_roa_top1_paper`, BE) **-11.53%**. The gap widened again today — control lost another 4.54% on BE's continued short-seller overhang while cascade's WDC loss (-4.64%) was driven by an unrelated sector rotation (memory chips), not a company-specific problem. Standing unchanged: cascade > cash > BE-control, now the widest gap yet recorded in this comparison.

**Sector arm:** Control (`sector_top4_paper`, XLE/XLK/XLI/XLB) +0.93% vs. overlay (`llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper`, ex-XLE) -0.32% vs. cascade (`llm_cascade_sector4_paper`, XLV substituted for XLE) -0.84%. **This is the first day since the 07-07 XLE veto that the comparison resolved cleanly**: the Hormuz oil spike pushed XLE +3.01% while XLK's -2.42% chip-driven decline hit both the control and the ex-XLE variants equally (both hold XLK) — so today the control's XLE exposure was pure upside the other two arms didn't get, and the veto looks costly on this one session. One session is not a trend; the kill-switch (12-month/≥30-pick window) remains untriggered on both arms.

### 5. Risk flags and upcoming events

- **WDC below stated stop, no automated enforcement** — unchanged from this morning; closed $555.55, under the cascade's $560 level, with no cascade-sleeve stop-check in `daily.bat`. Two consecutive sessions below the level now (pre-market Monday, and now the official close).
- **07-13 official NAV mark PENDING** — universe coverage 4,386/5,000 floor; expect this to heal within 1-2 days per the 07-10 precedent. Tomorrow's report should confirm whether it cleared.
- **Heavy data/earnings week continues**: June CPI/PPI Tuesday 07-14 (8:30am ET), JPMorgan/Goldman/Citi/Wells Fargo/BofA earnings Tuesday, Fed Chair Warsh testifies before House Financial Services Tuesday-Wednesday, AEHR earnings Tuesday after close (held in 6 sleeves).
- **BE earnings confirmed 2026-07-28** (unchanged).
- **EACO/KFII stale again** (residual_roa_6535_paper) — no 07-13 close cached for either; immaterial, previously tracked, not escalating.
- **Iran/Hormuz remains the dominant macro risk**, now actively driving both sides of today's portfolio dispersion (energy gains, chip losses) rather than a background worry.

### 6. Strategic insights

**A. The sector-arm XLE veto cost the overlay/cascade sleeves real, measurable return today — the cleanest single-session read this comparison has produced.** The 07-07 decision to exclude XLE (on valuation/technical grounds, while explicitly flagging the oil-premium risk as "fragile") is now down a combined ~0.3-0.8pp against the control on this one session alone, driven entirely by the Hormuz-shipping-blockade announcement. This is exactly the risk the veto's own rationale anticipated could materialize — it materialized. One session isn't a verdict on the veto's process (a geopolitical supply shock is a poor test of a valuation-based sector call), but it's a real, dated data point against the LLM sector-overlay's stock-picking edge so far.

**B. The diversification-vs-concentration pattern extended through its first genuinely dual-shock session.** Flagged 07-08 through this morning: diversified sleeves (`residual_roa_6535`, `spy_benchmark`, `sector_top4_full`) again outperformed the concentrated momentum book (-4.1% to -4.9% today) on a day with two simultaneous, unrelated shocks (chip selloff + oil spike) rather than one. This is now five-plus sessions of the same pattern holding under different shock types — still not proof of a structural edge (concentration risk showing up as expected volatility isn't itself informative about long-run returns), but the pattern has not broken once since it was first flagged.

**C. No new sleeve concept proposed.** Today's moves are squarely within the existing framework: a geopolitical oil shock and a sector-specific chip-valuation unwind, both mechanisms the current sleeves are already built to be exposed to (via sector ETFs and momentum-driven stock selection respectively). The one process item worth flagging again is the cascade stop-enforcement gap (§5) — an operational decision for Evan, not a new research finding.
