### 2. Overnight / pre-market moves (2026-07-14, before the open — no intraday data exists yet)

**WDC — a confusing, potentially favorable reversal that needs same-day confirmation.** Monday's official close was **$555.55, -4.64%** (GuruFocus, 2026-07-13, https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8956264/western-digital-corp-wdc-shares-fall-46-gf-value-says-still-overvalued) — this matches the cascade sleeve's self-computed NAV above almost exactly (implied ~$555.85/share from the sleeve's 189.26 WDC shares), so treat $555.55 as the solid Monday-close data point. Separately, a live stockanalysis.com pull (https://stockanalysis.com/stocks/wdc/) captured this morning showed **$592.91, +6.72%** pre-market, alongside reports that Goldman, Cantor Fitzgerald, BofA, and Melius all raised WDC price targets into a **$650-$1,050** range (Benzinga, exact publish date within the 07-13/07-14 window but not pinned down precisely — https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60418479/western-digital-stock-drops-sk-hynix-reversal-is-shaking-up-chip-sector references some of this). **This report cannot fully reconcile the size/timing of the reported +6.72% pre-market move against a same-day -4.64% close** — it may be genuine (bank upgrades landing after Monday's close) or a stale/live-quote artifact. If real, it would put WDC back above both the cascade's $560 stop and the stock-overlay's $480 stop, reversing Monday's sub-$560 concern; this needs verification once 07-14 actually trades, not assumed from a single live pull.

**Chip/memory complex — Asia stabilizing, US names still mixed as of Monday's close.** SK Hynix fell as much as 9% intraday Tuesday in Seoul before rebounding to close **+3%**, after Monday's historic -15.4% rout (Bloomberg, 2026-07-14, https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-14/sk-hynix-shares-waver-in-seoul-after-wall-street-ai-memory-rout); Samsung +3.3%, KOSPI +0.7%. But this is an Asia-session recovery, not yet a confirmed US pre-market one: Monday's US session (07-13) saw **AMAT -4.31%** and **LRCX -5.28%** (TradingKey, 2026-07-13, https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262026619-market-movers-lrcx-20260713), both held indirectly via momentum-sleeve exposure to the broader complex. **No confirmed Tuesday pre-market quotes were found for MU, AMD, INTC, MRVL, ALAB, COHR, AMKR, APLD, STX, DOCN, AAOI, or TER individually** — flagged as not found rather than estimated. Yahoo Finance's market-live page (2026-07-14) describes AI chip stocks facing "headwinds from rate-hike speculation and profit-taking" pre-market alongside further SK Hynix softness — i.e., mixed/fragile, not a clean rebound.

**Oil — still escalating, a fresh toll adds a new wrinkle.** WTI is +3% to **$80.55/bbl**, Brent +4.3% to **$86.85/bbl**, the highest since June 15, as the US struck Iran for a third consecutive day and IRGC forces attacked tankers in the Strait of Hormuz (Al Jazeera, 2026-07-14, https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/7/14/oil-hits-1-month-high-as-us-iran-fighting-clouds-strait-of-hormuz-outlook; CNBC, 2026-07-14, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-hormuz-trump-toll-iran.html). New this morning: Trump announced Monday a **20% cargo toll on ships transiting Hormuz**, reportedly set to take effect this (Tuesday) afternoon — a fresh escalation beyond what Monday's report captured, relevant to the sector arm's XLE-control-vs-XLE-ex-overlay test.

**Residual-sleeve names (value/mega-cap tilt, distinct from the momentum book):** VLO continues rallying on refining-margin optimism (Jefferies PT raised to $312, undated within the 07-13/07-14 window — StocksToTrade, https://stockstotrade.com); CAT cited for a record $63B backlog and raised 2026 sales-growth outlook (dividend ex-date 2026-07-20); JNJ near all-time highs after its 64th consecutive annual dividend increase; GOOGL closed 07-13 at $350.97, -0.44%. **No 07-13/07-14-specific news found for XOM, APA, DINO, PARR, FTI, or NUE** — flagged as not found rather than assumed quiet.

**DOCN and AEHR:** No follow-up news found for DOCN since its 06-29 Russell 1000 reclassification. AEHR reports FY26 Q4/full-year results **today after the close** (call 5:00pm ET); consensus EPS -$0.02, revenue $18.69M; prior quarter beat EPS estimate by 37.5% but missed revenue by 20%, and management had guided FY26 revenue to the high end of $45-50M with a forecast return to profitability in Q4 — a real binary event for the 6 sleeves holding it tonight, not this morning.

### 3. Catalyst and macro review

**June CPI — a materially cooler-than-expected print, the dominant catalyst of the morning.** Released 8:30am ET: headline **-0.4% MoM, +3.5% YoY** vs. consensus of -0.2% MoM / +3.8% YoY; core (ex food/energy) **0.0% MoM, +2.6% YoY** vs. +0.2% MoM / +2.9% YoY consensus (May core was 2.9%) — a decline in energy prices plus easing services/housing costs were the cited drivers (CNBC, 2026-07-14, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/consumer-price-index-inflation-report-june-2026.html). Futures reaction as of ~7:39am CDT (Yahoo Finance live blog, 2026-07-14, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-tuesday-july-14-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-070833816.html): Dow -0.34% (52,586, -178), S&P 500 -0.22% (7,546, -17), Nasdaq +0.17% (29,526.50, +50.75) — a muted/mixed reaction given bond markets had already been pricing in hike risk ahead of the release.

**June PPI is NOT releasing today** — correcting an assumption in today's research brief: BLS has June PPI scheduled for **2026-07-15**, tomorrow, not today. The most recent actual PPI print remains May 2026 (final demand +1.1% MoM, +6.5% YoY, largest 12-month rise since Nov 2022 — BLS, https://www.bls.gov/news.release/archives/ppi_06112026.htm). Do not cite a "June PPI" figure until tomorrow's report.

**Bank earnings, pre-market:** JPMorgan beat sharply — EPS **$6.14** vs. $5.85 consensus, revenue **$58.02B** vs. $50.19B consensus, described as one of JPM's largest beats in recent history and the "highest quarterly profit in US banking history" (Yahoo Finance live page, CNBC live-updates, 2026-07-14, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/jpm-bank-of-america-citi-bank-earnings-live-updates.html). Wells Fargo also beat: EPS **$2.00** vs. $1.72 estimate. Citigroup was described qualitatively as a "turnaround" quarter with no exact figures found. **Goldman Sachs and Bank of America exact EPS/revenue figures were not found** in this research pass — both are described generally as "positive"/"strong," which this report will not convert into specific numbers without a direct source.

**Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the House Financial Services Committee today, 10:00am ET** (day one of two — Senate Banking Committee tomorrow, 07-15, after the PPI release). Prepared testimony reportedly calls this "a hinge point in history," stating the Fed needs "humility about what we know — and the courage to revisit our prior views," and "if we get policy right ... the inflation surge of the last five years will be a thing of the past" (source: financial-news aggregation of prepared remarks, 2026-07-14; CNN live-updates page, https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/14/business/live-news/cpi-inflation-kevin-warsh-capitol-hill-testimony). Lawmakers are reportedly expected to press him on Fed independence from the White House. No post-testimony market reaction distinct from the CPI-driven futures move was found as of this research (testimony had not yet concluded).

**Rate-path discrepancy (flagged Monday) — partially clarified, still not fully resolved.** Pre-CPI, July-hike odds had climbed to roughly 50% from under 10%, driven partly by hawkish comments from Fed Governor Christopher Waller (Bloomberg, 2026-07-14, https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-14/fed-rate-hike-bets-mount-before-inflation-data-warsh-testimony). Post the cooler CPI print, one source (exact methodology/publisher unclear) put July-hike odds back down to roughly 17% from roughly 42% Monday. A separate Kalshi-based source from 07-09 (CNBC, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/09/kalshi-traders-see-roughly-50percent-odds-of-a-rate-hike-in-2026-as-fed-is-split-on-policy.html) had traders at ~50% odds of a hike sometime in 2026. **No current source was found citing any "rate cut" probability** — every current reference is to hike odds, which supports treating Saturday's "61% September cut" framing as stale, but no single clean same-methodology CME FedWatch snapshot was found to formally supersede it. Treat as resolved-in-direction (hike framing, not cut framing, is current) but not resolved-in-magnitude.

### 4. LLM overlay status

No new overlay/cascade decisions have been logged since 2026-07-07 (`llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` checked directly through 07-14 pre-market; none found — consistent with no rebalance trigger before 08-01).

**Stock arm:** Cascade `llm_cascade_top1_paper` (WDC) remains the best-performing sleeve in the roster despite Monday's -4.64% day, at +5.24% self-computed since 07-06. Control `mom_roa_top1_paper` (BE) remains worst at -11.53%. Treatment `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` stays flat in cash (BE VETO'd 07-07) — a cash position that, by construction, cannot lose but also has not participated in WDC's run. Standing since 07-06 unchanged: cascade > cash treatment > BE control.

**Sector arm:** Control `sector_top4_paper` (XLE/XLK/XLI/XLB) +0.93% self-computed; overlay treatment (ex-XLE) -0.32%; cascade (XLV substituted) -0.84%. The control still leads both LLM variants. Today's fresh Hormuz-toll escalation (§2/§3) is a live continuation of the same test flagged since 07-07 — the veto worried the oil premium was fragile and would reverse; instead it has now extended for a third consecutive session. Kill-switch status unchanged on both arms.

### 5. Risk flags and upcoming events

- **WDC's reported pre-market reversal needs same-day confirmation before being treated as real.** If genuine, it resolves Monday's sub-$560 stop concern for the cascade sleeve; if it's a sourcing artifact, the sleeve's largest position may still be sitting below its stated stop levels with no automated enforcement (unchanged gap, first flagged 2026-07-13: no `check-invalidation`-equivalent exists for either cascade sleeve).
- **AEHR reports after today's close** (6 sleeves hold it) — consensus EPS -$0.02, revenue $18.69M; prior quarter's pattern was an EPS beat paired with a revenue miss, so a repeat mixed print is plausible and this report does not predict the outcome.
- **Rate-path discrepancy** (§3) is directionally clarified (hike framing is current, not cut) but not magnitude-resolved — avoid citing a specific probability until a single clean source is found.
- **Iran/Hormuz risk has escalated again**, now with a concrete new mechanism (the 20% cargo toll) rather than just rhetoric — this is the fourth consecutive report flagging this as the dominant unhedged macro risk, and it has not abated once across that span.
- **07-13 coverage remains PENDING** (4,386/5,241 baseline closes per the last `mtm_catchup` run) — official NAV is still anchored at 07-10; all 07-13 figures in this report remain self-computed, not verify-confirmed.
- **EACO/KFII** (residual_roa_6535 sleeves) remain the recurring stale-price NOCLOSE anomaly — immaterial, thin micro-caps, unresolved but not escalating.
- **Monthly rebalance** next fires 2026-08-01 per the `monthy-llm-rebalance` scheduled task; no activity since 07-01.

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. Today is the first "good macro news" data point after a string of bad ones, and it's a genuine test of the diversification-vs-concentration pattern from the other direction.** Every prior instance of that pattern (flagged 07-08 through 07-13) involved a shock hitting the concentrated momentum book harder than the diversified sleeves. A cooler CPI print plus bank-earnings beats is, directionally, good news — if the pattern is really about volatility exposure rather than a one-way bad-news bias, today should show the concentrated sleeves (cascade especially, if the WDC reversal is real) outperforming on the way up too. That would be more informative than another down day confirming the same thing again.

**B. The rate-path whipsaw itself (sub-10% to ~50% to ~17% hike odds within days) is arguably more consequential to the sector arm's framework than any single stock-specific headline this week.** The sector LLM overlay is explicitly trying to synthesize macro regime shifts into sector allocation; a week where the market's own rate-hike probability estimate swings by 40+ percentage points in under 72 hours is close to the hardest possible test case for that framework, independent of how the Hormuz/oil story resolves.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** CPI, bank earnings, and Fed testimony are all within the scope of existing macro exposure already embedded in the sector-arm design; nothing here suggests a new systematic signal. The one process item still open is the cascade stop-enforcement gap (§5), unchanged from Monday — an operational decision for Evan, not a research finding.

## Report: 2026-07-14 (Tuesday) — Post-Market Close Analysis

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **NAV standings (self-computed, 2026-07-14 close — official `verify_run`-confirmed mark remains 2026-07-13, see §2):** `llm_cascade_top1_paper` **+6.71%** ($106,713, WDC) · `residual_roa_6535_paper` +6.04% ($106,040) · `spy_benchmark_paper` +4.33% · `sector_top4_full_paper` +3.23% · `residual_roa_6535_0701_paper` +2.22% · `sector_top4_paper` +1.39% · `spy_benchmark_0701_paper` +0.07% · `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper` +0.06% · `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` +0.04% · `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` 0.00% (cash, BE vetoed) · `mom_v2_0701_paper` -0.68% · `mom_v1_0701_paper` -0.90% · `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` -0.94% · `mom_roa_6535_paper` -3.38% · `mom_v1_paper` -5.80% · `mom_v2_paper` -5.86% · `mom_roa_top1_paper` **-7.77%** ($92,225, BE).
> **Today's session:** A materially cooler June CPI (headline +3.5% YoY vs. +3.8% est.) sparked a broad relief rally led by chips: **S&P 500 closed +0.38% (7,543.59), Nasdaq Composite +0.9% (26,107.01), Dow +0.02% (52,508.27, +9.63pts)** (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-14). AMD +5%, Intel +4%, Micron +5%, SK Hynix ADR +15.19% recouping most of Monday's historic rout. Every momentum sleeve had its best day in over a week (+2.2% to +4.2%). Bank earnings were strong on paper (JPM record profit, GS record trading revenue) but stock reactions were mixed-to-negative (WFC -3%, Citi -5% despite beats, BAC +1.3%). Oil gave back some of its overnight spike: Brent settled **+1.72% to $84.73/bbl**, well off the ~$87 morning print, after CNBC reported **Trump abandoned the threatened 20% Hormuz cargo toll** — Gulf states will "invest in the US" instead — even as the US launched fresh Iran airstrikes and reimposed a coastal blockade at 4pm ET.
> **Key movers (held names):** AAOI +12.13% (Pearland TX manufacturing expansion for 800G/1.6T optics; also same-day CEO/CFO insider selling, ~$15M combined) · FCEL +11.90% (UBS upgrade to Buy, PT $22→$27, Siemens/Fit Energy deals) · WDC +1.40% to $563.32 (round-tripped a ~$589.65 intraday high — confirms this morning's pre-market pop was real, not a data artifact, but it faded; new-today analyst moves were UBS $375→$560 and Citi $685→$800, not the Goldman/Cantor/BofA/Melius targets flagged this morning, which actually dated to 06-29–07-08) · BE +4.24% (rebound from the Hunterbrook short-seller report, not new fundamentals — earnings 07-28 is the real test) · AEHR +5.88% to $72.01 pre-earnings, then **beat after the close** (EPS $0.11 vs. -$0.02 est., revenue $18.84M vs. $18.69M est., +34% YoY) — AH quotes near $93 are not yet in our own price feed. Losers: ANAB -10.46%, WULF -7.08% to $19.41 (NY moratorium on new hyperscale datacenter permits), KRYS -6.61% (despite a same-day BofA PT raise), STRO -6.50%, SPHR -5.49% — the first three look like broad biotech-sector profit-taking (Moderna -11%, Sarepta -8%, XBI -4% today) rather than name-specific news; **flagged discrepancy**: outside sources found different closing prices for ANAB (-7% to $66.40) and SPHR (-0.1%, no negative driver) than our own DB shows — unreconciled, reporting our DB figures as the ones that drive NAV.
> **Macro:** Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testified before the House Financial Services Committee, explicitly said the cooler CPI is "not mission accomplished," gave no forward hike/cut signal, and pledged Fed independence. CME FedWatch: July-meeting hike odds fell to **17%** (from 42% Monday) post-CPI; September sits near **60%** hike odds per the same reporting — this is the clearest single-source reconciliation yet of the rate-odds whipsaw flagged in the last two reports.
> **LLM overlay status:** No new decisions logged since 2026-07-07. Stock arm: cascade (WDC) still best performer in the roster (+6.71%); BE control still worst (-7.77%); cash treatment unchanged at 0.00%. Sector arm: today is the **first day the XLE veto looks wrong on a same-direction basis** — control's XLE was +0.37% while the cascade's XLV substitute was **-1.93%** (likely the same biotech-rotation pressure hitting ANAB/KRYS/STRO); still, oil-driven XLE strength has been much smaller than the crude spike itself would suggest, so this is one data point, not a reversal.
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) Today's own coverage check **FAILED** (4,376/5,225 baseline closes, 83.7% — below the 90% floor) — same lagging-publication pattern as 07-10 and 07-13; all 07-14 figures in this report are self-computed from `price_cache`, not `verify_run`-confirmed (only 3 of 158 held tickers were actually missing a 07-14 close: EACO/FMBM/KFII, the same recurring stale thin-microcap trio, so the self-computed marks should be reliable despite the low aggregate-universe coverage number). (2) WDC's stop-enforcement gap is unchanged — no automated invalidation check exists for either cascade sleeve — but WDC's $563.32 close now sits comfortably above both the $560 and $480 levels, so the immediate concern from this morning has passed for today. (3) AEHR's earnings beat is confirmed but its AH price move is not yet reflected in our DB; expect a real re-mark across 6 sleeves once 07-15's close settles. (4) ANAB/SPHR price discrepancies vs. external sources are unresolved — noted, not corrected, since the DB is what actually drives sleeve NAV.

### 1. Market summary — 2026-07-14

**Indices, close:** S&P 500 **7,543.59 (+0.38%)**, Nasdaq Composite **26,107.01 (+0.9%)**, Dow Jones **52,508.27 (+0.02%, +9.63pts)** (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-14, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-tuesday-july-14-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-070833816.html). The rally was driven by the cooler-than-expected June CPI (headline -0.4% MoM/+3.5% YoY vs. +3.8% YoY consensus; core 0.0% MoM/+2.6% YoY vs. +2.9% consensus — CNBC, 2026-07-14, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/consumer-price-index-inflation-report-june-2026.html), which eased rate-hike fears and lifted semis in particular. IBM was a notable index drag, -25% on weak software/infrastructure guidance (CNBC live updates, 2026-07-14, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/stock-market-today-live-updates.html) — not a name our sleeves hold.

**Chips (XLK +1.29% today, 56.74→56.95 vs. 181.28→183.62):** SK Hynix ADR **+15.19% to $175.44**, recouping most of Monday's -15.4% rout; Micron **+5%**; AMD **+5%**; Intel **+4%** (247wallst, 2026-07-14, https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/14/amd-rallies-5-intel-rises-4-as-cooling-inflation-sparks-a-chip-rebound/; Bloomberg, https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-14/sk-hynix-shares-waver-in-seoul-after-wall-street-ai-memory-rout). No 07-14-specific price data was found for Marvell, Astera Labs, Coherent, Amkor, Applied Digital, or Seagate individually (only stale analyst-target updates, e.g. UBS raising MRVL to $340 and ALAB to $400 — dates attached to the articles, not confirmed same-day price catalysts).

**Bank earnings — beats, mixed stock reactions:** JPMorgan EPS **$6.14** vs. $5.85 est., revenue **$58.02B** vs. $50.19B est. (described as a record quarterly profit, ~$6B trading windfall) — JPM's own share reaction was not found in this pass. Wells Fargo EPS **$2.00** vs. $1.72 est., revenue $22.62B vs. $21.84B est. — **shares fell ~3%** on CEO Charlie Scharf's cautious tone. Citigroup EPS **$3.15**, revenue $24.8B (10-year high, record equity-trading revenue) — **shares fell ~5%** despite the beat. Bank of America EPS **$1.21** vs. $1.13 est., revenue $31.56B vs. ~$30.73B est. — **shares rose ~1.3% to $60.27**. Goldman Sachs EPS **$20.98**, net revenue $20.34B, record $7.42B equity-trading revenue. (CNBC bank-earnings live updates, 2026-07-14, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/jpm-bank-of-america-citi-bank-earnings-live-updates.html; Goldman Sachs press release, https://www.goldmansachs.com/pressroom/press-releases/2026/2026-07-14-q2-results; FinancialContent on BAC, https://www.financialcontent.com/article/stockstory-2026-7-14-bank-of-americas-nysebac-q2-cy2026-beats-on-revenue). None of our sleeves hold these banks directly, but the sector arm's XLK/XLI/XLB/XLE mix has indirect financial exposure via broad-market beta.

**Oil — spiked, then a genuine de-escalation on the toll, but conflict continues by other means.** Brent settled **+1.72% to $84.73/bbl** (CNBC/TradingEconomics, 2026-07-14), down from the ~$86.85 pre-market print and well off the ~$87 intraday peak seen over the prior two days. The most consequential update: CNBC (2026-07-14, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-hormuz-trump-toll-iran.html) reports **Trump abandoned the 20% Hormuz cargo toll** floated Monday — instead, Gulf states will "invest in the US" as compensation, and Tehran agreed to no toll for 60 days under the existing June 17 interim deal. This is a real de-escalation of the specific toll mechanism flagged this morning, but the underlying conflict has not cooled: the US launched fresh Iran airstrikes and reimposed a port/coastal blockade at 4pm ET today. WTI's exact close was not found in this pass. XLE closed **+0.37%** (56.74→56.95 in our own price_cache) — a muted reaction given the scale of the oil move, consistent with the sector overlay's original 07-07 XLE-veto thesis (oversupply concerns capping the upside of a geopolitical premium) even as the position itself isn't held by the control-vs-overlay comparison today.

**Fed:** Chair Kevin Warsh testified before the House Financial Services Committee. Per the Federal Reserve's own transcript and CNN's live coverage (both 2026-07-14, https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/testimony/warsh20260714a.htm; https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/14/business/live-news/cpi-inflation-kevin-warsh-capitol-hill-testimony), he explicitly pushed back on reading too much into today's CPI print: *"There might be some that look at this morning's data and say, 'mission accomplished.' That is not my view."* He gave no explicit forward guidance on hikes vs. cuts, said balance-sheet changes ($6.7T) would be telegraphed before acting, and pledged Fed independence. Senate Banking Committee testimony is tomorrow (07-15), after the June PPI release. **Rate-odds resolution:** CME FedWatch (CNBC, 2026-07-14) put July-meeting hike odds at **17%**, down from **42% Monday**; September sits near **60%** hike odds per the same reporting — this is the cleanest single-source snapshot found across the last three reports' repeated flagging of this whipsaw, and it resolves both the direction (hike-framing, not cut-framing, is current) and roughly the magnitude.

No trades or overlay decisions logged 07-08 through 07-14; next scheduled rebalance is 2026-08-01. `check_anomalies` flagged 3 NOCLOSE warnings for 07-14 (EACO, FMBM, KFII, all held only by `residual_roa_6535_paper`) — the same recurring, immaterial thin-microcap staleness, nothing new.

### 3. Key position analysis

**WDC — the pre-market "reversal" was real, and the sourcing behind it is now corrected.** Confirmed intraday: prior close $555.55 → intraday high **$589.65** → close **$563.32 (+1.40%)** (The Markets Daily, 2026-07-14, https://www.themarketsdaily.com/2026/07/14/western-digital-nasdaqwdc-trading-1-4-higher-heres-why.html) — this closely matches this morning's $592.91 pre-market print, confirming it was a genuine (if faded) move, not a stale-quote artifact. However, the bank price-target raises cited in this morning's report were **not from today**: BofA $610→$732 (2026-07-01), Cantor Fitzgerald $660→$900 (2026-06-29), Goldman Sachs $400→$650 (2026-07-08), Melius $1,050 (undated, "after a 20%+ pullback") — all correctly directionally described this morning but misdated as same-day. Today's actual new analyst moves were **UBS** ($375→$560, kept Neutral) and **Citi** ($685→$800, kept Buy) (The Markets Daily, 2026-07-14). Next catalyst: Q4/FY26 earnings **2026-08-05**. WDC's close of $563.32 sits above both the cascade sleeve's $560 stop and the stock-overlay's $480 stop — today's immediate concern has passed, but the design gap remains: no automated invalidation check exists for either cascade sleeve (unchanged since first flagged 07-13).

**AEHR — earnings beat, real binary event resolved positively, but the AH move isn't in our data yet.** Reported after today's close: EPS **$0.11** (beat the -$0.02 estimate), Q4 revenue **$18.84M** (vs. ~$18.69M est., +34% YoY from $14.1M), though full-year revenue was down 15% to $50M (Quiver Quantitative, 2026-07-14, https://www.quiverquant.com/news/AEHR+TEST+SYSTEMS+%28%24AEHR%29+Releases+Q4+2026+Earnings%2C+Stock+Rises; Investing.com transcript, https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-aehr-test-systems-tops-q4-2026-forecasts-stock-jumps-29-93CH-4791974). External sources report the stock jumping further after-hours to roughly $93 (+29% from the $72.01 regular-session close) — that AH price is not in our own `price_cache` and won't be until 07-15 settles; flagging it as reported-but-unconfirmed-in-our-system rather than using it. Six sleeves hold AEHR: `mom_v1_paper`, `mom_v2_paper`, `mom_roa_6535_paper`, `mom_v1_0701_paper`, `mom_v2_0701_paper`, `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper` — all six should see a real markup once tomorrow's close settles, assuming the AH strength holds.

**AAOI — the day's biggest gainer, on real news, with a caution flag.** +12.13% ($111.88→$125.45) on the company breaking ground on a ~400,000 sq ft manufacturing expansion in Pearland, TX to scale 800G/1.6T optical-transceiver production for AI/cloud demand, plus Rosenblatt Securities naming it a top pick for H2 2026 (247wallst, 2026-07-14, https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/14/applied-optoelectronics-rallies-6-lumentum-climbs-5-as-texas-expansion-fuels-ai-optics-trade/; StockTitan, https://www.stocktitan.net/news/AAOI/aoi-begins-expansion-of-pearland-manufacturing-campus-to-scale-800g-doh25djr30if.html). Same day, however, sizable insider selling was reported: CEO ~59,000 shares (~$9.8M), CFO Murry ~33,000 shares (~$5.5M) (Timothy Sykes, 2026-07-14, https://www.timothysykes.com/news/applied-optoelectronics-inc-aaoi-news-2026_07_14-2/) — not necessarily bearish (could be scheduled/10b5-1 sales) but worth flagging alongside the rally. Held across mom_v1/v2/roa_6535, both original and 0701 cohorts.

**BE — a short-covering bounce, not new fundamentals.** +4.24% ($233.49→$243.40), characterized in coverage as a rebound from the Hunterbrook Capital short report (~10.5% short interest) that alleged Chinese-scandium supply-chain dependency risk and disputed Bloom's ~$20B backlog against ~$492M in audited performance obligations; Bloom has called the allegations "false and misleading" (Barchart, 2026-07-14, https://www.barchart.com/story/news/3226408/be-stock-alert-bloom-energy-stock-remains-in-focus-following-short-report-rebuttal; GuruFocus, https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8953748/bloom-energy-be-stock-drops-amid-short-seller-report). Earnings on 2026-07-28 will be the first real test under scrutiny. `mom_roa_top1_paper` (control, 378.49 shares) remains the single worst sleeve in the roster at -7.77%.

**FCEL — an analyst upgrade with real substance.** +11.90% ($19.08→$21.35) after UBS upgraded Neutral→Buy with target raised $22→$27, citing a Siemens MOU for electrical balance-of-plant on 100MW+ fuel-cell installations and a new Fit Energy deal (Benzinga, 2026-07-14, https://www.benzinga.com/analyst-stock-ratings/upgrades/26/07/60448992/fuelcell-energy-stock-soars-after-ubs-upgrade). Context: the stock had been depressed by a dilutive $225M secondary priced at $21.

**Today's decliners — mostly a biotech-sector rotation, with one clean company-specific driver and two unreconciled price discrepancies.** WULF -7.08% to $19.41 — confirmed driver: New York Governor Hochul's executive order imposing a statewide moratorium on new hyperscale datacenter permits, bearish for crypto miners with datacenter ambitions (Benzinga, 2026-07-14, https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60452589/why-is-terawulf-stock-falling-on-tuesday) — this figure closely matches external reporting (-7.16% to $19.39). ANAB -10.46% to $62.51, KRYS -6.61%, STRO -6.50% look like broad biotech profit-taking: Moderna -11%, ImmunityBio -8%, Sarepta -8%, XBI -4% today, described as "no immediate negative catalyst," rotation after a parabolic run (Yahoo Finance/247wallst, 2026-07-14, https://finance.yahoo.com/healthcare/articles/moderna-drops-11-immunitybio-sarepta-160438552.html). Two flags: (1) external sources put ANAB at only -7% to $66.40 and found a same-day BofA price-target **raise** for KRYS ($364→$396, Buy) despite the stock falling — neither reconciles cleanly with a bearish name-specific story; (2) SPHR -5.49% in our own data vs. external reporting of only a -0.1% move alongside positive F1-Vegas-GP partnership news — this report cannot reconcile the gap and reports our DB's own figure, since that's what drives the sleeves' actual NAV, without claiming to know which is "right."

### 4. LLM overlay status

No new overlay/cascade decisions logged since 2026-07-07 (`llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` checked directly through 07-14 close; none found).

**Stock arm:** `llm_cascade_top1_paper` (WDC) remains the best-performing sleeve in the entire 17-sleeve roster at +6.71% since 07-06. `mom_roa_top1_paper` (BE control) remains worst at -7.77%. `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` (cash treatment, BE vetoed 07-07) stays flat at 0.00% — a position that has neither participated in WDC-style upside nor BE's short-seller-driven downside. Standing since 07-06 unchanged: cascade > cash treatment > BE control.

**Sector arm:** Control `sector_top4_paper` (XLE/XLK/XLI/XLB) +1.39% self-computed since 07-06; overlay treatment (ex-XLE) +0.04%; cascade (XLV substituted for XLE) -0.94%. **Today is notable**: XLE (+0.37%) beat XLV (-1.93%) on a same-day basis for the first time since this test began — the cascade's XLV substitution looked like the wrong call today, likely because XLV absorbed some of the broad biotech-sector selloff (Moderna, Sarepta, ImmunityBio) described above, an exposure the veto's original rationale (healthcare momentum/defensive rotation) didn't anticipate. This is one data point, not a reversal of the multi-week pattern (oil-driven XLE strength has repeatedly underwhelmed relative to the size of the crude moves); the sector arm's overall standing (control still ahead of both LLM variants) is unchanged. Kill-switch status unchanged on both arms.

The week was dominated by two shocks in opposite directions: the SK Hynix/chip-complex rout (Monday, hit all momentum sleeves for -4% to -4.8% single-day) and today's CPI-driven relief rally (recovered roughly half of Monday's damage across the momentum book in one session). Net effect over the week: diversified/value-tilted sleeves (residual, sector, SPY benchmarks) stayed flat-to-positive throughout; concentrated momentum sleeves (`mom_v1/v2`, `mom_roa_6535`, both cohorts) round-tripped through a -4% drawdown and partial recovery, ending the week only modestly negative; the two single-name LLM sleeves diverged hardest — cascade (WDC) +5.86% vs. control (BE) **-9.70%**, the widest same-week gap seen in this experiment so far, driven mostly by BE's short-report overhang rather than WDC's own strength.

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. This week is the cleanest test yet of the diversification-vs-concentration pattern flagged repeatedly since 07-08, and it confirms the pattern cuts both ways.** Monday's chip-complex shock hit concentrated momentum sleeves far harder than diversified ones; today's CPI-driven chip rally reversed roughly half of that in a single session, again for the concentrated book specifically. This is consistent with a volatility-exposure explanation (concentrated momentum sleeves carry more single-factor/single-sector beta, so they overshoot on both the downside and the recovery) rather than a one-way "momentum sleeves have bad luck" story. It does not, on its own, argue for or against running concentrated momentum sleeves going forward — it argues that their higher realized volatility is symmetric, which is a more favorable (if still noisy) finding than an asymmetric-downside pattern would be.

**B. The sector-arm veto's first same-direction "miss" (XLV underperforming XLE today) is worth logging as a counterpoint, not treated as a verdict.** Every prior sector-arm data point since 07-07 favored the control's XLE holding; today the substitute (XLV) underperformed for a plausible, distinct reason (sector-wide biotech rotation, not an oil-thesis failure). A single day's reversal driven by an unrelated cause doesn't change the standing evaluation of the 07-07 XLE veto, but it's a reminder that the cascade's XLV pick carries its own uncorrelated risk that the original veto rationale didn't price in.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** CPI, bank earnings, Fed testimony, and today's individual stock catalysts (AAOI capacity expansion, FCEL analyst upgrade, WDC target raises, BE short-covering, biotech-sector rotation) are all within the scope of existing exposures already embedded in the current sleeve roster. The two open process items remain unchanged from prior reports: the cascade stop-enforcement gap (§3, first flagged 07-13) and the still-not-fully-reconciled ANAB/SPHR price discrepancies (§3) — both are operational/data-hygiene items for Evan, not research findings that argue for a new strategy.

## Report: 2026-07-15 (Wednesday) — Pre-Market Overnight Research

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **NAV standings (self-computed, 2026-07-14 close, since-inception — official `verify_run`-confirmed mark remains 2026-07-13, see §1):** `llm_cascade_top1_paper` **+6.71%** ($106,713, WDC) · `residual_roa_6535_paper` +6.04% ($106,040) · `spy_benchmark_paper` +4.33% · `sector_top4_full_paper` +3.23% · `residual_roa_6535_0701_paper` +2.22% · `sector_top4_paper` +1.39% · `spy_benchmark_0701_paper` +0.07% · `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper` +0.06% · `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` +0.04% · `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` 0.00% (cash, BE vetoed) · `mom_v2_0701_paper` -0.68% · `mom_v1_0701_paper` -0.90% · `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` -0.94% · `mom_roa_6535_paper` -3.38% · `mom_v1_paper` -5.80% · `mom_v2_paper` -5.86% · `mom_roa_top1_paper` **-7.77%** ($92,225, BE).
> **Pre-market moves (2026-07-15):** Futures point to a third straight up day, chip-led: as of ~9:18am ET, **Dow +0.02% (52,802, +11), S&P 500 futures +0.12% (7,600, +8.25), Nasdaq-100 futures +0.38% (29,904, +114.50)** (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-15). The catalyst is **ASML**, which beat Q2 estimates (€9.33B net sales, €2.92B net income) and **raised full-year 2026 guidance for the second time this year** (to €43-45B sales, 54-56% gross margin) on "extremely strong" AI-driven chip-equipment orders, plus a plan to expand production capacity 30% over two years — ASML +3%, **Intel +3%, Lam Research +3%**, VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) +1.2% (CNBC/FX Leaders/NAI 500, 2026-07-15). June PPI printed **cooler than expected** at 8:30am ET: final demand **-0.3% MoM** (vs. 0.0% consensus), core (ex food/energy/trade) **+0.1% MoM** (down sharply from +0.8% in May) — a second consecutive soft inflation print after Tuesday's CPI. Oil is **moderating**, not extending: WTI +0.64% to $79.85/bbl, Brent +0.58% to $85.22/bbl, after the US struck Iran's coast for a third consecutive night and reimposed its Strait of Hormuz naval blockade; Hormuz transit volume fell >50% week-over-week (57 transits Fri-Sun vs. the prior week).
> **Macro catalyst:** Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the **Senate Banking Committee at 10:00am ET today** — his second and final congressional appearance this week (House was yesterday), covering the Fed's Semiannual Monetary Policy Report. Markets are watching for any signal on a September hike; futures pricing (per a Kalshi-linked source, dated) put roughly 69% odds of **no rate cuts at all in 2026**. **AEHR reported FY26 Q4 results after Tuesday's close and beat sharply**: EPS $0.11 (vs. -$0.02 est.), revenue $18.84M (vs. $18.69M est., +34% YoY), record $60.7M quarterly bookings, and **FY27 revenue guidance of $130-150M vs. $85.14M consensus** — shares **+40% after-hours**; not yet reflected in `price_cache`. Held by 6 sleeves.
> **LLM overlay status:** No new decisions logged since 2026-07-07 (checked through today). Stock arm: cascade (WDC) still best performer in the 27-sleeve roster (+6.71%); BE control still worst (-7.77%); cash treatment flat. Sector arm unchanged, control still ahead of both LLM variants.
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) AEHR's +40% AH pop is real but unconfirmed in our own data until 07-15 settles — 6 sleeves should see a material markup once it does. (2) 07-14 coverage check still hasn't cleared (4,376/5,225 baseline closes as of the last run) — official NAV remains anchored at 07-13; all 07-14 figures here are self-computed, carried from yesterday's post-market report. (3) WDC is back in **Kioxia NAND-flash merger talks** (Bloomberg, reopened ~07-09 window) — a real structural catalyst distinct from the momentum/analyst-target story already priced in. (4) The cascade sleeves' stop-enforcement gap remains unaddressed (first flagged 07-13).

### 2. Overnight / pre-market moves (2026-07-15, before the open — no intraday data exists yet)

**Broad market — a third straight up session forming, led by chips.** As of ~9:18am ET (Yahoo Finance live blog, 2026-07-15, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-wednesday-july-15-dow-sp-nasdaq-091813320.html): Dow futures +0.02% (52,802, +11pts), S&P 500 futures +0.12% (7,600, +8.25pts), Nasdaq-100 futures +0.38% (29,904, +114.50pts). The move is being driven almost entirely by **ASML's earnings beat and guidance raise** (see §3) rather than the cooler PPI print, which is a secondary tailwind.

**AEHR — the largest single overnight catalyst for the momentum book, not yet in our data.** Reported FY26 Q4 results after Tuesday's close: EPS **$0.11** (beat -$0.02 est. by $0.13 per the original estimate cited 07-07, or beat a -$0.01 est. by $0.12 per the day-of consensus figure — sources vary slightly on the exact estimate; the beat itself is unambiguous), revenue **$18.84M** (vs. $18.69M est., +34% YoY from $14.09M), record quarterly bookings **$60.7M**, record backlog **>$100M**, and — the standout figure — **FY27 revenue guidance of $130-150M vs. $85.14M analyst consensus** (implying ~160-200% YoY growth) (StockTitan, 2026-07-14, https://www.stocktitan.net/news/AEHR/aehr-test-systems-reports-fiscal-2026-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-aytbj239efe5.html; StockTwits, 2026-07-14, https://stocktwits.com/news-articles/markets/equity/aehr-stock-pops-40-percent-after-hours-on-earnings-beat-retail-bulls-point-to-upbeat-fy-27-guidance/cZZRXeiR7qt). Shares **surged over 40% after-hours** off the $72.01 regular-session close (implying a ~$100+ AH print). This is not yet reflected in `price_cache` (07-14 close there is the pre-earnings $72.01) and won't be until 07-15 settles. Six sleeves hold AEHR and should see a real, material markup once tomorrow's close is marked: `mom_v1_paper`, `mom_v2_paper`, `mom_roa_6535_paper`, `mom_v1_0701_paper`, `mom_v2_0701_paper`, `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper`.

**WDC — steady near $560-566, plus a real structural catalyst (Kioxia).** Live quote shows WDC trading between $560.00-$565.94 today (stockanalysis.com pull via search, 2026-07-15), essentially flat to Tuesday's $563.32 close and comfortably above both the cascade sleeve's $560 stop and the stock-overlay's $480 stop. Analyst target raises continue: **UBS** $375→$560 (kept Neutral), **Citi** $685→$800 (kept Buy), **Wells Fargo** $575→$730 (kept Overweight). Separately, and more consequentially: Western Digital has **reopened merger talks with Kioxia Holdings** (Japan) to combine NAND flash operations — Bloomberg reports the talks as active, following earlier attempts that stalled on valuation/regulatory concerns (Yahoo Finance/Simply Wall St, dated within the 07-09 to 07-14 window, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/western-digital-wdc-reopens-kioxia-151411891.html). A combination would create one of the largest NAND suppliers alongside Samsung/SK Hynix/Micron. This is a distinct catalyst from the HDD-demand/analyst-target story already priced into the stock — worth tracking separately since it changes WDC's business mix (adds NAND scale) rather than just validating the existing HDD thesis. Next earnings 2026-08-05.

**STX (Seagate) — the other half of the AI-storage story, held broadly across the momentum book.** Up 216% YTD / 485% over the past year on a hyperscaler storage cycle; Q3 data-center revenue +55% to $2.5B, management raised its minimum annual revenue growth target to 20% (24/7 Wall St, 2026-06-30, https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/06/30/up-250-ytd-does-seagate-have-more-upside-ahead/). Reports FY Q4/full-year results **2026-07-28** — the same day as BE's earnings. STX is held across `mom_roa_6535_paper`, `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper`, `residual_roa_6535_paper` and most of the residual weight-ladder sleeves — no 07-15-specific news found, flagged as a structural (not daily-catalyst) driver.

**Chip complex — broad rally on ASML, no held-name-specific pre-market quotes confirmed.** Intel and Lam Research each +3% pre-market/early session on the ASML read-through; SMH (VanEck Semiconductor ETF) +1.2%. No confirmed 07-15 pre-market quotes were found for the specific held names AMD, MU, MRVL, ALAB, COHR, AMKR, APLD, CIEN, DOCN, TER, AAOI individually — flagged as not found rather than estimated. One dated note: BNP Paribas raised its ALAB target from $260 to $475 (outperform maintained), though the exact date within the past week is not fully pinned down.

**BE — pre-market softer, no new fundamental news.** A live pre-market pull shows BE around **$237.95, roughly -1.9%** from a recent reference price (search-tool aggregation, 2026-07-15) — flagged with caution since the exact prior-close reference this figure is measured against is ambiguous in the source data; treat as directional (modestly softer), not a confirmed exact percentage, until 07-15 closes. No new fundamental news since Tuesday's short-report-rebuttal bounce (+4.24% to $243.40); Brookfield's AI-infrastructure financing partnership (expanded from $5B to $25B) remains the most recent substantive catalyst, dated within the past two weeks. Earnings 2026-07-28.

**AAOI — no new 07-15 news found.** Coverage located in this pass is a re-surfacing of Tuesday's story (Pearland TX manufacturing expansion, Rosenblatt top-pick designation, ~$15M combined CEO/CFO insider selling) rather than fresh 07-15 developments. One separate, undated-within-window item: AAOI's China subsidiary doubled its credit line with Shanghai Pudong Development Bank to RMB 500M (~$74M) for working capital/expansion — timing not confirmed as 07-15-specific.

**Oil / Iran — de-escalating in price even as the conflict continues.** WTI +0.64% to $79.85/bbl, Brent +0.58% to $85.22/bbl (CNBC, 2026-07-15, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/15/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-hormuz-blockade.html) — both well off Monday's ~$87 Brent peak. The US struck Iran's coast for a third consecutive night; Iran's IRGC hit two oil tankers in the strait and launched missile/drone strikes on US assets in Kuwait and Bahrain in retaliation. Trump has abandoned the specific 20% Hormuz cargo-toll mechanism but reimposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports with new US transit fees. Maritime traffic data: only 57 Hormuz transits recorded Friday-Sunday, a **>50% drop** from the prior week — a real, measurable supply-chain effect distinct from the price action. Relevant to the sector arm's XLE-veto test (§4).

### 3. Catalyst and macro review

**ASML earnings — the dominant catalyst of the morning, driving the whole semiconductor complex.** Reported Q2 net sales of €9.33B and net income of €2.92B, both above estimates, and **raised full-year 2026 guidance for the second time this year** to €43-45B sales ($49B) with 54-56% gross margin, citing "extremely strong" AI-driven orders as customers accelerate capacity expansion (CNBC, 2026-07-15, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/15/asml-2q-earnings-ai-chips-orders.html). ASML also announced plans to expand its own chipmaking-equipment production capacity by 30% over the next two years, and disclosed a "High-NA" lithography validation win with Intel. Stock +3% pre-market/early session; Intel and Lam Research each +3%; SMH +1.2% (FX Leaders/NAI 500/CNBC, 2026-07-15). This is a read-through catalyst for every semiconductor-heavy sleeve in the roster (mom_v1/v2, mom_roa_6535, and both 0701 cohorts, all top-heavy in chip/chip-equipment names).

**June PPI — a second consecutive cooler-than-expected inflation print.** Released 8:30am ET: final demand **-0.3% MoM** (vs. 0.0% consensus), core (ex food, energy, trade services) **+0.1% MoM** (down sharply from +0.8% in May) (Bloomingbit/BLS, 2026-07-15, https://www.bls.gov/news.release/archives/ppi_07152026.htm). Combined with Tuesday's cooler CPI, this is the second straight day of disinflationary data — consistent with the futures-market relief rally, though PPI's market reaction was muted relative to CPI's (PPI is a smaller market-mover generally).

**Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the Senate Banking Committee today, 10:00am ET** — his second and final congressional appearance of the week (House Financial Services was yesterday, 07-14). This is the Fed's Semiannual Monetary Policy Report. Background context found: inflation had climbed to around 4.2% earlier in the year on an energy shock, and one source describes markets pricing roughly **69% odds of no rate cuts at all in 2026** — Warsh reportedly became the first Fed chair since the dot plot's 2012 debut to withhold his own personal rate projection, shifting the signaling burden onto testimony itself (CryptoTimes/CryptoBriefing, 2026-07-09/07-14, https://www.cryptotimes.io/2026/07/09/fed-chair-kevin-warsh-testifies-before-senate-july-15-what-it-means-for-crypto/). No post-testimony market reaction exists yet (hearing has not occurred as of this report). This is the cleanest single macro event remaining this week after CPI/PPI/bank-earnings have all released.

**Jobless claims and retail sales — not confirmed as today's releases.** Weekly initial jobless claims typically publish Thursdays (next: 2026-07-16); the most recent print (week ending 07-04) was 215K, down from 217K, with continuing claims at 1,814K (up 8K, highest since late March but below the 1,820K estimate) (TradingEconomics, dated). No confirmed same-day retail-sales release was found for 07-15 specifically in this pass — flagged as not found rather than assumed.

**Oil/Iran — see §2 for the price detail.** The structural point for today: **Hormuz transit volume itself has fallen over 50% week-over-week**, a real supply-chain data point distinct from the price action, and one that historically has more lasting relevance to the sector arm's oil/XLE thesis than any single day's headline price move.

**Stock arm:** Cascade `llm_cascade_top1_paper` (WDC) remains the best-performing sleeve in the entire 27-sleeve roster at +6.71% self-computed since 07-06, and now carries a distinct new tailwind candidate (the Kioxia merger talks, §2) beyond the original supply-contract thesis logged 07-07. Control `mom_roa_top1_paper` (BE) remains worst at -7.77%; BE's pre-market softness today (§2) would extend that gap modestly if it holds through the close, though the reference price is unconfirmed. Treatment `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` stays flat in cash (BE VETO'd 07-07) — has not participated in either WDC's run or BE's short-report drawdown/rebound cycle. Standing since 07-06 unchanged: cascade > cash treatment > BE control.

**Sector arm:** Control `sector_top4_paper` (XLE/XLK/XLI/XLB) +1.39% self-computed; overlay treatment (ex-XLE) +0.04%; cascade (XLV substituted for XLE) -0.94%. Yesterday was the first session where the XLV substitute underperformed XLE on a same-day basis (biotech-sector rotation, not an oil-thesis failure); today's oil moderation (§2/§3) is a live continuation of the underlying test the 07-07 XLE veto was making. Kill-switch status unchanged on both arms.

### 5. Risk flags and upcoming events

- **AEHR's 40%+ AH pop is confirmed by two independent sources but not yet in our own `price_cache`** — expect a real, material markup across all 6 holding sleeves once 07-15 settles; do not assume the AH print holds through the regular session.
- **07-14 coverage remains unresolved** (4,376/5,225 baseline closes as of the last `daily.bat` run, 17:23 CDT 07-14) — official NAV is still anchored at 07-13; every 07-14 figure in this report is self-computed and carried forward, not `verify_run`-confirmed. This is now the third instance of the same lagging-publication pattern in the past week (07-10, 07-13, 07-14).
- **The cascade sleeves' stop-enforcement gap is unchanged** (first flagged 07-13): no automated invalidation check exists for either `llm_cascade_top1_paper` or `llm_cascade_sector4_paper`. WDC currently trades comfortably above its $560/$480 levels, so this is not an active concern today, but the design gap itself persists.
- **Kevin Warsh's Senate testimony at 10:00am ET is the last major scheduled macro catalyst before Thursday's jobless claims** — a rate-path surprise here (hike/cut signal) would likely move the sector arm's XLK/XLI/XLB/XLE mix more than any single stock headline this week.
- **WDC's Kioxia merger talks are unconfirmed as a binding deal** — reported as active discussions, not an announced transaction; any concrete update (confirmation, denial, or valuation terms) would be a material catalyst for the largest single position in the roster (`llm_cascade_top1_paper`).
- **BE and STX both report earnings 2026-07-28** — the same day, a real binary-event cluster for the stock-overlay control (BE) and the broader momentum book (STX, held across most momentum/residual sleeves).
- **EACO/FMBM/KFII** (residual sleeves) remain the recurring stale-price NOCLOSE anomaly — immaterial, thin micro-caps, unresolved but not escalating.
- **Monthly rebalance** next fires 2026-08-01 per the `monthy-llm-rebalance` scheduled task; no activity since 07-01.

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. AEHR's guidance beat is a genuinely different kind of catalyst than most of what this week has produced, and it's worth distinguishing from noise.** Most of this week's held-name moves (AAOI, FCEL, BE, WULF, the biotech-sector rotation) have been sentiment/analyst-driven or macro-pass-through. AEHR's FY27 guidance of $130-150M against an $85M consensus is a **62-76% guidance beat**, not a modest raise — a genuine re-rating catalyst with a specific fundamental driver (semiconductor test/burn-in capacity tied to AI-chip production ramps). If it holds through 07-15's close, it would be the single largest fundamentally-driven (not macro-driven) daily gain any held name has produced in the period this report series has covered. Worth tracking whether the momentum-factor sleeves' exposure to genuine small-cap fundamental catalysts like this — versus large-cap macro-beta names — is where any structural edge in the momentum book actually lives, as opposed to the macro-whipsaw exposure that has dominated headlines the past week.

**B. WDC's Kioxia talks are a reminder that the cascade sleeve's largest position carries idiosyncratic M&A risk the original 07-07 BUY thesis didn't price in.** The 07-07 rationale for WDC (multi-year HDD supply contracts, AI/cloud demand, valuation discipline vs. BE) was a pure fundamentals-and-momentum case. A NAND-flash merger with Kioxia would change WDC's business mix, balance sheet, and possibly trigger deal-arbitrage-style volatility unrelated to the original thesis — not necessarily bad, but a distinct risk category (M&A execution/regulatory risk) that the invalidation-level framework (a price stop) may not be well-suited to capture. This is a design observation, not a call to act.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** ASML/chip-equipment strength, cooler PPI, Warsh's testimony, and the individual stock catalysts (AEHR, WDC, STX, BE) are all within the scope of exposures already embedded in the current roster (momentum-factor concentration in semiconductors/storage, macro-sensitive sector overlay, single-name LLM overlay experiment). The two open process items remain the cascade stop-enforcement gap (§5) and the unresolved 07-14 coverage lag (§1/§5) — both operational items for Evan, not research findings that argue for a new strategy.

## Report: 2026-07-15 (Wednesday) — Post-Market Close Analysis

> **DAILY SUMMARY**
> **NAV standings (self-computed, 2026-07-15 close, since-inception, ranked best-to-worst — official `verify_run`-confirmed mark remains 2026-07-14, see §2 for the full 27-sleeve table):** `residual_w5545_paper` **+6.62%** ($106,617) leads all 27 sleeves · `residual_w5050_paper` +6.21% · `residual_w6040_paper` +6.08% · `residual_roa_6535_paper` +5.04% ($105,036) · `spy_benchmark_paper` +4.74% · five more residual-ladder variants +2.1–4.4% · `sector_top4_full_paper` +2.60% · `residual_roa_6535_0701_paper` +1.02% · `sector_top4_paper` +0.78% · `spy_benchmark_0701_paper` +0.47% · `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` -0.36% · `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` -1.35% · `mom_roa_6535_0701_paper` -2.38% · **`llm_cascade_top1_paper` -2.65%** ($97,349, WDC) · `mom_v1_0701_paper` -3.12% · `mom_v2_0701_paper` -3.19% · `mom_roa_6535_paper` -5.60% · `mom_v1_paper` -7.91% · `mom_v2_paper` -8.39% · `mom_roa_top1_paper` **-9.30%** ($90,704, BE).
> **The day's dominant story is `llm_cascade_top1_paper`'s collapse.** It was the single best-performing sleeve in the entire 27-sleeve roster yesterday (+6.71% since inception); today it fell to -2.65% — a **~9.4 percentage-point single-day swing**, entirely attributable to its sole holding, WDC, which fell **-8.78%** (our `price_cache`: $563.32→$513.84) on news it **reopened NAND-flash merger talks with Kioxia** (GuruFocus/TradingKey, 2026-07-15). This is precisely the M&A-risk scenario flagged as a design gap in yesterday's report (§6, point B) — realized one trading day after it was raised.
> **Today's session:** Cooler June PPI (final demand -0.3% MoM vs. 0.0% consensus; core +0.2% MoM vs. +0.3% est.) extended the week's soft-inflation narrative. **S&P 500 closed +0.38% to 7,572.40, Nasdaq Composite +0.62% to 26,269.23, Dow +0.29% (+150.37pts) to 52,658.64** (CNBC/The Motley Fool, 2026-07-15). Mega-cap tech led: **Apple +4% to a fresh all-time-high ~$327** on a report it gained approval to launch generative-AI features in China (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-15); Amazon and Alphabet each +~3%, Microsoft +~3%. But underneath the index gain, **AI-hardware and memory/storage names were hit hard**: Dell **-14%** (intraday), HPE -8%, Super Micro -5% — driven by a report that Meta is developing a plan to lease surplus AI training/inference capacity to enterprise customers, reviving "did hyperscalers overbuild" concerns, plus Evercore ISI flagging memory-pricing margin risk and a GF Securities downgrade to Hold on valuation (24/7 Wall St./Cryptonomist/MoneyCheck, 2026-07-15). Micron -3% to -8% (sources vary) and SanDisk -6% to -8% extended Monday's SK Hynix-triggered rout after a one-day pause Tuesday. Corning -7.8% on its own guidance cut (decelerating carrier-network/display demand), not the AI-hardware theme.
> **Key movers (held names):** WDC -8.78% (Kioxia merger talks, see above) · DELL -9.80% (broad AI-hardware selloff, no company-specific news) · MRVL -7.27%, GLW -7.05% (own guidance cut), CIEN -6.37% — all swept into the same complex · **CELC -17.60%** ($111.05→$91.51) — FDA approved its first marketed drug, gedatolisib (brand Revtorpyk) for PIK3CA-wildtype advanced breast cancer, but a delayed commercial launch (late Q3) and tolerability concerns overshadowed the approval (Yahoo Finance/Daily Political, 2026-07-15) · **AAOI -13.04%**, giving back essentially all of yesterday's +12.13% Pearland-expansion pop — no fresh negative catalyst found in this pass; consistent with profit-taking after a parabolic move plus the CEO/CFO insider-selling flagged yesterday · ANAB -10.70% continuing the biotech-sector rotation flagged in prior reports — no new company-specific catalyst found for today specifically · **AEHR +21.91%** ($72.01→$87.79), the regular-session close finally reflecting Tuesday's after-hours earnings beat (EPS $0.11 vs. -$0.02 est., FY27 guidance $130-150M vs. $85M consensus) · FCEL -5.15%, continuing to weigh from last week's dilutive $225M secondary priced at $21.
> **Macro:** Fed Chair Kevin Warsh gave his second and final congressional appearance of the week (Senate Banking Committee, after Tuesday's House testimony) — reaffirmed inflation-fighting commitment, gave no new forward rate signal, and was drawn into a contentious, market-irrelevant exchange with Sen. Elizabeth Warren over a colleague's blackout-period conduct (CNBC/CNN, 2026-07-15). Oil barely moved despite continued escalation: Brent settled **+$0.22 to $84.95/bbl**, WTI **+$0.26 to $79.60/bbl**, even as the US carried out a third consecutive night of strikes on Iran and maintained the Strait of Hormuz naval blockade (CNBC, 2026-07-15).
> **LLM overlay status:** Stock arm — cascade (WDC) plunged from best-in-roster (+6.71%) to -2.65%, a 1-week change of **-6.62%** vs. control's -5.86% 1-week (BE, now -9.30% since inception, still the roster's worst sleeve) and cash treatment's flat **0.00%** (unchanged since the 07-01 BE veto — has now avoided both BE's short-report drawdown and WDC's M&A-news crash). The invalidation level for the current WDC decision (07-07, `llm_overlay_log`) is **$480**; today's self-computed close of $513.84 sits above it (~6.5% cushion) but is the closest any tracked position has come to a logged stop level in this report series. Sector arm: control still ahead of both LLM variants; closest ETF to an invalidation level is XLB at $50.50 vs. a $49.50 stop (~2% cushion, sector-overlay `llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` 07-07 decision) — not breached.
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) 07-15's own coverage check is **PENDING** (4,385 closes vs. the 5,000 hard floor per `var/last_daily_run.log`), same lagging-publication pattern as recent sessions — all 07-15 figures in this report are self-computed from `price_cache` (only EACO and UYSC lacked a 07-15 close among all held tickers, per `check_anomalies`; both are the recurring stale thin-microcap names, immaterial). (2) WDC's $480 invalidation level (07-07 decision) is not breached today, but the stop-enforcement fix (record BZ, deployed 2026-07-15) doesn't take effect until tomorrow's 2026-07-16 5:15pm run — if WDC gaps down further overnight on Kioxia-deal follow-through, the position rides through one more evening without an active automated check. (3) The cascade sleeve's M&A-risk exposure, flagged as a hypothetical design gap in yesterday's report, has now materialized in the NAV — worth carrying into the next kill-switch review (12mo/30-picks). (4) AAOI's and ANAB's declines lack a confirmed same-day specific catalyst; reported as likely profit-taking/sector-rotation, not fabricated as confirmed news.

### 1. Market summary — 2026-07-15

**Indices, close:** S&P 500 **7,572.40 (+0.38%)**, Nasdaq Composite **26,269.23 (+0.62%)**, Dow Jones **52,658.64 (+0.29%, +150.37pts)** (CNBC, 2026-07-15, https://www.cnbc.com/amp/2026/07/14/stock-market-today-live-updates.html; The Motley Fool, https://www.fool.com/coverage/stock-market-today/2026/07/15/stock-market-today-july-15-markets-rise-on-cooler-inflation-and-strong-earnings-start/). The rally was underpinned by a cooler-than-expected June PPI: final demand **-0.3% MoM** (vs. 0.0% consensus), core (ex food/energy/trade) **+0.2% MoM**, down sharply from May's +0.8% (Trading Economics/Motley Fool, 2026-07-15) — the second consecutive soft inflation print this week, after Tuesday's CPI.

**Mega-cap tech vs. AI-hardware/memory — the day's central divergence.** Apple **+4% to a fresh all-time-high near $327**, its 15th intraday record of 2026, on a report the iPhone maker gained approval to launch generative-AI features in China; the stock has added over half a trillion dollars in market value this month, nearing a $5T valuation (Yahoo Finance/247wallst, 2026-07-15, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-wednesday-july-15-dow-sp-nasdaq-waver-asml-091813320.html; https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/15/apple-is-up-20-in-2026-what-will-it-take-for-aapl-stock-to-hit-350/). Amazon and Alphabet each **+~3%**, Microsoft **+~3%** (CNBC, 2026-07-15). In sharp contrast, AI-hardware and memory/storage names sold off hard: **Dell -14%** intraday, **HPE -8%**, **Super Micro -5%** — the proximate trigger was a report that Meta Platforms is developing a plan to lease surplus AI training/inference capacity to enterprise customers, reviving concern that hyperscale cloud providers over-built AI infrastructure and could slow future server orders; compounding factors included Evercore ISI flagging memory-pricing margin pressure (Dell gross margin compression toward 18%) and a GF Securities downgrade of Dell to Hold on valuation after a ~200% YTD run to a ~34x forward P/E (Cryptonomist/AOL/FXLeaders/247wallst/MoneyCheck, 2026-07-15, https://en.cryptonomist.ch/2026/07/15/dell-technologies-inc-stock-plunges-14-in-ai-hardware-selloff/; https://www.aol.com/articles/dell-falls-14-hpe-super-164251000.html; https://www.fxleaders.com/news/2026/07/15/dell-stock-dips-below-400-as-cheaper-competition-hits-hardware-sector/; https://moneycheck.com/dell-dell-stock-plunges-13-amid-ai-overcapacity-concerns-and-downgrade). Micron fell (sources range -3% to -8% across outlets, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/micron-shares-slide-semiconductor-selloff-122643510.html) and SanDisk/Marvell also declined (-6% to -8%, -6.62% respectively) as the memory-and-AI-hardware complex gave back more of its parabolic 2026 gains after Monday's SK Hynix-triggered rout paused Tuesday, per 24/7 Wall St. (2026-07-15, https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/15/sk-hynix-drops-5-sandisk-slides-6-western-digital-slips-4-as-traders-take-profits-in-memory-stocks/) and TradingKey market-movers pages (https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262032735-market-movers-mrvl-20260715).

**Fed:** Chair Kevin Warsh testified before the **Senate Banking Committee** — his second and final congressional appearance this week, following Tuesday's House Financial Services Committee session, and his inaugural semiannual Monetary Policy Report testimony overall as the 17th Fed Chair (sworn in 2026-05-22). He reaffirmed the Fed's inflation-fighting commitment and gave no new forward guidance on the rate path (CNBC, 2026-07-15, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/15/watch-fed-chairman-kevin-warsh-testify-live-before-senate-banking-committee.html). Sen. Elizabeth Warren pressed him on whether he had questioned a colleague about a reported blackout-period meeting with bankers that may have violated Fed rules, producing a heated but market-irrelevant ethics exchange (CNN, 2026-07-15, https://us.cnn.com/2026/07/15/economy/fed-chairman-kevin-warsh-senate-testimony).

**Oil — largely stable despite continued escalation.** Brent settled **+$0.22 to $84.95/bbl**, WTI **+$0.26 to $79.60/bbl** (CNBC/Fortune, 2026-07-15, https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/15/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-hormuz-blockade.html; https://fortune.com/article/price-of-oil-07-15-2026/). US forces carried out a third consecutive night of strikes against Iran and maintained the naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz; prices stayed little-changed even so, per CNBC, consistent with the pattern flagged in recent reports where the size of the geopolitical premium has repeatedly underwhelmed relative to the scale of the military escalation.

### 3. Key position analysis

**WDC — the Kioxia merger talks are the single biggest story of the day for this portfolio.** WDC fell **-8.78%** in our own data ($563.32→$513.84); external sources confirm a ~9% move to roughly $510-514 (TradingKey/GuruFocus, 2026-07-15, https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/262032737-market-movers-wdc-20260715; https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8960543/western-digital-wdc-reopens-merger-talks-with-kioxia). The trigger: WDC "initiated renewed discussions regarding a merger with Kioxia, aiming to consolidate their NAND flash production" — a real structural M&A catalyst, distinct from the momentum/analyst-target story that had been driving the stock upward. This matters far beyond WDC's own price because `llm_cascade_top1_paper` holds **189.26 shares of WDC as its sole position** — the sleeve went from the single best performer in the entire 27-sleeve roster yesterday (+6.71%) to -2.65% today, a swing of roughly 9.4 percentage points in one session. The current invalidation level on this position is **$480** (logged 2026-07-07, `llm_overlay_log`), and today's self-computed close of $513.84 sits above it — not breached, but the closest any tracked position has come to its stop in this report series (~6.5% cushion). The broader semiconductor sector was also weak: Micron -7.34%, SanDisk -7.91% same-day (TradingKey, 2026-07-15). WDC's Q4/FY26 earnings remain scheduled for **2026-08-05**.

**Dell and the AI-hardware complex — a sector-wide "overbuild" scare, not company-specific to any held name individually, but MRVL/GLW/CIEN ride the same wave.** DELL -9.80% in our data (external: as much as -14% intraday before settling), MRVL -7.27%, GLW -7.05% (Corning's own guidance cut on carrier-network/display demand — a distinct, company-specific driver), CIEN -6.37% (no distinct news found; likely swept into the same networking/AI-infrastructure-adjacent selloff). None of these four are individually large positions in any sleeve (all sub-5% weights, per `paper_positions.qty`), but their simultaneous decline explains a meaningful share of the broad -2.2% to -2.7% single-day decline across every momentum-family sleeve (`mom_v1/v2`, `mom_roa_6535`, both 05-01 and 07-06 cohorts) despite the S&P 500 itself closing up +0.38%.

**CELC — an FDA approval that the market read as a disappointment.** -17.60% ($111.05→$91.51), the day's largest decliner among held names. The FDA approved gedatolisib (brand name Revtorpyk) for PIK3CA-wildtype advanced breast cancer — Celcuity's first-ever marketed product — but shares fell because the commercial launch is delayed to **late Q3 2026** (company cited the need to secure adequate drug supply) and because of lingering treatment-tolerability concerns (Yahoo Finance/Daily Political, 2026-07-15, https://finance.yahoo.com/healthcare/articles/celcuity-shares-fall-delayed-breast-124344986.html; https://www.dailypolitical.com/2026/07/15/celcuity-nasdaqcelc-shares-gap-down-heres-why.html). Citigroup reissued an Outperform rating same-day despite the drop. Held across `mom_v1_paper`, `mom_v2_paper`, `mom_v1_0701_paper`, `mom_v2_0701_paper` (small weights).

**AAOI — gave back essentially all of yesterday's rally.** -13.04% ($125.45→$109.09), round-tripping most of yesterday's +12.13% Pearland-manufacturing-expansion pop. No fresh negative news was found in this pass — the most likely explanation is profit-taking after a parabolic move, compounded by the CEO/CFO insider selling (~$15M combined) flagged in yesterday's report, though that link is circumstantial, not confirmed by any single source found today. Held across `mom_v1/v2_paper` and `mom_roa_6535_paper`, both cohorts.

**ANAB and the ongoing biotech rotation.** -10.70% ($62.51→$55.82), the third consecutive session of biotech-sector weakness flagged in this report series (Moderna, Sarepta, ImmunityBio, XBI all lower this week per prior reports). No company-specific July 15 catalyst was found for ANAB in this pass; reporting it as continuation of the broader rotation rather than inventing a specific cause. Held by `mom_v1_paper`, `mom_v2_paper`, `mom_v1_0701_paper`.

**AEHR — the earnings beat finally lands in official data.** +21.91% ($72.01→$87.79) as the regular session caught up to Tuesday's after-hours reaction to the FY26 Q4 beat (EPS $0.11 vs. -$0.02 est., revenue $18.84M vs. $18.69M est., record $60.7M bookings, FY27 guidance $130-150M vs. $85.14M consensus). Held by six sleeves across both cohorts (`mom_v1/v2_paper`, `mom_roa_6535_paper`, and their `_0701` counterparts) — all six should show a material day-over-day lift from this position specifically.

**FCEL — dilution overhang continues.** -5.15% ($21.35→$20.25), extending the pressure from last week's $225M secondary priced at $21 (upsized from an originally announced smaller offering). No new news found for today specifically beyond continued digestion of the dilutive raise.

**BE — a modest pullback, not a new development.** -1.65% ($243.40→$239.38), a partial giveback of yesterday's +4.24% short-covering bounce; no fresh news found. `mom_roa_top1_paper` (control, 378.49 shares) remains the worst-performing sleeve in the entire roster at -9.30% since inception.

### 4. LLM overlay status

No new overlay/cascade decisions logged since 2026-07-07 (`llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` checked directly through 07-15 close; none found).

**Stock arm — today is the most consequential single day this experiment has produced.** `llm_cascade_top1_paper` (WDC) fell from the best-performing sleeve in the entire 27-sleeve roster (+6.71% since inception, 2026-07-14) to -2.65% today — a ~9.4pp single-day reversal, driven entirely by the Kioxia merger-talk news on its sole holding. This is the realization of the exact risk flagged as a hypothetical design gap in yesterday's report (§6, point B): *"a NAND-flash merger with Kioxia would change WDC's business mix, balance sheet, and possibly trigger deal-arbitrage-style volatility unrelated to the original thesis... a distinct risk category (M&A execution/regulatory risk) that the invalidation-level framework (a price stop) may not be well-suited to capture."* The current $480 invalidation level was not breached (WDC closed at $513.84, ~6.5% above it), but this is the closest any tracked position has come to its stop across this report series, and the stop-enforcement fix (record BZ) does not take effect until tomorrow's 2026-07-16 5:15pm run. `mom_roa_top1_paper` (BE control) is unaffected by today's news and closed -1.65% (a partial giveback of yesterday's bounce), remaining the roster's worst sleeve at -9.30% since inception. `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` (cash treatment) stayed flat at 0.00% — the treatment has now avoided both BE's short-report drawdown and WDC's M&A-news crash, the clearest day yet in favor of the veto-to-cash design versus either the control or the cascade variant.

**Sector arm — muted by comparison.** Control `sector_top4_paper` +0.78% self-computed since inception; overlay treatment (ex-XLE) -0.36%; cascade (XLV substituted for XLE) -1.35%. All four sector-arm ETF holdings (XLB $50.50, XLI $180.06, XLK $181.58, XLV $158.29 stale) moved less than 1.5% today; XLB sits closest to any logged invalidation level ($49.50 stop, ~2% cushion) but nothing breached. Kill-switch status unchanged on both arms (still well under the 12mo/30-picks review threshold).

The week (07-08/07-07 → 07-15) was dominated by the SK Hynix-triggered memory/AI-hardware rout (Monday 07-13's -6% to -15% single-day hits across WDC/Micron/SanDisk/SK Hynix), a partial CPI-driven recovery Tuesday, and today's Kioxia-news-driven relapse in WDC specifically. Net effect: diversified sleeves (SPY benchmarks, sector-arm, residual-ladder variants) stayed roughly flat to modestly positive across the week (-0.9% to +1.3%), while concentrated momentum sleeves and both single-name LLM sleeves took the brunt — `llm_cascade_top1_paper` went from the week's likely best performer (through Tuesday) to its worst by Friday-equivalent close, a round trip driven almost entirely by one stock's two distinct, unrelated catalysts (a sector-wide memory rout, then an idiosyncratic M&A headline).

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. The cascade sleeve's single-name concentration risk, flagged as hypothetical yesterday, is now an empirical data point — and it argues for treating the LLM-cascade design's risk profile as a feature to study, not dismiss.** A single stock swinging a sleeve's since-inception standing from #1 to #20 (of 27) in one session is a direct, measurable illustration of why the underlying experiment design (single-name concentration, tiny n) carries far more idiosyncratic risk than the diversified systematic sleeves it's compared against. This doesn't invalidate the cascade concept — it's exactly the kind of result the 12mo/30-picks kill-switch window exists to average out — but it's a concrete example worth citing when that review happens, since M&A/deal risk is a distinct category from the momentum/earnings risk the underlying strategy was designed to capture.

**B. Today's AI-hardware "overbuild" scare (Dell/HPE/SMCI) is a new, distinct narrative from the memory-supply-glut story that dominated the prior week, and it's worth distinguishing the two going forward.** The 07-13/07-14 memory selloff was about SK Hynix's HBM shipment guidance (a supply/execution question). Today's Dell-led move is about a demand-side worry — whether hyperscalers over-built AI infrastructure capacity, per the Meta capacity-leasing report. Both narratives pressure overlapping tickers (WDC, Micron, SanDisk all appear in each), but they're causally distinct, and conflating them would misread how correlated these positions' risk actually is going forward.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** PPI, Apple's China-AI-approval rally, Fed testimony, and the day's individual stock catalysts (CELC's launch-delay disappointment, AAOI's giveback, WDC's Kioxia news, the Dell-led AI-hardware selloff) are all within the scope of exposures already embedded in the current 27-sleeve roster (momentum-factor concentration in semiconductors/storage/AI-hardware, macro-sensitive sector overlay, single-name LLM overlay experiment). The standing open process items are unchanged: the cascade stop-enforcement gap not going live until tomorrow (§3/§4, first flagged 07-13, fix deployed 07-15 per record BZ) and the 07-15 coverage-pending lag (§2) — both operational items for Evan, not research findings that argue for a new strategy.

This is the sole holding of `llm_cascade_top1_paper`. S&P 500 -0.3%, Nasdaq -0.9%, Dow roughly flat (UnitedHealth +7% earnings beat offsetting chip weakness; Dow/UNH not held).
> **Macro catalyst:** June retail sales +0.2% MoM (vs. +0.3% consensus, but ex-gasoline +0.7%; YoY +6.7%) and initial jobless claims 208K (vs. 217K consensus, down 8K) — a stronger-than-expected consumer/labor read that markets interpreted as hawkish-for-the-Fed, reinforcing this week's already-high odds of no 2026 rate cuts.
> **LLM overlay status:** Stock arm — `llm_cascade_top1_paper`'s WDC position is trading below its $480 invalidation level intraday for the first time; whether this becomes an actual exit depends on tonight's `daily.bat` run (5:15pm), which is the **first scheduled live run of the newly-deployed settled-close stop-enforcement fix** (record BZ/CA) — see §4 for the open questions this raises. `mom_roa_top1_paper` (BE control) got a positive fundamental catalyst today ($1.7B Nebius AI-infrastructure financing project) but no confirmed price move yet. `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` (cash treatment) remains flat, uninvolved in today's stress. Sector arm: no confirmed same-day XLB/XLI/XLK/XLV moves found in this pass.
> **Critical outstanding items:** (1) WDC below its $480 stop intraday — the most consequential single data point this report series has produced for the LLM-overlay experiment; outcome depends on tonight's close and coverage status. (2) 07-15's own coverage/verify status was PASS 27/27 as of the 07-15 17:18 CDT run (settled≤07-14) — no new `daily.bat` run has occurred since; a `TradingMorningMTM` catch-up refresh was **in progress at report time** (started ~13:01 CDT today), unrelated to and not blocking this read-only report. ### 0. Why this is not a pre-market report

This task runs on a schedule intended to fire pre-market. It fired instead at approximately 13:00 CDT (11:00am local-session-time equivalent) with the market having been open since 9:30am ET (~4.5 hours before this report was compiled) — confirmed via system clock (`date` → `Thu Jul 16 12:59:40 CDT 2026` at the start of this research pass) and corroborated by every search result returning live intraday data (e.g., a WDC quote timestamped 12:36pm ET). Per standing instruction to never fabricate or misrepresent data: this report is explicitly framed as a **delayed, mid-session update**, not a pre-market briefing — consistent with the precedent set 2026-07-10 (see that date's "Delayed Same-Day Update" entry). All figures in §2 are intraday snapshots from third-party sources, not `price_cache`-confirmed closes; `price_cache`'s most recent settled data remains 2026-07-15.
