### 2. Today's session so far (intraday, unconfirmed — market open, not a close)

**WDC — the headline story: trading below its logged $480 invalidation level for the first time.** As of ~12:36pm ET, WDC had declined **-10.33% to $460.75** (Benzinga, 2026-07-16, https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60504724/why-is-western-digital-stock-falling-on-thursday), with the same source citing an intraday support level of $481 (broken) and resistance at $525. A separate, differently-timed pull put WDC's intraday range at $470.00–$498.72 (search-aggregated quote data, 2026-07-16) — the two sources roughly agree the stock is trading in the high-$450s to high-$490s today, all of it beneath its 20-day MA (-20.5%) and 50-day MA (-12.9%) per Benzinga's technical read. Drivers: (1) a continuation of yesterday's Kioxia NAND-merger-talk shock; (2) a fresh, broader memory-sector scare from **ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT)** filing for an approximately **$8.6–10 billion Shanghai IPO**, reviving fears of DRAM oversupply and intensifying Chinese memory competition — SK Hynix fell as much as -13% (FX Leaders, 2026-07-16, https://www.fxleaders.com/news/2026/07/16/semiconductor-stocks-flip-again-sk-hynix-loses-13/) or -7% (24/7 Wall St., 2026-07-16, https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/16/sk-hynix-and-sandisk-sink-7-micron-falls-5-as-chinas-cxmt-readies-an-8-6b-memory-ipo/, sources vary on the exact percentage), SanDisk -6% to -7%, Micron -3% to -5%. WDC's next earnings remain scheduled for **2026-08-05**.

**Broader semiconductor complex — a second, distinct pressure point: TSMC's capex guidance spooked rather than reassured.** TSMC reported record Q2 revenue and **raised its 2026 capex guidance to $60–64 billion** (up from prior guidance), but the stock still fell **-3.9%** as investors read the bigger capex number as a valuation/sustainability risk rather than a demand-confirmation signal (24/7 Wall St., 2026-07-16, https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/16/live-nasdaq-composite-chip-stocks-buckle-under-capex-pressure-as-markets-hunt-leadership/). Arm Holdings fell **-4%** to **-5.83%** (sources vary by time-of-day snapshot), Infineon **-2.8%**, and the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) **-2.2%** (Investing.com/TradingKey, 2026-07-16). Separately, Micron, AMD, SanDisk, Intel, and Broadcom were each down **~3%** as of the midday Yahoo Finance market recap (2026-07-16, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-thursday-july-16-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-103116735.html).

**Broad indices — a mixed, chip-led-down session.** As of midday: **S&P 500 -0.3%, Nasdaq Composite -0.9%, Dow Jones roughly flat** (pared earlier gains), per the same Yahoo Finance live blog. UnitedHealth Group **+7%** on an earnings beat and raised full-year guidance was the Dow's main offsetting support — not a held name in this portfolio. Away from tech: Uber **+3%** on a $14.8B acquisition of Delivery Hero, Eli Lilly **-1%** after acquiring AtaiBeckly for $3.8B — both macro-context items, neither held.

**BE — a positive fundamental catalyst today, price move not yet confirmed.** Industrial Development Funding (IDF) and Oaktree announced a **$1.7 billion** project investment specifically to fund Bloom Energy fuel-cell buildout for Nebius's AI-cloud infrastructure — Morgan Stanley served as tax-equity investor/placement agent, MUFG Bank provided senior debt (GuruFocus/StockTitan, 2026-07-16, https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8962588/bloom-energy-be-secures-17b-investment-for-ai-cloud-infrastructure; https://www.stocktitan.net/news/BE/industrial-development-funding-and-oaktree-announce-1-7-billion-4ccqgjrjvhtr.html). This extends a broader IDF/Bloom collaboration now totaling over $2.6B in projects. No confirmed same-day % price move was found in this pass — `mom_roa_top1_paper` (control, 378.49 BE shares) remains the roster's worst-performing sleeve as of the last official mark (-7.77%, 07-14).

**AEHR — normal profit-taking after Tuesday/Wednesday's earnings-driven surge.** Down **-7.55%** to **~$81.57** today (from Wednesday's $87.79 close), per a live quote pull (2026-07-16). This follows a +21.91% regular-session pop 07-15 and a +40%+ after-hours spike 07-14 on the FY26 Q4 beat and FY27 guidance ($130-150M vs. $85M consensus) — no new negative news found, consistent with a pullback after a parabolic move rather than a reversal of the underlying catalyst. Held across 6 sleeves (`mom_v1/v2_paper`, `mom_roa_6535_paper`, and their `_0701` counterparts).

**AAOI — continuing yesterday's AI-optics de-risking, no new company-specific news.** Fell -12.6% on 07-15 to ~$110 (giving back most of the prior day's Pearland-expansion pop) alongside Coherent and Lumentum, all swept into the same broad AI-hardware risk-off as chip names like Micron and Intel — high beta (~3.69) amplifying the move (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-15/16, https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/applied-optoelectronics-falls-12-coherent-181831720.html). No fresh 07-16-specific catalyst found; today's broad chip weakness (§ above) is a live continuation risk for this name specifically.

**Oil/XLE — still elevated, escalation risk growing.** Brent **-0.37% to $84.63/bbl** today but staying close to one-month highs; WTI holding above $80/bbl after adding >11% over the prior three sessions (Trading Economics, 2026-07-16). US forces conducted fresh airstrikes on Iranian missile-storage/launch sites near the Strait of Hormuz on 07-15, and reports indicate **President Trump is considering broadening operations, including a possible seizure of Kharg Island** (Iran's primary oil export terminal) — a materially larger escalation than anything reported so far this week (context aggregated from multiple 2026-07-16 oil-market summaries; Bloomberg, https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-15/latest-oil-market-news-and-analysis-for-july-16). Relevant to `sector_top4_paper`'s XLE holding (control only — both LLM sector variants vetoed/cascaded out of XLE on 07-07).

**STX — earnings date reconfirmed at 2026-07-28, not today.** One stale-looking scraper result suggested "Jul 16, 2026" as STX's next earnings date, but this appears to reflect an already-past fiscal Q3 2026 report (which included Q4 guidance of $3.45B ± $100M); a second, independent query (Nasdaq/Barchart, 2026-07-16, https://www.barchart.com/story/news/3272150/what-to-expect-from-seagate-technologys-q4-2026-earnings-report) confirms the actual Q4 FY2026 report lands **2026-07-28** — matching HANDOFF.md and consistent with prior reports. Flagging the discrepancy rather than picking one silently. No 07-16-specific STX news found; analyst price targets remain very elevated (UBS $860, Citi $1,240, BofA $1,150) against a +216% YTD run.

### 3. Catalyst and macro review

**Retail sales + jobless claims — a stronger-than-expected consumer/labor print, read as hawkish for the Fed.** June retail sales rose **+0.2% MoM** (vs. +0.3% consensus) to $768.6B, held back by a -5.3% drop in gasoline-station receipts; ex-gas sales were **+0.7%**, nonstore/e-commerce **+1.9%** (the largest gain among major categories), autos **+1.9%** — a headline miss masking real underlying strength (Census Bureau/CNN, 2026-07-16, https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/16/economy/us-retail-sales-june). Separately, initial jobless claims fell **8,000 to 208,000** (vs. 217,000 consensus) for the week ended 07-11 (Bloomberg, 2026-07-16, https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-16/us-jobless-claims-decline-to-208-000-below-economist-forecasts). Combined, markets read this as evidence the labor market and consumer remain resilient — reinforcing bets toward a hawkish Fed (no 2026 cuts, per the ~69%-odds figure cited in yesterday's report around Warsh's Senate testimony) rather than near-term easing.

**TSMC's Q2 beat + capex raise — a "good news is bad news" reaction that set today's semiconductor tone.** TSMC posted record Q2 revenue and raised 2026 capex guidance to $60-64B, but the market read the bigger number as a signal of capex-sustainability risk (are hyperscalers/foundries overbuilding?) rather than confirmed demand strength — TSM itself fell -3.9% despite the beat (24/7 Wall St., 2026-07-16). This is the direct read-through driver of today's broad semiconductor selloff (§2) and compounds the ChangXin-IPO-driven memory-specific selloff hitting WDC hardest.

**Oil/Iran escalation risk — see §2.** The reported consideration of a Kharg Island operation, if it materializes, would be a materially larger step than the naval-blockade/coastal-strikes actions taken so far this week.

### 4. LLM overlay status

**Stock arm — the most consequential single data point in this report series.** `llm_cascade_top1_paper`'s sole holding, WDC, is trading **below its $480 logged invalidation level** (2026-07-07 `llm_overlay_log`, id=10) for the first time — intraday prints as low as $460.75 (Benzinga, 12:36pm ET) or $470.00 (a separate aggregated quote), both under the stop. Three open questions this report cannot resolve on its own, stated honestly rather than guessed at:

1. **Whether this survives to the close.** WDC could recover intraday (as it partially did after Monday's -4.64% dip before Tuesday's +6.72% pre-market pop, per the 07-14 report) — today's close level, not the intraday low, is what matters for the settled-close stop check.
2. **Whether tonight's `daily.bat` run (5:15pm) evaluates against a 07-16 close at all.** The 07-15 coverage check was still PENDING as of the last confirmed run (07-15 17:18 CDT) — if the same lagging-publication pattern seen on 07-10/07-13/07-14/07-15 repeats, 07-16 may also land PENDING tonight, in which case the "last settled" close the stop check evaluates against could still be 07-15's $513.84 (above the $480 stop) rather than today's live level.
3. **Whether tonight is mechanically the first live firing of the fix at all.** Per HANDOFF.md (record BZ/CA, 2026-07-15): the invalidation-stop enforcement was DORMANT since deployment until fixed 2026-07-15, with **"first live run of the new path: 2026-07-16 5:15pm."** If it fires as designed tonight and WDC's settled close is confirmed below $480, this would be the first-ever automated exit test for either LLM-overlay sleeve. This report takes no position on the outcome — it will be verifiable in tonight's `var/last_daily_run.log` and `llm_overlay_log`.

`mom_roa_top1_paper` (BE control) is unaffected by today's WDC-specific stress; its own catalyst today (the $1.7B Nebius financing news, §2) is fundamentally positive but has no confirmed price impact yet. `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` (cash treatment) remains flat and uninvolved — worth noting explicitly, since a WDC stop-out tonight (if it happens) would be exactly the kind of idiosyncratic single-name risk the veto-to-cash design is meant to sidestep, distinct from the cascade design that took on this exposure by walking past the veto.

**Sector arm — no confirmed same-day developments.** No 07-16-specific live quotes were found in this pass for XLB, XLI, XLK, or XLV individually (search results returned mostly YTD/2026-H1 performance commentary, not today's price action) — reported as not found rather than estimated. The closest logged invalidation level heading into today was XLB at $49.50 (07-15 close $50.50, ~2% cushion); no information available to say whether that cushion held, widened, or narrowed today.

### 5. Risk flags and upcoming events

- **WDC trading below its $480 invalidation stop intraday — the top item to watch tonight.** See §4 for the specific open questions (close level, coverage status, whether the fix fires as designed). This is the single most consequential development for the LLM-overlay experiment since it launched.
- **The broader memory/semiconductor selloff has a new, distinct driver today** (ChangXin's Shanghai IPO filing) on top of yesterday's Kioxia-merger-talk shock and last week's SK Hynix-guidance rout — three separate catalysts hitting overlapping names (WDC, Micron, SanDisk, SK Hynix) inside one week. Worth tracking as compounding, not redundant, risk.
- **STX earnings date confirmed 2026-07-28** (resolving a stale-data discrepancy flagged in §2) — same day as BE's earnings per HANDOFF, a real binary-event cluster for the control sleeve and the broader momentum book.
- **Oil/Iran escalation risk has grown**: reported consideration of a Kharg Island operation (§2/§3) would be a step-change from the naval-blockade actions priced in so far — relevant to `sector_top4_paper`'s XLE holding.
- **A `TradingMorningMTM` catch-up run was in progress at report time** (started ~13:01 CDT) — purely a price-refresh/coverage-healing operation per its design (HANDOFF.md), not expected to write any MTM or NAV changes; flagged for completeness, not as a concern.
- **Monthly rebalance** next fires 2026-08-01 per the `monthy-llm-rebalance` scheduled task; no activity since 07-01.
- **No new `llm_overlay_log`/`sector_overlay_log` entries since 2026-07-07** — confirmed via direct query through report time.

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. If tonight's stop fires on WDC, it will be the first real-world test of both the LLM-overlay experiment's risk-control mechanism AND the newly-deployed stop-enforcement fix, on the same day — worth flagging as a coincidence of timing, not by design.** The fix (record BZ/CA) was deployed 07-15 specifically because the original mechanism had been silently dormant since inception; its first scheduled live evaluation happens to land on the same day WDC first breaches its logged level. Whatever happens tonight is a genuine out-of-sample data point on whether the mechanism works as intended — not something this report can pre-judge.

**B. The memory/semiconductor complex has now produced three distinct shocks to the same overlapping tickers inside one week** (SK Hynix guidance 07-13, Kioxia-merger-talk 07-15, ChangXin IPO 07-16) — each with a different underlying cause (execution/supply, M&A/business-mix, competitive-oversupply) but the same handful of names absorbing all three. This reinforces the point raised in yesterday's report: single-name concentration in the LLM-cascade design carries meaningfully more compounding idiosyncratic risk than the diversified systematic sleeves it's benchmarked against, and today is the clearest illustration yet.

**C. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** Retail sales, jobless claims, TSMC's capex guidance, and the individual stock catalysts (WDC, BE, AEHR, AAOI, oil/Iran) are all within the scope of exposures already embedded in the current 27-sleeve roster. The standing open process items are: (1) whether tonight's stop-enforcement fix fires as designed on WDC (§4, the report's central open question), and (2) the still-unresolved coverage-lag pattern that has left 07-15/07-16 without confirmed closing NAVs at report time — both operational items for Evan to check after tonight's `daily.bat` run, not research findings that argue for a new strategy.

Sector arm — no stops breached; XLK's cushion to its $172 stop narrowed to ~3.2% (from ~5.6% on 07-15) as Technology led today's sector losses.

(2) 2026-07-16 NAV is still officially PENDING (price_cache coverage 8,826/~9,723 tickers at query time, below the 5,000-floor auto-mark gate — actually above it, see §0 for the apparent contradiction with the 17:28 anomaly-scan reading of 4,414). (3) EACO (held in `residual_roa_6535_paper`) has shown an unchanged $101.01 close for 7+ consecutive trading days and posted NOCLOSE again today — a stale-price watch item, not a new bug.

### 0. Data status and methodology note

This report is compiled 2026-07-16 ~18:15 CDT (~19:15 ET), roughly 2h15m after the 4:00pm ET close — genuinely post-market this time (the same-day AM report published earlier today was mid-session, ~1pm CDT). `daily.bat` ran on schedule at 17:19–17:33 CDT (`var/last_daily_run.log`): overlay/sector-overlay stop checks ran `--settled` (priced as-of 2026-07-15, the last settled day), `mtm_catchup` found 2026-07-16 coverage at 4,414 tickers (below the 5,000-auto-mark floor) and left it **PENDING**, and `verify_run` passed 27/27 sleeves through 07-15 (`var/verify_report.log`). By the time this report queried `price_cache` directly (~18:05 CDT), coverage for 2026-07-16 had grown to **8,826 rows** (vs 9,723 for 07-15) — apparently past the 5,000 floor, but the day was already marked PENDING by the earlier run and will only get an official `paper_nav` row once a subsequent `mtm_catchup` pass (tomorrow's `daily.bat`, per the self-healing design in HANDOFF.md) re-evaluates it. **This report is READ-ONLY and does not run any MTM, rebalance, or catch-up script** — per its own rules — so all 07-16 figures below are computed directly from `price_cache` closes joined against each sleeve's actual open positions and cash (both read from `var/trades.db`), explicitly labeled "unofficial/estimate," never treated as the settled mark. One held ticker, EACO, has no 07-16 close (flagged by `check_anomalies` at 17:28 CDT: "NOCLOSE EACO no close on 2026-07-16 [HELD residual_roa_6535_paper]") and is excluded from that one sleeve's estimate (immaterial — EACO's cost basis is a small fraction of the 50-name book).

- Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) fell ~3%; SMH −2.2%, TSM −3.9%, ARM −4%, Infineon −2.8%, AVGO −2.15%, NVDA −2.81%. In Asia: SK Hynix −11% in Seoul (reversing the prior session's +8%), Samsung −8%+. [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/16/sk-hynix-falls-amid-asia-tech-rout-tracking-us-semiconductor-losses.html), [FX Leaders](https://www.fxleaders.com/news/2026/07/16/semiconductor-stocks-flip-again-sk-hynix-loses-13/), 2026-07-16.

- Macro data (both released this morning, ahead of the earlier same-day report): retail sales +0.2% m/m (prior revised up to +1.0%); initial jobless claims 208K (beat vs. 216K consensus). [Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/retail-sales-jobless-claims-and-manufacturing-data-due-thursday-93CH-4794041), [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/16/us-treasury-yields-wall-street-inflation-employment-data.html), 2026-07-16. Three Fed speakers today — Dallas Fed's Lorie Logan (12:30pm ET), KC Fed's Jeff Schmid (1:25pm ET), Fed Governor Philip Jefferson (7:00pm ET, after this report's compile time) — no market-moving quotes surfaced in this research pass; reported as **NOT FOUND**, not assumed benign.
- Oil: WTI ~$79.43/bbl (~−0.2%) despite reports of intensified US strikes on Iran overnight — source and exact figures for this specific line could not be pinned to one confirmed outlet in this pass; flagged as lower-confidence than the equity figures above.

- WDC −9.15% ($513.84→$466.81, now below its $480 invalidation level — see §4), GLW −9.19%, MRAM −10.52%, MRVL −8.71%, ACMR −8.56%, AAOI −8.11% (second straight double-digit-adjacent day), RFIL −8.10%, CIEN −7.09%, MU −5.65%, DELL −5.16%, EWTX −6.47%, POWL −4.54%, SEZL −4.30%, GOOGL −4.44%, GOOG −4.43%, SPHR −3.40%, CELC −3.52% (third straight down day, see below), AEHR −4.09% (giving back part of yesterday's +21.9% spike), VELO −7.72%, ASTC −5.16%, ANAB −2.99%.
- Relative outperformers: AAPL +1.76% (second straight up day, bucking the tech selloff), DAVE +0.99%, MQ +2.99%.

**Ticker-specific research:**
- **WDC**: today's incremental drop (on top of yesterday's) is attributed by Benzinga (2026-07-16, 12:36pm ET) to "broad, sector-wide selloff in semiconductor and AI memory stocks driven by massive profit-taking" plus ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) confirming a **July 27, 2026** Shanghai STAR Market IPO date seeking ~$8.6B — Asia's largest 2026 IPO and the largest-ever Chinese A-share semiconductor offering. CXMT's DRAM market share has reportedly tripled y/y to ~8% (Counterpoint Research) versus Micron's ~22%, reigniting DRAM-oversupply fears across the whole memory complex (Micron −5%, SK Hynix ADR −7%, SanDisk −7%, WDC −7% intraday per this source's own snapshot). No new Kioxia-merger-specific news dated 07-16 was found — that story remains dated 07-15; today's move reads as CXMT-driven, not a reversal of the merger-talk thesis. BofA's Wamsi Mohan (cited in the same Benzinga piece) maintains a constructive stance: tight NAND supply/demand persisting through 2027, pricing resilient through at least mid-2027. Technical levels cited: resistance $525, support $481 — i.e., Benzinga's own intraday snapshot ($460.75, −10.33%, as of 12:36pm ET) already had WDC below that support/near our $480 stop several hours before today's close; our price_cache close of $466.81 is consistent with that intraday read (small variance expected between an intraday snapshot and the final settle). [Benzinga](https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60504724/why-is-western-digital-stock-falling-on-thursday), [24/7 Wall St](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/16/sk-hynix-and-sandisk-sink-7-micron-falls-5-as-chinas-cxmt-readies-an-8-6b-memory-ipo/), [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/news/china-s-changxin-memory-technologies-sets-july-27-listing-date-sources-say-ce7f5edcdc8bf420), all 2026-07-16.
- **BE (Bloom Energy)**: fell hard today — Benzinga (2026-07-16, 1:22pm ET) put it at −12.11% to $210.39 intraday; our price_cache close shows −13.64% to $206.73 (again, intraday vs. settled-close variance, same direction/magnitude). Cause: investors "digesting short-seller allegations" from Hunterbrook Research and Crossroads Capital alleging BE understated its reliance on Chinese-sourced scandium (an input for its solid-oxide fuel cells). BE has rejected the allegations in an SEC filing, stating it has sufficient non-China-dependent scandium-oxide supply for up to 25 GW/yr of production. The article explicitly notes that Tuesday/Wednesday's ($1.7B Oaktree/Industrial Development Funding AI-cloud-infrastructure investment) news "failed to provide meaningful support" against the short-seller pressure and broader risk-off tape. BE is now ~33% off its 2026 high; next hard catalyst is Q2 earnings on 2026-07-28. [Benzinga](https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/movers/26/07/60506579/whats-going-on-with-bloom-energy-shares-on-thursday), [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/bloom-energy-biggest-selloff-months-061829587.html), both 2026-07-16.
- **AEHR Test Systems**: −7.55% per Simply Wall St's same-day framing (our price_cache shows −4.09%; direction agrees, magnitude differs by source/timestamp) — this follows the huge +21.9%/+21.91% pop after AEHR's 2026-07-14 Q4 beat (revenue $18.84M vs. $18.69M consensus, record bookings $60.7M, FY27 guidance of $130–150M / +160–200% y/y). Today's pullback reads as valuation reassessment/profit-taking after the post-earnings spike ("passes only 1 of 6 valuation checks" per the source) rather than any new negative catalyst — none was found for 07-16 specifically. [Simply Wall St](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/semiconductors/nasdaq-aehr/aehr-test-systems/news/aehr-test-systems-aehr-faces-an-overvalued-view-is-the-pullb), 2026-07-16; earnings context via [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/07/15/why-aehr-test-systems-stock-is-up-today/), 2026-07-15.
- **CELC (Celcuity)**: down −17.60% (07-15) then −3.52% more today, ahead of a confirmed **July 17, 2026** PDUFA goal date (tomorrow) for gedatolisib in HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wild-type advanced breast cancer (Priority Review). No news item dated specifically 2026-07-16 was found (no leak, analyst note, or FDA signal) — today's move reads as continued pre-decision de-risking, not a new catalyst. Held by `mom_v1_paper`, `mom_v2_paper`, `mom_v1_0701_paper`, and `mom_v2_0701_paper` — the largest single-name decliner in the momentum universe both yesterday and today, ahead of tomorrow's FDA decision. [StockTitan](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/CELC/8-k-celcuity-inc-reports-material-event-a18ca0c4ce46.html), [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/celcuity-ceo-sets-july-17-044249843.html).
- **GOOGL/GOOG**: reversed yesterday's +3.2%/+3.6% gain with a −4.44%/−4.43% drop today on a Bloomberg report that Alphabet is behind schedule delivering Gemini 3.5 Pro, its next flagship AI model. [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-thursday-july-16-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-chip-stocks-slide-103116735.html), 2026-07-16.
- **GLW, MRAM, MRVL, ACMR, RFIL, CIEN, MU, DELL, EWTX, SEZL, POWL**: no company-specific 07-16 catalyst was found beyond being swept into the broad semiconductor/memory/tech-hardware selloff described above (TSMC capex guide + CXMT IPO). Reported as such, not invented.

**Stop watch:**
- **WDC / $480 (llm_cascade_top1_paper)**: cached 07-16 close $466.81 is below the $480 level logged on 2026-07-07 (score 6, "Multi-year HDD supply contracts... stop below 50-DMA pullback"). **However, this level is a logged reference point only — the cascade sleeve has no automated stop-enforcement mechanism** (confirmed by reading `scripts/momentum/llm_overlay_ops.py`: it only checks and can only exit `llm_overlay.OVERLAY_STRATEGY` — i.e., `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` — never the cascade strategy; `daily.bat` calls the same two ops scripts, `llm_overlay_ops` and `sector_overlay_ops`, neither of which targets any `llm_cascade_*` sleeve). So even if 07-16's close settles at $466.81, no automatic sell will occur on `llm_cascade_top1_paper` tonight or any future night under the current pipeline — this is a design characteristic of the "always-invested cascade" concept (per HANDOFF.md: it "walks down the ranking past a veto instead of going to cash," with no stated stop-out behavior for an already-held name), not a bug, but worth Evan explicitly confirming is the intended behavior given the position is down ~19.9% from its 07-10 peak NAV mark.
- **Sector overlay (`llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper`)**: stops for its three held ETFs (XLK $172, XLI $170, XLB $49.50) enforced nightly via `sector_overlay_ops --settled`, ran clean tonight at 07-15 prices (XLB $50.50 > $49.50, XLI $180.06 > $170.00, XLK $181.58 > $172.00 — all holding, per `var/last_daily_run.log`). Today's (07-16) price_cache closes: XLK $177.52 (cushion to stop narrowed to ~3.2%, from ~5.6% on 07-15 — Technology led today's sector losses), XLI $180.15 (cushion ~6.0%, essentially flat), XLB $50.89 (cushion ~2.8%, actually widened — Materials bounced slightly today after 07-15's dip). None breached.
- **Stock overlay (`llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper`)**: no open position to check — confirmed all-cash since the 07-01 reset.

### 4. LLM overlay treatment vs. control

**Stock arm** (`mom_roa_top1_paper` control vs. `llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper` treatment vs. `llm_cascade_top1_paper` cascade):
- Control has held BE since 07-07 (100% weight, entry $263.94, 378.49 sh). NAV path: $100,000 (07-06) → $102,130 (07-07 peak) → $96,347 (07-08) → $97,380 (07-09) → $92,683 (07-10) → $88,474 (07-13) → $92,225 (07-14) → $90,704 (07-15, official) → **est. $78,346 (07-16, unofficial, −13.6% day)**. That's a ~23.3% drawdown from the 07-07 peak in seven trading days.
- Treatment (stock overlay) VETO'd BE on both 07-01 and again 07-07 (score 4, logged rationale "dup-check" on the 07-07 re-evaluation) and has held **zero positions, 100% cash, flat at exactly $100,000** every single day since. Versus control's est. −21.7% from $100,000 to $78,346 today, that is the single widest control/treatment divergence since this experiment's 07-06 reset — a real, if small-n (one ticker, one veto), data point in the treatment's favor. Total logged stock-arm decisions: 8, since 2026-05-29 — nowhere near the kill-switch threshold (30 picks / 12 months).
- Cascade (WDC, walks past a veto instead of cash): NAV path $100,000 (07-06) → $100,805 → $104,249 → $109,501 (07-09) → **$110,360 (07-10, all-time high)** → $105,243 (07-13) → $106,713 (07-14) → $97,349 (07-15, the −8.78% WDC day) → **est. $88,448 (07-16, −9.1% day)**. That's a ~19.9% drawdown from the 07-10 peak in four trading days — see the stop-watch note in §3 on why this position has no mechanical stop to arrest further decline.

**Sector arm** (`sector_top4_paper` control vs. `llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper` treatment vs. `llm_cascade_sector4_paper` cascade): control holds XLE/XLK/XLI/XLB; treatment vetoed XLE (rationale, 2026-07-07: "below 50-DMA... EIA demand forecast down 1.1mb/d... fragile geopolitical prop" — score 3) and sits in cash for that 25% slot, holding only XLK/XLI/XLB; cascade chained past the same XLE veto down to XLV (rank #5, "Cascade-only pick... capital rotating out of overextended tech... into healthcare," score 7) and holds all four sector slots (XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV). Since-inception (07-15 official): control +0.78%, treatment −0.36%, cascade −1.35% — control currently ahead of both LLM variants, the opposite pattern from the stock arm. Today's (07-16) XLE close was $57.02 (vs. control's veto point) — up modestly, so the vetoed slot would have been a small net positive today had the LLM overlay not stepped aside; XLV closed $161.80 (up ~2.2%, Health Care's outperformance benefiting the cascade's 4th slot). Total logged sector-arm decisions: 15, since 2026-05-29.

### 5. Week/period summary (07-09 → 07-15, official marks — the most recent full week available)

The past week cleanly separates the roster by design, not luck:
- **Raw momentum sleeves hit hardest**: llm_cascade_top1 −11.10%, mom_v2_0701 −7.51%, mom_roa_top1 −6.86%, mom_v2 −6.74%, mom_v1_0701 −6.46%, mom_roa_6535_0701 −6.46%, mom_v1 −6.22%, mom_roa_6535 −5.61%.
- **Residual-signal sleeves (weight ladder + residual_roa_6535) far more resilient**: −0.71% to −1.62% across all 11 residual-family sleeves, roughly a quarter to a third of the raw-momentum drawdown over the same week.
- **Flat-to-positive**: llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1 (cash) +0.00%, sector_top4_full +0.09%, sector_top4 +0.22%, both SPY benchmarks +0.41%.
- This is the clearest week-over-week illustration yet of the residual-momentum thesis (idiosyncratic/residual signal should carry less single-factor/single-name concentration risk than raw price momentum) — consistent with, not a new finding beyond, the original residual_roa_6535 backtest case for lower drawdown (HANDOFF.md backtest table: residual_roa_6535 held-out maxDD −20.1% vs. mom_v2's −34.8%).

### 6. Strategic insights — structural edge or new sleeve concepts?

**A. The residual-momentum family's live drawdown resilience this week (§5) is a genuine, if still-short, forward data point supporting its designed edge** — not a new finding, but the sharpest real-time confirmation since the 06-09 deployment decision (record: residual_roa_6535 beats mom_roa_6535 on return+Sharpe+DD in both backtest windows). A week of a real semiconductor-driven selloff hitting raw-momentum sleeves 4-7x harder than the residual-signal sleeves is exactly the kind of out-of-sample stress period the original backtest thesis predicted.

**B. The stock-level LLM overlay experiment produced its most striking single data point yet today.** Treatment (cash, VETO'd BE twice) vs. control (BE, down an estimated ~23% from its 07-07 peak) is now a ~21-22 percentage-point NAV gap on one ticker. This is still n=1 decision and n=8 total logged picks — nowhere near statistically meaningful, and the kill-switch design (30 picks/12 months) exists precisely because early divergences like this can reverse. But it is the single clearest illustration since inception of what the experiment is designed to test.

**C. The llm_cascade_top1_paper design gap (§3/§4) is worth flagging as a process item, not a sleeve-concept idea.** An "always-invested, walk-past-the-veto" cascade concept that holds a name through a stop-level breach with no mechanical exit is a coherent design choice (it's explicitly not meant to have the overlay's veto-and-cash behavior), but it means WDC's current position has no risk control beyond the next LLM re-evaluation cycle (which, per the log history, has run roughly monthly at rebalance, not on stop breaches). Worth Evan confirming this is intended before the position potentially deepens further.

**D. No new sleeve concept is proposed from today's data.** TSMC's capex-guidance reaction, the CXMT IPO date, the BE short-seller allegations, and the GOOGL Gemini-3.5-delay report are all within the scope of exposures already embedded in the current 27-sleeve roster (momentum universes hold WDC/MU/GOOGL-class names broadly; the stock-arm experiment already holds BE directly). The standing open items are: (1) whether Evan wants a stop mechanism added to the cascade design given today's WDC breach (§4/C), (2) the still-pending 07-16 official NAV mark (§0) — expected to resolve on a future `mtm_catchup` pass per the self-healing pipeline design, and (3) the EACO stale-price watch item (§0), which mirrors the pattern that produced the BKGM ghost-price finding in the 2026-06-01 audit and may be worth a manual check the next time Evan is in the DB.
